sections.label: 🧹 Cleanup
items: Remove unused _eplGlyphs arrow function (defined in the Pinned Filter List editor closure but never called — _eplRender uses plain ✓ checkmarks instead of HTML badge spans)
Remove unused _relFetchFaviconUrl function (dead code; also used GM_xmlhttpRequest which was replaced by fetch() in 9.99.586)

sections.label: 📝 Documentation
items: Add JSDoc to updateSubTableCollapseButton: documents collapse-button visibility logic and highlight tinting
Add JSDoc to _syncGfClearBtn: documents global-filter ✕ button visibility sync
Add JSDoc to _initRelTooltipListeners: documents idempotent tooltip delegation installer
Add JSDoc to initRelationshipsColumn: documents full execution flow (cache hierarchy, cell injection, WS2 fetch)
Add JSDoc to _artInitInlinePics: documents CAA/EAA inline thumbnail injection with ctx/cacheBust/targetTable params

Resync ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt against current codebase: add FILTER STATUS DISPLAY section (✓ Filtered N rows [GLOBAL/SUB-TABLE/COLUMN FILTER format]), add HIDDEN-MATCH INDICATOR ON MULTI-ROW COLLAPSE BUTTONS section, expand RELATIONSHIPS COLUMN section with rich filter-aware tooltip and filter-match highlighting

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix artist-releasegroups (and all other fetched pages) returning 0 tables on Firefox for Android: root cause identified via console fetch() test — a page-context fetch() to the same URL returned 425 KB of correct HTML with tables, while GM_xmlhttpRequest returned different content with no tables; GM_xmlhttpRequest in Tampermonkey for Firefox Android runs in the extension background context which does NOT share the foreground tab's cookie jar, so MusicBrainz served a different response; fixed by replacing GM_xmlhttpRequest with native fetch(url, {credentials:'same-origin'}) in fetchHtml(), which runs in the page context sharing the same session cookies and network stack as normal browser navigation; also reverts the incorrect navigator.userAgent header workaround added in 9.99.585 which was based on a wrong diagnosis

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix artist-releasegroups fetch returning 0 tables on Firefox for Android (Tampermonkey): GM_xmlhttpRequest was sending the raw Android mobile User-Agent instead of the browser's active UA; when 'Request Desktop Site' is enabled in Firefox, navigator.userAgent is overridden to a desktop UA for normal page loads but GM_xmlhttpRequest did not inherit that override, causing MusicBrainz to serve a mobile-layout page with no <table class="tbl"> elements; fixed by explicitly forwarding navigator.userAgent as the 'User-Agent' header in every GM_xmlhttpRequest call in fetchHtml()

Replace blocking alert('No tables found to resize') in toggleAutoResizeColumns() with a Lib.warn()-only path; the alert was confusing on mobile and could appear as a side-effect of any empty-fetch condition (e.g. mobile UA mismatch) rather than as a direct result of a user resize action

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix spurious filter/highlight matches in multi-row cells (Catalog#, Label, …): the 'mb-cell-collapse-toggle' UI widget ('▶ 2 ▤') was included in the filterable text, causing cross-tag matches between the last character(s) of a cell value and the numeric item-count badge (e.g. filter '12' matched trailing '1' of 'DT 0017-1311' plus '2' of the count badge); fixed by adding '.mb-cell-collapse-toggle' to _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL (getCleanColumnText clone-and-strip pass), to the FILTER_REJECT list in the highlightCrossTag TreeWalker, and to the secondary guard in getCleanColumnText's own TreeWalker

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix per-h3 filter-status going blank after pressing the H3 sub-table 'Clear all filters' button (.mb-subtable-clear-btn) when a global filter is still active: replaced the dispatchEvent('input') call (which scheduled a debounced applySubFilter() that fired after runFilter() and wiped the status span) with a direct DOM lookup of the STF ✕ button (id ending in '-clear' inside .mb-stf-input-wrap) to hide it without triggering applySubFilter()

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Remove unconditional filter-status overwrite ('✓ All column filters cleared') triggered by the H3 sub-table 'Clear all filters' button (clearSubTableColumnFilters), which wiped the filter status display even when a global filter was still active; debug log message for the button press is retained

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Remove unconditional filter-status overwrite ('✓ All filters cleared' / '✓ All column filters cleared') triggered by the 'Clear ALL column filters' and 'Clear ALL filters' buttons in the H2 header bar, which incorrectly replaced the current filter status even when filters were still active; debug log messages for both button presses are retained

sections.label: 🧹 Cleanup
items: Reorder configuration section
Add licenses from userscripts from which functionality has been incorporated
Change @name in userscript header

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Filter/sort status display — three format changes applied consistently to both single-table and multi-table modes: (1) Sort status: column names now use single quotes instead of double quotes — '"Country/Date"▲' → '\'Country/Date\'▲' — for both multi-sort (mb-sort-status span) and single-sort (Sorted by:) paths in both multi-table per-h3 and single-table main status displays. Also fixed the multi-table single-sort format to use the 'Sorted by:' form with parenthesised row count, matching the already-correct single-table single-sort path. (2) Filter status: column names in COLUMN FILTER entries no longer include multi-sort superscript suffixes (¹ ² ³ etc.) — the col-name strip regex at all filter-status build sites is extended to also remove Unicode superscript digits (⁰¹²³⁴⁵⁶⁷⁸⁹) so '\'Title ¹\':"II"' renders as '\'Title\':"II"'. (3) Single-table filter status: changed '✓ Global filter: N rows in Xms' to '✓ Filtered N rows in Xms' to be symmetric with the multi-table per-h3 format. The mode label (Regexp filter, Case-sensitive filter, etc.) that previously appeared as the prefix is now dropped — the filter details inside the brackets already convey that information via the mode flags.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Standardise filter status display format across single-table and multi-table modes: (1) Filter type labels are now UPPERCASE — 'GLOBAL:', 'SUB-TABLE:', 'N COLUMN FILTER(S)' — making them visually distinct from the quoted filter values. (2) Column filter entries are now always symmetric regardless of count — '1 COLUMN FILTER [\'Format\':"vinyl"]' uses the same bracketed list format as '2 COLUMN FILTERS [\'Format\':"vinyl\', \'Country\':"JP"]'. (3) Column names are always enclosed in single quotes — '\'Format\':\"vinyl\"' — distinguishing them from the double-quoted filter values. All three changes are applied consistently to both call sites in multi-table mode (runFilter per-h3 block and applySubFilter per-h3 block) and the single-table filterParts / filterInfo block.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add sub-table filter (STF) info to the per-h3 filter status span in multi-table mode. Previously the span (class mb-filter-status, sibling to the mb-sort-status span in the h3 header) only showed column filter details and was completely blank when only a global or STF filter was active. The status now mirrors the single-table h2 format: '✓ Filtered N rows [global:"x", sub-table:"y", ColA:"z"]', listing all active filter layers for this specific sub-table. Mode flags (case, rx, ex) are appended in parentheses when active: e.g. 'sub-table:(rx) "jp.*"'. Single column filter shown inline ('ColA:"val"'), multiple as '2 col filters [ColA:"v1", ColB:"v2"]'. The update happens in two call sites: runFilter() (covers global/column filter changes that do not trigger applySubFilter) and applySubFilter() at the end of step 5 (covers STF-only filtering which never calls runFilter). The status is cleared in both the !raw early-return branch and clearSubFilter().

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix 'mb-subtable-clear-btn' (Clear all filters) and 'mb-clear-all-filters-btn' (Clear ALL filters) buttons remaining visible after clearing the sub-table filter via Delete/Backspace keystroke: the applySubFilter !raw early-return branch (added in 9.99.575) was missing the window.updateFilterButtonsVisibility() call that clearSubFilter() performs. Added the call to the !raw branch so all three clear paths (Escape key, ✕ button, and Delete/Backspace) correctly hide those buttons when no filter is active.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix CAA/EAA bigbox (image stripe) not restoring all images after clearing the sub-table filter (STF) on multi-table pages: after filtering (e.g. 'jp' → 3 images shown), clearing the filter correctly restored all 4 rows but the bigbox still showed only 3 images (the non-matching row's anchor kept display:none from the previous applySubFilter pass). Root cause: two separate clear paths both failed to call caaUpdateBigBoxForTable / eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable. (1) The STF ✕ clear button click handler called filterInput.dispatchEvent(new Event('input')) which routed through debouncedApply() → applySubFilter(). When applySubFilter runs with an empty input, it takes the !raw early-return branch which restores table rows but has no bigbox sync call — only clearSubFilter() (called by Escape key) performed the bigbox sync. Fix: change the ✕ click handler to call clearSubFilter() directly, matching the Escape key handler. (2) The delete-key/backspace path also went through debouncedApply() → applySubFilter() → !raw branch with no bigbox sync. Fix: add caaUpdateBigBoxForTable / eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable calls to the applySubFilter !raw early-return block so that the bigbox is always resync'd when the filter is cleared regardless of which input method was used.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Single-table sort status: reformat single-column sort to match the multi-sort style — '✓ Sorted by: "Catalog#"▲ (7 rows in 42ms)' instead of the old '✓ Sorted by column "Catalog#" ▲: 7 rows in 42ms'. This makes single and multi-column sort statuses visually consistent.

Single-table filter status: expand the column-filter summary from '2 column filters' to '2 column filters ["Catalog#":"to", "Barcode":"val"]', listing each active column filter's column name and current value. Column names are derived from the thead th elements (sort/unique-values glyphs stripped). Multi-table pages are unchanged — per-table column details continue to appear in the h3 sort-status span.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add syntheticColumnExtractors with extractor 'eventParts' on sourceColumn 'Comment' and syntheticColumns ['Event-Type', 'Event-Date', 'Event-Detail', 'Event-Venue', 'Event-Venue-Detail', 'Event-City', 'Event-State', 'Event-Country'] to five page type definitions: artist-recordings, instrument-recordings, area-recordings-filtered, area-recordings, work-recordings.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Global filter status display: append ' cleared' to the mode label when the global filter string is empty AND no column filters are active, so the status reads '✓ Global filter cleared' (or '✓ Regexp filter cleared', etc.) instead of the ambiguous bare '✓ Global filter'. The suffix is only added when the cleared state is the sole active state — if column filters are still active the label stays as-is so the user knows filtering is still in effect. Applied to both the multi-table path (mb-filter-status-display in the header bar) and the single-table path (where the status also includes row-count and timing).

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement / 🐛 Fix
items: Revert 9.99.570 (caaYesCount/caaNoCount IIFE computation and makeArtItem calls) — it re-introduced the substring false-positive bug fixed in 9.99.569. The correct solution (requested by user after testing) is purely cosmetic: for CAA/EAA columns only, rename the two existing makeSynItem cell-structure labels to artwork-presence equivalents — '○ empty cells' → '✗ no artwork', '▶◀ any multi-row cells' → '✓ has artwork'. The underlying multirow-mode filtering (applyMultiRowMode / testRowMatch isMultiRowFilter path) is completely unchanged; the new labels simply make the intent clearer for image columns. Also remove the now-dead else-if branch that showed the old makeArtItem entries under a separate 'Cell structure' header for non-collapsable CAA/EAA columns (those cases are now fully handled by the renamed makeSynItem entries in the main collapsable block).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix '✓ has artwork' and '✗ no artwork' entries disappearing from the CAA/EAA column unique-values dropdown after 9.99.569 added '.mb-caa-sort-key' to _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL: caaYesCount and caaNoCount were computed from valueCounts.get('yes') and valueCounts.get('no'), but valueCounts is built by iterating getCleanColumnText on each cell — which now strips .mb-caa-sort-key, so 'yes'/'no' are never accumulated and both counts are always 0. Fix: compute caaYesCount and caaNoCount by directly querying '.mb-caa-sort-key' / '.mb-eaa-sort-key' in each visible tbody cell and checking textContent === 'yes' or 'no', bypassing getCleanColumnText entirely.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix CAA/EAA column '✗ no artwork' and '✓ has artwork' quick-filter entries matching false-positive rows: clicking '✗ no artwork' set the column filter value to the plain string 'no', which was then used as a substring match by getCleanColumnText / testRowMatch — matching 'not readable' in image comments, 'no' in sort-key spans, and any other visible text containing 'no'. Fix mirrors the existing mb-inline-art-sort-key sentinel pattern exactly: (1) Add '.mb-caa-sort-key' and '.mb-eaa-sort-key' to _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL so the sentinel values 'yes'/'no' are stripped from getCleanColumnText output and never participate in substring matching. (2) Add both classes to the getCleanVisibleText FILTER_REJECT list for the same reason. (3) Add both classes to the highlightCrossTag FILTER_REJECT list in getCleanColumnText to prevent the highlight pass from inserting highlight spans inside the invisible sort-key span. (4) Add a 'CAA/EAA sort-key bypass' block in testRowMatch immediately after the existing mb-inline-art-sort-key bypass: when the column filter value is 'yes' or 'no' (non-regexp mode), look up .mb-caa-sort-key / .mb-eaa-sort-key in the column cell and compare textContent with an exact-match test, bypassing the normal substring/regex matching. This ensures '✓ has artwork' / '✗ no artwork' match exactly the rows with artwork / without artwork respectively, with no false positives.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix Relationships rich tooltip showing all filter-layer highlights with the same visual style: _serialiseKeySpan was converting every highlight span (mb-global-filter-highlight, mb-subtable-filter-highlight, mb-column-filter-highlight, mb-pre-filter-highlight) to a generic <mark style="..."> element with a single hardcoded yellow/red inline style, so all three layers appeared identical. Fix: preserve the original class name from the DOM span by emitting <span class="{originalClass}"> instead of <mark>. The per-layer CSS rules already defined in the userscript stylesheet then apply naturally inside the tooltip div (which is part of the same DOM), making each filter layer visually distinct without any additional styling code.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix ReferenceError: _ensureRelPlainTooltip is not defined — second missing function from the same 9.99.565 cleanup-script accident. The function _ensureRelPlainTooltip (the singleton creator for div#mb-rel-plain-tooltip) was located between the two splice markers used by the cleanup and was therefore deleted along with the stale duplicates. Restored.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix Relationships column completely empty (cells show MBID but no icons, no data-rel-done attribute) — regression introduced in 9.99.565: the stale-duplicate cleanup script removed the four module-level let declarations (_relTooltipListenersInstalled, _relTooltipTimer, _relTooltipEl, _relPlainTooltipEl) that were between the removed block and _initRelTooltipListeners. Without them, _initRelTooltipListeners() throws ReferenceError: _relTooltipListenersInstalled is not defined on its very first line, which propagates out of the async initRelationshipsColumn() as a rejected promise — aborting the function before any WS2 fetches or cell population can occur. Fix: restore the four let declarations immediately before _initRelTooltipListeners.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix four related Relationships column highlight bugs (all caused by the tooltip and icon-match systems trying to re-compute filter matches independently instead of reading the authoritative highlight spans already stamped in the DOM): (1) Rich tooltip showed only ONE filter layer — _relGetActiveQuery returned the first non-empty layer and ignored the rest, so with global='discogs' + STF='item=' only 'item=' appeared in the tooltip and 'discogs' was silently dropped. (2) Rich tooltip used wrong highlight span class — _relBuildTooltipHTML re-applied the filter string with its own regex, producing generic <mark> instead of preserving the actual mb-global-filter-highlight / mb-subtable-filter-highlight / mb-column-filter-highlight classes that are already correctly stamped in the .mb-rel-filter-key span by the main highlight pipeline. (3) mb-rel-icon-match (red border on matching icons) was permanently cleared when STF changed — applySubFilter step 2 cleared all mb-rel-icon-match markers from anchors, then the STF hit block only restored them for STF matches; the global and column filter markers set by testRowMatch were never restored. Clearing STF left all icons without the red border even though the global filter was still active and matching. (4) Tooltip gate checked wrong condition — _relQueryMatchesCell re-evaluated filter strings from live inputs rather than inspecting the DOM highlight spans. Fix for all four: _relBuildTooltipHTML now serialises the existing .mb-rel-filter-key child nodes directly (_serialiseKeySpan), converting any highlight span of any class to a <mark> — the complete, accurate union of all active filter layers with no re-computation. _relQueryMatchesCell now checks the DOM for any highlight span inside .mb-rel-filter-key (one querySelectorAll, no regex). _relHasAnyActiveFilter provides a cheap first gate (checks inputs are non-empty). applySubFilter step 2 now immediately re-derives mb-rel-icon-match after clearing: it scans each td.mb-rel-cell anchor's .mb-rel-filter-key for existing mb-global-filter-highlight or mb-column-filter-highlight spans and restores the class if found, so the global/column icon state is preserved across STF changes.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement / 🐛 Fix
items: Relationships rich HTML tooltip: show only when filter matches (not merely when any filter is active). Previously the rich tooltip appeared whenever a filter string was present on any layer (global, STF, column), even if that filter matched a different column and left the hovered Relationships cell's URLs completely unhighlighted. New helper _relQueryMatchesCell(cell, filterCtx) checks whether the active filter query matches at least one URL in the hovered cell's .mb-rel-filter-key spans; if it does not match, the custom panels are suppressed entirely and the native browser title tooltip is shown instead (the title attribute is no longer blanked on that hover). The native tooltip is now ONLY suppressed when both conditions are true: a filter is active AND it matches this specific cell.

Relationships rich HTML tooltip: change default colors to white background (#ffffff) and black text (#000000) for both the rich URL-list panel and the plain-text panel. Previously both panels used a dark-navy (#1e1e2e) background with soft-white (#cdd6f4) text. Colors are applied dynamically at show-time (read from settings on each hover) so changes take effect without a page reload.

Add two new settings to the RELATIONSHIPS COLUMN SECTION of configSchema: 'sa_rel_tooltip_bg' (color_picker, default #ffffff) — background color for the rich HTML Relationships tooltip panels; 'sa_rel_tooltip_color' (color_picker, default #000000) — text color for the same panels. Both apply to both the rich URL-list panel and the plain-text anchor-title panel displayed to its right.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Enhancement
items: Fix native browser title tooltip overlapping the rich HTML Relationships tooltip (introduced in 9.99.562): the browser's built-in tooltip appears after its own OS-level delay and renders on top of the rich panel in an unpredictable position. Fix: on mouseenter, blank the anchor's title attribute immediately (storing the original in data-rel-title-saved) so the browser never shows its native tooltip; on mouseleave, restore the original value so keyboard-focus tooltips and subsequent hovers still work. Add a second custom plain-text tooltip panel (div#mb-rel-plain-tooltip, same dark-panel aesthetic) that renders the saved title text and is positioned to the top-right of the rich tooltip (8px gap). If the plain panel would overflow the right edge of the viewport it flips to the left side of the rich panel instead. Both panels are hidden together by a shared _hideAll() helper on mouseleave from either the anchor or the cell.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add rich HTML tooltip popup to Relationships column icon anchors (td.mb-rel-cell a). Previously, hovering an icon showed the browser's native title-attribute tooltip containing the raw URL or 'relType: Title\nURL' text. The new tooltip is a styled dark-panel popup (matching the art bigbox tooltip aesthetic) that lists all relationship URLs present in the hovered cell — one per line — each prefixed with the site favicon. If a filter is active on any layer, occurrences of the filter string within each URL are highlighted in yellow/red (matching the global filter highlight colours). Filter query resolution probes three layers in order, using the first non-empty one: (1) column-level filter on the Relationships column (per-sub-table Rx/Case checkboxes respected), (2) sub-table filter (STF) for the owning h3 (per-sub-table Rx/Case checkboxes respected), (3) global filter (global Rx/Case checkboxes used). Ended relationships are shown with a subtle '(ended)' annotation. The tooltip appears after a 300ms hover delay to avoid flicker, follows the anchor horizontally (clamped to viewport), and is dismissed immediately on mouseleave. Implemented as three new helpers (_ensureRelTooltip, _relGetActiveQuery, _relBuildTooltipHTML) and a body-level capture-phase delegation listener (_initRelTooltipListeners) installed idempotently once from initRelationshipsColumn.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix ghost column (empty grey cells with no header or filter field) appearing as the last column on pageType 'place-performances' (multi-table mode) and potentially any other multi-table page type that uses both injectedColumns with injectedColumnExtractors and the Release-events suppress guard. Root cause was a four-step race: (1) Initial renderGroupedTable call (query='', new-table path) moves source rows from groupedRows into the tbody and calls _suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks, which finds the ICE <th> elements (Release country, Release date, R-DD, R-MM, R-YYYY, R-Day, R-Month), removes them from the thead, and calls tr.deleteCell() on each source row to remove the corresponding mb-ice-cell <td>s. (2) The async initReleaseEventsColumn() callback fires after render, populates mb-re-cell cells, then calls applyInjectedColumnExtractors(). (3) applyInjectedColumnExtractors calls _ensureIceCells on every source row in groupedRows: it sees the rows have 0 mb-ice-cell cells (suppress already removed them) and re-appends all 7 ICE placeholder cells, then _processRow fills them. (4) On the next render (runFilter or view switch), clones of the re-injected source rows carry 7 mb-ice-cell cells; _suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks finds no ICE <th> in the existing thead (they were removed in step 1), so _indicesToRemove is empty and the function returns early without removing the ICE cells from the clones. The 7 orphan cells render as ghost columns beyond the 13 real header cells. Fix: in _suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks step 2, after deleting ICE cells from each row, stamp data-mb-ice-suppressed='1' on the row. In _ensureIceCells inside applyInjectedColumnExtractors, check for this flag and return immediately without re-adding ICE cells. Since cloneNode(true) copies dataset attributes, the flag propagates to all clones produced on subsequent render passes, so the ghost column never reappears regardless of how many filter/sort/view-switch re-renders occur.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Remove the Relationships column from pageType 'place-performances-filtered' (and any other single-table pageType that injects Relationships) when no /release/ or /release-group/ link is present in the table — e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/place/.../performances?direction=1&link_type_id=693. Root cause: _suppressRelationshipsIfNoReleaseOrReleaseGroupLinks() was only called in the multi-table path inside renderGroupedTable(). The single-table path in renderFinalTable() already called the parallel _suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks() but was missing the Relationships guard. Fix: add the missing _suppressRelationshipsIfNoReleaseOrReleaseGroupLinks(mainTable) call in renderFinalTable() immediately after _suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks(), before initRelationshipsColumn() creates mb-rel-cell placeholder <td>s.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix global and column-level filtering in the 'Complete Discography (merged)' view on artist-releasegroups pages: (1) filtering showed only one of the N combined rows (e.g. only the Official match for 'Devils & Dust', missing the Non-Official match); (2) clearing the filter afterwards showed only the Official rows — the merged combination was permanently lost until the user switched views. Root cause: runFilter() iterates groupedRows per-group (group 0 = Album Official: 21 rows, group 1 = Album Non-Official: 2 rows) and builds filteredArray accordingly, then calls renderGroupedTable(filteredArray) which replaces each table's tbody with only that group's matching rows. For the merged view, the first-occurrence 'Album' table's merged tbody (containing all 23 combined rows) was silently replaced with only the 21 Official rows, destroying the merged state. Sub-table filtering was unaffected because applySubFilter operates on the live DOM rows directly without touching groupedRows. Fix: in the runFilter() multi-table forEach loop, when discographyViewState === 'merged', two special cases are applied. (a) Hidden duplicate tables (h3.dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true'): contribute nothing — an empty rows:[] entry is pushed to filteredArray so renderGroupedTable does not touch their hidden tbody, and their row count is subtracted from totalAbsolute to avoid double-counting in the h2 stat. (b) First-occurrence merged tables: reconstruct the combined row source by collecting all groupedRows entries that share the same category name (mirroring the _mergedFirstIdxByName logic in _applyDiscographyViewFilter), then filter against that combined set. After renderGroupedTable runs, a post-render fixup re-stamps data-mb-merged-tbody='true' on the first-occurrence tables so the restore-from-merged pre-pass in _applyDiscographyViewFilter correctly identifies them when the user next switches views.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: When switching discography views on artist-releasegroups pages (📋 Complete / 📀 Official / 📼 Non-Official / 🗂️ Complete merged), all filter, sort, highlight, h3-collapse, and CAA/EAA bigbox state is now unconditionally reset to the initial rendered state before the new view is applied. Previously, active filters, sort orders, and highlight spans from one view leaked into the next, causing wrong row counts, stale highlights, incorrect sort indicators, and visual artefacts in the merged view. The reset is performed by the new helper _resetDiscographyViewState(), called at the top of _applyDiscographyViewFilter() before any DOM manipulation. The eight reset steps are: (1) global filter cleared to prefix-only; (2) all column filter inputs cleared; (3) all sub-table filters (STF) cleared — value, STF-hidden row markers, mb-subtable-filter-highlight spans, border/shadow, container visibility, and toggle-icon active state; (4) brute-force removal of any remaining filter-highlight spans (mb-global-filter-highlight, mb-column-filter-highlight, mb-pre-filter-highlight, mb-subtable-filter-highlight); (5) sort state reset — multi-sort column tints cleared, .sort-icon-active stripped from all sort buttons, superscript priority digits removed, mb-sort-status spans blanked, multiTableSortStates and multiSortTintRegistry cleared, and all sub-table tbody rows re-inserted in the original groupedRows order; (6) runFilter() called to re-render all visible tables with the clean filter state; (7) h3 collapse state reset to initial shouldStayOpen logic (expanded for single sub-tables and small album/official groups, collapsed otherwise); (8) all CAA/EAA bigboxes collapsed (display:none) with dataset.caaVisible/eaaVisible set to 'false' so the h3 click handler and _artRestoreBigboxesForTable see the correct collapsed baseline when the user later expands a sub-table.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix two Unicode character picker keyboard bugs caused by the mb.unicodechars userscript by Smeulf being active on the same MusicBrainz page simultaneously: (1) ArrowUp/Down keys did not navigate the list immediately after opening — only after pressing Escape once; (2) closing the dialog required three Escape presses instead of one. Root cause: mb.unicodechars loads before this script and therefore registers its bubble-phase Ctrl+U and navigation keydown listeners first. When Ctrl+U was pressed, mb.unicodechars opened ITS dialog first (bubble phase, registered earlier), then our bubble-phase handler opened OURS on top — so both dialogs were open simultaneously. With both open, mb.unicodechars navigation handlers fired before ours and called stopImmediatePropagation(), preventing our saUnicodeHandleKey from ever being reached. The three-Escape sequence was: 1st Escape closes mb.unicodechars dialog (their handler fires first, stops propagation), 2nd Escape is caught by the STF/col-filter input Escape handler (clears or blurs, stops propagation), 3rd Escape finally reaches our saUnicodeHandleKey. Fix: move both the Ctrl+U opener and the saUnicodeHandleKey navigation out of the bubble-phase keydown listener and into a new dedicated capture-phase listener (document.addEventListener('keydown', ..., true)). Capture-phase listeners fire before any bubble-phase listener regardless of registration order. saUnicodeHandleKey already calls e.stopImmediatePropagation() for all handled keys (ArrowUp, ArrowDown, Enter, Escape), so once it runs, neither the mb.unicodechars bubble handler nor the STF/col-filter Escape handlers see the event. This is the same strategy already used by the Ctrl+M prefix-key handler to defeat the 'mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL' script's bubble-phase Ctrl+M listener.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add visual 'hidden match' indicator (yellow background / red ▶ glyph) to CAA/EAA art column expand buttons ([data-caa-expand-btn]) when any filter layer (global, column, or sub-table) matches content hidden inside a collapsed art cell. The indicator fires for all three filter modes and is cleared when the cell is expanded or the filter is cleared. Mirrors the existing mb-collapse-toggle-has-match behaviour that already existed for renderMultiRowCell columns (Label, Catalog#).

Fix dead code in initCollapsableColumns art-cell hidden-match pass: the toggle was looked up via ':scope > .mb-cell-collapse-toggle' but art cells were refactored to use [data-caa-expand-btn] inside li-0 of ul.mb-caa-art-ul, so the old selector never matched and the pass silently did nothing. Replaced with a correct lookup of the [data-caa-expand-btn] span inside the li-0 summary row.

Extend _syncCollapseHasMatchInTable with a second pass (Pass 2) that re-evaluates mb-collapse-toggle-has-match on all [data-caa-expand-btn] spans in art cells — previously _syncCollapseHasMatchInTable only processed td.mb-has-collapse-toggle cells and completely missed art cells. Pass 2 is called from applySubFilter (after clearing and after stamping STF highlights) and from _artHighlightArtCell (after async art-cell rebuilds).

Add mb-collapse-toggle-has-match handling to the ensureCollapseDelegate CAA/EAA click branch: when expanding, remove the class; when collapsing, check whether any image li still contains a filter highlight and set/clear accordingly.

Extend updateSubTableCollapseButton and updateGlobalCollapseButtonHighlight highlight-detection selectors to include 'td .mb-caa-art-ul .<highlight-class>' in addition to the existing 'td.mb-has-collapse-toggle .<highlight-class>' selectors, so the per-sub-table and global collapse button tints also react to highlights inside art cells.

sections.label: 🎨 Style
items: Add CSS rules '[data-caa-expand-btn].mb-collapse-toggle-has-match' and '[data-caa-expand-btn].mb-collapse-toggle-has-match:hover' with identical yellow-background / red-glyph / box-shadow styling to the existing .mb-cell-collapse-toggle.mb-collapse-toggle-has-match rules. Added border-radius:2px to match the art button's visual style.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add visual 'hidden match' indicator (mb-collapse-toggle-has-match — yellow background / red glyph on the ▶ cell-collapse toggle) for collapsed multi-row cells (renderMultiRowCell columns such as Label / Catalog#) when the sub-table filter (STF) matches content that is currently hidden inside the collapsed cell. Previously this indicator only fired for global-filter and column-filter matches; STF matches in collapsed items were silently invisible to the user.

sections.label: 🔧 Refactor
items: Introduce module-level constant _COLLAPSE_MATCH_SEL as the single authoritative CSS selector string for detecting any filter-highlight span inside a collapse-toggle cell ('.mb-global-filter-highlight, .mb-column-filter-highlight, .mb-pre-filter-highlight, .mb-subtable-filter-highlight'). All six call sites that previously hard-coded the three-class selector string now use this constant, eliminating the risk of future highlight classes being missed in one place.

Introduce helper function _syncCollapseHasMatchInTable(table) that re-evaluates mb-collapse-toggle-has-match on every .mb-cell-collapse-toggle inside a sub-table. Called from applySubFilter at two points: (1) after step 2 clears STF highlights (removes the indicator from toggles that were lit solely by an STF match), and (2) after step 4 applies new STF highlights (stamps the indicator onto any toggle whose collapsed items now contain an STF match). The helper uses _COLLAPSE_MATCH_SEL so it always reflects the union of all active filter layers regardless of which combination is in effect.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix sub-table filter (STF) silently failing to match rows after any previous filter pass has left fragmented text nodes in the live DOM: when applySubFilter step 2 clears mb-subtable-filter-highlight spans it does `n.replaceWith(document.createTextNode(n.textContent))`, which leaves the replacement text node as a sibling of the surrounding text nodes WITHOUT merging them. The browser does not automatically call normalize() on parent elements after replaceWith(). Consequently getCleanColumnText() sees e.g. three sibling text nodes '"U"', '"nited States"', '"(US)"' instead of one contiguous '"United States (US)"'. Because getCleanColumnText joins all collected text nodes with a space (textParts.join(' ')), the result is '"U nited States (US)"', and '"Un"' no longer matches (example 1). The same fragmentation breaks any query token that straddles a previously-highlighted boundary: '"(Pi"' fails to match '"(Pitman pressing)"' because the '"("' and '"Pi..."' fragments get a space inserted between them (example 2); '"-01-"' fails to match '"1973-01-05"' for the same reason (example 3). Fix: add root.normalize() in getCleanColumnText() immediately after the clone-and-strip step and before the TreeWalker. normalize() merges all adjacent sibling text nodes in the subtree, restoring contiguous text so join(' ') only inserts spaces at genuine inter-element boundaries. Calling normalize() on root (the clone when stripping was needed, or the live element otherwise) is safe — it never alters visible content, only the internal text-node structure.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix ALL art-li highlights disappearing whenever both a sub-table filter (STF) and any second filter (global or column) are active simultaneously (regression introduced in 9.99.552): root cause was a `ReferenceError` — the `_artHighlightArtCell` function referenced `highlightEnabled`, which is a closure-local `let` variable declared inside `createSubTableFilterContainer` and completely out of scope at module level. JavaScript short-circuit evaluation masked the bug when `_stfRaw === ''` (the `&&` chain never reached `highlightEnabled`), so the crash only occurred as soon as the STF input had any text, silently aborting the entire highlight loop before column-filter or global-filter marks were applied. Fix: replaced the invalid `highlightEnabled` reference with a live DOM read of the per-sub-table highlight-toggle button's `dataset.highlightEnabled` attribute (set by `createSubTableHighlightButton`), which is the authoritative and always-up-to-date source of the same flag. Falls back to `true` (highlight ON) when the button element cannot be found.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix sub-table filter (STF) highlights disappearing from CAA/EAA art image `<li>` rows when a column filter is also active on multi-table pages (e.g. STF regexp 'obi|page' highlighted correctly, then column filter 'in' applied → 'obi'/'page' highlights vanished): root cause was a timing race between `applySubFilter()` and the async `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell()` rebuild. When a column filter changes, `runFilter()` calls `renderGroupedTable()` which triggers `_artEnrichIcon` → `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell` (may be async on IDB/network path); then `reapplyAllSubTableFilters()` → `applySubFilter()` applies `mb-subtable-filter-highlight` to art li items. When `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell` fires after `applySubFilter` has already run, it creates fresh `<li>` elements and calls `_artHighlightArtCell` → `_artHighlightImageLi` which only applies `mb-global-filter-highlight` and `mb-column-filter-highlight` — the `mb-subtable-filter-highlight` marks from `applySubFilter` are never stamped onto the new li items. Fix: added Step 3 and Step 5 to `_artHighlightArtCell`: Step 3 reads the active STF query from the h3's `.mb-subtable-filter-container input[type='text']` (using the already-resolved per-sub-table Rx/Case/Ex checkbox values); Step 5 calls `highlightCrossTag(li, _stfRegex, 'mb-subtable-filter-highlight')` on each image li immediately after `_artHighlightImageLi`, ensuring that both column/global highlights and STF highlights are applied atomically at the end of every `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell` rebuild regardless of async timing. The early-bail guard (was: no global + no col) now also checks `!_stfRegex`.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix three bugs with CAA/EAA art-column filtering and highlighting: (1) Global plain-text filter did not match CAA/EAA art cells at all: `getCleanVisibleText(row)` (used by the plain-text global path) deliberately omits `ul.dataset.mbArtSearch` (the image-type/comment search index built by `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell`) to keep sort keys clean; the regexp global path already uses `getCleanColumnText(cell)` per cell which includes the dataset. Fix: added an `mbArtSearch` fallback after the existing rel-cell fallback — when `getCleanVisibleText` misses, each cell is checked for a `ul.mb-caa-art-ul` with a `dataset.mbArtSearch` and a plain `includes()` test is run against it, mirroring the rel-cell pattern. (2) Single-table pages (e.g. artist events): CAA/EAA column-filter and global-filter highlights never appeared in art image `<li>` rows: source rows in `allRows` carry `data-caa-enriched`/`data-eaa-enriched` markers from the initial render; `cloneNode(true)` copies those markers; when `initCaaPics()`/`initEaaPics()` ran after `renderFinalTable()`, `_artEnrichIcon` saw the copied marker and returned early without calling `_artBuildMultiRowArtCell` → `_artHighlightImageLi` — so art li items in filtered clones had no highlight spans. Fix: added `Array.from(clone.cells).forEach(td => _stripTransientCellState(td))` to the single-table clone-map loop, making it identical to the multi-table path; source rows in `allRows` keep their markers so subsequent filter keystrokes are served from cache without re-fetching. (3) Sub-table filter (STF) did not highlight CAA/EAA art image `<li>` rows: `highlightCrossTag(td, regex, ...)` FILTER_REJECTs `.mb-caa-art-li-image` elements when called on the whole `<td>`, so image-row li items were skipped; `applySubFilter` had no fallback for them. Fix: after calling `highlightCrossTag(td, ...)` for each td, query all `ul.mb-caa-art-ul > li.mb-caa-art-li-image` descendants and call `highlightCrossTag(li, regex, 'mb-subtable-filter-highlight')` directly on each one — the FILTER_REJECT only prevents descending INTO `.mb-caa-art-li-image` from a parent, not calling `highlightCrossTag` with the li as the root, so the walk runs freely inside the li.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix global plain-text filter still matching all rows whose cells contain a loaded inline thumbnail on both single-table and multi-table pages (e.g. filtering 'nl' selected every row because 'caa-i[nl]ine-yes' was present in the hidden `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` span of every cell with a loaded CAA image): 9.99.549 correctly added `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` to `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` — which is consumed only by `getCleanColumnText` — but the global plain-text filter path uses `getCleanVisibleText(row)`, which has its own independent exclusion list and was never updated. Fix: added `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` to the `FILTER_REJECT` conditions inside `getCleanVisibleText`'s TreeWalker `acceptNode` callback, mirroring the `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` addition.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix false-positive filter matches caused by the invisible sentinel text 'caa-inline-yes' / 'caa-inline-no' in `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` spans leaking into general filtering: e.g. filtering for 'nl' matched rows solely because 'caa-i**nl**ine-yes' was present in a cell's hidden sort-key span. Root cause: `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` was deliberately absent from `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` (by design, so that `applyUniqVal('caa-inline-yes')` column-filter clicks could drive filtering through the normal `getCleanColumnText` text pipeline without a special bypass). This meant the sentinel text was included in every `getCleanColumnText` call — global regexp filter, global plain-text rel-cell fallback, STF row-match (since 9.99.548), sort keys, and unique-values dropdown counts. Fix: added `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` to `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` so it is stripped from all general text-extraction paths. The `applyUniqVal` / column-filter path is preserved via a direct bypass in `testRowMatch`: before the normal `getCleanColumnText` text comparison, the column-filter code now queries `cell.querySelector('.mb-inline-art-sort-key')` directly and performs an exact (case-insensitive or case-sensitive) string equality check against `f.val`; a `match = true` from this check short-circuits the subsequent text comparison. The bypass is intentionally skipped for regexp column filters (regexp matching against sentinel values would be unusual and unreliable). Also added `.mb-inline-art-sort-key` to the element-level `FILTER_REJECT` list in `highlightCrossTag` so that the sentinel text is never wrapped in a highlight span.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix two bugs with Relationships column rel-icon red-box highlighting introduced in 9.99.546: (1) Sub-table filter (STF): when a row matched via the h3 sub-table filter input, no red border was drawn around the matching relationship icon — root causes were (a) step-2 reset in `applySubFilter` did not clear `.mb-rel-icon-match` classes left by a previous STF pass (fixed: added `table.querySelectorAll('.mb-rel-icon-match').forEach(a => a.classList.remove('mb-rel-icon-match'))` alongside the subtable-filter-highlight clear), (b) `applySubFilter` never called `_highlightRelCellIcons` (fixed: added call after `highlightCrossTag` for every `.mb-rel-cell` td in the hit block), and (c) the STF row-match test used raw `cell.textContent` (which includes ALL hidden DOM text, including CAA/EAA emoji counts, cache-hint glyphs, etc.) rather than `getCleanColumnText(cell)` — switched both the regexp and plain-text STF match paths to use `getCleanColumnText` for consistency with the column-filter and global-regexp paths (the plain-text path now joins `getCleanColumnText` per cell instead of `c.textContent`). (2) Global plain-text filter: the global filter did not match rows when the Relationships column URL was the only matching content — root cause was that the plain-text global path uses `getCleanVisibleText(row)` which deliberately `FILTER_REJECT`s `.mb-rel-filter-key` spans (to prevent URL noise in sort keys), while the regexp global path uses `getCleanColumnText(cell)` per cell which does include the URL text. Fix: after the `getCleanVisibleText` match fails, additionally test each `.mb-rel-cell` via `getCleanColumnText` using a plain `includes()` check, mirroring the same targeted rel-cell fallback already applied to the regexp path.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix per-sub-table 'Case Sensitive' (Cc), 'RegExp' (Rx), and 'Exclude' (Ex) checkboxes in the h3 header line on multi-table pages not being honoured when filtering on the column level: root cause was that `runFilter()` only read the per-sub-table Rx checkbox for passing to `getColFilters()`, while the Case and Exclude checkboxes were never read. `getColFilters()` now receives all three per-sub-table flags (`_colIsCase`, `_colIsRegExp`, `_colIsExclude`) which it embeds as `{isCaseSensitive, isRegExp, isExclude}` into every filter object `f` it produces. `testRowMatch()` reads them back via `f.isCaseSensitive`, `f.isRegExp`, `f.isExclude` (with fallbacks to the global ctx flags for single-table pages and legacy filter objects). Three additional fixes in the same patch: (1) Single-table `getColFilters` call now passes the global `isExclude` so column-filter objects correctly embed `f.isExclude` instead of always receiving `false` from the default parameter — previously, the global Exclude checkbox was silently ignored for single-table column filters; (2) `_activeFilterHighlightCtx` is now also updated with `isCaseSensitive` per group (alongside the existing `isRegExp` update) so that art-cell highlight rebuilds via `_artHighlightImageLi` use the correct per-sub-table case flag; (3) Relationship icon column-filter highlight in `testRowMatch` finalHit block: moved the `colFilters.forEach` rel-cell highlight loop outside the global `!isExclude` guard so it uses only its own per-filter `!_fIsExclude` gate — previously, a checked global Exclude checkbox suppressed all column-level rel-icon match-box highlights even when the sub-table's own Ex checkbox was unchecked.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix per-subtable Case Sensitive (Cc), RegExp (Rx), and Exclude (Ex) checkboxes being ignored for column-level filters on multi-table pages — only the global h2 checkbox states were honoured. Root cause was three-fold: (1) `runFilter()` multi-table path only read the per-subtable Rx checkbox (`[id$="-rx-checkbox"]`) and passed the global `isCaseSensitive` and `isExclude` to `getColFilters`; (2) `getColFilters()` did not embed any checkbox flags in its returned filter objects, so `testRowMatch()` had no per-filter state to read; (3) `testRowMatch()` used `ctx.isRegExp`, `ctx.isCaseSensitive`, and `ctx.isExclude` (all global) for every column-filter test regardless of the sub-table's own checkboxes. Fix: (a) `getColFilters(table, isCaseSensitive, isRegExp, isExclude = false)` gains an `isExclude` parameter and now embeds `isRegExp`, `isCaseSensitive`, and `isExclude` on every pushed filter object; (b) `runFilter()` multi-table path also reads the per-subtable `[id$="-case-checkbox"]` and `[id$="-ex-checkbox"]` elements and passes all three to `getColFilters`; (c) `testRowMatch()` column-filter loop resolves `_fIsRegExp`, `_fIsCase`, and `_fIsExclude` from the filter object (falling back to global ctx values for single-table pages and backward compatibility), uses them for cell-text matching, and gates both `highlightText()` and `_highlightRelCellIcons()` calls on `!_fIsExclude`; (d) the async art-cell highlight path (`_artHighlightImageLi` outer call) also reads all three per-subtable checkboxes and passes them to `getColFilters`, and the column-filter loop inside `_artHighlightImageLi` uses `f.isRegExp`/`f.isCaseSensitive` per-filter (with ctx-level fallback) when building the per-column highlight regex.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: When a filter expression matches a row because of content in the Relationships column, each individual relationship icon whose URL matches is now visually surrounded by a red 2px outline box so it is immediately clear which icon caused the match. Implementation: new helper `_highlightRelCellIcons(cell, queryRaw, isCaseSensitive, isRegExp)` iterates every `` in a `.mb-rel-cell` ``, tests its hidden `.mb-rel-filter-key` URL text against the active filter (regexp or plain-text, case-aware), and adds the CSS class `mb-rel-icon-match` to each matching anchor. A new CSS rule `td.mb-rel-cell a.mb-rel-icon-match { outline: 2px solid red !important; border-radius: 2px; }` is injected alongside the existing rel-cell rules; its one-higher specificity (extra class selector) overrides the baseline `outline: none !important` that suppresses MusicBrainz sprite artefacts on all other rel-cell anchors. `_highlightRelCellIcons` is called from `testRowMatch()` in the `finalHit && !isExclude` block — after the standard `highlightText()` pass — for both the global filter (all `.mb-rel-cell` columns) and per-column filters (only the matched column index). `highlightText()` alone cannot produce a visible result for rel cells because the filter key lives in a `display:none` span and the icon itself is an ``. Stale `.mb-rel-icon-match` classes are cleared at the start of each `testRowMatch()` call (alongside `.mb-global-filter-highlight`/`.mb-column-filter-highlight`), in the unhighlight-all button handler, and in the clear-all-filters handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix permanent Relationships (and Release-events) column-header destruction on multi-table pages when a global filter removes ALL rows from any sub-table, followed by clearing (or changing) the filter: root cause was that both `_suppressRelationshipsIfNoReleaseOrReleaseGroupLinks(table)` and `_suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks(table)` are called inside `renderGroupedTable()` after rows are appended to each group's `<tbody>`. When `runFilter()` passes a filter that matches zero rows, `tbody.innerHTML = ''` is executed first and then the empty group's rows (none) are appended — leaving the tbody with no `<tr>` elements. The two suppression helpers then find no `/release/` or `/release-group/` links (false negative due to empty tbody) and unconditionally delete the `Relationships` / Release-events `<th>` from the thead. On the next `renderGroupedTable` call (`query = 're-run'`, also truthy so existing tables are reused), the real rows are reinserted but the suppression guards now return early because links ARE found — leaving the thead permanently missing the already-deleted `<th>`. The browser then shifts every subsequent column left by one: `.mb-rel-cell` (Relationships data) appears under the Picard column header, and the Picard `<td>` has no header at all. Fix: added an empty-tbody guard at the top of both suppression functions — `if (!_tbodyForCheck || !_tbodyForCheck.querySelector('tr')) return;` — so that suppression is never triggered when the tbody has zero rows (state that is inherently indeterminate and is always due to a filter pass, not to the absence of release links in the data).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix regexp highlighting not applying for `^…` (start-of-string) patterns in cells whose first DOM content is a decorative/UI element rather than plain text: (1) cells on series pages whose Release column combines a `[data-erg-btn]` `▶` toggle span and a `.mb-caa-inline-ph` inline-thumbnail span before the actual text — the `▶` glyph and the `🗄️` cache-hint emoji inside the inline-ph span both inflated the highlight walker's `fullText` at position 0, so `^` referred to before those characters; (2) the extracted Country column on release-group pages whose cells contain a flag `` followed by a formatting-whitespace text node (` `) before the `` country text — the space shifted the `^` anchor by one character. Root cause: `highlightCrossTag` used a `NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT`-only TreeWalker, which cannot issue element-level `FILTER_REJECT` to prune entire subtrees; every text node (including those inside `[data-erg-btn]`, `.mb-caa-inline-ph`, and whitespace-only inter-element nodes) was included verbatim in `fullText`. The filter path uses `getCleanColumnText()` which clones-and-strips those same elements and skips whitespace-only / decorative-icon text nodes, so the filter matched but the highlighter did not. Fix: switch `highlightCrossTag` to `NodeFilter.SHOW_TEXT | NodeFilter.SHOW_ELEMENT`, add element-level `FILTER_REJECT` for the same exclusion set as `_CLEAN_STRIP_SEL` / `getCleanColumnText` (`script`, `style`, `[data-erg-btn]`, `.mb-caa-inline-ph`, `.mb-eaa-inline-ph`, all `.mb-art-cache-hint-*`, `.mb-caa-count-badge`, `.mb-eaa-count-badge`, `.mb-caa-art-li-image`, `.artwork-icon`, `.caa-icon`, `.icon`), and add text-node `FILTER_REJECT` for whitespace-only nodes and decorative-icon glyphs (mirroring the `isDecorativeIcon()` guard in `getCleanColumnText`). All other element nodes receive `FILTER_SKIP` so their children are still visited. The resulting `fullText` is now positionally equivalent to the string `runFilter()` tested the regexp against, making `^` anchors highlight the correct character in the first real data text node.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix global CAA/EAA cover-art toggle button (`#mb-caa-toggle-btn-global`), global CAA/EAA retry button (`#mb-caa-toggle-btn-global-retry`), and global Relationships retry button (`#mb-rel-retry-global`) not rendered in the h2 header line on multi-table pages after 9.99.530: root cause was that `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton()` (called from `initCaaPics()`/`initEaaPics()` inside `renderGroupedTable()`) requires `h2 .mb-row-count-stat` to exist as the DOM insertion anchor (`countStat.after(btn)`), but 9.99.530 intentionally suppressed the pre-render `updateH2Count` call for `tableMode === 'multi'` pages (to avoid an inflated See-all-row count) and only called it *after* `renderGroupedTable()` completed. At that deferred point `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton` had already returned early with `'deferring'` and the `updateH2Count` re-anchor loop had nothing to reposition. Fix: after the deferred `updateH2Count(totalRowsAfterRender, totalRowsAfterRender)` call, explicitly invoke `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(CAA_CTX)` and `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(EAA_CTX)` (guarded by `Lib.settings.sa_enable_caa_pics`) so the buttons are created/repositioned now that `.mb-row-count-stat` is present. The calls are fully idempotent — the function reuses an existing button element when one is already in the DOM.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add `_suppressRelationshipsIfNoReleaseOrReleaseGroupLinks(table)` helper that applies the same data-driven guard used by `initPicardTaggerColumn` and `_suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks` to the Relationships injected column. On multi-table pages that declare `injectedColumns: ['Relationships']` — `area-releases` (Relationships button), `place-performances`, `label-relationships`, `artist-relationships`, `artist-releasegroups`, `releasegroup-releases`, `recording-releases`, and `collections-releases` (Release groups entityFeature) — the Relationships `<th>` is unconditionally injected by `cleanupHeaders()` for every sub-table regardless of whether that sub-table's rows link to a release or release-group. The helper scans `tbody td:not(.mb-sticky-col):not(.mb-rel-cell)` cells for any `a[href*="/release/"]` or `a[href*="/release-group/"]` anchor; if none is found it removes the Relationships `<th>` (identified by `.mb-injected-column` + `data-col-name="Relationships"`), all `.mb-rel-cell` `<td>` elements from every body row (matched by class rather than column index so the removal is robust when `_suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks` has already shifted column indices in the same render pass), and the corresponding cell from every secondary `thead tr` (filter row etc.). Unlike the Release events column, the Relationships column has no ICE synthetic children, so only one `<th>` and one `<td>` per row are removed. The helper is hooked into the multi-table group loop immediately after `_suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks(table)`, before `initRelationshipsColumn()`, so that `mb-rel-cell` placeholder `<td>`s are never created for suppressed sub-tables.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add `_suppressReleaseEventsIfNoReleaseLinks(table)` helper that applies the same data-driven release-link guard used by `initPicardTaggerColumn` to the Release events injected column and all ICE synthetic columns derived from it (`Release country`, `Release date`, `R-DD`, `R-MM`, `R-YYYY`, `R-Day`, `R-Month`). When a rendered sub-table or single table contains no `a[href*="/release/"]:not([href*="/release-group/"])` anchor in its tbody data cells (excluding `.mb-sticky-col` and `.mb-rel-cell`), the helper removes the Release events `<th>`, all `mb-ice-th` synthetic `<th>`s, their corresponding `<td>` cells from every body row, and the matching cells from every secondary thead row (filter row etc.). This prevents the Release events column from appearing on non-release relationship sub-tables on `place-performances` and `label-relationships` multi-table pages (e.g. "Held concert at", "Member of") and on `place-performances-filtered` / `label-relationships-filtered` single-table pages when the filtered relationship type is not a release relationship. The helper is hooked into the multi-table group loop immediately after each sub-table's tbody rows are appended, and into the single-table pipeline immediately after `renderFinalTable` + `cleanupHeaders`. It runs before `initReleaseEventsColumn()` in both pipelines so that `mb-re-cell` placeholder `<td>`s are never created for suppressed tables.

Fix sticky column hover highlight: `applyStickyColumn` sets an explicit inline `style.background` on each body sticky cell (snapshotted from the zebra-stripe CSS class so the cell remains opaque while scrolling horizontally). Inline styles have unconditional cascade priority — they win over every author-sheet rule including `!important`, so MusicBrainz's native `tr:hover > td` rule cannot override the sticky cell's background. Fix: `applyStickyColumn` now stores the rest-state colour in `cell.dataset.mbStickyBg` (refreshed on every `_apply()` call so re-runs after zebra re-striping always have the current value), and attaches `mouseenter`/`mouseleave` handlers on each `<tr>`. On `mouseenter` the handler reads the hover background from the first non-sticky sibling `<td>` via `getComputedStyle` — at that moment the browser has already applied its `:hover` rules to siblings, so the value is the actual live hover colour — and writes it directly to `c.style.background`. A fallback of `#e2e2e2` (MusicBrainz's known hover tint) is used when the sibling's computed background is still transparent, matches the rest colour, or is otherwise unavailable. On `mouseleave` the handler restores `c.style.background` from `c.dataset.mbStickyBg`. Listener wiring is clone-safe: handlers are stored as named JS properties on the `<tr>` element (`tr._mbStickyEnter` / `tr._mbStickyLeave`) rather than guarded by a dataset flag. A dataset flag (`tr.dataset.mbStickyHoverWired`) would be copied by `cloneNode(true)` and cause post-sort/filter cloned rows to skip wiring entirely; JS object properties are not copied by `cloneNode`, so cloned rows always start with `undefined` properties and receive fresh listeners. On every `_apply()` call `removeEventListener` with the stored reference cleanly removes any prior listener before the new one is added, guaranteeing exactly one live listener pair per row regardless of re-renders.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix real root cause of the ghost filter column on place-events, artist-events, artist-works, artist-recordings, and event-series pages: another userscript (e.g. a Rating-column hider) can set `display:none` on the native MusicBrainz Rating `<th>` without removing it from the DOM. When `sa_remove_rating` is false our script previously kept the hidden `<th>` in the header row. `addColumnFilterRow` then built a filter cell for it (copying the `display:none` style, so the filter cell was initially hidden too), but a subsequent browser re-style or the external script restoring visibility caused the filter cell to become visible while the header `<th>` remained invisible — producing the ghost. Fix: both `cleanupHeaders()` and the fetch-phase header-index scanner (`referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th')` loop) now check `th.style.display === 'none' || window.getComputedStyle(th).display === 'none'` in addition to the `sa_remove_rating` setting. If either condition is true the Rating column is removed from the header row and excluded from index tracking, exactly as if `sa_remove_rating` were enabled. A dedicated debug log entry distinguishes the external-hide path from the setting-driven path. This makes the column-removal logic robust against any external agent that hides a column header without touching its data cells, regardless of the user's `sa_remove_rating` preference.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix ghost filter column on place-events and event-series pages: `toggleAutoResizeColumns` used `table.querySelectorAll('thead th')` which matched ths in ALL thead rows including the `tr.mb-col-filter-row` injected by `addColumnFilterRow`, making the `headers` array twice as long as the actual column count (e.g. 32 for a 16-column table). The `headers.forEach((th, idx) => { if (columnVisible[idx]) th.style.minWidth = ... })` loop then iterated over filter-row ths with out-of-bounds `columnVisible[idx]` accesses (undefined/falsy), which in some browsers produces subtle DOM or layout side-effects that manifest as a misaligned ghost column. Fix: changed selector to `thead tr:first-child th` (matching the already-correct sub-table resize variant at line ~15119) so only the real header row is ever consulted for measurement, visibility, and min-width assignment.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactor
items: Remove `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS` (RegExp[] constant, ~70 lines) and `_picardPageIsSupported()` (function, ~40 lines) entirely. These are no longer needed now that `initPicardTaggerColumn` uses a universal per-table data-driven guard — `tbody td:not(.mb-sticky-col):not(.mb-rel-cell) a[href*="/release/"]:not([href*="/release-group/"])` — to decide whether to inject the Picard column. Any table on any page type that contains no `/release/<mbid>` link in its data cells is automatically skipped; any table that does contain such a link gets the column, regardless of URL pattern, H2 entity type, or any other page-type-specific heuristic. The `if (!_picardPageIsSupported()) return` early-exit in `initPicardTaggerColumn` is removed; the sole remaining entry guard is `if (!Lib.settings.sa_enable_picard_tagger) return`. JSDoc for `initPicardTaggerColumn` updated accordingly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix ghost filter column on single-table event pages (e.g. `/place/<mbid>/events`, event `/series/<mbid>`) and potentially any future page type: replace the blind idempotency guard in `addColumnFilterRow` (`if (filterRow already exists) return`) with stale-detection rebuild logic. The new logic compares the existing filter row's cell count against the current header row cell count on every call; if they match the function is still a no-op (cheap); if they differ the stale filter row is removed, any in-progress filter values typed by the user are snapshotted by `data-col-idx`, the row is rebuilt from the current header cell set, and the saved values are restored. This self-heals the ghost-cell symptom (a filter `<input>` with no `<th>` above it, or a header `<th>` with no filter input below) that arises whenever any column modifier runs after the filter row has already been built — regardless of which modifier caused it and on which page type.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactor
items: Simplify `initPicardTaggerColumn` per-table guard to a single universal data-driven check: the three page-type-specific flag variables (`_isMultiEntitySubTablePage`, `_isRelationshipsMultiPage`, `_isRelationshipsFilteredPage`) and their corresponding guard blocks introduced in 9.99.536 are replaced by one CSS selector query — `tbody td:not(.mb-sticky-col):not(.mb-rel-cell) a[href*="/release/"]:not([href*="/release-group/"])` — applied unconditionally to every `table.tbl`. Any table whose tbody contains no anchor linking to a MusicBrainz `/release/<mbid>` page is silently skipped; all others receive the Picard column regardless of page type, column layout, or URL structure. The `:not([href*="/release-group/"])` exclusion is required because `/release-group/` contains the substring `/release/`. This approach is page-type-agnostic and automatically correct for all existing supported pages (tag/user-tag/artist-credit multi-entity sub-tables, artist-relationships/place-performances multi-table and filtered pages) as well as any future page types added to `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS` without requiring any further code changes. Updated JSDoc for `initPicardTaggerColumn` and `_picardPageIsSupported()` to document the unified guard strategy.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix first-column detection in `initPicardTaggerColumn` extracting sort/stats glyphs (⇅▲▼NN📊) as part of the column header text, causing every sub-table to be incorrectly skipped on `/tag/<value>` multi-entity pages (e.g. 'Release ⇅▲▼10📊' was not equal to 'Release'). Root cause: the guard used `_firstTh.textContent.trim()` instead of the existing `_cleanColHeaderText()` helper, which correctly resolves `dataset.colName`, the first text-node of `.mb-col-hdr-flex`, or a stripped clone-fallback. Fix: replace the raw `textContent.trim()` call with `_cleanColHeaderText(_firstTh)` throughout the guard.

Extend the multi-entity sub-table first-column guard (`_isMultiEntitySubTablePage`, formerly `_isMultiEntityTagPage`) to also cover `/artist-credit/<id>` overview pages. These pages render multiple h3 sub-tables for different entity types (Release groups, Releases, Recordings, Tracks); only the sub-table whose first column header is 'Release' should receive the Picard column.

Add per-table release-link guard for `artist-relationships` and `place-performances` multi-table overview pages (`_isRelationshipsMultiPage`). All h3 sub-tables on these pages share the same 'Title' first-column header, so a header-name check is insufficient. Instead, each sub-table is tested at runtime for the presence of at least one `a[href*="/release/"]` link in its tbody data-cells (excluding `.mb-sticky-col` and `.mb-rel-cell`). Only sub-tables with actual release data cells (e.g. 'Composed release', 'Instruments release', 'Liner notes for release') receive the Picard column.

Add release-link guard for the filtered single-table variants of the above pages (`_isRelationshipsFilteredPage`, i.e. `artist-relationships-filtered` and `place-performances-filtered` page types, identified by `?link_type_id=…` in the URL). The Picard column is injected only when the rendered table contains at least one `/release/<mbid>` link in its tbody, matching the multi-table guard logic above.

Update JSDoc for `initPicardTaggerColumn` to document all three per-table guard strategies (multi-entity header guard, relationships/performances release-link guard, filtered page release-link guard) and their rationale. Update JSDoc for `_picardPageIsSupported()` to reference `_cleanColHeaderText()` and the new data-driven release-link guards.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix regression introduced in 9.99.534: the per-table first-column guard (`_firstTh.textContent.trim() !== 'Release'`) was applied globally to every `table.tbl` on every page, not only to the tag/user-tag multi-entity pages it was designed for. This broke all pages where the first column header is not literally 'Release' but rows still contain release links — e.g. release series pages (first column `#`), `label/<mbid>/relationships` (first column `Type`), and `artist/<mbid>/relationships` (first column `Type`) were all incorrectly skipped. Fix: the guard is now scoped with an `_isMultiEntityTagPage` flag computed once before the `forEach` loop by testing the current pathname against `/\/tag\/[^/]+(?:[/?]|$)/` and `/\/user\/[^/]+\/tag\/[^/]+(?:[/?]|$)/`; the first-column check runs only when this flag is true, leaving all other page types unaffected.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix Picard column missing on tag-entity and user-tag pages that list releases. Two related fixes: (1) **`/tag/<value>/release`** (`tag-value-entity` pageType) — was not matched by any pattern; added `/\/tag\/[^/]+\/release(?:[/?]|$)/` to `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS`. The entity type is explicit in the URL so no runtime check is needed; other entity sub-pages (`/artist`, `/recording`, `/label`, …) do not match because the pattern anchors on the literal segment `release`. (2) **`/tag/<value>`** (`tag-value` pageType) and **`/user/<name>/tag/<value>`** (`user-tag-value` pageType) — these are multi-h3-subtable pages listing all entity types tagged with the given value (Areas, Artists, Releases, Recordings, Works, etc.); added patterns `/\/tag\/[^/]+(?:[/?]|$)/` and `/\/user\/[^/]+\/tag\/[^/]+(?:[/?]|$)/`. Because these pages contain sub-tables for multiple entity types, a page-level H2 check is insufficient; instead, a **per-table first-column guard** was added at the top of the `_tables.forEach` loop inside `initPicardTaggerColumn`: any `table.tbl` whose first `<th>` text is not exactly `'Release'` is silently skipped, so the Picard column is injected only into the release sub-table. Updated `_picardPageIsSupported()` JSDoc to document this per-table delegation.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix Picard column appearing on non-release collection pages (`/collection/<mbid>`): collections can hold any entity type (Areas, Artists, Events, Genres, Instruments, Labels, Recordings, Release groups, Works, etc.) but Picard can only queue releases. Added a runtime check in `_picardPageIsSupported()` for paths matching `/collection/[a-f0-9-]{36}` that reads the live H2 text via `_getH2EntityType()` and returns `false` when it does not contain 'releases' — mirroring the identical series check introduced in 9.99.532. Only release collections (first column header 'Release', H2 text 'Releases') now receive the Picard column. Updated JSDoc on `_picardPageIsSupported()` to document the new collection exclusion.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Further refinement of `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS` and `_picardPageIsSupported()` to reflect that Picard can only queue **releases** — not recordings, release groups, or other entity types. **False positive removed**: `/area/<mbid>/recordings` was included in 9.99.531 but recordings cannot be submitted to Picard directly; removed `/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/recordings/` from patterns. **False positive removed**: `/artist-credit/<mbid>/recording` and `/artist-credit/<mbid>/release-group` — the broad `/\/artist-credit\//` pattern matched all artist-credit sub-pages; added a runtime exclusion in `_picardPageIsSupported()` for paths matching `/artist-credit/[^/]+/(?:recording|release-group)`. **False positive removed**: series pages of type 'Release groups' (and 'Recordings') — the previous runtime series check in `_picardPageIsSupported()` allowed 'release groups' and 'recordings' in addition to 'releases'; narrowed to `_h2Text.includes('releases')` only, so only release series pass through. **False negative fixed**: `/label/<mbid>/relationships` — the previous label pattern `/\/label\/[a-f0-9-]{36}(?:\/releases|$|\?)/` did not match `/relationships`; extended to `/\/label\/[a-f0-9-]{36}(?:\/releases|\/relationships|$|\?)/`. **Comments and JSDoc updated** throughout `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS` and `_picardPageIsSupported()` to consistently state that only **releases** (not recordings/release groups) are queuable in Picard, correcting the previous 'release/recording' wording.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix `_PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS` and `_picardPageIsSupported()` to correctly include/exclude pages for the Picard tagger column. **False positives removed** (Picard column was appearing where it should not): (1) `/label/<mbid>/tags` — the old `/\/label\/[a-f0-9-]{36}/` pattern was too broad and matched all label sub-pages; narrowed to `/\/label\/[a-f0-9-]{36}(?:\/releases|$|\?)/` so only the bare overview and `/releases` sub-page match. (2) `/area/<mbid>/artists`, `/events`, `/places`, `/recordings`, `/users`, `/works`, `/aliases` — the old `/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}/` pattern matched all area sub-pages; replaced with two precise patterns `/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases/` and `/\/area\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/recordings/` (the only area sub-pages that list Picard-queuable entities). (3) `/recording/<mbid>/fingerprints` — the old `/\/recording\//` pattern matched all recording sub-pages; narrowed to `/\/recording\/[a-f0-9-]{36}(?:\/releases|$|\?)/`. (4) `/series/<mbid>` for non-release series (Event series, Work series etc.) — the `/\/series\//` pattern is retained but `_picardPageIsSupported()` now reads the live H2 text via `_getH2EntityType()` and returns `false` when the entity type is not Releases, Release groups, or Recordings. **False negatives fixed** (Picard column was missing where it should appear): (5) `/artist/<mbid>/relationships` (both the multi-table overview and the `?link_type_id=…` filtered view) — added `/\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/relationships/`. (6) `/artist-credit/<mbid>` overview and `/artist-credit/<mbid>/release` entity sub-page — added `/\/artist-credit\//`. (7) `/instrument/<mbid>/releases` — added `/\/instrument\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/releases/`. (8) `/place/<mbid>/performances` (both filtered and overview variants) — added `/\/place\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/performances/`.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix h2 total row count too high by the number of h3 headers on first render for pageTypes `tag-value`, `user-tag-value`, and `artist-credit` (e.g. `/tag/country` showed 99 instead of 91 with 8 overflow groups; `/user/vzell/tag/live` showed 14 instead of 13 with 1 overflow group): root cause was that `updateH2Count(totalRows, totalRows)` was called in `startFetchingProcess` *before* `renderGroupedTable()` ran. At that point the `groupedRows` sum included the trailing 'See all N …' `<tr>` in each group's `rows` array; `renderGroupedTable()` only splices those rows out of `group.rows` (and removes them from the DOM) during its own execution. The count was therefore inflated by exactly the number of groups that carried a 'See all' row. After save/load the rows were already absent, so the figure was correct — explaining why the bug self-healed on disk round-trip. Fix: for `tableMode === 'multi'` pages, suppressed the pre-render `updateH2Count` call and instead added a deferred call immediately after `await renderGroupedTable()` completes, recalculating the true total via `groupedRows.reduce((acc, g) => acc + g.rows.length, 0)` on the now-correct post-removal arrays.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
Fix h3 sub-table headers hidden after disk-load from initial page on multi-h2 pages (e.g. `artist-credit` which has both `

Artist list

` and the native `

Uses

`): `makeH2sCollapsible` uses the presence of `.mb-row-count-stat` on an h2 to determine whether it is the main data header (`isMainDataHeader = true` → icon='▼' → contentNodes left visible). Without `.mb-row-count-stat`, every h2 is treated as a non-data header and its contentNodes are set to `display: none`. In `loadTableDataFromDisk`, `makeH2sCollapsible` was called at line ~34579 but `updateH2Count` (which stamps `.mb-row-count-stat`) was called much later at line ~34758 — so when `makeH2sCollapsible` fired, neither h2 had the stat span, and the h3/table pairs under `

Uses

` were all hidden. Fix: moved `updateH2Count(loadedRowCount, loadedRowCount)` and the immediately following `_relocateTrailingH2Sections()` call to run immediately before `makeH2sCollapsible`, with an explanatory comment. The downstream `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton` calls that followed the old position are retained in place (they only need the count stat to exist, which it now does from the earlier call).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix inline CAA/EAA thumbnail images still absent after disk-load (Bug 1): root cause was `runFilter`'s `filteredArray.push({ category, rows: matches })` — only `category` and `rows` were preserved; `colName`, `entityFeatures`, `key`, and `originalRows` were silently dropped. The per-group inline-thumbnail loop in `renderGroupedTable` reads `group.entityFeatures` from the passed `dataArray` (here `filteredArray`); with `entityFeatures` absent it skipped every group. Fix: changed to `filteredArray.push({ ...group, rows: matches })` so all group properties are preserved. Also fixes per-group column-name patching (the `colName` property that patches the first `<th>` of each sub-table's header) in the `runFilter` re-render pass.

Fix h3 headers and CAA image stripe missing after disk-load from the initial page (Bug 2): `applyInsertH2` was not idempotent. On disk-load the pre-processing block calls it unconditionally; on the final rendered page (where `startFetchingProcess` had already called it) this inserted a second identical `<h2>`, shifting the `allH2s` sequence that `renderGroupedTable` uses to locate `targetHeader`. When the duplicate h2 preceded `firstTable`, `targetHeader` resolved to the duplicate instead of the intended anchor, causing subsequent group h3/table pairs to be inserted in the wrong position (or with `lastInsertedElement = null`). Fix: added an idempotency guard at the top of `applyInsertH2` that scans for an existing `<h2>` with the same text and returns immediately when found.

Fix h3 total row count wrong after disk-load (Bug 3): `saveTableDataToDisk` serialises `group.rows` which still contained the 'See all N …' trailing TR (it was only removed from the DOM by `_lastRow.remove()`, not from the in-memory array). On disk-load the row was reconstructed into `reconstructedRows` and counted in `loadedRowCount`, inflating the `updateH2Count` total (e.g. 99 instead of 91 with 8 overflow groups).
