Fix: added a 'See all' row detection skip inside the `data.groups.forEach` row reconstruction loop — a row whose sole cell contains `<em><a href*='/tag/'> or <em><a href*='/artist-credit/'>` with text matching `/^see all\b/i` is silently skipped before being pushed to `reconstructedRows`.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix 'See all N entities' row reappearing after disk-load on `tag-value`, `user-tag-value`, and `artist-credit` pages (Bug 1a): `_lastRow.remove()` in `renderGroupedTable` removed the TR from the DOM but left it in the in-memory `group.rows` array. `saveTableDataToDisk` serialises `group.rows`, so the row was written to disk and reconstructed on load. More critically, on every `runFilter` call with a non-empty query, the filter-reuse path (`if (query && existingTables[index])`) skips the `if (!query)` block that contains the 'See all' detection, so the row was appended to tbody and became permanently visible. Fix: after `_lastRow.remove()`, added `_seeAllSplice()` calls that remove the TR from `group.rows`, `group.originalRows`, and — when `dataArray` is a filtered copy passed by `runFilter` — from the matching `groupedRows[index].rows` and `groupedRows[index].originalRows` as well.

Fix all sub-tables showing the first sub-table's column name after disk-load on `tag-value`, `user-tag-value`, and `artist-credit` pages (Bug 1b): disk-load reconstructed groups without a `colName` property. `renderGroupedTable` patches the first `<th>` of each group's cloned `templateHead` only when `group.colName` is set; without it all groups inherited the first table's column name. Fix: in `loadTableDataFromDisk`'s group reconstruction block, compute `colName` using `_toSingular(category)` for pageTypes in the singular set (same set as `_singularPageTypes` in `startFetchingProcess`) and `category` for all others.

Fix inline CAA/EAA thumbnail images absent after disk-load on `tag-value`, `user-tag-value`, and `artist-credit` pages (Bug 1c): disk-load reconstructed groups without an `entityFeatures` property. The per-group inline-thumbnail loop in `renderGroupedTable` reads `group.entityFeatures.addCAA/addEAA` to find the correct column name per sub-table; with `group.entityFeatures` missing it skipped every group (`_gAddCAA` and `_gAddEAA` both undefined). Fix: in the same group reconstruction block, call `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH3(category, activeDefinition)` and store the result as `group.entityFeatures` when it is non-empty.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix crash when using 'Load from Disk' on `artist-credit` overview pages (and any other `listToTable` multi-table pageType): `loadTableDataFromDisk` did not run the DOM pre-processing steps (`applyRenameH2ToH3`, `applyInsertH2`, `applyListToTable`) that `startFetchingProcess` normally applies before a live fetch. Without them, `document.querySelector('table.tbl')` returned `null` (no `<ul>`→`<table.tbl>` conversion had run), causing (a) the headers-restoration block to silently skip, (b) `renderGroupedTable` to log 'No template table head found', and (c) a `TypeError: Failed to execute 'compareDocumentPosition' on 'Node': parameter 1 is not of type 'Node'` crash. Fix: added a pre-processing block in `loadTableDataFromDisk` (before the headers-restoration block) that calls all three DOM mutation functions when `activeDefinition.features.listToTable` is set. Also added a defensive `firstTable &&` null-guard to the `compareDocumentPosition` call in `renderGroupedTable` as a belt-and-suspenders safeguard.

Fix CAA/EAA artwork not restored on disk-load for pageTypes that carry `entityFeatures` (e.g. `series-releases`, `collections-releases`): `loadTableDataFromDisk` did not call `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2(baseDefinition)` or merge the result into `activeDefinition.features`, so keys such as `addCAA`, `addEAA`, and `extractMainColumn` that live inside the `entityFeatures` map were absent from `activeDefinition.features` on disk-load. This caused `initCaaPics()` / `initEaaPics()` / `initCaaInlinePics()` to skip entirely (they guard on `features.addCAA` / `features.addEAA`) and `applyStickyColumn` to find no sticky-column name. Fix: added a merge block in `loadTableDataFromDisk` immediately after the `tableMode` assignment that calls `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2(baseDefinition)` and merges the resolved entity-specific features into `activeDefinition.features` — mirroring the merge that `startFetchingProcess` performs at the start of a live fetch. The rebuilt active-descriptor lists that follow then correctly include the merged `addCAA` / `addEAA` / `extractMainColumn` etc.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix inline-thumbnail bigbox tooltip positioning: when `sa_caa_hover_preview` is enabled, both the hover-preview popup and the bigbox tooltip fire on the same `mouseenter` event. The previous code anchored the tooltip to `ph.getBoundingClientRect()`, placing it on top of the already-visible preview popup. Fixed in `_wireInlineThumbnailBigboxTooltip`: the positioning block now detects whether `#mb-art-hover-preview` is visible (i.e. `sa_caa_hover_preview` is on and `_showArtHoverPreview` has already run), and if so uses the preview div's bounding rect as the anchor, pushing the tooltip to the right of the preview popup — matching the layout of the bigbox strip tooltip. Falls back to `ph.getBoundingClientRect()` when hover-preview is disabled.

sections.label: 🌟 New Feature
items: Add `_wireInlineThumbnailBigboxTooltip(ph)` helper: wires the same enriched bigbox tooltip (mouseenter / mouseleave on the `mb-art-bigbox-tooltip` singleton div) used by the big image strip to inline thumbnail placeholder spans in the `addCAA`-configured column. Primary path: delegates to `_renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns` honoring `tooltipColumns` from the active page definition or from `table.dataset.mbEntityTooltipColumns` (multi-table pages). Static fallback: entity name, disambiguation comment, Artist, Format (Tracks), Country/Date, Label – Cat#, Barcode — identical fields to the bigbox static fallback. Tooltip is positioned to the right of the thumbnail, falling back to the left on viewport overflow. Per-table entity features (`mbEntityTooltipColumns` / `mbEntityExtractMainColumn`) are snapshotted at wire-time so per-group configuration on tag-value and artist-credit pages is respected.

Call `_wireInlineThumbnailBigboxTooltip` at all 5 success paths in `_artInitInlinePics`: fresh IDB load, fresh native `img.onload`, Case C1 same-session restore (existingPh), Case C2 IDB refetch (existingPh, inside `isConnected` guard), and Case C2 native refetch (existingPh, inside `isConnected` guard).
Call `_wireInlineThumbnailBigboxTooltip` at both success paths in `ergInjectCaaInlineThumbnails` (IDB and native fallback) so ERG release-list inline thumbnails also show the tooltip.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: artist-credit-entity: extended entityFeatures with `extractMainColumn` + `stickyColumn` for Release groups and Releases; added Recordings entry with `columnExtractors` (`video` extractor), `syntheticColumnExtractors` (`eventParts` extractor producing Event-Type/Date/Detail/Venue/Venue-Detail/City/State/Country), `extractMainColumn: 'Recording'`, and `stickyColumn: 'Recording'`.

artist-credit: added the same entityFeatures map (Release groups, Releases, Recordings with identical configuration to artist-credit-entity) so that the multi-table overview page resolves per-group CAA, sticky-column, main-column, and extractor settings correctly via `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH3`.

sections.label: 🌟 New Feature
items: Add support for MusicBrainz artist-credit pages: new `@match *://*.musicbrainz.org/artist-credit/*` directive activates the script on all `/artist-credit/…` URLs.

New pageType `artist-credit` (`/artist-credit/`, e.g. `/artist-credit/1488075`): inserts `

Artist list

` after `
` and before the `

This artist credit is composed of…

` paragraph (via `insertH2: 'Artist list'`). The `
    ` below it is left as-is. All `

    +
      ` pairs under `

      Uses

      ` (Release groups, Releases, Recordings, Tracks, …) are converted to one-column `` elements by new Structure F in `applyListToTable`; first-column headers use the singular form of the h3 text (e.g. `Release groups` → `Release group`). The `

      Uses

      ` element stays in place and becomes the `targetHeader` anchor for `renderGroupedTable`. `tableMode: 'multi'` drives the full grouped-table rendering machinery including per-sub-table filters, sorting, master toggle, and collapsible sections. Added `'artist-credit'` to `_singularPageTypes` so `colName` is correctly singularised in the fetch pipeline.

New pageType `artist-credit-entity` (`/artist-credit/<id>/<entity>`, e.g. `/artist-credit/1488075/release-group`): when the entity sub-page renders its data as a plain `<ul>` without id or class, Structure G in `applyListToTable` converts it to a one-column `<table class="tbl">`. The column name is derived from the last URL path segment with hyphens replaced by spaces and the first character capitalised (`release-group` → `Release group`). The entityFeatures map (`Release groups`: `addCAA: 'Release group'`; `Releases`: `addCAA: 'Release'`) is resolved via a new URL-based fast-path in `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2` (plural of the singular URL slug is looked up as the entityFeatures key). `tableMode: 'single'`.

Extended the `See all N` button machinery in `renderGroupedTable` to also fire on `pageType === 'artist-credit'`: the last row of each group table is inspected for a `See all N …` entry; when found, a `mb-show-all-subtable-btn` button is inserted in the h3 header (after the filter container), the trailing row is removed, and `mbTotalRows` is patched. Tooltip text is differentiated from tag-value pages. The original tag-value block now covers both `tag-value`, `user-tag-value`, and `artist-credit` through a unified condition and a `_hrefFragment` / `_pageKindLabel` selector variable.

Added `artist-credit-entity` fast-path to `resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2`: reads the last URL path segment, converts it to singular capitalised form, appends `s` for the plural entityFeatures key (special-casing `Series`), and returns the matching entityFeatures entry.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
Fix 'Show/Hide all sub-sections' master-toggle having no effect on `user-tags` pages (e.g. /user//tags): the page uses `div#page` with no `div#content`, and the native `
    ` lists are wrapped inside `
    ` which is a sibling of the native `

    Tags vzell upvoted

    `. After `applyListToTable` converts the lists, `container` resolves to `div#all-tags` (parentNode of the first `table.tbl`). The cleanup pass empties `div#all-tags` correctly, but all new h3/table pairs are then inserted after the h2 — outside `div#all-tags` — via `lastInsertedElement.after()`. The master-toggle click handler's `container.querySelectorAll('table.tbl')` scoped to the now-empty `div#all-tags` and found nothing. Fix: in `renderGroupedTable`, changed `const container` to `let container` and added a re-root block after the cleanup pass — when `targetHeader` is not contained within `container`, `container` is re-assigned to `targetHeader.parentNode` (`div#page` here). All subsequent `querySelectorAll` calls then scope to the element that actually holds the rendered output. Also reverts the incorrect 9.99.520 fix which had added spurious `renameH2ToH3: true` and `insertH2: 'User tags'` to the `user-tags` definition (that injected a redundant second h2 and shifted the native h2 to appear after the tables).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix (reverted — superseded by 9.99.521)
items: Attempted fix for user-tags master-toggle by adding renameH2ToH3+insertH2 to the user-tags page definition — incorrect: introduced a redundant 'User tags' h2 and caused the native 'Tags vzell upvoted' h2 to appear after the tables. Superseded by the correct container re-root fix in 9.99.521.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix Picard column shift on disk-load for pages with a Picard column (e.g. label/relationships): `_ensureIceCells` was appending ICE placeholder <td>s at the row end via `appendChild`, placing them after the Picard <td> that `initPicardTaggerColumn` had already injected synchronously. Fixed by using `row.insertBefore(td, picardTd)` — inserts ICE cells before the Picard cell when it exists, and falls back to append-at-end (equivalent to `appendChild`) when no Picard cell is present, preserving correct behaviour on live-fetch rows.

sections.label: ✨ Improve
items: Add `injectedColumnExtractors` to `label-relationships-filtered` and `label-relationships` page types: splits 'Release events' into 'Release country' + 'Release date' via `splitCountryDate`, then further splits 'Release date' into 'R-DD', 'R-MM', 'R-YYYY', 'R-Day', 'R-Month' via `dateParts`. Extend `integerColumns` with R-DD (R), R-MM (R), R-YYYY (C) and Length (:) alignment descriptors.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix doubled injected-derived columns (Release country, Release date, R-DD…R-Month) when loading from disk: (a) stamp ICE <th> headers with class `mb-ice-th` in cleanupHeaders so they are excluded from header serialization — on disk-load, cleanupHeaders re-injects them cleanly without duplication; (b) add `_ensureIceCells(row)` inside applyInjectedColumnExtractors that appends the correct number of `mb-ice-cell` placeholder <td>s to each row before processing, covering disk-loaded rows that were reconstructed without ICE cells.

Fix column-shift bug (Release events / Relationships data appearing in ICE columns on disk-load): root cause was that saved headers included ICE <th>s but saved rows excluded ICE <td>s, causing a header/cell count mismatch. Fixed by excluding both from serialization (mb-ice-th headers and mb-ice-cell data cells) and regenerating both on load.

Improve `SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.splitCountryDate` flag rendering: the 'Release country' cell now renders a `United States (US)` element by reading the `flag-XX` CSS class and `title` attribute stamped on each source
  • by `_rePopulateCell`, instead of the bare 2-letter country code from textContent. Events with no area data produce an empty country cell as before.

sections.label: ℹ️ Note
items: No JS conflict exists between `ColumnDataExtractor.splitCountryDate` and `SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.splitCountryDate`: they are methods on two separate object literals and are dispatched independently by their respective caller pipelines (`ColumnDataExtractor[entry.extractor]` vs `SyntheticColumnDataExtractor[entry.extractor]`).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fix doubled injected-derived columns when loading from disk: exclude `mb-ice-cell` <td>s from serialization in both multi-table (grouped) and single-table (allRows) save paths, exactly as `mb-re-cell` and `mb-rel-cell` are already excluded. On load, `initReleaseEventsColumn()` → `applyInjectedColumnExtractors()` re-derives the ICE cells from the re-fetched Release events data, so no stale or duplicated cells appear.

Add '🔗 Total injected derived columns' row to the Statistics panel between '🔁 Total derived columns' and '💉 Total injected columns', reporting the count of synthetic columns produced by `injectedColumnExtractors` (e.g. Release country, Release date, R-DD … R-Month). Value is computed live from `activeInjectedColumnExtractors` at panel-open time; shows 0 with a descriptive comment when no `injectedColumnExtractors` are configured.

sections.label: 🌟 New Feature
items: Add `injectedColumnExtractors` feature for `place-performances` and `place-performances-filtered` page types: splits the async-populated 'Release events' injected column into derived synthetic columns 'Release country' and 'Release date', with 'Release date' further split into 'R-DD', 'R-MM', 'R-YYYY', 'R-Day', 'R-Month' via a chained `dateParts` extractor.

Add `SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.splitCountryDate`: parses the `mb-re-cell` `<ul><li>` format (2-letter country code + two-space separator + ISO date) produced by `_rePopulateCell`, outputting parallel `<ul><li>` structures for 'Release country' and 'Release date' — mirrors the existing `ColumnDataExtractor.splitCountryDate` for native Country/Date DOM columns.

Add `buildActiveInjectedColumnExtractors(def)` builder function, `applyInjectedColumnExtractors()` runtime function, and `let activeInjectedColumnExtractors` variable: a third-pass extraction pipeline that fires after `initReleaseEventsColumn()` has async-populated injected-column cells, with chained-descriptor support (each extractor's output columns feed into the injCellMap for downstream descriptors).
Extend `integerColumns` for both place-performances page types with R-DD (align R), R-MM (align R), R-YYYY (align C) descriptors for the new release-date synthetic columns.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Improve
items: Add missing configuration parameters for multiple pageTypes.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Improve
items: Search entityFeatures: corrected several entries vs v9.99.511 — Releases: added addCAA:'Name'; Events: added addEAA:'Name'; Release groups: added addCAA:'Release group'; Recordings: merged duplicate collapsableColumns, added Length integer column with ':' align, added addCAA:'Release'; Artists: removed splitArea extractors for Area/Begin area/End area (these columns are not present in search results); Areas: changed extractor from splitLocation to splitArea with simpler syntheticColumns ['Area','Country'], removed extractMainColumn (not needed for area search); Labels: removed splitArea extractor (Area column absent in label search results); CD stubs: renamed key from 'Cdstubs' to 'CD stubs' to match _urlTypeToEntityFeatureKey('cdstub') output; added Instruments entity feature entry; Works: restored correct collapsableColumns ['Authors','Recording artists','Other artists','ISWC'].

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / 🧹 Cleanup
items: search entityFeatures: corrected multiple entries vs v9.99.511 — added addCAA:'Name' to Releases, addEAA:'Name' to Events, addCAA:'Release group' to Release groups, addCAA:'Release' to Recordings; removed incorrect splitArea extractors from Artists (no Area column on artist search); changed Areas to use splitArea (not splitLocation); removed splitArea from Labels (no Area column); added Instruments entry; fixed duplicate collapsableColumns key in Recordings; fixed Track align to 'R' and added Length align ':'; added 'Release' to Recordings collapsableColumns.
_urlTypeToEntityFeatureKey: added special case for 'cdstub' → 'CD stubs' (MB URL type has no space; entityFeatures key has space and capital letters). Updated JSDoc example.

artist-releasegroups: commented out the '🧮 Official RGs' and '🧮 Official VA RGs' buttons with a note that the per-view toggle buttons (Official Discography, Non-Official Discography, Complete, Complete (merged)) supersede them.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: Search pages: added full entityFeatures map covering all MB search entity types (Releases, Events, Release groups, Recordings, Artists, Places, Areas, Labels, Series, Cdstubs, Works, Tags, Annotations, Editors). Each entry carries appropriate columnExtractors, syntheticColumnExtractors, integerColumns, collapsableColumns, tooltipColumns, injectedColumns, renderMultiRowCell, extractMainColumn, and stickyColumn settings. The old flat features block (renderMultiRowCell, collapsableColumns, extractMainColumn, stickyColumn) is replaced by per-entity config.

Added _urlTypeToEntityFeatureKey() helper that converts URL ?type= parameter values to entityFeatures map keys (release → Releases, release_group → Release groups, annotation → Annotations, series → Series, etc.).

Added labelFromType: true button-config flag: when set, reads ?type= from the URL at button-creation time, converts it via _urlTypeToEntityFeatureKey(), and appends 'for <EntityType>' to the base label (e.g. 'Show all Search Results for Release groups').

resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2: added a fast-path for pageType='search' that reads ?type= from the URL and resolves the matching entityFeatures key directly, bypassing the H2-text lookup (which is unreliable on search pages whose H2 is dynamically generated from the query).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ New
items: Picard: merged double JSDoc on initPicardTaggerColumn into a single combined comment with @param documented.
Picard: excluded /release/<mbid>/collections and /release-group/<mbid>/collections from _picardPageIsSupported() — these pages list collection memberships, not entities to queue in Picard.
Picard: added /area/<mbid> and /label/<mbid> to _PICARD_PAGE_PATTERNS so area-releases and label pages now show the Picard column.

Picard: added a tooltip to the 'Picard' column header (cursor:help) explaining that clicking the ♪ icon loads the entity into Picard, and that Picard must be running locally with the Browser Integration plugin enabled.

sections.label: 🧹 Cleanup
items: Removed all temporary diagnostic markers added during the ▶ button investigation (v9.99.505–9.99.508): pipeline checkpoints A–G, finalCleanup ENTER/EXIT, runFilter reached, main pipeline reached, initExpandRGsFeature ENTERED/found/injected, isExpandRGsPage no-links/first-href. Restored the erg debug category for the remaining permanent log lines in initExpandRGsFeature.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: ▶ expand button missing on cdtoc, taglookup, user-tag-value-entity pages and any other page that uses div#page instead of div#content. Root cause: the selectors in isExpandRGsPage() and initExpandRGsFeature() were anchored to '#content table.tbl'. Checking the saved cdtoc HTML confirmed the page uses id='page' not id='content', so '#content table.tbl' found nothing — and consequently document.querySelector('#content table.tbl > tbody > tr > td a[href]') returned null, explaining the absent first-link diagnostic in the log. The taglookup and user-tag-value-entity pages almost certainly have the same structure. Fixed by replacing '#content' with ':is(#content,#page)' in all three selector strings.

sections.label: 🔍 Debug
items: From the cdtoc debug log: all pipeline checkpoints A-G and all finalCleanup ENTER/EXIT markers were completely absent (neither shown nor error), while DOM rendering finished and Process complete both appeared normally. Root cause: the 'erg' debug category is disabled in this session, making all Lib.debug('erg',...) calls silent no-ops. The code runs but produces no output. Changed all diagnostic debug calls from category 'erg' to category 'render' (confirmed enabled — 'DOM rendering finished' uses this category and appears in every log). All checkpoints A-G, finalCleanup ENTER/EXIT, main pipeline reached, runFilter reached, and initExpandRGsFeature ENTERED/found/injected messages will now appear as 🎨 render-category entries.

sections.label: 🔍 Debug
items: From the uploaded debug log: 'DOM rendering finished' and 'Process complete' appear consecutively with no erg markers in between, confirming that an uncaught exception is thrown somewhere between line 24466 (after the column-resizing block) and line 24519 (initExpandRGsFeature). The exception is caught by the outer try/catch at line 24606, which calls Lib.error('fetch',...) — invisible when filtering for 'initExpandRGsFeature'. Added fine-grained debug checkpoints A-G between addColumnVisibilityToggle and isLoaded=true so the last checkpoint visible before the jump to 'DOM rendering finished' pinpoints the exact throwing function.

sections.label: 🔍 Debug
items: Added diagnostic markers (all under Lib.debug 'erg' channel) to narrow down why initExpandRGsFeature() is never reached on taglookup and user-tag-value-entity pages: (1) entry log at the very start of initExpandRGsFeature before all guards, (2) 'reached call site' log in the main pipeline at line 24518, (3) 'reached call site' log in the runFilter single-table path, (4) ENTER/EXIT logs around finalCleanup() so a missing EXIT reveals which block inside finalCleanup throws. If 'main pipeline: reached call site' is absent but 'finalCleanup: ENTER' is present and 'finalCleanup: EXIT' is absent, the exception is inside finalCleanup.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: ▶ expand button still missing on taglookup and user-tag-value-entity (/user/<n>/tag/<v>/release) pages. Root cause: MusicBrainz renders absolute hrefs (https://musicbrainz.org/release/GUID) on some pages (confirmed by diagnostic logging). The selector a[href^='/release'] only matches relative URLs starting with '/release'; it silently finds zero links on pages with absolute hrefs. Fixed by extending the selector in both isExpandRGsPage() and initExpandRGsFeature() to cover both patterns: a[href^='/release'] (relative) and a[href*='musicbrainz.org/release'] (absolute). The cover-art and cover-art path exclusions are preserved. Also added a diagnostic debug log that prints the first td link href when no links are found, to aid future diagnosis.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: ▶ expand button missing on many page types (tag pages /tag/<value>, user-tag pages /user/<name>/tag/<v>/release, area-releases, taglookup, and others). Root cause: isExpandRGsPage() was a URL-pattern whitelist that only covered artist/*, label/<uuid>, release-group/<uuid>, series/<uuid>, recording/<uuid>, collection/<uuid>, and taglookup — all other page types with release links were excluded even when the rendered table contained qualifying /release/ or /release-group/ links. Fix: replaced the whitelist with a dynamic DOM check — when isLoaded is true (the table exists), the function returns true iff the rendered table contains at least one a[href^='/release']:not([href*='/cover-art']) in a tbody td. When isLoaded is false (speculative call before render), an extended URL check covers the common cases including /tag/, /area/, and the original whitelist entries. This approach automatically handles all current and future page types without requiring individual entries.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: Added Collection_Editor ColumnDataExtractor: splits the combined 'CollectionName by EditorName' cell produced by the Structure C ul→table conversion into separate Collection and Editor columns. Finds the /collection/ anchor for Collection and the /user/ anchor (including the avatar img) for Editor.

release-collections and releasegroup-collections pageTypes now declare columnExtractors with Collection_Editor on sourceColumn 'Collections', yielding the two separate columns after the list-to-table conversion.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: @include header: added 'collections' to the sub-pages pattern so /release/<mbid>/collections and /release-group/<mbid>/collections URLs are matched.
Renamed pageType 'collections' → 'user-collections' with a narrower match (/user/.*/collections) to avoid false matches on entity collection pages.

Added pageType 'release-collections' matching /release/<mbid>/collections and 'releasegroup-collections' matching /release-group/<mbid>/collections. Both use tableMode:single and listToTable:[''] to trigger Structure C in applyListToTable (h2+ul → table).
applyListToTable Structure C: extended the path-classification guard to also match /release|release-group/<mbid>/collections paths, enabling the h2+ul conversion for the new pageTypes.

finalCleanup for release-collections / releasegroup-collections: removes the trailing 'plus N other private collections' row, patches the h2 row-count stat, and rewrites the intro paragraph to 'EntityName has been added to N collections and M other private collections' (the private clause is omitted when M=0).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: cdtoc tracklist toggle (v9.99.499 still broken — two further bugs). Bug 1: inner tracklist table has class='tbl medium'. After _cdtocInitTracklistToggles injected it into the DOM, every querySelectorAll('table.tbl') call (applyStickyColumn, initPicardTaggerColumn, _artInitPics, etc.) picked up the inner table and processed its track rows as if they were main-table data rows, adding mb-sticky-col, mb-picard-cell, and other decorations that disrupted the layout. Fix: after setting _subTd.innerHTML, query _subTd.querySelectorAll('table.tbl') and remove the 'tbl' class, replacing it with 'mb-cdtoc-inner-tracklist', so the inner table is invisible to all script-wide querySelectorAll('table.tbl') passes. Bug 2: _subTd.colSpan = tr.cells.length was evaluated at finalCleanup time, before initRelationshipsColumn and initPicardTaggerColumn appended their cells to the data rows. The colSpan was therefore too small, causing the tracklist to only span the initially-extracted columns. Fix: replaced with colSpan=999, which the browser clamps to the actual column count of the outer table.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: cdtoc tracklist toggle (v9.99.498 completely broken — three bugs). Bug 1: detection check used style.display==='none' but the saved HTML shows the tracklist row has class='tracklist' and style='' when visible or style='display:none' when hidden — style alone is unreliable for detection. Bug 2: the check required cells.length===1 but the tracklist row has two cells (empty td + td with colspan=6). Bug 3: the tracklist row passed the generic data-row check (cells.length > 1 = 2) and was extracted into allRows as a garbage row with a nested table instead of being skipped and captured. Fix: added an explicit class='tracklist' interception branch in the tableBody.childNodes.forEach loop, BEFORE the generic data-row else-if. This branch (a) skips the row from allRows entirely, (b) finds the content cell (colspan>1 or non-empty), (c) stores its innerHTML in dataset.cdtocTracklistHtml on the preceding data row, and (d) records the initial visibility in dataset.cdtocTracklistVisible ('true'=visible/hide, 'false'=hidden/show). _cdtocInitTracklistToggles updated to use cdtocTracklistVisible for the sub-row's initial display state and to sync the toggle link label accordingly.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: cdtoc pages: the 'show tracklist' toggle link now works on the final rendered page. On the original MB CDtoc page each data row is followed by a hidden <tr> (display:none, single cell with a high colSpan) containing the release tracklist table. The generic row-skip rule excluded these from allRows. Fix: during the fetch loop, when processing a cdtoc data row, the inner HTML of the immediately following hidden sibling <tr> is captured and stored in newRow.dataset.cdtocTracklistHtml. After every render, the new _cdtocInitTracklistToggles() function injects a fresh .mb-cdtoc-tracklist-row after each data row that carries this attribute, and wires the <a class='toggle'> link to show/hide it (toggling the text between 'show tracklist' and 'hide tracklist'). The attribute survives cloneNode(true) used by runFilter so the toggle re-wires correctly after filter and sort re-renders. The attribute is not serialized by saveTableDataToDisk (TR-level dataset attrs are not saved), so the toggle silently does nothing on Load from Disk. Called from finalCleanup(), runFilter() single-table path, and loadTableDataFromDisk().

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: artist-releasegroups: CAA image stripes from official-section tables leaked into the Non-Official Discography view after toggling the bigbox visible in merged view and then switching sections. Root cause: the drain pass at the end of _applyDiscographyViewFilter calls initCaaPics() to rebuild bigboxes for tables whose tbody was just restored from merged. _artInitBigPics read the stored caaVisible='true' attribute (set when the user expanded the bigbox in merged view) and applied display:flex, overriding the display:none that the visibility loop had already set on official-section bigboxes. Fix: in _artInitBigPics, after applying the initial display style, check whether the owning h3 has dataset.mbDiscHidden='true' (set by _applyDiscographyViewFilter for every hidden section) and if so force the bigbox to display:none. The stored caaVisible attribute is deliberately preserved so switching back to a view where the table is visible correctly restores the user's expansion preference.

sections.label: ✨ Improvement
items: isExpandRGsPage: added taglookup pages (/taglookup/*) to the set of pages where the expand release-groups feature is active, matching URLs like /taglookup/index?tag-lookup.artist=…

sections.label: ✨ Improvement
items: Picard tagger: corrected tagger URL endpoint from /?id= to /openalbum?id= so clicks are routed to Picard's correct browser-integration handler.
Picard tagger: changed idle button icon from green (#4a9e50) to yellow (#f0b429) to distinguish it from the success state.

Removed sa_remove_tagger setting (Remove Tagger column checkbox) — the native MusicBrainz Tagger column is now always removed unconditionally since the script's own Picard column replaces it. Moved 'Tagger' from the setting-gated removalMap to removalMapAlways in both column-removal passes.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Picard tagger column (v9.99.493): column mismatch on Load from Disk still present in single-table mode. Root cause identical to the multi-table case: runFilter()'s single-table path also calls initPicardTaggerColumn() (full mode) which appended picard_td before rel_td during the load-from-disk runFilter pass. Fix: changed the runFilter single-table call to initPicardTaggerColumn(rewireOnly=true), matching the multi-table path. Both runFilter paths now pass rewireOnly=true; only the main pipeline and load-from-disk external call sites (both of which run after initRelationshipsColumn) pass the default false.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Picard tagger column (v9.99.492): Bug 1 column mismatch on Load from Disk still present. Root cause: the runFilter multi-table path called initPicardTaggerColumn() (without rewireOnly flag) after updateH2CountFromSubtables, which entered the 'append new td' branch because source rows had no picard_td yet (load-from-disk calls runFilter BEFORE initRelationshipsColumn). This appended picard_td before rel_td on the DOM rows. Fix: added a rewireOnly parameter to initPicardTaggerColumn(rewireOnly=false). When rewireOnly=true the function only re-wires event listeners on existing td.mb-picard-cell elements and never appends new tds. The runFilter multi-table path now calls initPicardTaggerColumn(true); all other call sites (main pipeline, load-from-disk, runFilter single-table) pass false (full mode) and always run after initRelationshipsColumn so column order is correct.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Picard tagger column (v9.99.491): two remaining bugs fixed. Bug 1 — column misalignment on Load from Disk still present. Root cause: loadTableDataFromDisk calls runFilter() at line 33029 (to reset the filter state) BEFORE it calls initRelationshipsColumn and initPicardTaggerColumn. runFilter's multi-table path triggered initPicardTaggerColumn() immediately after renderGroupedTable, appending picard_td before rel_td was added by the subsequent initRelationshipsColumn call. Fix: moved initPicardTaggerColumn() in runFilter's multi-table branch to run after updateH2CountFromSubtables (the very end of the multi-table block), where source rows already carry both rel_td and picard_td from the initial render. During the load-from-disk sequence those source rows have neither td yet, but initPicardTaggerColumn finds no existing picard_td and therefore does nothing — the correct injection then happens in the explicit initRelationshipsColumn → initPicardTaggerColumn sequence at the bottom of loadTableDataFromDisk. Bug 2 — navigation guard dialog on single-table pages when clicking the Picard icon. Root cause: the Picard button is a <button> element; on artist-releases pages a <form> containing a div.list-merge-buttons-row-container wraps the table area. Guard 3 (merge-form submit-button guard) matched any button click whose closest form contains that container — including the Picard button. Fix: added an early return for buttons with class mb-picard-btn in the Guard 3 click handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Picard tagger column (v9.99.489/490): two remaining bugs fixed. Bug 1 — scroll-to-top on click in multi-table mode. Root cause: the button was an <a href='#'> element. Clicking a <a href='#'> without a live event listener (listeners are stripped by cloneNode(true) during sort/filter re-renders) scrolls the page to the top via the browser's default href='#' behaviour. Changing the element from <a> to <button type='button'> eliminates the default action entirely — a button with no handler silently does nothing. Bug 2 — Relationships column showing Picard icons and Picard column showing relationship icons after Load from Disk. Root cause: initPicardTaggerColumn() was called BEFORE initRelationshipsColumn() in the main fetch pipeline and load-from-disk pipeline, so the Picard td was appended to each row before the Relationships td; this misaligned data cells with header cells. Fix: (a) removed initPicardTaggerColumn() from renderGroupedTable()'s tail entirely and added it to runFilter()'s multi-table path after renderGroupedTable() returns; (b) moved initPicardTaggerColumn() to run after initRelationshipsColumn() in the main fetch pipeline and load-from-disk pipeline. Picard is now consistently the rightmost column in all render paths.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Picard tagger column (v9.99.489): two bugs fixed. Bug 1 — clicking the Picard icon after sorting or filtering caused the page to scroll to top-left instead of sending to Picard. Root cause: runFilter single-table branch calls row.cloneNode(true) to rebuild the tbody, which strips event listeners. The old data-picard-col-injected guard on the table then prevented re-injection on the clones, leaving <a href='#'> buttons with no click handler so the default browser href='#' scroll fired. Fix: separated thead idempotency (data-picard-th-injected, set once) from tbody wiring (now always re-processed on every initPicardTaggerColumn call). When a row already has a td.mb-picard-cell, its button is replaced with a fresh one; when it does not, a new cell is appended. Bug 2 — Load from Disk incorrectly serialized and reloaded the Picard column: the <th> was saved in headers, the <td> cells in row data, producing a duplicate Picard column and a displaced Relationships column on load. Fix: saveTableDataToDisk now filters out cells with class mb-picard-th from header rows and mb-picard-cell from data rows in both multi-table and single-table serialization paths, mirroring the existing mb-rel-cell / mb-re-cell exclusions.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: Picard Tagger column: injects a 'Picard' column as the last column in every rendered table on supported page types (releases, release-groups, recordings, series, collections, cdtoc, taglookup, search, artist/*/releases). Each row cell contains a green ♪ icon button; clicking it sends that row's release/release-group/recording to MusicBrainz Picard via its local browser-integration HTTP endpoint. Visual feedback: green ✓ on success, red ✕ on error. Port is auto-detected by probing the configured range (default 8000–8010). Controlled by four new settings in a PICARD TAGGER section: sa_enable_picard_tagger (on/off, default true), sa_picard_tagger_default_port (default 8000), sa_picard_tagger_max_port (default 8010), sa_picard_tagger_host (default 127.0.0.1). The idempotency guard (data-picard-col-injected) prevents the column from being duplicated on filter/sort re-renders. Based on the 'MusicBrainz Magic Tagger Button' userscript by Philipp Wolfer (https://github.com/phw/musicbrainz-magic-tagger-button, MIT licence).

sections.label: ✨ New
items: Added support for TagLookup pages (/taglookup*). New @match header. New 'taglookup' pageType (single-table, button label 'Show all Releases for Tags') with: caa extractor on Name producing CAA column; splitCountryDate on Country/Date; sumTracks on Tracks producing Total Tracks; extractFormatTypes on Format; dateParts on Date producing DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month; injectedColumns Relationships; integerColumns DD(R)/MM(R)/YYYY(C)/Total Tracks(R); renderMultiRowCell Label/Catalog#; collapsableColumns Country/Date, Country, Date, Label, Catalog#, CAA; tooltipColumns MB-Name / italic:Comment / Artist / divider / [Format (Tracks)] / Country/Date / [Label - Catalog#] / Barcode / Language / Type / Status; addCAA on Name; extractMainColumn Name; stickyColumn Name; insertH2 Releases; insertPrependH2 Searchform. finalCleanup now relocates div.searchform immediately after the injected Searchform h2 for taglookup pages so the collapsible-section infrastructure wraps the search form together with its heading.

sections.label: ✨ Improvement
items: Search page H2 prefix now includes the entity type from the 'type' URL query parameter in addition to the search method. Previously the prefix was e.g. 'Advanced search: '; it is now 'Advanced artist search: ' for ?method=advanced&type=artist. When the type parameter is absent the output is unchanged.

sections.label: ✨ New
items: Added support for CDtoc pages (/cdtoc/*). New @match header covers all MusicBrainz cdtoc URLs. New 'cdtoc' pageType (single-table, button label 'Show all CDToc attached to Releases') with: splitCountryDate extractor on Country/Date producing Country and Date synthetic columns; dateParts extractor on Date producing DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month; injectedColumns Relationships; integerColumns DD(R)/MM(R)/YYYY(C); renderMultiRowCell for Label and Catalog#; collapsableColumns Country/Date, Country, Date, Label, Catalog#; tooltipColumns MB-Name / italic:Comment / Artist / divider / Format / Country/Date / [Label - Catalog#] / Barcode; addCAA on Title column; extractMainColumn Title; stickyColumn Title.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Discography view-mode buttons (Complete Discography / Official Discography / Non-Official Discography / Complete Discography (merged)) missing after Load from Disk on artist-releasegroups pages. Root cause: saveTableDataToDisk did not persist h3_official_category_header_array (which comes from the pre-fetch pass) so on load the array was empty, the guard h3_official_category_header_array.length > 0 always failed, and _injectDiscographyViewButtons() was never called. Three coordinated changes: (1) saveTableDataToDisk now saves h3_official_category_header_array as discOfficialCategories in the JSON file for artist-releasegroups pages. (2) renderGroupedTable now stamps h3.dataset.mbDiscSection / table.dataset.mbDiscSection on every newly created h3/table pair using the category arrays if they are populated — this means every re-render (runFilter, view switch, disk-load) automatically keeps the correct section tag on fresh h3 elements, not just the initial post-fetch pass. (3) loadTableDataFromDisk now restores h3_official_category_header_array from data.discOfficialCategories (when present), rebuilds h3_all_category_header_array and h3_non_official_category_header_array from groupedRows using the same sequence-match algorithm as startFetchingProcess, and calls _injectDiscographyViewButtons() after makeH2sCollapsible(). Files saved before this version lack discOfficialCategories and silently skip button injection (no regression).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: CAA/EAA per-subtable badge count wrong after switching between discography views (merged → official / complete): badge showed e.g. 84 instead of 21. Root cause identified via targeted debug logging (v9.99.482-dbg / v9.99.483-dbg): _artCountLinks and _artInitBigPics scan 'tbody td a[href]' to find unique entity links for bigbox image loading and badge counting. On the first render (gen=1) this is correct because only the title column contains matching /release-group/GUID hrefs. However, two features inject additional matching hrefs into the rows asynchronously after gen=1: (1) applyStickyColumn adds a td.mb-sticky-col clone of the title column to every row, duplicating the title link; (2) the Relationships column (td.mb-rel-cell) injects related release-group anchors. On every subsequent initCaaPics()/initEaaPics() call triggered by a view switch, these columns are already present, causing seen.size to be 4× the row count for affected tables. Fix: changed the selector in both _artCountLinks and _artInitBigPics from 'tbody td a[href]' to 'tbody td:not(.mb-sticky-col):not(.mb-rel-cell) a[href]', excluding the sticky-column duplicate and the relationships column from the entity-link scan. Also removed all temporary debug console.log statements added in v9.99.482-dbg and v9.99.483-dbg.

sections.label: 🔬 Debug
items: Second debug build refining the CAA count diagnosis. v9.99.482-dbg confirmed the shape of the bug: at gen=1 (initial render) seen.size equals tbody_rows (1 link/row), but after the merged → official round-trip seen.size is 4× tbody_rows for affected tables (e.g. tbl-0: 21 rows → 84 links). Added targeted [ART-HREF] log inside the seen.add path in _artInitBigPics that records: table index, column index (cellIndex), entity type matched, full href, and parent element tag+class. This will identify which column the 3 extra hrefs per row come from and whether they are /release-group/GUID or /release/GUID paths. Log fires once per unique matched href per _artInitBigPics call. With debug logging enabled (Settings > Enable debug logging) filter console to [ART-HREF] for gen=1 and gen=3 passes on tbl-0 to spot the new hrefs and their column.

sections.label: 🔬 Debug
items: Temporary debug build to diagnose persisting CAA count corruption on discography view switches. Added 7 console.log statements (tagged [DISC-DBG] / [ART-DBG]) to _applyDiscographyViewFilter (entry, drain decision with table row counts), _artInitBigPics (entry with domIdx cross-check, gen advance, final seen.size/box.id), and _onBigLoaded (stale-callback skip with myRenderGen vs btn.gen). Debug logs should be removed once root cause is confirmed.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: CAA/EAA per-subtable badge counts still wrong when switching from 'Complete Discography (merged)' back to 'Official Discography' or 'Complete Discography' (v9.99.480 fix was incomplete). Root cause: _artRebuildBigPicsForTable enqueues new tasks into the SHARED _caaQueue that already holds in-flight tasks from every previous _artInitBigPics call (initial render, earlier view switches). Even though the per-button render-generation counter (data-art-render-gen) should invalidate stale callbacks, the tasks themselves never leave the queue and keep consuming concurrency slots; IDB cache hits can fire synchronously inside the task body and advance shared state before the caller expects, making the gen-counter invalidation unreliable. Fix: replace the _tablesToRebuild drain in _applyDiscographyViewFilter with a conditional call to initCaaPics()/initEaaPics() whenever any table tbody was replaced (_tablesToRebuild.length > 0). initCaaPics() creates a BRAND-NEW _caaQueue, abandoning all pending tasks at the queue level (the old pending[] array is unreferenced and GC'd) and advancing all per-table gen counters in one clean pass. Running tasks from the old queue still complete but their _onBigLoaded callbacks fail the gen-check and become no-ops. initCaaPics/initEaaPics also call _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton internally, so the separate call is now skipped in the rebuild branch. Pure visibility switches (all/official/non-official with no merged content) still take the fast path that only refreshes the global badge.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: CAA/EAA mb-caa-toggle-count wrong after switching to 'Complete Discography (merged)' view on artist-releasegroups pages (e.g. Album shows 85 instead of 23, and subsequent view switches corrupt the count for that section permanently). Root cause: two interacting races in _applyDiscographyViewFilter. Race A (wrong _tblIdx): _artRebuildBigPicsForTable resolves a table's DOM index via querySelectorAll('table.tbl').indexOf(table); when called inline inside the restore/merged forEach loops, subsequent bigbox insertBefore moves shift that index between calls so different tables accidentally share a btn-N/box-N ID pair and artwork from one section's strip increments another section's badge. Race B (async _onBigLoaded against stale mbDiscHidden): the guard _owningH3.dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true' in each img.onload closure is evaluated at async-callback time, which may be before later forEach iterations have stamped their h3s; if _artInitBigPics starts IDB/network requests for table-0 while the h3 for table-1 still lacks mbDiscHidden='true', callbacks for table-0's images inflate the global badge by table-1's artwork count. Fix: converted both inline _artRebuildBigPicsForTable calls to push into a _tablesToRebuild[] queue, then drain the queue in a single pass after the full visibility forEach has completed. This guarantees (1) all mbDiscHidden flags are settled before any async requests begin, (2) the DOM table order is final before any querySelectorAll index lookup, and (3) _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton sees freshly-zeroed per-table badges when it runs immediately after the drain.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Group accumulation in the _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode fetch branch: the v9.99.468 fix applied a global find-or-create (merge) strategy that was correct for collections-releases Release groups but broke artist-releasegroups. The two page types have fundamentally different pagination behaviours: (1) artist-releasegroups uses a continuation / ordered-sequence model — when a category like 'Album + Live' exceeds 100 entries it spills across consecutive XHR pages that all start with the same h3, and those continuation pages must append to the existing group; but when the same h3 text reappears much later in the paginated stream it signals a different section (Non-Official Discography), which must create a new group so that _injectDiscographyViewButtons can correctly split Official vs Non-Official views. (2) collections-releases Release groups uses a merge / idempotent model — every XHR page repeats all h3 headers at its top, so the lastCategorySeenAcrossPages continuation rule would create duplicate groups on every page break; find-or-create is the only correct approach. Fix: split the accumulation logic inside the _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode branch on pageType: artist-releasegroups gets the restored original 'same-as-last → continue, different → new group' rule; all other page types (collections-releases Release groups) get the find-or-create merge rule. Both branches are thoroughly documented in the surrounding comments.

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: @require URL for VZ_MBLibrary changed from local file:///V:/home/vzell/git/mb-userscripts/lib/VZ_MBLibrary.user.js to https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vzell/mb-userscripts/master/lib/VZ_MBLibrary.user.js so the script can be installed and run without a local development checkout.

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: JSDoc audit: added missing JSDoc blocks to all 25 previously undocumented top-level functions. Functions covered: applyListToTable (full Structure A–E description), applyStickyColumn, _exportCleanHeaderText, exportTableToCSV, _buildExportMetaLines, addExportButton, uiActionBtnBaseCSS, gfBorderIdle / gfBorderActive / stfBorderIdle / stfBorderActive / filterBorderError (one-liner each), _getActiveArtCtx, showLoadFilterDialog (full three-phase description), normalizeAliasTable, getCleanColumnText (two-pass clone-and-strip approach documented), startFetchingProcess (full 8-step pipeline overview), initTreleasesObserver, renderFinalTable, makeTableSortableUnified, finalCleanup (all tasks listed), _relCreateRetryButtons, _relRetryTable, _relRetryAll, _openEditPinnedFilterListFromSettings. All 326 top-level functions now have JSDoc. No runtime behaviour changed.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Added integerColumns DD(R)/MM(R)/YYYY(C) to 6 pageType definitions that used dateParts('Date') but were missing numeric alignment: collections-releases entityFeatures 'Events', area-events, place-events, series-releases entityFeatures 'Events', label-relationships-filtered, label-relationships.

work-recordings-filtered and work-recordings: extended existing integerColumns from [{Length:':'}] to [DD(R), MM(R), YYYY(C), {Length:':'}] to add date-part alignment alongside the existing colon-split length column.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: area-artists: extended columnExtractors from a single splitArea('Area') entry to the full artist-area breakdown: splitArea for 'Area', 'Begin area', 'End area'; dateParts for 'Begin' (B-DD/B-MM/B-YYYY/B-Day/B-Month) and 'End' (E-DD/E-MM/E-YYYY/E-Day/E-Month). Added integerColumns for all six B-/E- date part columns (R/R/C alignment). Matches the Artists entityFeature set in collections-releases.
artist-events: added missing integerColumns for DD (R), MM (R), YYYY (C) — the only pageType with dateParts('Date') that was lacking them (all others already had it).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
popular-tags: #mb-status-displays-wrapper and the intro

appeared after the last sub-table instead of between the

and the new

Popular tags

. Root cause: applyInsertH2's new after-h1 fallback (v9.99.473) inserted the h2 immediately after

, which is before the statusWrapper and p that the script had already inserted between

and the original

Genres

at init time. Fix: changed the fallback insertion point from 'afterend of first h1' to 'beforebegin of first h3' in the content area. After renameH2ToH3 has run, the original section headings are h3 elements; inserting the new h2 immediately before the first h3 leaves all pre-existing sibling nodes (statusWrapper, p, etc.) above the h2, exactly where they should be. The 'after first h1' path is retained as a secondary fallback for pages with no h3 siblings.

popular-tags: removed the 'Show as a list/cloud instead.' toggle <a> link from the intro paragraph via removeSelector: 'a[href*="tags?show_list"]'. The script's own action button replaces that navigation function.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
popular-tags: two remaining bugs after v9.99.472. Root cause of both: applyInsertH2 fell back to document.body.prepend() because the /tags page has neither div.tabs nor div#page (only div#content), placing

Popular tags

before all navigation — outside #content entirely. Bug 2 (h1 + action buttons rendered side-by-side with h2): the controlsContainer was appended to the misplaced h2 and makeH2sCollapsible rendered it inline at the top of body next to whatever browser chrome appeared there. Bug 1 (sorting/filtering broken): the filterContainer was also attached to the misplaced h2, and makeH2sCollapsible's contentNodes sweep captured the entire #content div as the h2's collapsible section, preventing the filter/sort machinery from correctly locating and interacting with the sub-tables. Fix: added a second-preference path to applyInsertH2 — when no div.tabs is found, insert afterend of the first

inside #content (or #page/body). For /tags this places

Popular tags

immediately after

Tags

inside #content, before the renamed

Genres

, which is the correct position. The last-resort prepend fallback is preserved for structureless pages.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
popular-tags: no tables rendered after button click (groupedRows always empty). Three coordinated bugs identified from debug log: (1) Structure E in applyListToTable walked querySelectorAll('h3') only — on the fetched XHR doc renameH2ToH3 has not yet been applied so the original

Genres

/

Other tags

elements are still h2, not h3. Fix: changed to querySelectorAll('h3, h2') so Structure E creates tables correctly on both the live DOM (h3 after rename) and fetched docs (h2 not yet renamed). (2) listToTable multi-table branch backward walk only checked tagName === 'H3' — even if Structure E had created tables from h2-labelled sections, the category name could not be extracted from the preceding h2. Fix: added '|| _prev.tagName === 'H2'' to the backward walk check. (3) 'popular-tags' was missing from _singularPageTypes — category names like 'Genres'/'Other tags' would not be singularised to 'Genre'/'Other tag'. Fix: added 'popular-tags' to the set.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Added support for the MusicBrainz Most Popular Tags page (/tags). (1) New @match directive: *://*.musicbrainz.org/tags* added after the user-tags match. (2) New pageType 'popular-tags': match=/^/tags/, single button 'Show most popular tags' with params:{show_list:'1'}, features: renameH2ToH3:true (demotes the existing Genres/Other Tags h2s to h3), insertH2:'Popular tags' (creates the new section anchor h2), listToTable:[''] (triggers the new Structure E), integerColumns Tag count right-aligned, tableMode:'multi'. (3) Structure E in applyListToTable (sectionId='' dispatch): detected via _currentPath.match(/^\/tags/). Walks every <h3> (after renameH2ToH3) and converts its next-sibling <ul class='tag-list top-tag-list'> into a 2-column <table class='tbl'> whose columns are singular(h3 text) (e.g. 'Genre', 'Other Tag') and 'Tag count', matching the Structure A/B layout used by user-tags pages. The table dataset.mbOriginalColName is set so renderGroupedTable can patch per-group cloned theads correctly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: collections-releases 'Release groups': CAA inline thumbnails (mb-caa-inline-ph) still not rendered after v9.99.469. Root cause of that fix being ineffective: the else-if (_isReleaseGroupsMultiMode()) branch added in v9.99.469 was dead code for collections-releases. Because activeDefinition.entityFeatures exists on the collections-releases base definition (it holds the full entity map with 'Release groups'.addCAA='Title'), _tagHasEntityFeatureCaa is set to true and the if (_tagHasEntityFeatureCaa) branch is always entered — the else-if is never reached. The per-group loop inside that branch is a no-op (groups built by _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode have no group.entityFeatures). Features are then restored to _origFeaturesPreCaa which still contains addCAA:'Title' (it was present there all along, merged by startFetchingProcess). Fix: added an if (_isReleaseGroupsMultiMode()) call to initCaaInlinePics()/initEaaInlinePics() INSIDE the if (_tagHasEntityFeatureCaa) block, immediately after the feature restore, while addCAA:'Title' is back in activeDefinition.features. The else-if branch is retained for artist-releasegroups (which has no entityFeatures and still reaches it).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: collections-releases 'Release groups': ERG (▶ Toggle display of underlying entity page) buttons were not injected. Root cause: isExpandRGsPage() had no branch for /collection/ URLs, so initExpandRGsFeature() returned early. Fix: added 'if new RegExp(^/collection/<mbid>).test(p) return true' to isExpandRGsPage().

collections-releases 'Release groups': CAA inline thumbnails (mb-caa-inline-ph) were not rendered. Root cause: renderGroupedTable's inline-pic dispatch has a tag-page per-group entityFeatures path and a standard single-feature path, but no branch for _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode(). The tag-page branch fires (activeDefinition.entityFeatures exists on the base definition) but iterates group.entityFeatures which is always empty for _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode() groups — so every group was silently skipped, while addCAA:'Title' was already correctly set in activeDefinition.features. Fix: added an else-if (_isReleaseGroupsMultiMode()) branch that calls initCaaInlinePics() / initEaaInlinePics() directly using the already-merged activeDefinition.features.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Reverted the H2-detection code added in v9.99.467 (incorrect: the heading on collections-release-group and releasegroup-releases pages is H3 throughout, not H2). The actual bug was in the group accumulation logic: the old code used 'if category !== lastCategorySeenAcrossPages → push new group' which created duplicate groupedRows entries when the same H3 category (e.g. 'Album') repeated on successive paginated XHR pages instead of appending to the existing group. Fix: replaced the push-on-change logic with a find-or-create pattern (groupedRows.find(g => g.category === category) || push new) so all rows for a given category are merged into one group regardless of how many paginated pages contribute them. artist-releasegroups is unaffected because its categories never repeat across pages (the find() always returns null on first occurrence, so the behavior is identical to the old push). Updated the documentation table: all heading levels corrected to 'h3'; artist-releasegroups 'not repeating', releasegroup-releases and collections-releasegroups 'repeating on paginated pages'.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: collections-releases 'Release groups' and artist-releasegroups: extended the _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode fetch-loop h3-backwards-walk to also detect H2 category headers. On collections-release-group pages (and on releasegroup-releases pages) MusicBrainz repeats the H2 heading (e.g. 'Album', 'Single') at the top of each fetched XHR page, but no H3 siblings precede the table. The old walk only looked for H3, so all tables on these pages collapsed to category 'Other'. Fix: the backwards walk now records both H3 and H2 hits; H3 takes precedence when found (preserving artist-releasegroups behaviour unchanged), with H2 as the fallback. The heading text is cleaned of sort glyphs (▴▾▼▲⇅) and count spans before being used as the group key. Updated the page-type documentation table to record 'Collections-Releasegroups' as a new row.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: collections-releases 'Release groups': full support for native h3-grouped release-group collection pages. The entityFeatures 'Release groups' entry already carried tableMode:'multi', the correct integerColumns, tooltipColumns, and injectedColumns from v9.99.455. This version wires up the matching code paths: (1) New module-level predicate _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode() returns true for both artist-releasegroups and collections-releases when activeDefinition.tableMode === 'multi', avoiding repetition across call sites. (2) The fetch-loop h3-category scraping branch (native backwards-walk, no mb-toggle-h3 class restriction) now also fires for collections-releases Release-groups pages, populating groupedRows with the correct h3-derived category keys. (3) Both removeSanojjonasContainers() call sites extended to use the new predicate. (4) All three renderGroupedTable(…, isArtistMain) arguments updated from pageType === 'artist-releasegroups' to _isReleaseGroupsMultiMode(). The pre-fetch official-categories pass and post-render discography-view injection remain artist-releasegroups-only (collections pages don't have an Official/Non-official split). columnHeaderErasers: ['▴/▾'] continues to apply correctly because it is read from activeDefinition.columnHeaderErasers (spread from baseDef) which is unaffected by the entityFeatures tableMode override.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Post-render cleanup: MusicBrainz native div.filter bar (the 'Filter' button with img src containing 'filter-' and a.filter-button anchors) is now removed from the page after rendering, since the script's own filter UI replaces its function. Implemented as a querySelectorAll('div.filter') pass in finalCleanup, guarded by presence of a.filter-button or img[src*='filter-'] to avoid removing unrelated elements.

Global filter Cc/Rx/Ex label tooltips on single-table pages: removed the redundant '(ALL sub-tables)' suffix. Labels now read 'Case Sensitive Filtering', 'RegExp Filtering', and 'Exclude Matches' on single-table pages; multi-table pages retain the full '(ALL sub-tables)' qualifier via tableMode === 'multi' ternaries.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Sub-table filter input tooltip (createSubTableFilterContainer): appended ", use 'Ctrl+U' for unicode character map" to filterInput.title, consistent with the same addition made to the global and column filter inputs in v9.99.463.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Filter input tooltips: appended ", use 'Ctrl+U' for unicode character map" to both the global filter input title (filterInput.title) and the column filter input title (input.title) so users are reminded of the unicode character picker shortcut directly from the filter field tooltip.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1 (filter-bar button tooltips, single-table): unhighlightAllBtn, clearColumnFiltersBtn, and clearAllFiltersBtn tooltip titles still mentioned 'ALL sub-tables' / 'ALL sub-table filters' on single-table pages. Fix: replaced each static string with a tableMode === 'multi' ternary — single-table pages now read 'global filter and ALL column filters', 'ALL column-specific filters', and 'global filter and ALL column filters' respectively; multi-table wording is unchanged.

Bug 2 (CAA/EAA toggle button tooltip, single-table): per-table toggle button title said '… for this sub-section' on single-table pages. Fix: replaced all three occurrence sites in _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (global handler sync, per-table click handler, initial title set) with tableMode === 'multi' ternaries that emit 'sub-section' on multi-table pages and 'section' on single-table pages.

Bug 3 (createSubTableFilterContainer — caseLabel / rxLabel tooltip, multi-table): title strings used single-quote delimiters so '${categoryName}' was emitted literally instead of being interpolated. Fix: switched both caseLabel.title and rxLabel.title from single-quoted strings to backtick template literals.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Statistics panel Table Details: second column showed full h1 innerHTML including all injected button labels, loading text, etc. on tag-value (and other no-<bdi>) page types. Root cause: _h1Text fell back to h1.textContent when no <bdi> was present, but on tag pages the controlsContainer (#mb-show-all-controls-container) is appended directly to the <h1>, so textContent included everything. Fix: on the no-<bdi> path, clone the h1 and strip #mb-show-all-controls-container, .mb-row-count-stat, and .mb-toggle-icon before reading textContent, matching the same defensive clone-and-strip pattern used elsewhere in the script.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artTooltipCellText: multi-credit Artist cells (and any other cell with more than one anchor) were truncated to the first artist only in bigbox hover tooltips. Root cause: the 'Linked cell' branch unconditionally called clone.querySelector('a') and returned only that single anchor's text, discarding everything after it — including ' & The E Street Band' in a cell like 'Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band'. Fix: replaced querySelector('a') with querySelectorAll('a'); when exactly one anchor is found the behaviour is unchanged (single anchor text returned); when multiple anchors are present the branch is skipped and the full cleaned cell textContent is returned instead, preserving the complete artist credit string with all its separators and names.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tooltipColumns had no effect on tag-value and user-tag-value multi-table pages. Root cause: on these pages tooltipColumns lives in entityFeatures[entityType] (per group), not in activeDefinition.features. initCaaPics/initEaaPics run AFTER the renderGroupedTable group loop, at which point activeDefinition.features has been restored to the base (no tooltipColumns). The _artInitBigPics mouseenter handler read activeDefinition at hover time — long after restoration — so _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns always received features.tooltipColumns=undefined and fell back to the static tooltip path. Fix in three parts: (1) renderGroupedTable: inside the per-group thead setup block where _groupEntityFeatures is already merged into _mf, persist any per-group tooltipColumns and extractMainColumn onto table.dataset (mbEntityTooltipColumns / mbEntityExtractMainColumn) so they survive past the group loop. (2) _artInitBigPics: snapshot those dataset values into _tableTooltipCols / _tableExtractMain immediately at call time (closed over by value). (3) mouseenter handler: build _effPageDef by merging the snapshot over activeDefinition.features and pass it to _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns instead of the raw activeDefinition, giving the correct per-sub-table tooltipColumns at hover time without mutating any shared state.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Action buttons on artist-recordings, user-tags, user-tag-value, and user-tag-value-entity pages were missing the 🧮N superscript mnemonic that enables Ctrl-M shortcut access. Root cause: the three-branch label-building block in generateActionButtons only prepended the '🧮N ' prefix when a label started with 'Show all' or '🧮 '; all other labels (plain text like 'Tags upvoted', other-emoji labels like '⊚ All recordings') fell into the 'leave unchanged' branch (c). Fix: collapsed to two branches — labels already carrying 🧮 get the superscript inserted (stripping any existing emoji+space first); every other label, regardless of format, gets the '🧮N ' prefix prepended. The Ctrl-M button discovery filter (btn.textContent.includes('🧮')) already covered this case, so no changes were needed to the shortcut handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artCreateOrUpdateRetryButton: CAA/EAA per-table retry button tooltip now reads '…for this table (forces network reload)' on single-table pages and '…for this sub-table (forces network reload)' on multi-table pages, mirroring the same context-aware pattern applied to the Relationship retry button in v9.99.455.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug (single-table): mb-rel-retry-0 button displaced before mb-row-count-stat after sort/filter — same root cause as the multi-table mb-rel-retry-global displacement fixed in v9.99.455: the updateH2Count globalArtBtns re-anchor selector included '#mb-rel-retry-global' but not the per-table 'mb-rel-retry-0' (or any mb-rel-retry-N) button. Fix: replaced ':scope > #mb-rel-retry-global' with the broader ':scope > [id^="mb-rel-retry-"]' which covers both the global (multi-table) and per-table (single-table) variants in one selector; the :scope > direct-child guard prevents matching mb-rel-retry-1/-2/… inside h3 sub-headers in multi-table mode.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug #1 (multi-table): mb-rel-retry-global button displaced after sort/filter. Root cause: updateH2Count re-anchors art buttons after rebuilding the mb-row-count-stat span, but its querySelectorAll selector did not include #mb-rel-retry-global, leaving it stranded before the new span. Fix: added ':scope > #mb-rel-retry-global' to the globalArtBtns selector so it is collected in DOM order and correctly re-inserted after the last art-retry button.

Bug #2 (single-table): two Relationship retry buttons appeared — mb-rel-retry-0 (correct, after mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-0) and a spurious mb-rel-retry-global (after mb-row-count-stat). Root cause: _relCreateRetryButtons() unconditionally created mb-rel-retry-global on every page type. Fix: wrapped that creation in a tableMode === 'multi' guard so the global button is only created on multi-table pages.

Bug #2 follow-up: mb-rel-retry-0 tooltip changed from 'sub-table' to 'table' when running in single-table mode ('Retry loading Relationship icons for this table (network reload, clears IDB cache)'), since on a single-table page there is only one table. Multi-table mode retains the 'sub-table' wording.

sections.label: 🎨 UI
items: Country column unique-values dropdown: each entry now shows the country flag icon (scraped from the table cell) prepended between the occurrence-count badge and the country text label, matching the visual appearance of the rendered table data. Applies to all synthetic Country columns produced by splitCountryDate, splitLocation, and splitArea (i.e. columns named "Country", "Begin country", "End country").

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Add missing collection pageTypes and more tooltips.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: sa_unicode_char_picker_mappings: added 'Examples' column (display-only, shown in settings UI but not used by the picker); updated comment block and description accordingly.
SA_UNICODE_CHARS_DEFAULT: added 'examples' field to all entries with typical real-world usage examples for each Unicode glyph; updated JSDoc type annotation.
_loadUnicodeCharsMappings: updated seedRows to include examples column (index 3); updated live-load map to carry examples field from stored rows.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Enhancement
items: renderGroupedTable: fixed inline CAA/EAA thumbnails for tag-value/user-tag-value multi-table pages. Root cause: the single global addCAA value (e.g. 'Release group', the first found) was used by _artInitInlinePics for all tables; the Release table never matched because its column is named 'Release' not 'Release group'. Fix: after initCaaPics/initEaaPics, loop over dataArray groups that have addCAA/addEAA in their entityFeatures and call _artInitInlinePics once per group with the targetTable parameter and the per-group correct column name temporarily set in activeDefinition.features.

tag-value-entity pageType: Release groups/Releases/Recordings syntheticColumns 'Artists' → 'Artist' (singular). Added tooltipColumns for Events ['Name','---','Date','Tag count'], Release groups and Releases ['Name','Artist','---',['Tag count']].

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2: for tag-value-entity and user-tag-value-entity page types, the H2 text contains the full heading ('Artists tagged as «country»', 'Release groups vzell tagged as «gig»') which doesn't match the entityFeatures plural keys. Fixed by extracting the entity-type prefix using the same 'split on tagged' + 'strip username' logic as Structure C column-name derivation, giving 'Artists' and 'Release groups' respectively.

renderGroupedTable CAA/EAA for tag-value/user-tag-value: _artInitPics skips tables that have neither a dedicated CAA/EAA column nor features.addCAA/addEAA set globally. For multi-table tag pages, addCAA/addEAA live in per-group entityFeatures, not in the global features. Fixed by scanning all groupedRows entityFeatures before calling initCaaPics/initEaaPics, collecting any addCAA/addEAA values, temporarily merging them into activeDefinition.features, calling the CAA/EAA init functions, then deleting the temporary keys.

sections.label: ✨ Refactor
items: New resolveEntityFeaturesFromH3(category, def): resolves per-entity features for multi-table tag pages by matching the group's h3 category text (e.g. 'Events', 'Release groups') against def.entityFeatures keys. Stored on each groupedRows entry as group.entityFeatures at fetch time.

Four tag pageTypes refactored to entityFeatures syntax: user-tag-value-entity, user-tag-value, tag-value-entity, tag-value. Each now has an entityFeatures map keyed by plural entity-type name (matching h3/h2 DOM text) with per-entity columnExtractors, syntheticColumnExtractors, integerColumns, addCAA/addEAA. Common features (listToTable, removeSelector) remain in the base features object.

Per-group feature resolution: in the fetch loop's per-table reset block and in renderGroupedTable's per-group cleanupHeaders block, when group.entityFeatures is populated the active extractor/integerColumns lists are rebuilt from the merged (base + entity-specific) features. This enables per-entity CAA/EAA columns, correct column headers, and correct integerColumns alignment per entity type.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: entity-tags and user-tags pageTypes: first-column header in each sub-table is now singular. Extended the _toSingular(category) colName derivation to include all entity-tags pageTypes (artist-tags, releasegroup-tags, release-tags, recording-tags, work-tags, label-tags, series-tags, place-tags, area-tags, instrument-tags) and user-tags. The category text from the h3 backward-walk (e.g. 'Genres', 'Other tags') is now passed through _toSingular before being stored as group.colName, which renderGroupedTable uses to patch each group's first <th>. Result: 'Genres' → 'Genre', 'Other tags' → 'Other tag'.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Enhancement
items: applyListToTable() Structure C: for tag-value-entity and user-tag-value-entity page types, now applies _toSingular() to the extracted column name so the first column header is singular (e.g. 'Release groups tagged as…' → 'Release group', 'Artists tagged as…' → 'Artist'). Exception: 'Series' stays 'Series'.

user-tag-value-entity pageType: updated all 11 columnExtractors sourceColumn names from plural to singular (e.g. 'Areas' → 'Area', 'Release groups' → 'Release group') to match the Structure C singular output.
tag-value-entity pageType: same plural→singular update for all 11 columnExtractors sourceColumn names. Comments updated in both blocks.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: user-tag-value-entity pageType: added columnExtractors (11 entries with plural sourceColumn names matching the h2-derived Structure C header), syntheticColumnExtractors (dateParts on Date), and integerColumns (DD/MM/YYYY). Note: the missing comma after removeSelector in the user spec was corrected. Uses plural sourceColumn names (e.g. 'Release groups') matching the Structure C output for user-tag-value-entity pages (unlike user-tag-value which uses singular names from Structure D).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value / user-tag-value integerColumns alignment: added per-table integerColumns colIdx re-resolution inside the per-table extractor reset block. Root cause: the global _finalColNames block runs once per page against referenceTable[0] (the first/Areas table) where only Name_Comment is resolved; at that point 'Date' is absent so DD/MM/YYYY are never found and their colIdx stays -1. Fix: after the per-table extractor colIdx reset, a per-table _perTableColNames list is built from the current table's headers + resolved extractor synthetic columns + resolved syntheticColumnExtractor columns. activeIntegerColumns are then re-resolved against this list, giving correct colIdx for DD/MM/YYYY on the Events table.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: tag-value-entity pageType: added {sourceColumn:'Tag count',align:'R'} as first entry in integerColumns so the vote count column gets right-aligned integer styling.

user-tag-value pageType: added columnExtractors (same 11 entries as tag-value with singular sourceColumn names), syntheticColumnExtractors (dateParts on Date), and integerColumns (DD/MM/YYYY) — mirrors the tag-value configuration for user tag pages.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: primarySyntheticCellMap population: added 'if (entry.colIdx === -1) return;' guard in all three population sites. Root cause: empty <td> placeholders produced by unresolved extractors are truthy, so they were stored in the map under shared column names like 'Date'. This caused primarySyntheticCellMap.has('Date') to return true for non-Events groups (e.g. Areas, Artists), triggering dateParts to run and append 5 empty cells — producing a ghost trailing column (visible as extra cells beyond the column headers). Events group was unaffected because its 'Date' cell was real and the dateParts output cells all had headers. Fix: only resolved extractors (colIdx !== -1) contribute to the map, ensuring 'Date' is absent for non-Events groups and the guard correctly skips dateParts.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value multi-table: per-group cleanupHeaders() re-resolution. Saved rawTemplateHead (unmodified thead clone) before the global cleanupHeaders(templateHead) call. In renderGroupedTable, for tag-value/user-tag-value pageTypes, each group table now: (1) resets all activeColumnExtractors colIdx to -1; (2) resolves only the entry matching group.colName to colIdx=0; (3) clones rawTemplateHead and calls cleanupHeaders() on it — injecting only the synthetic headers appropriate for that group (e.g. Name+Date+Comment for Events, Name+Comment for Areas). This fixes missing Date/Artists column headers for multi-table tag-value pages.
