renderGroupedTable first-column patch: Series bug fixed by removing the '!== categoryName' guard (previous fix 9.99.441 left this as 'if (group.colName && group.colName !== categoryName)'; changed to 'if (group.colName)'). Was already patched in the previous commit but the re-resolution approach supersedes it — the cleanup now happens inside the per-group cleanupHeaders path.

integerColumns _finalColNames: fix confirmed correct — only resolved extractor synthetic columns are included, and syntheticColumnExtractors are guarded on source column presence. For tag-value-entity Events page: ['Events','Name','Tag count','Date','Comment','DD','MM','YYYY','Day','Month'] gives correct colIdx values for DD/MM/YYYY.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: renderGroupedTable per-group column-name patch: removed the '!== categoryName' guard so 'Series' (where colName === categoryName) also gets patched. Previously the guard was intended to skip the no-op case but it blocked the required patch for Series.

listToTable multi-table branch: for tag-value and user-tag-value pageTypes, activeColumnExtractors colIdx is now reset to -1 and re-resolved against the CURRENT table's header cells before each table's rows are processed. Previously colIdx was only reset once per page, so after processing the first table ('Area' extractor colIdx=0), processing subsequent tables would have stale colIdx values from earlier tables, causing multiple extractors to fire on every row and produce duplicate or wrong synthetic cells.

integerColumns _finalColNames computation: changed to only include synthetic column names from RESOLVED primary extractors (colIdx !== -1) and only include syntheticColumnExtractor columns when their sourceColumn is actually present in _finalColNames. Previously ALL extractors' syntheticColumns were pushed regardless of resolution, inflating the list with 30+ phantom column names that offset indexOf() lookups and caused 'DD'/'MM'/'YYYY' colIdx to resolve to wrong positions (or not at all).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value first-column name fix (revised): replaced the DOM-lookup approach from 9.99.439 which had two bugs: (1) chained .replace() corrupted 'Series' → 'Serie' causing lookup misses, (2) all groups fell back to the template's 'Area' header. New approach: (a) _toSingular() promoted to module-level (was local inside Structure D's forEach); (b) the fetch-loop listToTable branch now stores colName on each groupedRows entry as _toSingular(category) for tag-value/user-tag-value pageTypes; (c) renderGroupedTable patches the first <th> directly from group.colName — no DOM query, no fragile string manipulation at render time.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value multi-table: fixed every sub-table showing 'Area' as its first column name. Root cause: renderGroupedTable clones a single templateHead from the first table.tbl in the DOM (always the 'Areas' table when sorted alphabetically), so all groups inherit 'Area' as their first column header. Fix: (1) applyListToTable Structure D stores the per-group singular column name as dataset.mbOriginalColName on each converted <table>. (2) renderGroupedTable, after cloning templateHead for a new group table, looks up the source table whose mbOriginalColName matches the singularised categoryName and patches the first <th> text and dataset.colName accordingly.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: applyListToTable() Structure D: fixed column name — now uses the singular form of the h3 text rather than the raw h3 text. A local _toSingular() helper handles: 'Series' → 'Series' (unchanged); '... groups' → '... group'; '... events' → '... event'; all others → strip trailing 's'. So 'Release groups' → 'Release group', 'Recordings' → 'Recording', etc.

New ColumnDataExtractor functions Name_Comment, Name_Date_Comment, Name_Comment_Artists: mirror the tagCount_* variants but omit the Tag count column. Each delegates to _tagCountBase for Name and Comment extraction, discarding the count cell with destructuring. Used for tag-value pageType where the entity cells have no leading vote count.

tag-value pageType: added columnExtractors (singular sourceColumn names to match Structure D output), syntheticColumnExtractors (dateParts on Date from Events), and integerColumns (DD/MM/YYYY). The syntheticColumnExtractors dateParts guard (primarySyntheticCellMap.has) ensures date sub-columns only appear on /tag/.../event equivalent pages.
_synthColTooltip: added tooltip entries for Name_Comment, Name_Date_Comment, and Name_Comment_Artists.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value-entity pageType: added syntheticColumnExtractors:[{sourceColumn:'Date',extractor:'dateParts',syntheticColumns:['DD','MM','YYYY','Day','Month']}] and integerColumns:[{sourceColumn:'DD',align:'R'},{sourceColumn:'MM',align:'R'},{sourceColumn:'YYYY',align:'C'}]. These only activate on /tag/.../event pages because the 'Date' synthetic column only exists when the Events extractor (tagCount_Name_Date_Comment) is resolved.

syntheticColumnExtractor guard — cleanupHeaders(): synthetic extractor <th> headers are now skipped when their sourceColumn is absent from the set of columns produced by resolved primary extractors (colIdx !== -1). Computed once as _resolvedPrimaryCols = Set of all syntheticColumns from active primary extractors with colIdx !== -1. Prevents DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month headers appearing on non-Events tag pages where 'Date' was never extracted.

syntheticColumnExtractor guard — row processing (all 3 sites): synthetic extractor cell appending now checks primarySyntheticCellMap.has(entry.sourceColumn) before appending. Uses the per-row map rather than _resolvedPrimaryCols (which is scoped to cleanupHeaders). When 'Date' is absent from the map, dateParts cells are not appended, keeping row structure consistent.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New shared helper _tagCountBase(sourceCell): extracts the common Tag count / Name / Comment fields shared by all tagCount_* extractors. Handles optional country-flag span (<span class="flag flag-XX">) before the entity link — when found, clones flag+link together into tdName. Uses ':scope > span.comment' selector to avoid matching artist-comment spans nested inside <bdi>.
tagCount_Name_Comment: refactored to delegate to _tagCountBase, no behaviour change except the flag-span support added via the helper.

New tagCount_Name_Date_Comment extractor for Events: extracts Name, Tag count, Date ('(2019-07-05)' → '2019-07-05' by walking direct text nodes after the entity link), and Comment. Added to columnExtractors for 'Events'.

New tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists extractor for Releases/Release groups/Recordings: extracts Name, Tag count, Comment, and Artists (the last direct-child <bdi> of the <td>, which contains the 'by …' artist credit, cloned with full HTML including multi-artist 'and' separators and artist comment spans). Added to columnExtractors for 'Release groups', 'Releases', 'Recordings'.
_synthColTooltip: added case entries for tagCount_Name_Date_Comment (Name, Tag count, Date, Comment) and tagCount_Name_Comment_Artists (Name, Tag count, Comment, Artists).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: columnExtractors with colIdx===-1 (unresolved source column): fixed two structural bugs. (1) cleanupHeaders() now skips header <th> injection for extractors whose sourceColumn was not found in the table (colIdx === -1). Previously the deduplication guard prevented duplicate headers, so this was already correct, but the explicit guard makes the intent clear. (2) Row processing in all three extractedSyntheticCells.forEach call sites now guards on activeColumnExtractors[i].colIdx === -1 and skips cell appending for unresolved entries. Previously every unresolved extractor appended 3 empty <td> cells per row — for tag-value-entity with 11 extractors and only 1 matching, this produced 30 empty extra cells per row against a 3-column synthetic header, breaking the table structure.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New ColumnDataExtractor 'tagCount_Name_Comment': splits the combined entity cell on tag-value-entity pages ('26 - Johnny Cash (country music legend)') into three synthetic columns — Name (the <bdi> link text, with the anchor preserved), Tag count (the leading integer), and Comment (the <bdi> inside span.comment). Returns [tdName, tdCount, tdComment] as required by the ColumnDataExtractor contract.

tag-value-entity pageType: added columnExtractors covering all eleven entity types (Areas, Artists, Events, Instruments, Labels, Places, Release groups, Releases, Recordings, Series, Works), each using tagCount_Name_Comment and producing syntheticColumns ['Name', 'Tag count', 'Comment']. The sourceColumn matches the h2-derived table header produced by Structure C.
_synthColTooltip: added case 'tagCount_Name_Comment' with descriptions for all three synthetic column names.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Enhancement
items: applyListToTable() Structure C column-name derivation for tag-value-entity: changed from 'first word only' to 'everything before tagged', e.g. 'Release groups tagged as «gig»' → 'Release groups' (previously gave 'Release' only).

applyListToTable() Structure C column-name derivation for user-tag-value-entity: changed from 'first word only' to 'everything before tagged minus the trailing username', e.g. 'Release groups vzell tagged as «gig»' → 'Release groups' (previously gave 'Release' only). The username is identified as the last whitespace-separated word in the pre-tagged segment.
sa_ui_artist_role_main_performer_color default: changed from dark green #006400 to bright green #57ff5a.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New configuration section '🎨 ARTIST ROLE COLOURS' added before the CAA/EAA section, with two color_picker settings: sa_ui_artist_role_main_performer_color (default dark green #006400) and sa_ui_artist_role_guest_performer_color (default orange #e07000).

_artTooltipArtistRoles(): rewritten to build the tooltip as DOM nodes instead of plain text. Artist names are plain text; role labels (e.g. '(main performer)', '(guest)') are wrapped in a <span> with configurable colour and font-weight:bold. Matching: text containing 'main performer' → main colour; text containing 'guest' → guest colour; all other roles → plain text node. The list items are comma-separated inline rather than joined as a single string.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyStickyColumn: fixed ghosting artefact when the sticky column is not the first column (e.g. 'Name' after 'Year'/'Date'/'#'). Root cause: _leftOffset() computed the total width of all preceding columns and set left:Npx on the sticky cell. With CSS position:sticky, left:Npx means the element only snaps when it would go past Npx from the viewport left — creating a transparent gap in 0..Npx through which other columns visibly scroll (the ghost). Fix: _leftOffset() now always returns 0 so the sticky column immediately snaps to the left viewport edge (left:0px) regardless of how many non-sticky columns precede it. A non-zero offset is only meaningful when multiple preceding columns are also sticky (stacking), which this script does not implement. Also removed the MutationObserver on colgroup that was used to recompute _leftOffset() after auto-resize — it is no longer needed since the offset is always 0.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: place-performances-filtered and place-performances pageTypes: added integerColumns:[{sourceColumn:'Length',align:':'}] to both features blocks so the Length column is formatted/aligned as a time value.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyListToTable() Structure C column-name derivation: for user-tag-value-entity pages the h2 text is 'Events vzell tagged as «gig»', so splitting on '\s+tagged' gave 'Events vzell' instead of 'Events'. Fixed by taking the first whitespace-delimited word of the pre-tagged part (_beforeTagged.split(/\s+/)[0]), which correctly extracts 'Events'. For tag-value-entity ('Labels tagged as «country»') the behaviour is unchanged since the first word is the only word before 'tagged'.
user-tag-value-entity pageType comment: updated to document the correct extraction example 'Events vzell tagged as «gig»' → 'Events'.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: New 'user-tag-value-entity' pageType: matches /user/<username>/tag/<tag>/<entity> paths (e.g. /user/vzell/tag/gig/event). Uses listToTable:[''] (Structure C), tableMode 'single', two buttons Entities upvoted/downvoted with show_downvoted params, removeSelector:'form[style*="margin-top"]'. Placed before 'user-tag-value' since its regex /user/[^/]+/tag/[^/]+/.+ is narrower.

applyListToTable() Structure C: added _isUserTagValueEntity flag (matches /user/[^/]+/tag/[^/]+/.+) to the guard condition and to the column-name derivation (uses first word before 'tagged', e.g. 'Events tagged as ...' -> 'Events').

user-tags pageType: fixed removeSelector from 'form:has(select[name="show_downvoted"])' to 'form[style*="margin-top"]' — the attribute selector is more reliable across browsers and Tampermonkey contexts. The same selector is used for user-tag-value-entity.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: search finalCleanup: extended the searchform relocation to also move the injected 'Searchform' h2 alongside the div.searchform. Both elements are now inserted immediately after div#mb-status-displays-wrapper in document order: h2 first (via insertAdjacentElement('afterend') on the wrapper), then div.searchform immediately after the h2. Resulting order: div#mb-status-displays-wrapper → h2 Searchform → div.searchform.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ♻️ Refactor
items: search pageType: reverted the moveBeforeTableH2 / moveAfterH2 approach entirely. Removed features.moveBeforeTableH2 from the search pageType, removed the applyMoveBeforeTableH2() function, and removed its call site in the pre-processing block.

search pageType: div.searchform is now relocated in finalCleanup() via insertAdjacentElement('afterend') on div#mb-status-displays-wrapper, placing it immediately below the script's status/controls bar in the final rendered view. features.insertPrependH2:'Searchform' is retained so the section heading is still injected.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
search pageType: replaced features.moveAfterH2 with features.moveBeforeTableH2:'div.searchform'. The previous moveAfterH2 placed the search form immediately after the 'Searchform' h2 (making it a sibling of that h2), but the intent is to position it immediately before the h2 that precedes the actual results table — i.e. between the 'Searchform' h2 and the 'Search results' h2. Resulting DOM order:

Searchform

→
→

Search results

→ .
Replaced applyMoveAfterH2() with applyMoveBeforeTableH2(): finds the first table.tbl via _findPrecedingH2(), then inserts the target element immediately before that h2 via parentNode.insertBefore().

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: search pageType: added features.insertPrependH2:'Searchform' and features.moveAfterH2:{h2Text:'Searchform',selector:'div.searchform'}. On click, a 'Searchform' <h2> is inserted before the existing search-results <h2>, then <div class="searchform"> is relocated to immediately follow it — giving the search form its own collapsible section in the rendered view.
New applyInsertPrependH2(def): inserts a new <h2> with the text from features.insertPrependH2 immediately before the first existing <h2> in div#content (fallback: prepend to div#page / body).

New applyMoveAfterH2(def): finds the <h2> whose text matches features.moveAfterH2.h2Text and relocates the element matching features.moveAfterH2.selector to immediately after it via insertAdjacentElement('afterend').
Both functions are called in startFetchingProcess() in the pre-processing block, after the existing renameH2ToH3/insertH2 calls.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Enhancement
items: Re-added 'editor-subscribers' pageType (accidentally dropped when consolidating subscription types in 9.99.421): matches /subscribers paths, tableMode 'single', listToTable:[''] (Structure C), button 'Show all Editor Subscribers for User'. Placed immediately after 'user-subscriptions'.

Added showAllTags:true and removeSelector:'div.all-tags' to all nine entity-tags pageTypes: artist-tags, releasegroup-tags, release-tags, recording-tags, work-tags, label-tags, series-tags, place-tags, instrument-tags. These were already present on area-tags but missing from the other entity tag page types. The two features ensure hidden zero-score/downvoted tags are exposed before conversion and the all-tags container is removed after rendering.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: virtualPath h2 label patch: when a button carries virtualPath (e.g. 'user-subscriptions'), the live DOM h2 still reflects the page the browser is on (e.g. 'Artist subscriptions') even though the fetched data comes from a different path. Inserted a pre-render patch block that strips the emoji+superscript prefix from buttonConfig.label, finds the h2 preceding the first table via _findPrecedingH2(), and updates its text node to the clean label (e.g. 'Label subscriptions') before updateH2Count() reads it. Preserves existing child elements (toggle icon, count stat span).

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: Consolidated five separate subscription pageTypes (artist-, collection-, label-, series-, editor-subscriptions) into a single 'user-subscriptions' pageType with five per-button virtualPath values ('/artist', '/collection', '/label', '/series', '/editor').

New virtualPath button parameter: when a button carries virtualPath, the last URL path segment is replaced before fetching. Example: on /user/vzell/subscriptions/artist, clicking '🧮 Label subscriptions' (virtualPath='/label') fetches /user/vzell/subscriptions/label. Implemented in three places: (1) _effectivePath computation for maxPage determination (new else-if branch calls fetchMaxPageGeneric on the effective path); (2) fetchUrl.pathname replacement inside the fetch loop; (3) the 'use existing document' optimisation is disabled for virtualPath buttons (always fetches from network since a different path is targeted).

user-subscriptions finalCleanup: removes the subscription-type navigation

(the [ Artist subscriptions | Collection subscriptions | … ] bar) since the per-button virtualPath switching replaces its function.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: editor-subscribers finalCleanup: (1) the trailing 'Plus N other anonymous users' row is now removed from the rendered table after parsing N; (2) table.dataset.mbTotalRows and the h2 .mb-row-count-stat span are patched to reflect the reduced row count so the header shows the correct number; (3) the subscriber-count paragraph is rebuilt as DOM nodes with elements around the real and anonymous counts so they render in bold: 'There are currently R real and N anonymous users subscribed to edits that you make:' (previously the numbers were plain text and the anon row was left in the table).

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items:
editor-subscribers: in finalCleanup(), when pageType is 'editor-subscribers', the last rendered table row is inspected for the pattern 'Plus N other anonymous users'. When found, N is parsed, and the preceding

element matching 'There are currently M users subscribed…' is rewritten to 'There are currently M-N real and N anonymous users subscribed…'. This gives editors an accurate count of real (named) subscribers vs. anonymous ones.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: @match *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/subscribers added.

New 'editor-subscribers' pageType: matches /subscribers paths, tableMode 'single', button 'Show all Editor Subscribers for User', listToTable:[''] (Structure C). Placed before 'editor-subscriptions' to avoid match overlap.
applyListToTable() Structure C: extended guard to also activate for /subscribers paths (_isSubscribers flag). JSDoc updated with user-subscribers variant before/after example.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyListToTable() sectionId==='' dispatch order: Structure D's old guard '_currentPath.includes("/tag/")' matched ALL tag paths including two-segment /tag/<v>/<entity> paths, so it ran first for tag-value-entity pages, found no <h3> elements, and returned — Structure C was never reached and applyListToTable produced no tables. Fixed by pre-computing three mutually-exclusive flags: _isTagValueEntity (/tag/[^/]+/.+, two segments), _isTagValue (!_isTagValueEntity && /tag//, one segment only), _isAreaUsers. Structure C is now checked first (isAreaUsers || isTagValueEntity), Structure D second (isTagValue). A fallback skip-log handles any remaining unrecognised paths.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New 'tag-value-entity' pageType: matches /tag/<value>/<entity> paths (e.g. /tag/country/labels). Must be listed before 'tag-value' since its regex is narrower. Uses listToTable:[''] (Structure C), tableMode 'single', button 'Show all Entities tagged'.

applyListToTable() Structure C: extended to also activate for /tag/<value>/<entity> paths (tag-value-entity pageType), not just /area/<mbid>/users. The guard now checks _isAreaUsers OR _isTagValueEntity. Column-name derivation is path-dependent: for area-users the full h2 text is used (e.g. 'Users'); for tag-value-entity the first word before 'tagged' in the h2 text is extracted (e.g. 'Labels tagged as "country"' → 'Labels'). The li→td mapping now clones full li content (all child nodes) for tag-value-entity rows that contain ranking numbers and flag spans alongside links. JSDoc updated to document both variants with before/after examples.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value / user-tag-value 'See all' button tooltip: updated text to append '(MusicBrainz Tag-Values pages have currently a limit of 10 rows rendered at most per entity type section)'.

tag-value / user-tag-value row count bug: after removing the trailing 'See all' row, table.dataset.mbTotalRows is now patched to tbody.rows.length (the real post-removal count) and the h3 .mb-row-count-stat span is updated to '(N)' with its stale data-mbtt tooltip attribute cleared. Previously mbTotalRows was set to group.rows.length (which included the 'See all' row) before the row was removed, causing updateSubTableRowCount() to display '(10 of 11)' instead of '(10)'.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement / 🐛 Fix
items: tag-value pageType: removed removeSelector (global tag pages have no vote/sort form to remove).

renderGroupedTable(): for pageTypes 'tag-value' and 'user-tag-value', after each group's rows are appended to tbody, the last row is inspected for a trailing 'See all N <entity-type>s' cell containing <em><a href="/tag/...">.  When found: (1) a 'Show all N rows' button (class mb-show-all-subtable-btn, using the sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg and sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg_clicked colour settings) is inserted in the h3 after the filter-container span (or filter-toggle-icon as fallback); clicking opens the full listing in a new tab and sets the button background to the clicked colour; (2) the trailing 'See all' row is removed from the table. The button title reads 'Click to show all N <entity-type> in a new browser tab for this sub-section'. The implementation reuses the existing mb-show-all-subtable-btn pattern used by artist-relationships overflow tables.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: @match directives added: *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/tag/* (user tag value pages) and *://*.musicbrainz.org/tag/* (global tag pages).

New 'user-tag-value' pageType: matches /user/*/tag/* paths, tableMode 'multi', two buttons Entities upvoted/downvoted with show_downvoted params, listToTable:[''] (Structure D), removeSelector for vote/sort form. Must be listed before 'tag-value' due to narrower match.
New 'tag-value' pageType: matches /tag/* paths, tableMode 'multi', single button 'Show all Entities tagged', listToTable:[''] (Structure D), removeSelector for vote/sort form.

applyListToTable() Structure D (new): triggered when sectionId==='' AND window.location.pathname contains /tag/. Scans div#content for every <h3>, walks up to 5 next siblings to find a <ul>, builds a single-column table labelled with the h3 text (e.g. 'Areas', 'Artists'). Each <li>'s full inner content (including span.flag and nested <a>) is cloned into a single <td>. The <ul> is replaced by the bare <table>.

applyListToTable() Structure C restricted: now only activates when sectionId==='' AND path matches /area/<mbid>/users. All other sectionId==='' cases are routed to Structure D (tag pages) or log a skip message. All internal DOM creation calls updated from hardcoded document.createElement to docContext.createElement for correctness on fetched DOMParser documents.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: @match *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/tags*: added trailing wildcard so the script activates on /user/*/tags?show_downvoted=1 and other query-string variants.

Renamed pageType 'tags' to 'user-tags'. Updated match to /\/user\/[^/]+\/tags/ (explicit user-path regex instead of generic path.includes). Replaced single 'Show all Tags for User' button with two filter buttons: 'Upvoted' (params: {show_downvoted:'0'}) and 'Downvoted' (params: {show_downvoted:'1'}). Removed integerColumns (Tag count alignment). Added removeSelector: 'form:has(select[name="show_downvoted"])' so the vote/sort form is removed from the final rendered page by finalCleanup() after rendering is complete.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: features.removeSelector execution timing: moved from just-before-rendering (post-fetch) to finalCleanup() (post-render). Root cause: renderGroupedTable() clones the <thead> from the first live table.tbl in the DOM to build its output template. When removeSelector removed div.all-tags before renderGroupedTable ran, the converted tables were gone, causing 'No template table head found' and a TypeError in compareDocumentPosition. finalCleanup() runs after renderGroupedTable/renderFinalTable have built all output tables, so the original source tables are no longer needed at that point and the container can safely be removed.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: features.removeSelector execution timing: moved from performClutterCleanup (which runs before parseDocumentForTables in the fetch loop) to immediately before the rendering block (after all rows have been collected into allRows / groupedRows). Root cause: on area-tags the converted <table class="tbl"> elements live inside <div class="all-tags">, so removing that div in performClutterCleanup also removed the tables before parseDocumentForTables could find them, resulting in 'Found 0 total tables' and 0 rows rendered. At the new call site the DOM tables have already been read and are no longer needed, making the removal safe.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Syntax error introduced in 9.99.408: the str_replace that inserted the applyShowAllTags function body accidentally consumed the opening '/**' of the immediately following buildActiveIntegerColumns JSDoc comment, leaving the comment body as bare code ('* activeDefinition object.' etc.) which the JavaScript parser rejected as 'Unexpected token *'. Restored the missing '/**' opening line.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: area-tags pageType: added features.showAllTags: true and features.removeSelector: 'div.all-tags'.

New async applyShowAllTags(def): when features.showAllTags is true, finds the 'Show all tags.' anchor inside div.all-tags (by href='#' and text content) and programmatically clicks it so that MusicBrainz reveals hidden zero-score and downvoted <li> elements in the sibling tag/genre <ul> lists. Awaits Promise.resolve() after the click to yield to the microtask queue so the synchronous MusicBrainz DOM mutation completes before the caller proceeds. Called as the very first step in the pre-processing block (before renameH2ToH3, insertH2, and listToTable) so the full tag list is visible before any DOM restructuring or <ul>→<table> conversion runs.

New features.removeSelector support in performClutterCleanup(): when the active page definition carries a CSS selector string in features.removeSelector, the matching element is removed from the live DOM during the clutter-cleanup pass. Used by area-tags to remove the <div class="all-tags"> container (which holds the 'Show all tags.' link, info paragraphs, and the tag-submission form) after the <ul> lists have already been converted to tables and the form is no longer needed.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Generic else branch row guard: changed 'node.cells.length > 1' to also accept rows with exactly 1 cell when that cell's colSpan is <= 1. The old strict '> 1' guard silently skipped all 100 rows per page for area-users because the single-column Users table (produced by applyListToTable Structure C) has exactly 1 cell per row. The new condition is: (cells.length > 1) OR (cells.length === 1 AND cells[0].colSpan <= 1). A single cell with colSpan > 1 still indicates a section separator or 'no results' spanning row and continues to be skipped.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: @include directive: added 'users' to the entity sub-page path alternation so the script activates on /area/<mbid>/users (and any other entity type that gains a /users sub-page in future).

applyListToTable(def, docContext=document): added optional docContext parameter (defaults to the live document). All internal DOM queries (getElementById, querySelector, querySelectorAll) now use docContext instead of the hardcoded global document. This allows the function to convert <ul> lists in remote DOMParser documents fetched during the pagination loop.

Fetch loop: added a call to applyListToTable(activeDefinition, doc) immediately before parseDocumentForTables for fetched pages (doc !== document) when features.listToTable is set. This fixes the 'Found 0 total tables' error on pages 2+ for area-users, where the raw fetched HTML still contains <ul> lists rather than <table class="tbl"> elements.

listToTable/groupByH3 branch condition: added tableMode === 'multi' guard so single-table pageTypes that use listToTable (e.g. 'area-users', tableMode:'single') fall through to the generic else branch and deposit rows in allRows rather than groupedRows. Previously area-users went through the h3-backward-walk branch, assigned category 'Other' (no h3 precedes the table), and rendered 0 rows when the stop button was pressed.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New 'area-users' pageType: matches /area/<mbid>/users, tableMode 'single', button 'Show all Users for Area'. Uses listToTable: [''] (empty-string sectionId) to trigger Structure C in applyListToTable.

applyListToTable(): added Structure C, triggered when a sectionId entry is '' (empty string). Scans div#content (falling back to body) for every <h2>, walks its next element siblings up to 5 steps looking for a <ul>. When found, builds a single-column <table class="tbl"> whose column header is the h2 text content (e.g. 'Users'). Each <li>'s <a> link (including nested img and bdi) is cloned into the single <td> per row. The <ul> is replaced by the bare <table> in the parent DOM. All h2+ul pairs in the content area are processed in one pass; the forEach callback returns early after all pairs are handled. JSDoc updated to document Structure C alongside A and B.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Reverted syntheticColumnExtractors (dateParts on 'Release events') from label-relationships-filtered and label-relationships — added prematurely in 9.99.403.
_rePopulateCell(): changed release-event country-code prefix from bracketed '(CA) ' to bare 'CA  ' (no brackets, two spaces between code and date), e.g. 'CA  1988-11'.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: _rePopulateCell(): release-event list items now render as '(XX) YYYY-MM-DD' instead of just 'YYYY-MM-DD'. When a country ISO-3166-1 code is available the 2-char code is prepended in brackets (e.g. '(CA) 1988-11'). This makes the country visible as text alongside the flag icon. Rows without a country area or without ISO codes continue to render the date only, unchanged.

label-relationships-filtered and label-relationships: added syntheticColumnExtractors with dateParts sourcing from the 'Release events' injected column, so the Release events cell content (country+date tuples) can feed into the DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month synthetic columns for sorting and filtering by date.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: area-events, place-events, artist-events, collections-releases Events, series-releases Events: moved dateParts from syntheticColumnExtractors into columnExtractors (sourceColumn: 'Date'), so it now operates directly on the native 'Date' column in the original DOM table rather than on a secondary synthetic cell. Removed the now-redundant syntheticColumnExtractors arrays from these pageTypes.

label-relationships-filtered and label-relationships: added dateParts columnExtractor (sourceColumn: 'Date') so the native Date column on label-relationship pages gains DD/MM/YYYY/Day/Month synthetic columns.
work-recordings-filtered: added dateParts columnExtractor (sourceColumn: 'Date'); kept existing eventParts syntheticColumnExtractor for Comment. work-recordings: added dateParts columnExtractor.

New ColumnDataExtractor.dateParts(sourceCell): primary-column variant of the identically-named SyntheticColumnDataExtractor function. Operates on a native table <td> rather than a secondary synthetic cell. Date range detection: when the text after the first ISO date token starts with '–' or '-', the function logs the range to the debug console and returns five empty synthetic cells instead of attempting to split — a date range cannot be represented by a single scalar date-part tuple. Full JSDoc documents the primary vs. synthetic distinction. SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.dateParts JSDoc updated to cross-reference the new ColumnDataExtractor counterpart.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancement
items: artist-recordings: narrowed match from path.includes('/recordings') to /\/artist\/[a-f0-9-]{36}\/recordings$/ and replaced the single generic button with three filter buttons: '⊚ All recordings' (params: {all:'1'}), '♫ Standalone' (params: {standalone:'1'}), '🎬 Video' (params: {video:'1'}).

collections-releases 'Events' feature set: added EAA and primaryAlias extractors, expanded collapsableColumns to include Location/EAA/Place/Area/Country (was only Artists), reordered extractors to cancelledEvent → caa → splitLocation → primaryAlias.
area-events: added cancelledEvent and primaryAlias columnExtractors, added syntheticColumnExtractors with dateParts, added tooltipColumns.
place-events: added syntheticColumnExtractors with dateParts.

series-releases 'Events' feature set: added EAA and primaryAlias extractors, expanded collapsableColumns to Location/EAA/Place/Area/Country, reordered extractors to cancelledEvent → caa → splitLocation → primaryAlias.
artist-events: added syntheticColumnExtractors with dateParts.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Subscriptions pageType split into five specific entries: 'artist-subscriptions', 'collection-subscriptions', 'label-subscriptions', 'series-subscriptions', 'editor-subscriptions'. Each matches the corresponding /subscriptions/<type> path so button labels are specific (e.g. 'Show all Artist Subscriptions for User'). The editor-subscriptions entry has no extractMainColumn/stickyColumn features since editor tables have a different structure. The entries are ordered before the tags block and all carry narrower path matches than the previous single generic /subscriptions entry.

Nine new entity tags pageTypes added after 'artist-tags', each matching /entity/<mbid>/tags with the renameH2ToH3 + insertH2 + listToTable feature set: 'releasegroup-tags' (Release-group), 'release-tags' (Release), 'recording-tags' (Recording), 'work-tags' (Work), 'label-tags' (Label), 'series-tags' (Series), 'place-tags' (Place), 'area-tags' (Area), 'instrument-tags' (Instrument). All use tableMode 'multi' and follow the same pre-processing pipeline as 'artist-tags'.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
Root cause identified via uploaded DOM: on collection pages the entity-type

(e.g. 'Release groups') is a direct sibling of the
that wraps the table, NOT a sibling of the table itself. The flat previousElementSibling walk in both _getH2EntityType() and the labelFromH2 button code was scoped to the table's own sibling list (h3, nav — all inside the form), so it never escaped the form boundary. It fell through to document.querySelector('h2') which returned the first

Description

inside
— hence the button read 'Show all Description for Collections'.

New _findPrecedingH2(startNode, maxAncestorSteps=5, maxSiblingSteps=10) helper: walks previous siblings at the start node's DOM level; if no H2 is found, climbs to the parent element and repeats, up to maxAncestorSteps levels. Stops at document.body. This correctly handles collection pages (table nested inside form, H2 is a sibling of the form) as well as all existing page types where table and H2 are direct siblings.

Both _getH2EntityType() and the labelFromH2 button-label substitution code now use _findPrecedingH2() instead of the flat sibling walk. The 'Description' false-positive is eliminated: the ancestor walk finds 'Release groups' (sibling of the form) before ever reaching the description div.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1 — entity feature resolution for collections-releases: renamed _seriesH2EntityType() to _getH2EntityType() and resolveSeriesEntityFeatures() to resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2() so both functions are clearly generic and not series-specific. Both old names are preserved as backward-compatibility aliases (const resolveSeriesEntityFeatures = resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2). Updated startFetchingProcess() to call resolveEntityFeaturesFromH2() and updated its comment to list both 'series-releases' and 'collections-releases' as supported pageTypes. The underlying H2-detection and entityFeatures-lookup logic was already generic; the rename ensures no ambiguity about scope.

Bug 2 — collections multi-table rendering (all 3 h3-groups collapsed to 1 'Unknown' table): added features.groupByH3: true to the 'collections' pageType. Extended the fetch-loop branch condition from 'else if (features.listToTable)' to 'else if (features.listToTable || features.groupByH3)' so the h3-backward-walk multi-table path also activates for pages with native table.tbl elements already under <h3> headers (no list conversion needed). The 'collections' user-collections page has multiple <h3>-headed table.tbl siblings ('Own collections', 'Subscribed collections', etc.) that were previously all merged into a single 'Unknown' group by the generic else-branch.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: @match *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/collections added so the script activates on user collection-list pages.
@include extended: added 'collection' to the entity-type alternation so the script activates on individual collection URLs like /collection/<mbid>.
New 'collections' pageType: matches /collections paths (user collection lists), tableMode 'multi', button 'Show all Collections for User'.

New 'collections-releases' pageType: matches /collection/<mbid> paths, tableMode 'single', button 'Show all Releases for Collections' (with labelFromH2:true for dynamic entity-type substitution). Carries columnHeaderErasers:['▴/▾'] and an entityFeatures map with feature sets for Releases, Events, Release groups, Recordings, and Works — mirroring the series-releases pattern so the correct extractors, syntheticColumnExtractors, injectedColumns, integerColumns, collapsableColumns, tooltip and CAA/EAA features are activated per entity type.

New buildActiveColumnHeaderErasers(def): reads the top-level columnHeaderErasers array from the page definition (not inside features) and returns a copy. Token list is built in startFetchingProcess() alongside the other active-* lists.

New applyColumnHeaderErasers(referenceTable, eraserTokens): when the '▴/▾' token is present, walks every <th> in the referenceTable <thead> and replaces its content with plain text extracted from the <a> link(s) inside it. Single-link <th>: uses the first text node inside the <a>. Multi-link <th> (e.g. Country/Date): walks all child nodes of the <th>, collecting the first text node from each <a> and any literal TEXT_NODE children between them, then concatenates without spaces. Headers with no <a> children are left untouched. applyColumnHeaderErasers() is called immediately before the referenceTable.querySelectorAll('thead th') scanning loop so that headerNames[] stores the cleaned names and all feature lookups (extractors, erasers, integerColumns, etc.) match correctly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyListToTable(): added support for Structure B (bare-ul), used by entity tag pages (/artist/<mbid>/tags and similar). Previously the function only handled Structure A (div-wrapped), where a <div id="genres"> wrapper contains the <ul>; on entity pages there is no such wrapper — the <ul class="genre-list"> is a direct DOM sibling of the <h3>. The function now auto-detects the structure per sectionId: if getElementById(sectionId) succeeds, it uses the div-wrapped path (replacement target = div); otherwise it falls back to querySelector('ul.<singular>-list') and uses the bare-ul path (replacement target = ul itself). The debug log now reports the detected structure ('div-wrapped' or 'bare-ul').

applyListToTable(): column-name derivation changed from stripping the trailing 's' of the sectionId string to extracting the type substring before the '-list' suffix in the <ul> class attribute (e.g. class="genre-list" → "genre" → "Genre", class="tag-list" → "tag" → "Tag"). This is more robust and works correctly for both DOM structures since the <ul> class is always available.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Pre-processing call order corrected in startFetchingProcess(): applyRenameH2ToH3 and applyInsertH2 now run BEFORE applyListToTable. Previously listToTable ran first; on entity tag pages (/artist/<mbid>/tags) the <div id="genres"> containers are only reachable after the heading DOM has been restructured by renameH2ToH3/insertH2, so running listToTable first found 0 divs, produced 0 tables, and caused the entire pipeline to abort with 'Found 0 total tables'. New order: (1) applyRenameH2ToH3, (2) applyInsertH2, (3) applyListToTable — the internal renameH2ToH3-before-insertH2 constraint is preserved.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: pageDefinitions order: moved 'artist-tags' before the generic 'tags' entry so the narrower regex match (/artist/<mbid>/tags) is tested first and is not shadowed by the broader path.includes('/tags') match.

artist-tags pageType: added missing features.listToTable: ['genres','tags'] so the <ul>-to-table pre-processing step (applyListToTable) also runs for entity tag sub-pages, converting the native genre/tag lists into <table class="tbl"> elements before the fetch pipeline processes them.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: @include directive extended: added 'tags' to the entity sub-page path alternation so the script activates on URLs like /artist/<mbid>/tags, /release/<mbid>/tags, etc.

New 'artist-tags' pageType: matches /artist/<mbid>/tags, tableMode 'multi', button 'Show all Tags for Artist'. Uses two new DOM pre-processing features: renameH2ToH3 (demotes all page <h2>s to <h3> so they become sub-table labels) and insertH2 with value 'Artist tags' (injects a synthetic <h2> anchor after <div class="tabs"> for the collapsible-header infrastructure to attach to).

New applyRenameH2ToH3(def) function: when features.renameH2ToH3 is true, replaces every <h2> in the live DOM with an <h3> preserving all attributes and child nodes. This is necessary for entity tags pages where the MusicBrainz server emits <h2> section headings ('Genres', 'Other tags') that must become <h3> sub-table labels for the multi-table rendering pipeline.

New applyInsertH2(def) function: when features.insertH2 is a non-empty string, creates a new <h2> with that text and inserts it immediately after the last <div class="tabs"> container found in the page (falls back to prepending into div#page). This synthetic <h2> serves as the anchor node for updateH2Count(), the filter container, and the collapsible-header toggle machinery.

Both functions are called in startFetchingProcess() after stopOtherScripts() and after the listToTable pre-processing block, in the required order: applyRenameH2ToH3 first (so existing <h2>s are demoted before the new one is inserted), then applyInsertH2 (so the injected <h2> is not immediately demoted).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Fixed multi-table rendering for listToTable pages (e.g. /user/*/tags): the generic else-branch in the page-fetch loop used doc.querySelector('table.tbl tbody') which always picked only the FIRST converted table (5 genre rows), silently ignoring the second table (107 tag rows). Additionally, the group name defaulted to the literal string 'Unknown' because the else-branch derives group names from in-table <subh> rows — which do not exist in tables converted from <ul> lists by applyListToTable(). Added a dedicated 'else if (activeDefinition.features?.listToTable)' branch that (1) iterates ALL tables in tablesToProcess, (2) walks backwards through DOM siblings up to 5 steps to find the preceding <h3> and uses its text as the group/category name (falling back to 'Other'), and (3) runs the full shared row-processing pipeline (column erasers, render-multi-row cells, column extractors, MB-Name/Comment extraction, synthetic-column extractors, excluded-column deletion, injected-column placeholders, integer-column styling) so all existing features remain available for future listToTable page types.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
applyListToTable(): changed DOM replacement from _div.replaceChild(_table, _ul) (which left the
wrapper in place and replaced only the
    inside it) to _div.parentNode.replaceChild(_table, _div) (which replaces the entire
    wrapper with the bare in the parent DOM). The resulting structure is now

    Genres

    …
    with no enclosing div. JSDoc Before/After example updated accordingly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: tags pageType definition: corrected tableMode from 'single' to 'multi' so each converted section (genres, tags) renders as a separate labelled sub-table consistent with the h3-headed multi-table layout on the /user/*/tags page. Replaced extractMainColumn: 'Tags' with integerColumns: [{sourceColumn: 'Tag count', align: 'R'}] so the tag-count column is right-aligned as a numeric column.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Added support for MusicBrainz user tags pages (e.g. /user/vzell/tags): new '@match *://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/tags' directive added to the userscript header so Tampermonkey/Violentmonkey activates the script on these URLs.

New 'tags' pageType definition added to pageDefinitions with features.listToTable: ['genres', 'tags'], tableMode: 'single', and extractMainColumn: 'Tags'. The listToTable feature array names the <div id="..."> sections on the page that should be converted from <ul>-based lists to tables before the standard pipeline runs.

New applyListToTable(def) function: converts <ul class="genre-list"> / <ul class="tag-list"> structures inside named <div id="genres"> / <div id="tags"> containers into proper <table class="tbl"> elements. Column naming derives the first column header by stripping the trailing 's' from the div id and capitalising ('genres' → 'Genre', 'tags' → 'Tag'); the second column is always 'Tag count'. Each <li> becomes a <tr> preserving the odd/even class, with the <a> tag-link in the first cell and the .tag-vote-buttons span in the second. The replacement is performed in-place inside the existing <div>, leaving all surrounding DOM structure intact.

applyListToTable() is called in startFetchingProcess() immediately after stopOtherScripts() and before maxPage determination, ensuring the converted tables are visible to determineMaxPageFromDOM() and all subsequent fetch/filter/sort infrastructure.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: finalCleanup() / artist-releases VA footer removal (third and final attempt): confirmed via saved HTML that the footer is THREE BARE SIBLING NODES — a text node ('Showing releases by this artist. '), an <a> element, and a text node ('.') — that are direct children of div#content with no wrapper element whatsoever. Previous fix (9.99.387) introduced a blank-page regression: the anchor-closest fallback's parentElement path returned div#content itself (the direct parent of the bare <a>), removed it, and wiped the entire page. The form-sibling strategy also silently failed because nextElementSibling skips text nodes and landed on the <a> itself, then querySelector searched descendants of <a> (finding nothing). Correct fix: use document.body.querySelector('a[href*="/releases?va="]') to locate the link, then surgically remove (a) the <a> element, (b) its previousSibling text node (the sentence start), and (c) its nextSibling text node (the trailing period). The containing div#content is never touched.

sections.label: 🔙 Revert
items: Reverted the broken two-strategy VA footer approach from 9.99.387 which caused the blank-page regression.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items:
finalCleanup() / artist-releases VA footer removal (second attempt): replaced the querySelectorAll('p') and '#content p' selectors — both of which fail silently when the footer element is not a

tag — with a two-strategy approach. Strategy 1 (positional): walks nextElementSibling from the 'form[action*="/release/merge_queue"]' to the first

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: finalCleanup() / artist-releases VA footer removal: widened the DOM selector from '#content p' to 'document.body.querySelectorAll("p")' so the 'Showing releases by this artist.' paragraph is found regardless of which wrapper element (div#content, div.content, div#page, …) MusicBrainz places it under. The narrow '#content p' selector was silently producing zero matches when the paragraph lived outside #content, leaving the stale footer visible after the consolidated render.

buildActiveInjectedColumns(): added an explicit 'artist-works' branch that resolves the WS2 entity type to 'work' with inc options ['url-rels', 'artist-rels']. Previously the function fell through to the 'release' default, causing initRelationshipsColumn() to query '/ws/2/release/<work-MBID>' — a wrong entity-type path that always returned HTTP 404 and therefore never populated any Relationships cells on artist-works pages.

_extractMbidFromRow(): extended the capture regex from '/(release-group|release)/' to '/(release-group|release|work)/' so that work-entity links of the form '/work/<MBID>' are recognised and their UUID extracted. Without this change every row on an artist-works page returned null, causing initRelationshipsColumn() to skip injecting rel-cells entirely.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: artist-releases pageType: the trailing MusicBrainz footer paragraph ('Showing releases by this artist.' / 'Show Various Artist releases instead' link) is now removed in finalCleanup() after the consolidated render, as it is stale and misleading once all pages are merged. Detection uses a querySelectorAll on '#content p' elements containing an anchor whose href matches '/releases?va=', making it MBID-agnostic and idempotent across re-renders.

artist-works pageType definition: added 'injectedColumns: [ Relationships ]' to the features block so that the Relationships column is injected into the consolidated works table, consistent with other artist pageTypes that expose relationship data.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New setting 'sa_remove_checkbox_cell' (default: true) in the OPTIONAL COLUMN REMOVAL FROM FINAL RENDERED PAGE section: when enabled, removes the <th class="checkbox-cell"> header column and its corresponding body cells from all final rendered tables on all pageTypes. The column is identified by its CSS class rather than header text, consistent with MusicBrainz's DOM structure.

When 'sa_remove_checkbox_cell' is enabled, any 'div.list-merge-buttons-row-container' present on the page is hidden via display:none in finalCleanup(). The merge-button row is non-functional once checkboxes are removed; hiding it (rather than removing it) preserves the DOM structure required by the navigation-guard submit interceptor (initNavigationGuard).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Injected column cells (mb-rel-cell, mb-re-cell) now show the correct zebra-stripe background. Root cause: the script's own injected CSS contained 'td.mb-rel-cell, td.mb-re-cell { background: none !important }' which overrode both the even/odd class rules and any inline style with higher priority. Fix: 'background: none !important' is removed from the selectors and kept only on their and children (which still need it to suppress the MusicBrainz sprite ghost applied via 'a[target=_blank]::after').

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyStickyColumn body cells: fixed zero-background regression. MusicBrainz's zebra-stripe CSS targets <td> elements directly (e.g. 'tr.even > td { background: #e4e4e4 }'), not the <tr> itself. Reading getComputedStyle(tr) therefore always returns transparent. Fix: clear cell.style.background first so the CSS class rule wins the cascade, then read getComputedStyle(cell).backgroundColor to get the actual rendered colour, then set it as an explicit inline background so the sticky cell is opaque over scrolling content. Injected column cells (td.style.backgroundColor = '') already benefit from the CSS class rules without any inline override.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: applyZebraStriping() no longer hard-codes row background colours. Instead it removes both 'even' and 'odd' CSS classes from each visible <tbody> row and re-adds the correct one based on the row's current visual position (odd=index 0, even=index 1, …). MusicBrainz's own stylesheet drives the actual colours, so the table always matches the page theme exactly.

applyStickyColumn() body-cell background updated to match: since rows now carry CSS classes instead of inline styles, getComputedStyle(tr).backgroundColor is used to read the actual rendered colour for the sticky cell's opaque background. Falls back to #ffffff only when the computed colour is transparent (e.g. odd rows in themes where only even rows have an explicit background).

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: New applyZebraStriping(table) function: unconditionally applies alternating white/#f8f9fc row backgrounds to all visible <tbody> rows after every render and filter/sort operation. Fixes two issues: (1) sticky column cells always had white background (applyStickyColumn reads tr.style.backgroundColor, now always set by applyZebraStriping); (2) sorting could leave adjacent rows with the same colour because the alternating pattern was baked in at import time and not updated after rows were reordered.

applyZebraStriping + applyStickyColumn are called together at the end of renderFinalTable, renderGroupedTable, and runFilter so the table always has a fresh consistent appearance after every render, sort, or filter change. border-collapse:separate (required for position:sticky) is also enforced at the same points.

Reverted the getComputedStyle-based approach from 9.99.379: the previous fix was complex and did not reliably capture alternating colours. The new approach is simpler and correct: applyZebraStriping sets tr.style.backgroundColor directly, so all consumers (sticky cells, injected cells clearing their loading tint) read the right value without needing computed-style lookups.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Sticky column: body cells no longer all show the same white background. Root cause: applyStickyColumn._apply() used tr.style.backgroundColor to set the cell background, but MusicBrainz alternating row colours come from CSS :nth-child rules (not inline styles), so tr.style.backgroundColor was always empty and the fallback #ffffff applied to every row. Fix: new _rowEffectiveBg(tr) helper checks inline style first, then getComputedStyle (captures CSS :nth-child), then falls back to white. Used in _apply() for all body cells.

Injected columns (Release events, Relationships): after the loading tint is cleared, cells now receive the row's effective alternating background instead of being left transparent. Applied at all three td.style.backgroundColor='' clearing sites in _populateCells (live rows and source rows) and the release-events population loop, using the same inline-style → getComputedStyle → '' priority chain.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: sa_enable_sticky_columns description: unescaped apostrophe in 'definition's' caused a SyntaxError at runtime. Fixed by switching the string delimiters to double-quotes for that concatenation segment.

stickyColumn pairs: the bulk-insertion script in the previous session inserted stickyColumn lines without adding a trailing comma to the preceding extractMainColumn line, and without adding the required comma to stickyColumn itself when further properties follow. All 37 pairs now have correct comma placement. Two edge cases (search features.transformToH2 and area-recordings features.columnExtractors) required a manual second pass.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: configSchema: sa_enable_sticky_headers and the new sa_enable_sticky_columns setting are now grouped together under a dedicated '📌 TABLE STICKINESS' divider section (previously sa_enable_sticky_headers was in the generic UI FEATURES section and sa_enable_sticky_columns was missing from the schema entirely).

stickyColumn property added to every pageType definition that has extractMainColumn but lacked an explicit stickyColumn override (37 definitions). Each stickyColumn value matches the corresponding extractMainColumn value so the visually most important column (entity name/title) is always kept visible during horizontal scrolling.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Sticky column header was not sticking during horizontal scroll. Root cause: makeColumnsResizable() runs after applyStickyColumn() and sets th.style.position = 'relative' on every header <th> in thead tr:first-child, overwriting the 'sticky' value set by applyStickyColumn(). The filter row's <th> and body <td> cells are not touched by makeColumnsResizable(), which is why they already worked correctly. Fixes: (1) makeColumnsResizable now skips setting position:'relative' when the <th> already has position:'sticky' — a sticky element already acts as a containing block for its position:absolute children (the resize handle). (2) applyStickyColumn calls are moved to AFTER makeColumnsResizable in both the single-table and multi-table render paths, so sticky is always the last writer.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New configSchema setting sa_enable_sticky_columns (checkbox, default true) added to the UI FEATURES section alongside sa_enable_sticky_headers. When disabled, applyStickyColumn() is not called on any table. Works independently of sticky headers.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: New sticky column feature: the first column of every table.tbl is made sticky (position:sticky) by default so it does not scroll away during horizontal scrolling. A pageType definition can override this with stickyColumn:'<column name>' in its features object to nominate a different column by header text.

applyStickyColumn(table): new function that resolves the sticky column index (from activeDefinition.features.stickyColumn or 0), calculates the required CSS left offset for non-first columns by reading colgroup <col> widths (or th.offsetWidth as fallback), applies position:sticky + left + z-index + background to every <th> and <td> in that column, and attaches a MutationObserver on the colgroup to re-apply the offset automatically whenever column widths change via drag-resize or auto-resize.
recording-releases definition: added stickyColumn:'Title' so the Title column stays visible while scrolling the wide multi-table release listing.

Sticky column and sticky header row work together: applyStickyColumn sets z-index 101 on header cells (above the sticky thead z-index 100) and z-index 1 on body cells. border-collapse:separate + border-spacing:0 is enforced unconditionally on each table so sticky positioning works regardless of whether sa_enable_sticky_headers is on or off.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: JSDoc: removed stale 'Mirrors the 3-tier format…' JSDoc that was stacked before _updateSubTableH3Tooltip(). It described updateH2CountFromSubtables() at an earlier, less complete stage; that function already has a full JSDoc immediately before its own declaration. The correct _updateSubTableH3Tooltip JSDoc remains.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Added @match directive for MusicBrainz user subscription pages (*://*.musicbrainz.org/user/*/subscriptions/*) so the script activates on e.g. https://musicbrainz.org/user/vzell/subscriptions/label.

Added 'subscriptions' pageType to pageDefinitions (before 'search'), with tableMode:'single' and extractMainColumn:'Name', enabling the 'Show all Subscription results' consolidation button on all /user/*/subscriptions/<type> pages.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: JSDoc: removed misplaced getResourceTimingHint JSDoc block that was stacked before function _hintFromSrc(). The correct _hintFromSrc JSDoc remains; getResourceTimingHint() retains its own JSDoc immediately before it.

JSDoc: removed misplaced rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti JSDoc block that was stacked before function _applyCollapseState(). The correct _applyCollapseState JSDoc remains; rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti() retains its own JSDoc immediately before it.

sections.label: ✨ Feature / 🐛 Fix
items: Removed configSchema settings sa_remove_rel, sa_remove_perf, and sa_remove_release_events. The columns 'Relationships', 'Performance Attributes', and 'Release events' (injected by third-party userscripts) are now always stripped unconditionally during header preparation, since the script provides its own injected versions of these columns when configured.

cleanupHeaders() and the fetch-loop header scanner now use two separate structures: removalMapAlways (Set of always-removed foreign userscript column prefixes) and removalMapSetting / removalMap (object of setting-gated columns: Rating, Tagger). The old single removalMap keyed to Lib.settings lookups is removed for the three unconditional columns.

JSDoc fix: removed the duplicate cleanupHeaders JSDoc block that was incorrectly placed before function _synthColTooltip(). The correct JSDoc on cleanupHeaders() itself is preserved and its column-removal example updated to remove references to the deleted settings.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: JSDoc cleanup — cleanupHeaders(): the JSDoc describing the three-pass header preparation (column removal, origin stamping, synthetic column injection) was missing from before function cleanupHeaders() after the previous session's fix moved it away; restored.
JSDoc cleanup — triggerStandardDownload(): the two stacked docs ('Standard download fallback…' and 'Trigger a browser file download…') merged into one definitive JSDoc.

JSDoc cleanup — getResourceTimingHint(): the detailed JSDoc describing the RT API classification rules (memory/disk/network/unavailable) was lost in the previous session and left the function undocumented; restored before function getResourceTimingHint().

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: series-releases entityFeatures — 'Release groups': added columnExtractors [{ sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'caa', syntheticColumns: ['CAA'] }] and collapsableColumns: ['CAA'], so that the CAA artwork column is extracted and collapsible on series Release-groups pages.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: ergInjectReleaseGroupButton: fixed 'extra column' bug in tableMode:'multi' pages (e.g. artist-releases with addCAA:'Title'). When the parent element is a plain <td> (no art data, cell not yet wrapped in ul.mb-caa-art-ul by _artBuildMultiRowArtCell), parent.after(table) incorrectly inserted the release <table> as a sibling of <td> inside <tr>, shifting all subsequent column cells to the right. Fix: detect isTd = parent.tagName === 'TD' and use parent.appendChild(table) in that case, appending the release table inside the <td> instead. The existing isSummaryLi path (tableContainer.appendChild for art cells) and the <li> in-place path (parent.after for multi-row cells) are unchanged.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / ✨ Feature
items: series-releases entityFeatures — 'Events': added cancelledEvent extractor ({ sourceColumn: 'Event', extractor: 'cancelledEvent', syntheticColumns: ['Cancelled'] }) and tooltipColumns ['MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Primary Alias', '---', 'Artists', 'Location', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled'].
series-releases entityFeatures — 'Recordings': corrected video extractor sourceColumn from 'Title' to 'Name' to match the actual MusicBrainz recordings column header.

series-releases entityFeatures — 'Release groups': added 'jesus2099' to columnErasers (removes the cover-art anchor injected by the jesus2099 userscript) and added tooltipColumns ['Title', 'Artist', '---', ['Type', 'Year', '(', 'Releases', ')']].

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: series-releases page definition refactored: the single flat 'features' object is replaced by an 'entityFeatures' map keyed by H2 entity-type name ('Releases', 'Events', 'Release groups', 'Recordings', 'Works'). Each entry carries its own independent feature set (columnErasers, columnExtractors, syntheticColumnExtractors, injectedColumns, integerColumns, collapsableColumns, tooltipColumns, addCAA/addEAA, extractMainColumn) so that the correct features activate for whichever entity type the series page shows.

New helper _seriesH2EntityType(): reads the live H2 heading preceding the first table.tbl (stripping toggle icons and count badges) and returns the bare entity-type string. Uses the same three-step fallback chain as the labelFromH2 button-label code.

New helper resolveSeriesEntityFeatures(def): matches _seriesH2EntityType() against def.entityFeatures keys. Falls back to the first key ('Releases') when no match is found, preserving backward-compatible behaviour for unrecognised entity types.

startFetchingProcess() now calls resolveSeriesEntityFeatures(baseDef) before constructing mergedFeatures, so the entity-specific feature set is the base layer of the merge (lowest priority), overridable by baseDef.features and buttonConfig.features.

sections.label: 🔧 Refactor
items: JSDoc cleanup: resolved 20 groups of stacked/misplaced JSDoc blocks throughout the script. Each function now has exactly one JSDoc comment placed directly above its declaration. Actions taken per group: (1) eraser/injected/release — reordered so each of the three JSDocs precedes its own function (buildActiveReleaseEventColumns, buildActiveInjectedColumns, buildActiveColumnErasers). (2) applyIntegerColumnStyling — merged two overlapping JSDocs (general technique + colon-split detail) into one comprehensive block. (3) toggleColumn/subTableColVisRegistry — separated: @type comment placed on the const, function JSDoc placed on the function. (4) createSubTableCollapseButton/makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML — moved createSubTableCollapseButton JSDoc from before makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML to before its own function. (5) saveColVisState/getCurrentColVisState — separated the two stacked docs and placed each before its own arrow-function const. (6) toggleAutoResizeColumns — merged two redundant JSDocs into one. (7) highlightText — merged older brief JSDoc into the richer second block, preserving all @param entries. (8) updateH2CountFromSubtables/_updateSubTableH3Tooltip — separated; moved _updateSubTableH3Tooltip JSDoc to before its function. (9) ensureCollapseDelegate/updateGlobalCollapseButtonHighlight — separated; moved ensureCollapseDelegate JSDoc to before its function. (10) removeBrTags — removed orphaned JSDoc (function no longer exists in the codebase). (11) _relAppendIcon — removed three obsolete draft JSDocs (CSS-sprite variant, img variant, bare variant); kept and lightly merged the final definitive JSDoc. (12) _relFetchWs2 — removed two obsolete draft JSDocs (memory-only, fetch-only variants); kept and merged the definitive two-level-cache JSDoc. (13) initRelationshipsColumn — moved orphaned JSDoc from before extractReleaseEvents to directly before initRelationshipsColumn. (14) triggerStandardDownload — merged two redundant JSDocs into one. (15) _artIdbDelete — merged two JSDocs (one marked unused/images store, one active/rel-ws2 store) into one accurate block covering both use cases. (16) orphaned bigbox tooltip JSDoc — removed orphaned JSDoc that had no corresponding function declaration below it. (17) _artBuildImageLi/_artHighlightImageLi — swapped order so _artBuildImageLi JSDoc precedes _artBuildImageLi and _artHighlightImageLi JSDoc precedes _artHighlightImageLi. (18) rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti — added missing JSDoc (was lost when the stacked block was separated).

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: configSchema: sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg and sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg_clicked changed from type 'text' to type 'color_picker' so they now render with a live color preview swatch and the iro.js 🎨 color picker button in the Settings dialog.

configSchema: all popup_dialog entries that contain color-named sub-fields now declare a colorFields array listing those fields by name. This is consumed by VZ_MBLibrary 3.17.0 to render a live color-preview background and a 🎨 picker button for each color sub-field inside the Edit fields sub-grid. Affected entries: sa_ui_stop_btn_style (bg, color), sa_ui_settings_btn_style (bg, color), sa_ui_help_btn_style (bg, color), sa_ui_button_divider_style (color), sa_ui_subtable_btn_style (bg, bgHover), sa_ui_filter_bar_btn_style (bg), sa_ui_save_btn_style (bg, color, bgHover), sa_ui_load_btn_style (bg, color, bgHover).
Requires VZ_MBLibrary 3.17.0 or later for the color-picker sub-field rendering.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: configSchema: added new 'UNICODE CHARACTER PICKER' section with divider_unicode_char_picker, sa_unicode_char_picker (enable/disable checkbox, default true), and sa_unicode_char_picker_mappings (3-column table: Unicode Code Symbol / Name / Default) so the user can inspect and extend the character catalogue directly from the Settings dialog.
sa_shortcut_unicode_chars keyboard shortcut definition is now co-located under the new 'UNICODE CHARACTER PICKER' section (moved from the KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS section).
SA_UNICODE_CHARS converted from const to let; the built-in entries are preserved in SA_UNICODE_CHARS_DEFAULT and used as the fallback / seed when the config table is empty.

New _loadUnicodeCharsMappings() function reads the sa_unicode_char_picker_mappings config table and rebuilds SA_UNICODE_CHARS at runtime. On first run it seeds GM_setValue with the built-in defaults so they appear pre-populated in the Settings dialog.

initSaUnicodeCharsFeature() now calls _loadUnicodeCharsMappings() before the idempotency guard. If the catalogue length changed since the last build (e.g. user added rows), the stale menu DOM is removed and rebuilt with the updated entry list.

Unicode Character Picker feature (initSaUnicodeCharsFeature, showSaUnicodeCharsMenu) and its keyboard shortcut (sa_shortcut_unicode_chars, default Ctrl+U) are now fully gated on Lib.settings.sa_unicode_char_picker !== false, so disabling the setting in Settings silences both the menu init and the shortcut handler.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Relationship icons dropdown: entries are now sorted by display URL (name) ascending as primary key, count descending as secondary key — e.g. all amazon.com / amazon.de / amazon.it variants appear together in alphabetical order instead of being interleaved by count.

Relationship icons dropdown: springsteenlyrics.com URLs are now grouped by virtual path (without query string or fragment), producing separate entries for e.g. 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/bootlegs.php', 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/collection.php', 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/forum/viewtopic.php'. All other domains continue to group by hostname only.

Introduced PATH_SENSITIVE_HOSTS Set (currently containing 'springsteenlyrics.com') as a clean extension point for any future domains that require path-level grouping in the Relationship icons section.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Relationship icons dropdown — springsteenlyrics.com path grouping was not working: URLs were read from <a href> attributes (subject to browser normalisation) instead of the raw .mb-rel-filter-key span text, and the hostname check was an exact match that silently failed for www.-prefixed variants (e.g. www.springsteenlyrics.com). Now reads from .mb-rel-filter-key spans and uses _isPathSensitive() which also matches subdomains via endsWith('.<host>') so both bare and www. variants are handled correctly.
Relationship icons dropdown — PATH_SENSITIVE_HOSTS Set and _isPathSensitive() helper are now defined once before the row loop instead of being recreated on every row iteration.
Relationship icons dropdown — root-path '/' is normalised to the bare origin, so 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/' no longer generates a spurious extra entry alongside path-specific ones.
Relationship icons dropdown — count badges are now right-aligned with a fixed monospace minWidth (matching the regular unique-values list) so single- and multi-digit counts line up cleanly.

Relationship icons dropdown — item rows now use display:flex + align-items:center so the favicon icon column is perfectly aligned below each other across all entries, independent of badge digit count.

sections.label: ✨ Feature
items: Relationship icons dropdown: entries are now sorted by display URL (name) ascending as primary key, count descending as secondary key — e.g. all amazon.com / amazon.de / amazon.it variants appear together in alphabetical order instead of being interleaved by count.

Relationship icons dropdown: springsteenlyrics.com URLs are now grouped by virtual path (without query string or fragment), producing separate entries for e.g. 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/bootlegs.php', 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/collection.php', 'https://springsteenlyrics.com/forum/viewtopic.php'. All other domains continue to group by hostname only.

Introduced PATH_SENSITIVE_HOSTS Set (currently containing 'springsteenlyrics.com') as a clean extension point for any future domains that require path-level grouping in the Relationship icons section.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Added 'Relationships' column to 'Artist-Relationships', 'Recording-Releases', 'Series-Releases' and 'Area-Releases' columns
Added 'addCAA' column to 'Area-Releases' columns

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Relationship icons dropdown: each unique entry now shows the favicon followed by the base URL (origin) label, e.g. '[amazon.de favicon] https://www.amazon.de/'. Previously only the favicon was shown with no visible text.
iconFor map now stores the URL origin (e.g. 'https://www.amazon.de/') instead of the full representative href, so the displayed label is always a clean base URL.

Clicking a favicon+base URL entry in the unique-values dropdown still filters by hostname (domain), matching all rows where any URL from that domain appears in the Relationships cell via the hidden .mb-rel-filter-key spans.
Favicon img margin-right increased from 0 to 4px to provide visual separation between the icon and the base URL text label.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Release events collapsableColumns timing: initCollapsableColumns ran inside renderGroupedTable before cells were populated, finding 0 ul>li items. Now re-runs after initReleaseEventsColumn completes.
_rePopulateCell renders events as <ul><li> (no built-in toggle), letting collapsableColumns framework handle expand/collapse.

Relationship icons dropdown groups by domain — one favicon per unique domain (discogs.com, amazon.com…). Clicking filters by domain, matching all URLs for that domain via the hidden .mb-rel-filter-key span.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1 (collapsableColumns: ['Release events'] had no effect): initCollapsableColumns requires ul>li cell structure; mb-re-cell cells use <div> elements with a built-in 'show N more' toggle. Removed 'Release events' from collapsableColumns on all four page types (the column already handles its own collapse internally).

Bug 2 (icon dropdown showed raw URLs): Relationship icons section in the unique-value dropdown now shows only the favicon icon and the bare domain (e.g. 'discogs.com', 'amazon.com') without the full URL path. Clicking still filters by the full href via the hidden .mb-rel-filter-key span, enabling generic domain-level filtering.

Bug 3 (_artIdbDelete is not defined): the function was commented out with a '/* UNUSED */' block comment. _relRetryMbids calls it to purge cached WS2 relationship data from IDB when retrying. Uncommented and updated the JSDoc.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / 🚀 Feature
items: Bug 1: added 'Relationships' to injectedColumns and collapsableColumns for all four page types (place-performances-filtered, place-performances, label-relationships-filtered, label-relationships). These pages now show both a 'Release events' column and a 'Relationships' column.

Bug 2: Relationships column unique-value dropdown now has a 'Relationship icons' synthetic section. Each unique relationship URL is listed with its favicon, a count badge, and a truncated URL label. Clicking filters to rows containing that relationship. _relAppendIcon appends a hidden .mb-rel-filter-key span so getCleanColumnText includes the URL in column-filter matching. getCleanVisibleText excludes these spans so sort/global-filter text is unaffected.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Both Release events and Relationships columns missing on place/label pages: activeReleaseEventColumns was never populated during a live fetch. buildActiveReleaseEventColumns(activeDefinition) was only called in the disk-load path but was missing from startFetchingProcess. Added the call immediately after buildActiveInjectedColumns in startFetchingProcess.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Release events column showed relationship icons; actual release events column missing. Root cause: buildActiveInjectedColumns() was picking up all injectedColumns entries including 'Release events', treating them as Relationships. Fixed by filtering to colName === 'Relationships' only. Added missing Release Events functions (extractReleaseEvents, _rePopulateCell, initReleaseEventsColumn) and their call sites.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: New 'Release events' injected column for place-performances(-filtered) and label-relationships(-filtered) page types. One WS2 call per page entity (/ws/2/{type}/{guid}?inc=release-rels) populates each release row with country flag divs (MBS flag.flag-XX CSS) and dates. Events >4 collapse with 'show N more' toggle. Column injected BEFORE Relationships (if present), else last.
New config: sa_enable_release_events_column (default true) and sa_enable_release_events_debug (default true) in new RELEASE EVENTS COLUMN SECTION.

New functions: extractReleaseEvents(), _rePopulateCell(), initReleaseEventsColumn(). mb-re-cell tds excluded from serialization; ghost CSS applied; source-row sync ensures filter re-renders preserve content.
artist-relationships(-filtered): added missing 'video' columnExtractor.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Added addCAA: 'Title' to four page type definitions: label-relationships-filtered, label-relationships, place-performances-filtered, and place-performances. Enables cover art (CAA) thumbnails in the Title column for these page types.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: Relationship icon tooltips for non-URL rels (e.g. 'single from', release-group links) now include the entity title from the WS2 JSON response. No extra network request: the title is already present in the rel['release-group'].title or rel.release.title field of the data we already fetch.
