Tooltip format: 'relType: Title\nhref' (e.g. 'single from: Born to Run\n/release-group/e6e5a3fc-...'). _relAppendIcon gains an optional 5th parameter `tooltip` (defaults to url when absent).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Relationship icons vanish on filter after disk load: disk-load calls renderGroupedTable → runFilter() → initRelationshipsColumn(). runFilter clones source rows then puts clones in DOM; source rows detach. initRelationshipsColumn injected mb-rel-cell only into DOM (clones), not source rows. Every subsequent runFilter clones from source rows (no mb-rel-cell) → icons gone. Three-part fix: (1) _ensureRelCell injects into DOM rows AND groupedRows/allRows source rows; (2) _populateCells tracks source cells and marks them done; (3) queue.then syncs populated innerHTML from DOM cells to source-row cells.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: SyntaxError 'await is only valid in async functions': initRelationshipsColumn() was not declared async but v9.99.349 added `await Promise.all(_phase1)` inside it. Fixed by adding the `async` keyword to the function declaration.

sections.label: 🚀 Improvement
items: Relationship icons now load nearly instantly on repeated page visits (IDB warm). Root cause: initRelationshipsColumn used a single sequential queue with a fixed 1100ms inter-request delay between every MBID — including IDB hits that require no network request. With 7 rows all cached, that meant 7 × 1.1s = 7.7s to render all icons even though IDB reads take ~1ms. Fix: two-phase fetch strategy. Phase 1 checks all MBIDs against IDB in parallel (Promise.all) and populates cells immediately for hits. Phase 2 runs only the IDB misses through the throttled 1100ms queue for network fetches. Warm-cache pages now render all relationship icons simultaneously in milliseconds; cold-cache first visits are unchanged (1 icon/second).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: No relationship icons after disk load: renderGroupedTable and renderFinalTable append disk-loaded rows directly without going through the live row-builder pipeline (step 5b) that normally appends mb-rel-cell placeholder tds. Since v9.99.347 also removed rel cells from serialization, disk-loaded rows had no mb-rel-cell tds at all → initRelationshipsColumn found 0 cells → returned immediately. Fix: initRelationshipsColumn now checks every data row for a missing mb-rel-cell td and injects one (with data-mbid from _extractMbidFromRow) before collecting cells. This runs for both disk-load and live paths, is idempotent (skips rows that already have the td), and keeps serialization clean.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icons on disk load (true root cause): mb-rel-cell tds are excluded from row serialization (.filter() before .map() in groups and allRows paths). The renderer always creates fresh mb-rel-cell tds via activeInjectedColumns on rebuild. Saving them gave each row an extra empty td; on load the renderer appended another → doubled cells → ghost icons. getCleanCellHtml early-return from v9.99.346 reverted as no longer needed.

Retry buttons no effect / no debug output: _relRetryActive was reset synchronously after initRelationshipsColumn() returned, but the fetch queue is async — queued _relFetchWs2 calls saw false and hit cache. Fixed: reset moved to queue.then() completion handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1+2 (ghost icons on disk load): getCleanCellHtml() returns '' immediately for mb-rel-cell elements. Previous _stripTransientCellState fix was a no-op — freshly-created <td> elements on load have no class attribute.

Bug 2+3+4 (initial-page disk load — missing rels header, missing 🔗⟳ buttons, buttons inert): disk-load path now rebuilds activeInjectedColumns from activeDefinition. New standalone _relCreateRetryButtons() creates buttons anchored to existing h2/h3 UI without CAA infrastructure dependency.
Bug 5 (no press effect): [id^='mb-rel-retry-'] added to :active, :focus-visible, and normal-state button CSS selectors.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost/double relationship icons on 'Load from Disk': root cause was that data-rel-done is a TD-level dataset attribute and is NOT included in cell.innerHTML, so it is never saved to disk. On load, the reconstructed td has the saved icon HTML in its innerHTML but no data-rel-done attribute, so initRelationshipsColumn() treats it as unpopulated and appends new icons on top of the already-saved ones. Fix: _stripTransientCellState() now clears the innerHTML of mb-rel-cell elements (both when called on the td directly and when called on a parent row clone). On disk load the cells are empty; initRelationshipsColumn() re-populates them from IDB (instant, no network) or network if the IDB entry has expired.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Trailing h2 section relocation (sa_enable_h2_section_relocation_on_final_page) now works when 'Load from Disk' is used. Root cause: finalCleanup() identifies the data h2 by searching for .mb-row-count-stat, but during disk load updateH2Count() (which creates that span) runs at line 28463 — well after finalCleanup() at line 28329. So _dataH2 was always null and the relocation silently no-oped. Fix: extracted the relocation logic into a standalone _relocateTrailingH2Sections() function and added an explicit call after updateH2Count() in loadTableDataFromDisk. finalCleanup() still calls it for the live-fetch path (where the stat already exists). The function is idempotent and returns early if the stat is not yet in the DOM.

sections.label: 🚀 Feature
items: New config sa_rels_idb_enable (default true): caches WS2 relationship data in IndexedDB (store 'rel-ws2' in vz-mb-saed-art-cache, schema bumped to v2, 7-day TTL). _relFetchWs2 gains a two-level cache (L1 memory + L2 IDB); network result is written to IDB on success. _relRetryActive flag lets retry helpers bypass both caches.

New Relationship retry buttons 🔗⟳: one in h2 (id='mb-rel-retry-global', after the global CAA ⟳ button) and one per h3 sub-table (id='mb-rel-retry-N', after the per-table ⟳ button). Clicking clears L1+L2 caches for affected MBIDs, resets rel-done markers on all rel cells, and re-runs initRelationshipsColumn().
New internal functions: _relIdbGet, _relIdbPut, _relRetryMbids, _relRetryTable, _relRetryAll.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon: ghost appears behind real icon = background-image on <a> element from an MBS CSS rule (e.g. a[target=_blank] or a.external). Added background:none !important and background-image:none !important to td.mb-rel-cell a.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon / empty space: reverted content:'' back to content:none !important for ::before/::after suppression. content:'' makes pseudo-elements exist (just empty) so MBS can still attach background rendering to them via higher specificity rules. content:none makes pseudo-elements not exist at all — it cannot be overridden by display or background rules. Fixed the empty space (from v9.99.339) by setting margin:0 and padding:0 on td.mb-rel-cell a, and keeping img at margin:1px display:block.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Empty space between icons: changed ::before/::after suppression from display:none to position:absolute + width:0 + height:0 + overflow:hidden. This removes pseudo-elements from flow regardless of MBS stylesheet specificity — display:none can be overridden by higher-specificity MBS rules, but an absolutely-positioned 0x0 element always occupies zero layout space. Added position:relative on to contain the absolute pseudo-elements. Changed margin from 2px to 1px for tight icon packing. is now display:block inside the display:inline-block so layout is entirely block-based with no anonymous inline boxes.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon: MBS stylesheet adds ::before/::after pseudo-elements to <a> elements (e.g. external-link arrow via a[target=_blank]::after). These render as sprite icons alongside our Google favicon images. Fix: td.mb-rel-cell a::before and td.mb-rel-cell a::after are now suppressed via content:none !important. Also added font-size:0 on the cell, anchors, and images to collapse any anonymous inline boxes, and explicit width/height on img to ensure correct sizing.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon: removed 'relationships' class from injected elements (MBS td.relationships CSS adds visual content that appeared as ghost icons), and removed class='favicon' from elements (MBS .favicon CSS applies sprite background-image to any element with that class). Injected is now plain 'mb-rel-cell'. Google favicon carries no MBS classes.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon: removed 'relationships' class from injected elements (MBS td.relationships CSS adds visual content that appeared as ghost icons), and removed class='favicon' from elements (MBS .favicon CSS applies sprite background-image to any element with that class). Injected is now plain 'mb-rel-cell'. Google favicon carries no MBS classes.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon (definitive fix): switched from CSS sprites to Google favicon service (https://www.google.com/s2/favicons?sz=16&domain=...). CSS sprites (.favicon + .allmusic-favicon etc.) require the MBS td.relationships CSS context to render correctly — our consolidated table td cells don't reliably inherit that context, causing ghost icons regardless of what CSS we inject. Google S2 favicons are plain — no CSS sprites, no background-image, no anonymous inline struts, no ghosts, in any CSS context. _relAppendIcon now always generates . New _relGoogleFaviconUrl(url) helper returns the Google favicon URL for any domain. REL_*_CLASSES maps are kept for filtering (deciding which rels get icons); rendering switches to Google favicons. Discography entries also switch from async HEAD-probe (_relFetchFaviconUrl) to synchronous Google favicon — faster, no GM_xmlhttpRequest needed for favicons, no async delay.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon (root cause identified): The MBS stylesheet has rules scoped to td.relationships that suppress the sprite ghost (baseline-strut issue). Our td.mb-rel-cell did not carry that class, so MBS ghost-suppression rules never applied — no matter what we added to our injected CSS, we were fighting the wrong battle. Fix: injected placeholder <td> elements now carry BOTH classes: 'mb-rel-cell relationships'. The 'relationships' class makes MBS treat our cell exactly like its own relationship cells (ghost suppressed). The 'mb-rel-cell' class is retained for our own data-mbid/data-rel-done attribute targeting. Removed all the ghost-fighting CSS rules we had accumulated — none are needed once the correct MBS class is present.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon fix: reverted flexbox approach; applied line-height:0 to td.mb-rel-cell instead. Root cause: anonymous inline strut. When display:inline-block span.favicon sits inside an inline <a>, the browser inserts a 0-width strut character with height=line-height to align baselines. This strut forces the browser to paint the span's sprite background at position 0,0 — the 'folder' ghost. With line-height:0 the strut collapses to zero height and the sprite has no room to render at that position. This is the same technique MBS uses in its td.relationships CSS, which is why the reference script has no ghosts with identical span.favicon CSS. span.favicon and img.favicon remain display:inline-block with vertical-align:middle and margin:2px — matching the reference script exactly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon (definitive fix): td.mb-rel-cell is now a flex container. Root cause: display:inline-block on span.favicon inside an inline <a> causes browsers to insert an anonymous inline box for baseline-alignment (the 'strut'). This strut renders the MBS sprite at background-position 0,0 — the 'folder' ghost. In a flex formatting context there are no inline boxes and no baseline struts, so no ghost. The <a> is display:flex with align-items:center; span.favicon and img.favicon are display:block (no inline-block needed inside flex).

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1: All relationship icon links now open in a new tab. Added a.target='_blank' and a.rel='noopener noreferrer' to _relAppendIcon.

Bug 2: CSS-class sprite icons (allmusic, discogs, lyrics, rateyourmusic, etc.) now render visually. Root cause: MBS stylesheet provides background-image and background-position globally via .favicon/.{cls}-favicon, but display:inline-block and explicit 16x16 dimensions were only in the reference script's td.relationships-scoped rule. Added equivalent td.mb-rel-cell span.favicon { display:inline-block; width:16px; height:16px; vertical-align:middle; margin:2px; } to our injected CSS.

Bug 3: non-URL rel debug rewrite — added comprehensive _relDbg logging for all non-URL rels (type, target-type, entity ID), and defensive key lookup using both 'release-group' (hyphen) and 'release_group' (underscore) since WS2 JSON uses underscore in target-type but hyphen in the nested entity key.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / Feature
items: Non-URL relationships now rendered: _populateCells now processes release-group and release rels (fetched via release-group-rels inc) in addition to url-rels. A new REL_NON_URL_ICONS constant maps relation types to icon data-URIs/URLs (e.g. 'single from' → left-arrow GIF matching the reference script). The target href is /release-group/{id} or /release/{id}. Fixes missing 'single from' arrows that appeared in the reference script but not our output.
Tooltips: _relAppendIcon now sets a.title = url on every generated anchor, showing the relationship URL as a browser tooltip on hover.

Note on REL_*_CLASSES: these ARE working correctly per debug log — allmusic, discogs, lyrics, rateyourmusic, musiksammler, wikidata all resolve from REL_URL_ICON_CLASSES / REL_OTHER_DB_CLASSES. The 'none' resolutions in the log (45cat, spirit-of-rock, bbc reviews, etc.) are expected — those domains are not in the mapping tables.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon (final fix): _relAppendIcon now generates HTML identical to the reference script. elements have NO class, NO target, NO rel attribute — just href and (when ended) style='opacity:0.25'. elements have only 'favicon {cls}-favicon' — no extra class, no title. elements have class='favicon' and style='width:16px;height:16px;vertical-align:middle;margin:2px;' matching the reference exactly. All a.mb-rel-icon CSS rules removed since there is no longer a class to target.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Ghost icon (definitive fix): <a class='mb-rel-icon'> was display:inline-block. Inside it MBS .favicon also sets display:inline-block. Nesting two inline-blocks with vertical-align:middle causes browsers to insert an anonymous inline box rendered at sprite position 0,0 (the folder/default icon) — creating the ghost. Fixed: removed display:inline-block and line-height:1 from CSS; set display:inline on <a> via inline style so it is a plain inline element, matching the reference script exactly.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1 (ghost icon): _relAppendIcon now uses class 'mb-rel-icon' on <a> and exactly 'favicon {cls}-favicon' on <span> — matching the reference DisplayShortcutForRelationship script. The extra 'mb-rel-favicon' class combined with the CSS display:inline-block rule created a phantom rendered box alongside the real MBS sprite icon. CSS block updated from a.mb-rel-link to a.mb-rel-icon selectors.

Bug 1 (ghost icon): _relAppendIcon now uses class 'mb-rel-icon' on <a> (not 'mb-rel-link') and exactly 'favicon {cls}-favicon' on <span> — matching the reference script. The extra 'mb-rel-favicon' class was triggering conflicting CSS rules that rendered a phantom sprite box alongside each real icon. CSS selectors updated to match 'a.mb-rel-icon'.

Bug 2 (empty table editors): _loadMap() in _initRelMappings() now writes the built-in defaults to GM storage on first use, so all 4 relationship mapping table editors (sa_rel_discography_mappings, sa_rel_url_icon_classes, sa_rel_other_db_classes, sa_rel_streaming_classes) open pre-populated with defaults instead of empty.

Bug 2 (empty table editors): _loadMap() in _initRelMappings() seeds GM storage with built-in default rows on first use (empty storage). Table editors now open pre-populated with defaults for all 4 relationship mapping tables.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Issue 1 (LIB): LIBRARY_VERSION constant was not updated when version was bumped. Fixed: '3.14.3+2026-03-04' → '3.16.0+2026-03-27'.

Issue 2 (LIB+SA): Settings dialog not opening. Root cause: showModal(functionRegistry) was added to the public API wrapper but the actual showModal function had no parameter, so functionRegistry was always undefined in the event-binding code. Fixed: added functionRegistry parameter to showModal function definition with a safety guard. Also fixed the call in openSettingsWithConfigButtons to properly wrap the registry as {functionRegistry: {...}} instead of passing it flat.

Issue 3 (SA): Relationships column only showing custom-favicon icons, not CSS-sprite-based service icons (discogs, allmusic, etc.). Root cause: the previous fix removed the 'favicon' MBS class from icon spans, which provides the sprite background-image that '{cls}-favicon' background-position relies on. Also, the 'background-image: none !important' CSS override suppressed all backgrounds. Fixed: restored 'favicon' class to span className, removed the harmful CSS override.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Bug 1: removed 'favicon' CSS class from icon spans (_relAppendIcon). The MusicBrainz stylesheet's .favicon class carries a default background-image (folder icon) that rendered as an unwanted second icon alongside every relationship icon. The '{cls}-favicon' class alone provides the correct service icon. Added CSS override 'td.mb-rel-cell span.mb-rel-favicon { background-image: none !important }' for belt-and-suspenders protection.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Bug 2 / Feature: new sa_rel_completion_toast_duration setting (number, default 8s) and _showRelCompletionToast() function — shows a non-intrusive bottom-right toast when all Relationships column cells have been populated, reporting entity count and elapsed time. Styled with a dark blue-grey background to distinguish from the CAA artwork toast.

Feature 3a+b+c: four new type:'table' config entries (sa_rel_discography_mappings, sa_rel_url_icon_classes, sa_rel_other_db_classes, sa_rel_streaming_classes) replace hardcoded REL_* constants. All four REL_* variables changed from const to let. New _initRelMappings() function loads each table from GM storage via Lib.getTableRows(), falling back to the built-in defaults when a table is empty. Called at the start of every initRelationshipsColumn() run so live edits take effect without a page reload.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Relationships column: _relFetchWs2 now uses fetch() instead of GM_xmlhttpRequest for WS2 calls. /ws/2/... is a same-origin relative URL (musicbrainz.org), so fetch() works without any @connect declaration. GM_xmlhttpRequest was silently calling onerror() because musicbrainz.org was not listed in @connect, causing _populateCells to receive null data — grey background cleared but no icons rendered.

Relationships column: fixed wrong WS2 JSON field name. data['url-rels'] is the XML-API format; WS2 JSON format uses data.relations[] filtered by relation['target-type'] === 'url'. This was causing an empty array even when the fetch succeeded.
Added @connect * to header to permit GM_xmlhttpRequest favicon probing (_relFetchFaviconUrl) against arbitrary external domains.

sections.label: 🔧 Debug
items: _relFetchWs2: added debug logging for fetch URL, HTTP status, total relation count, and url-rel count.
_populateCells: added debug logging for null data, url-rel count, each relation type/URL, favicon resolution, and icon class resolution.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Relationships column header/data misalignment: the injected <th class='mb-injected-column'> was appended by cleanupHeaders() BEFORE the MB-Name and Comment headers, but the row-building pipeline appended injected <td> cells AFTER MB-Name/Comment (step 5b). This shifted MB-Name data under the Relationships header and Comment data under MB-Name. Fixed by moving the injected-column <th> injection to the very end of cleanupHeaders(), after the MB-Name/Comment block, so header and data column order match.
Relationships column now guaranteed last: injected columns always appear after every other synthetic column (extracted, derived, MB-Name, Comment) in both the header row and each data row.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: New config setting 'sa_enable_relationship_debug' (checkbox, default true) in the RELATIONSHIPS COLUMN section: gates all Lib.debug('relationships', ...) calls in initRelationshipsColumn() via a local _relDbg() shortcut. WS2 HTTP error logging also gated. Set to false once the feature is confirmed working to reduce console noise.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: New injectedColumns feature for pageTypes artist-releasegroups, artist-releases, label-releases, releasegroup-releases: features.injectedColumns:["Relationships"] declares async-populated synthetic columns
New buildActiveInjectedColumns(def): resolves entityType (release-group for artist-releasegroups, release otherwise) and WS2 inc options from pageType; runtime list stored in activeInjectedColumns[]
cleanupHeaders(): injects <th class='mb-injected-column'> for each injectedColumns entry with sa_ui_thead_th_injected_bg background and descriptive hover tooltip

Row building (multi-table and single-table paths): appends empty <td class='mb-rel-cell' data-mbid='...'> placeholder cells; MBID extracted from first release-group/release link in the row via _extractMbidFromRow()

New function initRelationshipsColumn(): collects unpopulated .mb-rel-cell elements, deduplicates MBIDs, fetches WS2 url-rels sequentially at <=1 req/s (MB rate limit), populates cells with favicon icon anchors; idempotent via data-rel-done attribute; wired after barcode-highlight init in both the live-fetch and disk-load render paths

New helpers: _extractMbidFromRow(row), _relFindIconClass(url, map), _relAppendIcon(cell, url, cls, iconUrl, ended), _relFetchFaviconUrl(targetUrl) [HEAD-probes https/http favicon.ico with GM_xmlhttpRequest; local DISCOGRAPHY_MAPPINGS override table], _relFetchWs2(mbid, entityType, incOptions) [in-memory cache via Map]

New relationship icon tables ported from DisplayShortcutForRelationshipWithCustomFavicons: REL_DISCOGRAPHY_MAPPINGS, REL_URL_ICON_CLASSES (allmusic, amazon, discogs, wikidata …), REL_OTHER_DB_CLASSES (dnb, rateyourmusic, worldcat …), REL_STREAMING_CLASSES (spotify, bandcamp, youtube, apple music …)

New config section '🔗 RELATIONSHIPS COLUMN': sa_enable_relationships_column (checkbox, default true); sa_ui_thead_th_injected_bg (color_picker, default '#b8b8d0') used for injected <th> background, placeholder <td> loading tint, and stats panel Table Detail row tint
CSS: new inline <style id='mb-rel-column-style'> block: th.mb-injected-column {font-style:italic}, td.mb-rel-cell, a.mb-rel-link, img/span.mb-rel-favicon, .mb-rel-ended (opacity 0.35)
Stats panel Global Statistics: new '💉 Total injected columns' row (_injectedCols / _statsSnapshot.injectedCols)
Stats panel Table Detail: colType 'injected' classification for mb-injected-column <th>; configurable tint from sa_ui_thead_th_injected_bg + '55'
_captureStatsSnapshot: counts mb-injected-column <th> into _injectedCols; included in _synthCols total
_synthColTooltip: added 'injectedColumns' extractor case describing async WS2 population

sections.label: 🎨 UI
items: Configuration section '🎨 FILTER HIGHLIGHT COLORS' renamed to '🎨 TABLE FILTER CONFIGURATION'

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: New generic type:'function' for configSchema entries (VZ_MBLibrary v3.15.0): renders an action button (🔧 <label>) in the settings dialog; clicking it calls the function registered under cfg.default in the functionRegistry passed to Lib.showSettings(registry). The save/snapshot/reset loops all skip function-type entries. Unknown registry keys emit a console.warn instead of throwing.

New schema entry 'sa_fn_edit_pinned_filter_list' (type:'function', default:'_openEditPinnedFilterListFromSettings') in the 'TABLE FILTER CONFIGURATION' section: clicking its button in the settings dialog opens the Edit Pinned Filter List dialog directly, reading from and saving to GM storage key 'persistent-sa-hist-list'.

New wrapper function _openEditPinnedFilterListFromSettings(): reads pinHistory from GM_getValue, calls showEditPersistentListDialog with an onSave callback that persists the updated list, positions dialog centred (no trigger-button anchor).
openSettingsWithConfigButtons() now passes a functionRegistry to Lib.showSettings() so the new and any future type:'function' entries can invoke their targets.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: New setting 'sa_ld_auto_resize_after_load' (boolean, default: true) in the '💾 LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK' section: when enabled, automatically triggers the '↔️ Resize' button (toggleAutoResizeColumns) immediately after table data has been loaded and rendered from a disk file. The call is deferred by one task (setTimeout 0) so the browser has completed a layout pass and offsetWidth/scrollWidth measurements are accurate. Only fires when column resizing is enabled (sa_enable_column_resizing) and the table is not already in a resized state (isAutoResized === false && isManuallyResized === false), preventing silent undo of any existing manual resize.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: clearAllFiltersBtn.title: added missing article 'the' — 'Clear global filter' → 'Clear the global filter'

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Synthetic column header tooltips: hovering over any synthetic column name now shows a tooltip describing which source column it was extracted from and what the column contains. Implemented via new helper function _synthColTooltip(extractor, sourceColumn, colName), called from cleanupHeaders() at all four <th> injection sites.

Covered extractors: splitCountryDate (Country, Date), splitLocation (Place, Area, Country), splitArea (MB-Area, Country), caa (CAA, EAA), cancelledEvent (Cancelled), primaryAlias (Primary Alias), video (Video), dateParts (DD, MM, YYYY, Day, Month), eventParts (Event-Type, Event-Date, Event-Detail, Event-Venue, Event-Venue-Detail, Event-City, Event-State, Event-Country), extractMainColumn (MB-Name, Comment). Unknown custom extractors receive a generic fallback tooltip.

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Edit Pinned Filter List — keyboard navigation: Tab in the quick-filter field moves focus to the first table row; ArrowUp/Down navigate rows; ArrowUp on the first row returns focus to the quick-filter; Shift+Tab moves backwards without marking; Enter opens the edit form; Delete triggers the Remove button

Edit Pinned Filter List — Tab-to-mark: pressing Tab on a focused row toggles its 'marked' state (☑/☐ indicator in the # column, yellow dashed highlight); Space toggles mark without moving focus; marks persist across re-renders via a Set<origIdx>

Edit Pinned Filter List — bulk removal: when any rows are marked the Remove button label changes to '✗ Remove (N marked)' and clicking it removes all marked entries at once; with no marks the button behaves as before (removes the single selected row)

Edit Pinned Filter List — mark hints: status bar shows 'Tab on row to mark for bulk remove' when no marks are active and '{N} marked' count when marks are set; first Escape press clears marks before closing the dialog
Edit Pinned Filter List — _eplSelectRow fixed: now uses data-orig-idx attribute to locate rows instead of visual index, so selection highlight is correct when the quick filter is active

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: Global CAA/EAA col-hdr toggle button (mb-caa-col-hdr-toggle-all-btn): now renders only ONE button for whichever archive column (CAA or EAA) is actually present; per-table .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn elements are tagged with data-caa-ctx on creation so each global toggle queries exclusively its own column

Global CAA/EAA col-hdr toggle button: shortened label from "Uncollapse/Collapse all {ARCHIVE} cover art images in all sections" to "Uncollapse/Collapse all {ARCHIVE} images"; full description retained in the tooltip
Global CAA/EAA col-hdr toggle button: added a thumbnail image (reused from the per-table column-header button) as the first child of the button element, kept in sync by _syncGlobalCaaColHdrBtn
Per-table CAA/EAA strip toggle button title: changed "for this section" to "for this sub-section"
Highlight toggle button titles: added missing article "the" — "for global filter" → "for the global filter"; "for sub-table filter" → "for the sub-table filter"

sections.label: 🎨 UI
items: Sub-table collapse button label: changed "ALL" to "all" (lower-case) in the stacked multi-row-cells span inside makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML
Global CAA/EAA art-strip toggle button title: changed from "Globally toggle … - Show/Hide" to "Show/Hide [stripeLabel]s in ALL sections"; new stripeLabel property added to CAA_CTX and EAA_CTX
Per-table CAA/EAA art-strip toggle button title: changed from "Show/Hide big artwork … stripe" to "Show/Hide [stripeLabel] for this section"
Sub-table filter placeholder text: changed from "just in this sub-table" to "just in this sub-category"

Sub-table h3 header button order: buttons after the Exclude checkbox now always follow the same sequence as the global h2 bar — Expand all multi-row cells → Toggle highlighting → Clear all COLUMN filters → Clear all filters; clearAllStfBtn (mb-stf-clear-col-btn) moved out of the collapsible filter panel into subTableControls so it stays visible when the panel is collapsed

New global button "Uncollapse/Collapse all CAA/EAA cover art images in all sections" (id: mb-caa-col-hdr-toggle-all-btn-{ctx.key}): inserted after #mb-col-collapse-all-btn in the h2 filter bar for multi-table page types that carry a CAA or EAA column; clicking it emulates a click on every .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn to expand or collapse all column thumbnails at once; implemented via new function _artInitGlobalCaaColHdrToggle(ctx)

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: fetchPage / page-document reuse: added _isSearchPage guard before the existing-document shortcut. Search pages are rendered client-side by MusicBrainz JS after the server response, so reusing the live `document` on the first visit caused a race condition where the script read the DOM before the table was injected, producing either an empty result or the transient 'does not contain enough information' paragraph. Fix: when activeDefinition.type === 'search', always perform a fresh network fetch so the DOMParser receives the fully server-rendered HTML, regardless of whether p === currentPageNum.

sections.label: 🎨 UI
items: Quick-filter history dropdown — standalone variant (filter-bar widget): converted the static '📌 Pinned' section header into a flex row and added a right-aligned '✎ Edit Pinned Filter List' button. Clicking the button closes the dropdown and opens the showEditPersistentListDialog, mirroring the existing Alt+E / Edit Pinned button behaviour. Hover highlight (fff3cd) matches the existing histEditBtn style.

Quick-filter history dropdown — full-dialog variant (load-data dialog): moved the '✎ Edit Pinned Filter List' button (id=sa-hist-edit-btn) from the standalone checkbox row into the '📌 Pinned' section header, right-aligned. The header div is now a flex row (justify-content:space-between). The checkbox row comment updated accordingly ('Checkbox row + Edit Persistent List button' → 'Checkbox row'). Button sizing reduced to match the header font (0.76 em) for visual coherence.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: makeH2sCollapsible / h2._mbToggle — multi-table bigbox interleaving fix: in multi-table mode, CAA/EAA bigbox divs ARE present in contentNodes at the time makeH2sCollapsible captures siblings (because renderGroupedTable → initCaaPics inserts them before makeH2sCollapsible runs). During h2 expansion, bigbox nodes previously fell through to the generic else-branch and were shown unconditionally via node.style.display='', ignoring (a) whether their owning h3 was disc-view-hidden, (b) whether the h3's sub-table was collapsed (icon ▲), and (c) the bigbox's own tracked caaVisible/eaaVisible state. This caused artwork strips to appear floating above hidden tables, to bleed across discography view boundaries (Official ↔ Non-Official), and to reappear after the user had explicitly hidden them. Fix: added a dedicated mb-caa-bigbox/mb-eaa-bigbox branch in the expansion path that walks backward past sibling bigboxes to find the owning h3, guards on mbDiscHidden and h3 icon state, and then restores the bigbox's tracked visibility attribute — mirroring exactly the logic already applied by _artRestoreBigboxesForTable.

makeH2sCollapsible / h2._mbToggle — single-table TABLE expand path: the else-branch (no mb-toggle-h3 before the table, i.e. single-table mode) was missing a call to _artRestoreBigboxesForTable(node). Without it, any CAA/EAA bigbox that was visible before the h2 collapsed remained hidden after re-expansion because _artHideBigboxesForTable had cleared its display without clearing the tracked state. Fix: added _artRestoreBigboxesForTable(node) immediately after the node.style.display='' so the bigbox is restored to its pre-collapse tracked state on h2 expand, exactly matching the multi-table behaviour for expanded h3 sub-tables.

makeH2sCollapsible / h2._mbToggle — collapse path comment corrected: the comment stated that bigbox divs 'are not in contentNodes' and must be hidden via _artHideBigboxesForTable. This is only true for single-table mode. In multi-table mode bigboxes are in contentNodes and are already hidden by the generic node.style.display='none' pass; the _artHideBigboxesForTable call is redundant (but harmless) for multi-table. Updated comment to document both cases accurately.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _createInfoDialogQuickFilter: removed the redundant standalone 🔍 label <span> that was prepended to the filter bar — the glyph is already present in the input placeholder text, so it was appearing twice.

_createInfoDialogQuickFilter: fixed the ✕ clear button positioning. The input now uses width:100% (instead of flex:1) so it fully fills its relative-positioned inputWrap container; the inputWrap carries flex:1 instead. This ensures the absolutely-positioned ✕ button overlays the right edge of the visible input field correctly in both dialogs.

sections.label: 📦 Feature
items: Keyboard Shortcuts and Application Help dialogs: added a quick-filter bar below the title bar in both dialogs. The filter bar is built by the new _createInfoDialogQuickFilter() helper and wired via a new opts.quickFilter / opts.quickFilterPlaceholder option on createInfoDialog(). Filtering highlights all occurrences of the query string inside the content using <mark class="mb-dialog-hl"> elements (same TreeWalker snapshot-then-replace algorithm as the stats panel). Individual shortcut rows (data-qf-item) and section groups (data-qf-section) are shown/hidden; the Application Help <pre> text is highlighted in-place. Escape semantics: first press clears the field (the input's own keydown handler fires and calls e.stopPropagation() so the dialog-level Escape handler is not reached), second press blurs the input. A ✕ clear button is visible inside the input whenever it is non-empty.

_createInfoDialogQuickFilter() extracted as a standalone reusable helper: builds the filter bar DOM (label, input inside relative wrapper, overlay ✕ clear button), _highlightNode(), applyQF(), and all event wiring. Can be used by any future dialog that calls createInfoDialog with quickFilter: true.

createInfoDialog() extended: new opts.quickFilter (boolean) and opts.quickFilterPlaceholder (string) options. When quickFilter is true, the filter bar is inserted between the title bar and the scrollable content area. The return value gains filterInput and applyQF fields (null when quickFilter is not set). The document-level Escape handler defers to the input's own two-press Escape logic via e.stopPropagation().

sections.label: 📦 Feature
items: place-events: added cancelledEvent and primaryAlias column extractors to columnExtractors (order: cancelledEvent, caa/EAA, primaryAlias). Updated tooltipColumns to [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Primary Alias', 'Type', '---', 'Artists', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ] — aligning with the artist-events pattern and adding Primary Alias support.

artist-events: updated tooltipColumns to [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Primary Alias', 'Type', '---', 'Role', 'Location', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ] — adds Primary Alias row and uses MB-Name/italic:Comment instead of the bare Event column spec.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / 📦 Feature
items: artist-events tooltip 'Cancelled' column: fixed 'cancelledyes' / 'no' appearing in tooltip. Root cause: _artTooltipCellText concatenated the hidden .mb-cancelled-sort-key span text ('yes'/'no') with the visible bdi text. Fix: Cancelled dispatch now reads only the <bdi> element directly from the cell, bypassing the sort-key span. Non-cancelled events (no <bdi>) produce no tooltip row.

artist-recordings tooltip 'Video' column: new 'Video' dispatch in _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns. When a span.video with title 'This recording is a video' is present in the cell, the span is cloned and rendered in the tooltip (preserving the CSS icon). When absent (audio-only recording) no tooltip row is emitted. Previously the hidden .mb-video-sort-key span leaked 'video' or 'audio' as plain text.

series pages: button label is now dynamically derived from the h2 section heading (labelFromH2: true on the series-releases button definition). The h2 text is read at button-creation time, stripping the injected toggle icon and count badge, then substituted into the label template 'Show all <EntityType> for Series'. Falls back to the static label when no h2 is found.

makeH2sCollapsible Ctrl+Click bigbox interleaving bug: fixed artwork image stripes from one discography view appearing intermingled with tables from another view after Ctrl+Click expand. Root cause: the expansion handler used node.previousElementSibling to find the owning h3, but after _artInitBigPics ran a div.mb-caa/eaa-bigbox was inserted immediately before the table, making previousElementSibling return the bigbox div instead of the h3. The disc-view-hidden guard was therefore never applied and the table was unconditionally unhidden. Fix: replaced node.previousElementSibling with findH3ForTable(node), which walks backwards past injected bigbox divs to find the true h3.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: artist-releasegroups tooltip title: fixed spurious '▶' ERG toggle glyph appearing in the first tooltip row. Root cause: _artTooltipSplitComment and _artTooltipCellText cloned the cell but did not strip [data-erg-btn] spans injected by initExpandRGsFeature; their ▶/▼ textContent leaked into clone.textContent. Fix: added '[data-erg-btn]' to the querySelectorAll cleanup selector in both _artTooltipCellText and _artTooltipSplitComment.

artist-events tooltip 'Cancelled' column: now conditionally rendered — silently omitted when the cell is empty (no blank row), rendered in dark red bold text when the value is 'cancelled'. Implemented via a new 'Cancelled' dispatch case in _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns.

ERG multi-row cell bug: fixed inline release table appearing after ALL release groups instead of directly below the toggled one. Root cause: ergInjectReleaseGroupButton used tableContainer.appendChild(table) where tableContainer = parent.closest('td'), which always places the table at the end of the <td> regardless of which <li> was clicked. Fix: replaced with parent.after(table) / table.remove() so the inline release table is inserted as a DOM sibling immediately after the toggled <li>. The legacy art-cell path (parent.classList.contains('mb-caa-art-li-summary')) still uses closest('td').appendChild so art-cell behaviour is unchanged.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / 📦 Feature
items: artist-events / place-events tooltip: fixed spurious 'caa-inline-yes' text appended to the Event title in the first tooltip row. Root cause: _artTooltipSplitComment cloned the cell but only stripped span.comment, leaving the hidden .mb-inline-art-sort-key span (containing 'caa-inline-yes'/'caa-inline-no') in the clone's textContent. Fix: expanded the clone cleanup selector in _artTooltipSplitComment to also remove .mb-inline-art-sort-key, .mb-caa-inline-ph/.mb-eaa-inline-ph, and other injected UI spans.

artist-events tooltip 'Location' column: now rendered via new _artTooltipLocation() helper that clones the full Location cell DOM — reconstructing 'Venue in City, Region, [flag] Country' with the flag CSS sprite cloned and rendered correctly. Previously only the place name was shown. Handles both flat and multi-row (<ul><li>) cell structures.
place-events tooltip: added tooltipColumns [ 'Event', '---', 'Type', 'Artists', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ] to the place-events page definition (previously had none).

place-events tooltip 'Date (Time)': fixed lone ')' being rendered when the Time cell is empty. Root cause: the ')' literal was suppressed only when no preceding column had a value, but 'Date' already provided one. Fix: _bracketedHasValue() now checks whether any non-literal token strictly between the matching '(' and ')' has a value; both '(' and ')' are suppressed when none do. Applies to all tooltipColumns array specs.

place-events tooltip 'Artists' column: new _artTooltipArtistRoles() helper reads ALL <li> elements from ul.artist-roles (including MusicBrainz-collapsed ones with display:none) and renders them as 'Name (role)' entries comma-separated on one tooltip line. Wired via new 'Artists' dispatch case in _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns.
artist-releasegroups tooltip: added tooltipColumns [ 'Title', 'Artist', '---', ['Year', '(', 'Releases', ')'] ] to the artist-releasegroups page definition.

Bracket literal spacing: tokens in array-spec tooltip rows are now joined with smart spacing — no space is emitted immediately after '(' or immediately before ')' — so 'Format (8)' renders instead of 'Format ( 8 )'.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix / 📦 Feature
items: tooltipColumns for artist-releases and releasegroup-releases: reverted 'Total Tracks' back to 'Tracks' and replaced "'Country', 'Date'" with 'Country/Date' to use the HTML-based renderer.

Country/Date tooltip rendering: new _artTooltipCountryDate() helper reads the release-events DOM directly (flag span, abbr title/text, release-date span, mb-day-of-week span) and renders each event as an HTML line: [flag icon] Country (Code) Date Weekday. Handles multiple release events.
_renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns: 'Country/Date' spec is now dispatched to _artTooltipCountryDate() for rich HTML rendering instead of plain text extraction.

Debug logging: all tooltip console.log() calls converted to if (Lib.settings.sa_enable_tooltip_debug) Lib.debug('tooltips', ...). New setting sa_enable_tooltip_debug (default: false) added to GENERIC SECTION of configSchema.

tooltipColumns added to recording-releases: [ 'Release title', 'italic:Comment', 'Release Artist', 'Release group type', '---', ['#', 'Title', '(', 'Length', ')'], 'Track artist', 'Country/Date', ['Label', '-', 'Catalog#'] ]
tooltipColumns added to artist-events: [ 'Event', '---', 'Type', 'Role', 'Location', ['Date', '(', 'Time', ')'], 'Cancelled' ]
tooltipColumns added to artist-relationships: [ 'Title', 'Artist', '---', 'Credited as', 'Attributes' ]
tooltipColumns added to label-releases: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Artist', '---', ['Format', '(', 'Tracks', ')'], 'Country/Date', 'Catalog#', 'Barcode' ]
tooltipColumns added to series-releases: [ 'MB-Name', 'italic:Comment', 'Artist', '---', ['Format', '(', 'Tracks', ')'], 'Country/Date', ['Label', '-', 'Catalog#'], 'Barcode' ]

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns (inline group): closing bracket ')' was always suppressed because the nextHas check found no following column after a trailing literal. Fix: '(' only requires a following column value; ')' only requires a preceding column value; separators ('-', '/', –, —) require both. Fixes 'Cassette ( 8' → 'Cassette (8)'.

_artTooltipCellText: raw JSON strings (e.g. '{"isrcs":[]}', '{"events":[...]}') from hidden data elements are now detected (value starts with '{' or '[') and suppressed, returning '' so the spec row is skipped.

_artTooltipCellText: multi-row cells (rendered by renderMultiRowCell as plain <ul><li>) are now handled by reading <li> text directly rather than the full textContent. This avoids the collapse-toggle noise ('▶N▤') that lives outside the <ul> in the <td>. Per-<li> anchor text is preferred (e.g. Label name).

_renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns (main column): the inline <span class='comment'> is no longer extracted and rendered when the main column is listed in tooltipColumns, because an explicit 'italic:Comment' spec always follows it. Fixes double italic-comment display on artist-recordings pages.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artTooltipCellText: fixed SyntaxError — Python-style \U0001F300 escape in JS regex is invalid. Corrected to JS \u{1F300}-\u{1FAFF} with the u flag.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artTooltipColIdx: fixed header matching — column <th> textContent is polluted with injected sort-toggle arrows (⇅▲▼), count badges, and stats icons (📊). The fix walks child nodes and stops at the first Element (all injected UI is wrapped in spans/buttons), falling back to splitting on the first ⇅ character. All column name lookups now resolve correctly.

_artTooltipCellText: fixed cell value extraction — raw textContent includes CAA inline placeholders, cache-hint spans, eraser remnants and other noise. The fix clones the cell, removes injected UI elements (same selector as _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL), then prefers the first anchor's text for linked columns (Artist, Label, …) or falls back to stripped plain text.

tooltipColumns for releasegroup-releases and artist-releases: switched to clean synthetic column names — 'MB-Name' (clean release title), 'italic:Comment' (disambiguation comment in italic, stored as separate synthetic column), 'Total Tracks', 'Country', 'Date' instead of the polluted 'Release', 'Country/Date', 'Tracks' raw columns.
tooltipColumns for artist-recordings: added 'italic:Comment' after 'Name' for the disambiguation comment.
_renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns: added 'italic:ColumnName' prefix support — column value is rendered in italic with surrounding parens, used for standalone disambiguation-comment columns.

sections.label: 🐛 Debug
items: Added console.log debug instrumentation to the bigbox strip tooltip pipeline: build-time _rowCellText data collection, mouseenter row resolution, _artTooltipColIdx header scan, _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns spec loop, and _addRow calls. Logs are unconditional (console.log, not gated by sa_enable_debug_logging) to diagnose the black-square tooltip regression.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artBuildImageLi (type badge pills): Tooltip now wired on img.mouseenter alongside the hover-preview listener, not on li.mouseenter. This ensures the tooltip fires exactly when (and only when) the user hovers the thumbnail image. The tooltip is positioned top-right of #mb-art-hover-preview when it is visible; falls back to top-right of the img itself when sa_caa_hover_preview is OFF or no bigSrc is available.

_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): Fixed row resolution in the mouseenter tooltip handler. Bigbox wrapper anchors live outside the table so closest('tr') always returned null. The fix reads data-caa-href / data-eaa-href (or href) from the wrapper and re-queries the live table tbody to find the matching anchor row each time the tooltip opens. This resolves the empty/black-square tooltip regression introduced in 9.99.302.

sections.label: 🐛 Fix
items: _artBuildImageLi (type badge pills): HTML tooltip now positions top-right of the hover-preview popup (#mb-art-hover-preview) when visible; falls back to top-right of the <li> element when the popup is hidden.
ShowAllEntityData_CHANGELOG.json: moved 9.99.302 entry from end of file to beginning (newest-first order).

sections.label: 📦 Feature
items: Added tooltipColumns feature key to pageType definitions for 'releasegroup-releases', 'artist-releases', and 'artist-recordings', specifying which columns to render in the bigbox tooltip and how to group inline tokens.

New helper _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns(tip, row, pageDef, table): generic tooltipColumns renderer. Supports: '---' divider, plain column names (main column rendered bold with italic comment below), and JS array groups for inline token rows (column values + literal punctuation, empty columns suppress adjacent literals).

New helpers _artTooltipColIdx, _artTooltipCellText, _artTooltipSplitComment: low-level building blocks for column-index resolution, cell text extraction, and title/comment splitting from <span class='comment'>.

_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): mouseenter handler now delegates to _renderBigboxTooltipFromColumns when the active page definition has tooltipColumns; falls back to the existing static data-attribute rendering for other page types.

sections.label: 📦 Feature
items: _artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): Enrich HTML tooltip with additional release row fields — Artist, Format (Tracks), Country/Date, Label (without comment) – Catalog#, Barcode — shown below the italic disambiguation comment when present.
_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): HTML tooltip is now always shown (unconditionally), not only when a disambiguation comment is present.
_artInitBigPics (bigbox strip): Plain non-HTML wrapper.title is no longer set; the HTML tooltip is the sole tooltip mechanism.

_artBuildImageLi (type badge pills): Replace plain img.title with an HTML tooltip on the <li> element, showing type pill labels in bold on line 1 and the italic comment (in parentheses) on line 2 when present.

sections.label: ✨ New Features
items: CAA/EAA expanded cell type badges redesigned as button-like pills: each image type string in the per-image <li> rows (visible after clicking the ▶ expand toggle on a CAA/EAA column cell) is now rendered as a separate grey pill button with a dark grey background (#c8c8c8), rounded corners, and bold text. Multi-type images (e.g. types=["Front","Back"]) display as two distinct pills separated by " / " text, making individual types visually distinct rather than a single concatenated label like "Front / Back".

CAA/EAA bigbox strip HTML tooltip with italic comment: the native browser title tooltip on bigbox wrapper anchors (the large artwork images in the strip above each table) has been replaced by a real HTML-based tooltip when the corresponding table row contains a <span class="comment"> disambiguation element. The tooltip is rendered using a singleton dark-panel div (#mb-art-bigbox-tooltip, styled to match the existing #mb-stat-tooltip aesthetic) and shows the entity name in normal weight on the first line and the comment text — inclusive of the opening ‘(’ and closing ‘)’ characters as found in the DOM — in italic on the second line. Falls back to the plain wrapper.title when no comment span is present.

sections.label: ✨ New Features
items: CAA/EAA expanded cell thumbnail tooltip: hovering over an image thumbnail inside an expanded in-cell <li> list (visible after clicking the ▶ toggle glyph on a CAA/EAA column) now shows a native browser tooltip containing the mb-caa-type-badge text (e.g. 'Front / Other') and, if present, the mb-caa-art-comment (e.g. 'fold‐out postcard sleeve, back'), separated by ' – '. The tooltip is applied only to per-image <li> rows (mb-caa-art-li-image); the summary <li> row (mb-caa-art-li-summary) and the existing popup image window are not affected.

Bigbox strip image tooltip enriched with disambiguation comment: the native title tooltip on each image in the 'big images stripe' (div.mb-caa-bigbox / div.mb-eaa-bigbox) above a table now includes the disambiguation comment from the corresponding table row's <span class="comment"> element (if present). Previously only the entity name (e.g. 'Greetings From Asbury Park, N.J.') was shown; now the full tooltip reads e.g. 'Greetings From Asbury Park, N.J. (Pitman pressing)' for CAA releases or '2025‐12‐14: The Stone Pony, Asbury Park, NJ, USA (Hungerthon Benefit)' for EAA events. The comment text is extracted from the live DOM of the matching <td>, stripped of leading/trailing whitespace, and re-wrapped with exactly one pair of parentheses.

sections.label: ✨ New Features
items: Filter history widget: both the global filter (single- and multi-table page types) and every sub-table-specific filter now have the same Pin (+) and History (🕑) controls as the 'Load Table Data' dialog. Pinned entries persist in GM storage (persistent-sa-hist-list) and recent entries are stored in the LRU list (lru-sa-hist-list), shared across all filter contexts. The dropdown supports keyboard navigation (↑↓ across both lists, Enter to apply, Esc to close / clear quick-filter), a live quick-filter input inside the panel, and Alt+H to toggle the panel from any filter input. Pressing Enter inside a filter input saves the current query to the LRU list.

Filter input redesign: the ✕ clear button for the global filter and for all sub-table filters is now positioned absolutely inside the input field (matching the 'Load Table Data' UI style). It is hidden while the field is empty and appears as soon as text is typed. The drag-to-resize handle (⋮) is retained as the flush-attached right-edge strip for the global filter and all sub-table filters. Code refactored: createFilterHistoryWidget() factory eliminates all duplication between filter contexts.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA type-badge and art-comment highlights still absent on multi-table page types after v9.99.297 — root cause identified: in multi-table (addCAA/addEAA) mode initCaaPics() is called from inside renderGroupedTable(), which runs after runFilter()'s groupedRows.forEach loop has completed. At that point _activeFilterHighlightCtx.colFilters holds the colFilters of the last group iterated, not the group that owns the cell being highlighted. All groups other than the last therefore received the wrong column filter set (usually empty), so _artHighlightArtCell was a silent no-op for every cell except those in the final sub-table. Fix: rewrote _artHighlightArtCell() to be fully self-sufficient — it no longer consults _activeFilterHighlightCtx at all. Instead it reads all filter state directly from the live DOM at the moment it is called: (1) global query from #mb-global-filter-input via stripFilterPrefix(), (2) isCaseSensitive / isRegExp from the module-level caseCheckbox / regexpCheckbox elements, (3) colFilters via getColFilters() called on artCell.closest('table') — the sub-table that actually owns the cell — with the per-sub-table Rx checkbox resolved via findH3ForTable(). This approach is immune to the timing race entirely: the DOM is always in the correct final state by the time _artEnrichIcon's async await chain resolves and calls _artHighlightArtCell.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA type-badge and art-comment highlights visible during typing but lost after debounce fires — v9.99.296 applied highlights inside _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() during the li build, but a second highlight wipe occurred after the async _artEnrichIcon() await chain settled: at that point _artBuildMultiRowArtCell ran again on the live DOM (rebuild path), creating fresh text nodes and wiping the spans that had been injected on the detached li before append. Root cause: _artHighlightImageLi() was called on detached li nodes before they were in the DOM, but the authoritative rebuild (on the already-live td, triggered by the async IDB/network await resolution) ran after, creating new plain text nodes. Fix: added _artHighlightArtCell(artCell, colIdx) helper that reads _activeFilterHighlightCtx at the latest possible moment (post-await, on the live td), snapshots the context to ensure all lis in the cell use identical regexps, then calls _artHighlightImageLi() on every .mb-caa-art-li-image li found in the cell. Wired into _artEnrichIcon() immediately after both _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() call sites: the main Tier-1/network path and the IDB Tier-2 early-return path. The per-li call inside _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() is retained as a defence-in-depth fast path for synchronous Tier-1 cache hits. Updated _artHighlightImageLi() to accept an optional ctxOverride parameter so _artHighlightArtCell() can pass a single context snapshot across all lis in one cell.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA type-badge and art-comment filter highlights wiped on art-cell rebuild — both CAA and EAA columns lost all mb-column-filter-highlight / mb-global-filter-highlight marks the instant _artBuildMultiRowArtCell rebuilt the <ul>/<li> structure. This always happens because initCaaPics/initEaaPics fire after renderFinalTable/renderGroupedTable, which is after testRowMatch() + highlightText() have already run. The EAA 'working' highlight example in the bug report was a rare IDB near-synchronous cache hit that painted before the wipe — both archives were equally broken. Additionally, highlight was absent entirely from .mb-caa-art-comment spans in both archives. Root cause: timing — the art-cell rebuild creates fresh text nodes for .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment, wiping any previously injected highlight spans. Fix: added module-level _activeFilterHighlightCtx (set by runFilter() before the filter pass, updated per sub-table in multi-table mode with correct per-table colFilters and isRegExp, cleared when no filters are active). Added _artHighlightImageLi(li, colIdx) helper that reads _activeFilterHighlightCtx and calls highlightCrossTag for the global query and for any column filter whose idx matches the art cell's column index. Called in _artBuildMultiRowArtCell() immediately after each _artBuildImageLi() call in both the first-build and rebuild paths, using the resolved artCell.cellIndex as _artColIdx. Covers .mb-caa-type-badge and .mb-caa-art-comment text nodes in both CAA and EAA columns.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Art-cell expand state lost on filter keystrokes — expanded CAA/EAA thumbnail cells (▼) collapsed back to ▶ the moment the user typed in any column filter field. Root cause: both runFilter() paths clone source rows before passing them to renderFinalTable/renderGroupedTable. In the multi-table (addCAA) path _stripTransientCellState() unconditionally resets every [data-caa-expand-btn] to 'collapsed' + ▶ on the clone. In the single-table (addEAA/addCAA) path allRows are detached source rows that never held live expand state in the first place, so cloneNode(true) produced clones that were always collapsed regardless of what the user had done. Fix: added _restoreArtExpandState(cloneRow) helper that reads from the authoritative expandedCells map (keyed 'rowIdx:colIdx', already synced on every toggle click) and replays expanded state — data-caa-expand-btn='expanded', ▼ glyph, correct tooltip, and li visibility — onto any clone whose row was expanded. Called in the multi-table path immediately after the _stripTransientCellState() loop, and in the single-table path inside the allRows.map() clone step (replacing the one-liner map with an explicit clone+restore block). No changes to expandedCells bookkeeping; the map was already correct.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: addCAA (multi-table) 'Cell structure' synthetic filter — selecting a syn-box entry showed zero rows. Root cause: runFilter() clones each row with cloneNode(true) and passes the clone to _stripTransientCellState() before testRowMatch(). _stripTransientCellState() was unconditionally removing all .mb-inline-art-sort-key spans from the clone, so getCleanColumnText() found no synthetic value and matched nothing. The single-table (addEAA) path was unaffected because renderFinalTable appends source rows directly without cloning them. Fix: removed the el.querySelectorAll('.mb-inline-art-sort-key').forEach(sk => sk.remove()) call from _stripTransientCellState(), replacing it with an explanatory comment. The span is idempotent — _artSetInlineSortKey() updates it in-place after every fetch cycle — so no explicit removal is ever needed anywhere.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeInlineArtItem / addCAA+addEAA 'Cell structure' filtering — definitive rewrite using invisible sort-key span (same pattern as the CAA/EAA icon columns). All previous attempts (v9.99.288–292) used bypass mechanisms (mbInlineArtMode, data-mb-stf-hidden, stfInput writes) that each failed in different ways. Root cause of all prior failures: every approach that stored the human-readable emoji label ('🖼️ front-image available' / '∅ NO front-image available') in a filter input caused getColFilters/testRowMatch or reapplyAllSubTableFilters to treat it as a real text filter, hiding all rows or failing to reset. The correct fix mirrors the mb-caa-sort-key pattern already used by the CAA/EAA icon columns, which work correctly: (1) Added _artSetInlineSortKey(ctx, td, loaded) helper — appends or updates an invisible <span class="mb-inline-art-sort-key" style="display:none"> inside td whose textContent is 'caa-inline-yes' (image loaded) or 'caa-inline-no' (404/error). The class is deliberately absent from _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL and the TreeWalker FILTER_REJECT set, so getCleanColumnText() picks it up via normal DOM text extraction — identical to how mb-caa-sort-key is read for CAA/EAA. (2) Wired _artSetInlineSortKey into all nine load/error paths in _artInitInlinePics: C1 (blob alive), C2 IDB success, C2 IDB catch (404), C2 native load success, C2 native load error, loadTask IDB success, loadTask IDB catch (404), loadTask native load success, loadTask native error. (3) Changed the syn-box cell counter from .mb-art-cache-hint-inline presence (cosmetic badge, absent when sa_rt_enable/sa_rt_show_inline are off) to reading .mb-inline-art-sort-key textContent — only settled cells (fetch completed) are counted; cells still in-flight are skipped. (4) Simplified makeInlineArtItem to call applyUniqVal('caa-inline-yes'/'caa-inline-no', table, colIndex) + closeUniqDrop() — same one-liner as makeArtItem uses for the CAA/EAA icon columns. No bypass mode, no DOM walk, no data-mb-stf-hidden markers. Because applyUniqVal writes the sort-key value to cfInp and fires 'input', the standard runFilter → testRowMatch → getCleanColumnText pipeline handles filtering. Escape, ✕, 'Clear ALL COLUMN filters', and 'Clear all filters' all reset the filter automatically without any special-casing. (5) Removed all dead mbInlineArtMode infrastructure: getColFilters bypass branch (isInlineArtFilter), testRowMatch continue branch, and all delete input.dataset.mbInlineArtMode cleanup calls scattered across Escape handler, ✕ onclick, input event listener, getColFilters empty-field check, applyUniqVal, clearColumnFiltersBtn, clearSubTableColumnFilters. (6) Added .mb-inline-art-sort-key span to the reset cleanup block (_artResetInlinePics area) alongside the existing .mb-caa-inline-ph / .mb-eaa-inline-ph removal so sort-key spans are cleared on re-render.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeInlineArtItem / addCAA+addEAA 'Cell structure' entries — definitive fix. All previous attempts (v9.99.288–291) failed because writing the entry label to cfInp.value caused runFilter() → testRowMatch() → getColFilters() to treat the emoji label as a real column filter text, matching nothing and hiding all rows. Root cause: getColFilters reads every .mb-col-filter-input value and passes it to testRowMatch as a text filter. Since no cell text matches '🖼️ front-image available', filteredRows=[] → renderFinalTable([]) → empty table immediately upon any filter interaction. Fix — mirrors the mbMultirowMode mechanism used by applyMultiRowStateFilter: (1) getColFilters: added an mbInlineArtMode bypass block that, when inp.dataset.mbInlineArtMode is set, adds { isInlineArtFilter:true } to the result without any regexp/text parsing — identical structure to the mbMultirowMode bypass. (2) testRowMatch: added isInlineArtFilter check that does 'continue' (always passes the row), so renderFinalTable/renderGroupedTable includes all source rows. The actual row visibility is managed by data-mb-stf-hidden markers. (3) makeInlineArtItem: sets cfInp.dataset.mbInlineArtMode='1' alongside cfInp.value=label; also sets boxShadow and clears borderColor/Width to match the active-filter visual style. (4) mbInlineArtMode cleared in: ✕ onclick, 'input' event listener, getColFilters empty-field check, applyUniqVal, Escape first-press handler, clearColumnFiltersBtn onclick, clearSubTableColumnFilters — exactly the same sites where mbMultirowMode is cleared. Escape on cfInp now also correctly clears the inline-art filter: first press calls delete e.target.dataset.mbInlineArtMode + runFilter(), which re-evaluates via testRowMatch (no bypass → all rows pass) and restores the view.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeInlineArtItem (addCAA + addEAA 'Cell structure' entries) — definitive fix for filter-reset failures affecting both archives. Previous attempts (v9.99.289–290) each solved one problem while introducing another: v9.99.289 wrote to stfInput — worked for addEAA (multi-table, STF container present) but broke for addCAA (single-table, findH3ForTable returns null → stfInput=null → no visible indication + no clear button); v9.99.290 also wrote to stfInput, which caused runFilter → reapplyAllSubTableFilters() to treat the label text (e.g. '🖼️ front-image available') as a real STF filter string — since no cell text matches the emoji label, all rows were hidden immediately after every renderGroupedTable rebuild, making it impossible to restore rows on multi-table pages for BOTH archives. Root cause of v9.99.290 failure: on multi-table pages runFilter calls reapplyAllSubTableFilters() after renderGroupedTable. reapplyAllSubTableFilters reads stfInput.value for each sub-table and calls applySubFilter if non-empty. The non-empty label value caused applySubFilter to immediately re-hide all rows. Definitive fix (three-part): (A) Write label to cfInp (column filter input for colIndex). cfInp exists on both single and multi-table pages. Writing it makes columnFiltersActive=true so updateFilterButtonsVisibility() shows 'Clear ALL COLUMN filters' / 'Clear all filters' buttons. Clearing them calls clearSubTableColumnFilters() → runFilter() which rebuilds/re-evaluates rows with cfInp=''; stf-hidden rows are new DOM nodes (multi) or re-shown (single) so all rows return. (B) Also apply data-mb-stf-hidden markers so the STF ✕ button can restore rows on single-table pages as a secondary clear path. (C) Do NOT write to stfInput. This prevents reapplyAllSubTableFilters from treating the label as an active STF text filter.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeInlineArtItem (addCAA 'Cell structure' entries) — clicking '🖼️ front-image available' or '∅ NO front-image available' correctly filtered rows (v9.99.289 fix worked for addEAA), but the ✕ clear button and 'Clear all filters' button were not shown for the addCAA case, making it impossible to restore all rows. Root cause: the click handler applied data-mb-stf-hidden markers and called updateFilterButtonsVisibility(), but updateFilterButtonsVisibility() checks stfActive = stfInput.value.trim() !== '' to decide whether to show the clear buttons. Since stfInput.value was empty (we never wrote to it), stfActive was false and the clear buttons stayed hidden. The addEAA case was unaffected because that page type has a different sub-table structure where the clear buttons were already visible from another active filter. Fix: write the entry label text (e.g. '🖼️ front-image available') into the STF filterInput element (located via findH3ForTable → '.mb-subtable-filter-container input[type=text]') and activate its border color with setSubTableFilterBorder(). No input event is dispatched — that would trigger the debounced applySubFilter listener which would immediately filter by the label text (matching nothing) and overwrite the manual stf-hidden state. updateFilterButtonsVisibility() (step 6) reads the now-non-empty stfInput.value and shows ✕ and 'Clear all filters'. When ✕ is clicked, the STF closure's clearBtn handler sets filterInput.value='' and calls clearSubFilter() which restores all data-mb-stf-hidden rows.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeInlineArtItem (unique-values dropdown, addCAA/addEAA 'Cell structure' entries) — clicking '🖼️ poster available' or '∅ NO poster available' correctly filtered the rows but there was no way to restore all rows afterwards. Root cause: the click handler wrote the entry label text (e.g. '🖼️ poster available') to the column filter input (cfInp.value) WITHOUT dispatching an input event. The filter system therefore had no knowledge of this text. On the next filter run (runFilter or applySubFilter), cfInp.value = '🖼️ poster available' was read as an active column filter, matched against real cell text (which never contains this string), and all rows were hidden. Additionally, the direct row.style.display='none' without a data-mb-stf-hidden marker caused applySubFilter to treat those rows as 'hidden by global/column filter' and skip restoring them. Fix: replaced the direct display:none approach with the same data-mb-stf-hidden mechanism used by applySubFilter. Step 1 restores any previously STF-hidden rows. Step 2 hides non-matching rows by setting data-mb-stf-hidden + display:none. Steps 3-5 update the h3 count badge/tooltip, sync the CAA/EAA bigbox strip, and update the h2 badge — identical to what applySubFilter does after filtering. The cfInp.value assignment is removed entirely: the standard STF clear button (✕), any subsequent applySubFilter/runFilter call, or pressing Escape on the sub-table filter input all restore the rows by removing data-mb-stf-hidden markers.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: openUniqDrop — 'Cell structure' synthetic entries for addCAA / addEAA inline-thumbnail columns (e.g. 'Release' on release-group pages, 'Event' on artist/event pages). When the 📊 unique-values dropdown is opened on a column that has .mb-caa-inline-ph or .mb-eaa-inline-ph spans injected by _artInitInlinePics, a new 'Cell structure' section is appended to the synBox with two entries: '🖼️ front-image available' (addCAA) or '🖼️ poster available' (addEAA) — rows where the inline placeholder span contains a .mb-art-cache-hint-inline child (meaning the thumbnail loaded successfully); '∅ NO front-image available' (addCAA) or '∅ NO poster available' (addEAA) — rows where the inline placeholder span exists but has no .mb-art-cache-hint-inline child (meaning no front image was found in the archive). Detection: the column is identified as an inline-art column by scanning visible tbody cells for .mb-caa-inline-ph or .mb-eaa-inline-ph spans; inlineArtType ('caa'/'eaa') is set on first match. The two counts (inlineArtYes, inlineArtNo) are computed in the same tbody scan loop. Clicking an entry applies a direct DOM show/hide pass on all rows (respecting data-mb-stf-hidden) and writes the entry label into the column filter input so the active filter is visible and clearable. New helper makeInlineArtItem() mirrors the existing makeArtItem() / makeSynItem() pattern. The section is appended after all other synBox sections (collapsable / CAA-sort-key / empty) and before the synDivider.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML — the stacked two-row label spans (font-size:0.9em, line-height:1) produced a button height of 2 × 0.9em = 1.8em, which exceeds the natural height of single-line inline-block neighbours in the h2 filter bar (toggle-highlight, clear-filters, etc.). Context fixes on filterContainer (font-size:1rem, line-height:1) could not resolve this because the h2 filter row height is determined by the flat-text single-line buttons, and a two-row stacked button is always taller than a single-line button at the same font-size. The sub-table h3 button (same HTML) fits in its row because h3 rows are naturally taller (larger padding, larger inherited font). Fix: reduce span font-size from 0.9em to 0.72em in makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML. Two stacked rows at 0.72em line-height:1 = 1.44em total, matching the effective height of a single-line button at ~1.4 line-height. The text remains legible and the h3 button (where there is more vertical room) also benefits from the smaller label text.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: mb-col-collapse-all-btn height — v9.99.285 fixed font-size inheritance but the button was still taller than the h3 equivalent because the h2 element also propagates a larger inherited line-height to the filterContainer and its inline-flex children. An inline-flex button's rendered height is driven by both the font-size and the line-height of its content; resetting only font-size was insufficient. Fix: add line-height:1 to the filterContainer span alongside font-size:1rem. Together these two properties fully neutralise both h2 inherited values, making every child button — including the global collapse button — render at exactly the same height as the identical buttons in the h3 sub-table context.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: mb-col-collapse-all-btn (global multi-row collapse button in h2) — correct fix for the height mismatch vs. the per-sub-table button (mb-subtable-collapse-btn in h3). The v9.99.284 attempt adding line-height:1 to the outer button did not fully resolve the issue because the root cause is a font-size inheritance mismatch, not line-height. Root cause: the filterContainer span (mb-filter-container) is appended as a child of the h2 element and inherits its large font-size (browser default h2 = 2em ≈ 32px). All child buttons use font-size:0.8em, which resolves to 0.8 × 32px ≈ 25.6px inside the h2 — roughly twice the size of the same 0.8em button in the h3 context (where h3 ≈ 1.17em × 16px ≈ 18.7px → 0.8 × 18.7px ≈ 15px). This causes the global collapse button, global filter input, Cc/Rx/Ex labels, and all other filterContainer children to render at disproportionate sizes relative to their h3 equivalents. Fix: add font-size:1rem to the filterContainer span's CSS. 1rem pins the effective font-size to the root/html element's font-size (typically 16px) regardless of the h2 ancestor, matching the effective size context of the h3-hosted buttons. The global collapse button style reverts to the standard uiFilterBarBtnCSS() baseline (removing the workaround line-height:1 from v9.99.284).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: mb-col-collapse-all-btn (global multi-row collapse button in h2) — the button was taller than the equivalent per-sub-table button (mb-subtable-collapse-btn in h3) because the global button inherited a taller line-height from the h2 context, causing the two stacked flex-column label groups to expand the inline-flex height. The sub-table button fits perfectly because h3 elements carry a shorter inherited line-height. Fix: the global button now uses explicit matching CSS (font-size, padding, border, background, transition — identical to the sub-table button) and adds line-height:1 directly on the outer button element. The makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML two-column flex-span layout is preserved unchanged; only the outer button's line-height is corrected. Reverts the v9.99.283 flat-text approach which went in the wrong direction. NOTE: the line-height:1 approach in this version did not fully solve the problem; see v9.99.285 for the correct fix.

sections.label: 🔍 Code Analysis Notes
items: CAA/EAA column art-cell collapse-state and highlight behaviour (reported as bugs 2a–2c) — after deep code analysis these appear to be working as designed: (2a) _artBuildMultiRowArtCell already preserves wasExpanded across rebuilds; applySubFilter hides rows by CSS only and never touches the [data-caa-expand-btn] state. (2b) applySubFilter uses raw cell.textContent for matching (includes display:none li-image text), and highlightCrossTag walks all text nodes including those inside hidden li-image rows — highlights are injected and become visible when the user expands the cell. (2c) Both CAA and EAA use identical _artBuildImageLi structure; type badges live in li-image rows for both archives. The observed CAA/EAA highlight difference is state-dependent: if the EAA cell was already expanded when the filter ran, its type-badge span was visible and the highlight appeared; the collapsed CAA cell hid the same highlight. No code change required — the feature is correct when cells are expanded.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel — filter-modifier checkbox states (Cc / Rx / Ex) are now shown visually as compact inline pills wherever a filter value is displayed. (1) Global Statistics row '🔍 Global filter': after the quoted filter string, any checked Cc / Rx / Ex checkboxes from the global filter bar are rendered as small blue-bordered pills (e.g. 'Cc' for case-sensitive, 'Rx' for regexp, 'Ex' for exclude-matches). Only active (checked) flags are shown; if none are checked nothing extra is rendered. (2) Table Details header row Col 3 'Active filter': the same pill rendering is applied after the STF or global filter value. When the filter source is the sub-table filter (STF), the per-sub-table Cc/Rx/Ex checkboxes (id pattern mb-stf-*-case/rx/ex-checkbox) are read; when the source is the global filter, the global filter Cc/Rx/Ex checkboxes (mb-global-filter-*-checkbox) are used. New helpers: _gfFlags (captured alongside _gfRaw), _stfFlags (captured per table alongside _stfInp), _filterFlagsHtml(flags) (renders the pills).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: h3 row-count badge on multi-table pages — sub-table filter (STF) displayed the wrong count in the h3 header span. Example: 'Promotion release' sub-table has 4 rows; filtering with 'jp' hides 1 row leaving 3 visible, but the badge showed '(4)' instead of '(3 of 4)'. The tooltip (data-mbtt) was already correct — it correctly read 3 out of 4 — so this was purely a textContent mismatch. Two independent sites were broken: (1) _updateSubTableH3Tooltip — called by applySubFilter via updateSubTableRowCount() on every STF keystroke — only updated data-mbtt (the hover tooltip) and never touched textContent. So the visible '(N)' text was always left at whatever renderGroupedTable last wrote, which is group.rows.length (the globally-filtered count, ignoring STF). Fixed by computing '(_visible === _total) ? (N) : (N of M)' and assigning to _countStat.textContent at the top of _updateSubTableH3Tooltip, before the tooltip block. (2) renderGroupedTable (the re-render path triggered by global filter / column filter) — the else-if branch that updates the existing h3 during filtering used group.rows.length as the numerator. group.rows.length is the row array passed into renderGroupedTable, which reflects only the global+column filter pass — not any active STF. When STF is active, some of those rows have data-mb-stf-hidden set and are DOM-hidden, so group.rows.length overstates the visible count. Fixed by querying tbody tr elements and filtering to r.style.display !== 'none' to get the true DOM-visible count, and reading table.dataset.mbTotalRows (set during initial render) for the unfiltered total instead of groupedRows[].rows.length.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artRetryTable (non-IDB path) — CAA column, bigbox stripe, and Release-column inline thumbnails all showed ⚠️ 'Timing unavailable' after pressing the retry button when sa_art_idb_enable=false.
