Two independent root causes fixed: (1) RT buffer exhausted: after a full initial page load the Performance Resource Timing buffer (browser default: 150 entries) is full. Cache-busted retry requests produce new entries that are silently dropped; performance.getEntriesByName() returns an empty array for every lookup → getResourceTimingHint returns 'unavailable' → ⚠️. Fixed by calling performance.clearResourceTimings() as a new step 5 in _artRetryTable, immediately before steps 6/7/8 queue their network requests. This makes room in the buffer so all retry requests get a proper RT entry and getResourceTimingHint returns 🔵 'network'. (2) CSS selector bug in done-marker and multiBuilt-marker clearing: both 'td[data-caaInlineDone]' (step 7) and 'td[data-caaMultiBuilt]' (step 3) used the raw camelCase dataset property name in a CSS attribute selector. The dataset API converts camelCase to kebab-case ('caaInlineDone' → attribute 'data-caa-inline-done'; 'caaMultiBuilt' → 'data-caa-multi-built'), so the selectors were looking for the wrong attribute names and never found any elements. For inlineDone this meant the 4 inline cells were skipped as 'done' instead of being retried. Fixed by replacing both querySelectorAll calls with JS forEach/filter loops that test td.dataset[ctx.inlineDoneAttr] / td.dataset[ctx.multiBuiltAttr] directly — immune to the camelCase↔kebab-case mismatch.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artInitInlinePics / _artRetryTable — Release-column inline thumbnail cache-hint overlays showed 🟢 'memory cache' after pressing the retry button instead of 🔵 'network'. Root cause: step 6 was removed from _artRetryTable in v9.99.278 (to avoid RT-entry competition), so inline thumbnails were not retried at all — they kept showing the previous hint. For filter re-renders (Case C1, live blob) the 🟢 hint is correct. But after retry the user expects 🔵 like bigbox and CAA column. Root cause of the original v9.99.277 step-6 bug: _artInitInlinePics called _artFetchCachedImage (GM_xhr) for the cache-busted URL. Because _artIdbMemCache is keyed by normalised URL and Date.now() returns the same millisecond for all three retry functions, the GM_xhr fetch stored the blob under the same URL that the native img.src requests use. Subsequent same-URL lookups returned fromMemory=true → 🟢. Fix: added '&& !cacheBust' to the IDB branch check in loadTask inside _artInitInlinePics. When cacheBust=true, loadTask always uses the native img.src path (same as _artInitBigPics and _artInitSmallPics), so the browser creates a real PerformanceResourceEntry for the https:// URL and getResourceTimingHint returns 🔵 'network'. Step 6 is re-added to _artRetryTable: clear per-cell inlineDoneAttr markers and call _artInitInlinePics(ctx, true, table). No RT-entry competition occurs because all three functions now use the native img.src path for cacheBust=true requests — no GM_xhr runs that could consume a PerformanceResourceEntry ahead of the native img.onload handler.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artRetryTable — regression introduced in v9.99.277: step 6 (the _artInitInlinePics call with cacheBust=true) caused the CAA column icons and bigbox strip to revert to ⚠️ 'Timing unavailable' after pressing the retry button. Root cause: all three functions (_artInitBigPics, _artInitSmallPics, _artInitInlinePics) are called synchronously in the same JS microtask, so Date.now() returns the same millisecond value for all, generating identical cache-bust query strings (e.g. ?_cb=1711065600123). _artInitBigPics with cacheBust=true uses the native img.src path (not IDB); it relies on getResourceTimingHint(this.src) in img.onload to detect 'network'. _artInitInlinePics with the same cache-bust URL uses _artFetchCachedImage (GM_xhr), which downloads the same resource from the redirect target (archive.org). The GM_xhr fetch consumes the PerformanceResourceEntry for that URL. When the native img.onload fires for bigpics/smallpics and calls getResourceTimingHint, the RT buffer entry has already been consumed, so it returns 'unavailable' → ⚠️. Fix: step 6 removed from _artRetryTable. The C1/C2 inline hint fixes from v9.99.277 already ensure correct hints during normal filter re-renders (which cover the vast majority of cases). Inline thumbnails in the addFeature column (Release/Event) after a retry will show whatever the previous hint was rather than updating — acceptable given that the retry correctly shows 🔵 on the CAA column and bigbox stripe.

_onBigLoaded (in _artInitBigPics) — structural bug introduced in v9.99.275 when the 'else if (bigMemoryHit)' branch was added to the cache-hint block. A stray extra closing brace '}' after the if/else-if/else chain prematurely closed the outer 'if (sa_rt_enable && sa_rt_show_bigbox)' block. The lines that update the emoji and hint span ('const emoji = cacheStatusEmoji(hintStatus)' through 'wrapper.appendChild(hintBadge)') were unreachable dead code outside _onBigLoaded, so the bigbox overlay was never applied on any path (idb, memory, or network). This is why bigbox and CAA column hints showed ⚠️ since v9.99.275. Fixed by removing the stray brace.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artInitInlinePics / Release-column inline cache-hint overlays — always showed ⚠️ 'no data' despite images being served from IDB memory cache or disk. Three independent root causes fixed: (1) Case C1 (same-session blob restore): _artInitInlinePics detected a live blob URL and returned early without updating the .mb-art-cache-hint-inline span — the span inherited whatever stale emoji was cloneNode'd from the previous render. Fixed by applying hintStatus='memory' to the existing (or newly created) hint span before returning. (2) Case C2 IDB path (stale-blob refetch): the .then() callback only destructured { objectUrl }, discarding fromIdb and fromMemory, and never touched the hint span at all. Fixed by destructuring { objectUrl, fromIdb, fromMemory } and applying the correct hint (idb / memory / getResourceTimingHint fallback) after stamping the done marker. (3) _artRetryTable: only called _artInitBigPics and _artInitSmallPics with cacheBust=true — inline thumbnails in the addFeature column (e.g. 'Release') were never retried, leaving their ⚠️ hint overlay permanently stale. Fixed by clearing the per-cell inlineDoneAttr markers and calling _artInitInlinePics(ctx, true, table) as a new step 6. _artInitInlinePics gains cacheBust=false and targetTable=null parameters: cacheBust appends a timestamp query string to the imgurl (same convention as _artInitBigPics/_artLoadIcon); targetTable limits iteration to the specified table element (used by _artRetryTable to avoid re-processing unrelated tables). On cacheBust=true the C1 early-return is bypassed so the retry path forces a genuine network refetch instead of reusing the stale blob.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: showExportDialog — 'Export failed: escAttr is not defined' crash when opening the settings configuration save dialog. Root cause: the h3 title fix in v9.99.275 used escAttr() to HTML-escape the customTitle parameter, but escAttr is a local const defined only inside the VZ_MBLibrary settings modal closure — it is not in scope inside showExportDialog. Fixed by inlining the three-replace HTML-escape expression directly in the template literal instead of calling escAttr.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Cache-hint indicators (🟢/🟡/🔵/🗄️/⚠️) — all four hint-decision sites (_artLoadIcon/_onIconLoaded, _artInitBigPics/_onBigLoaded, ergInjectCaaInlineThumbnails, _artInitInlinePics IDB path) always showed ⚠️ 'Timing unavailable' for in-session memory cache hits even though _artFetchCachedImage returned fromMemory=true / wasMemoryHit=true. Root cause: every site had an 'if (wasIdbHit)' branch that synthesised 'idb' directly, but the memory-hit case fell through to the 'else' branch which called getResourceTimingHint(blobUrl). Blob: URLs produced by URL.createObjectURL() are never recorded by the browser's Resource Timing API, so getResourceTimingHint always returned { status: 'unavailable' } → ⚠️. Fix: added 'else if (wasMemoryHit / fromMemory / bigMemoryHit)' branch in all four sites that synthesises 'memory' directly, mirroring the existing idb branch. _artInitBigPics/_onBigLoaded also required: (1) adding bigMemoryHit parameter to the _onBigLoaded function signature (default false), and (2) destructuring fromMemory from the _artFetchCachedImage promise result and forwarding it as the third argument to _onBigLoaded.

showExportDialog / _saveSettingsConfig — settings configuration export dialog showed the wrong h3 title '📥 Export Table Data — Settings Configuration' instead of '📥 Save Settings Configuration'. Fixed by adding an optional title parameter to showExportDialog (destructured as customTitle, default null); when provided it replaces the default 'Export Table Data — {format}' heading. _saveSettingsConfig now passes title: 'Save Settings Configuration'.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _saveSettingsConfig — reuses showExportDialog (the same dialog infrastructure used by the CSV/JSON/Org-Mode export buttons) instead of calling triggerStandardDownload directly. The user now sees the familiar draggable filename-editor dialog with a FILE METADATA block showing script ID, script version, schema version, export timestamp, and settings key count. The default filename (SCRIPT_ID-config-TIMESTAMP.json) is pre-filled and editable. Alt+S confirms, Escape cancels, clicking outside closes. The custom config meta block replaces the generic table-data block by writing to #sa-ed-meta-block immediately after showExportDialog returns (the dialog is appended synchronously).

_buildConfigJson — number-type settings are now explicitly coerced via Number() before JSON serialization. Root cause: after a RESET → SAVE cycle in the settings dialog, the library reads all input values via input.value (which is always a string) and stores them with GM_setValue. On the next read, Lib.settings[key] returns '250' (string) instead of 250 (number), causing the exported JSON to contain "sa_caa_small_img_size": "250" instead of the correct 250. Fix: _buildConfigJson now checks schemaCfg.type === 'number' and applies Number(live) before inserting into settingsBlock. Same coercion applied to checkbox type (string 'true'/'false' → boolean) for consistency. _loadSettingsConfig already coerced correctly; no change needed there.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Settings dialog — two new buttons injected into the footer: '💾 Save configuration' and '📂 Load configuration'. Save exports all current settings as a versioned JSON file (filename: <script-id>-config-<timestamp>.json). Load imports a previously exported file, applies matching keys via GM_setValue, reports a summary (applied / unknown / invalid counts), and reloads the page identically to the native SAVE button. The JSON format uses 2-space indent so each setting occupies its own line, making two exported files trivially diffable with any text diff tool. Format: { '_meta': { schema_version, script_version, exported_at, script_id }, 'settings': { key: value, … } }. On import: unknown keys (not in configSchema) are silently skipped; schema_version mismatch triggers a console warning but import proceeds; checkbox values are coerced from string 'true'/'false' to boolean; number values are coerced from string. All three call sites of Lib.showSettings() (⚙️ toolbar button, Ctrl+, shortcut, prefix-mode ',') are replaced with openSettingsWithConfigButtons() which arms a one-shot MutationObserver before calling showModal() so the buttons are injected immediately when the overlay is inserted into the DOM. Leftover debug console.log statements in the settings button onclick handler are also removed.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel — '🖼️ Total ${artKey} front artwork count' renamed to '🖼️ Total table rows with ${artKey} artwork'; per-sub-table column 5 header label 'Filtered CAA/EAA artwork count' renamed to 'Rows with CAA/EAA artwork'; comment text updated to match.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — artwork count was always equal to the total number of table rows. Root cause: all three count sources (snapshot capture in _captureStatsSnapshot, per-sub-table _caaCount in _tableData, and the live-DOM fallback in showStats) used _artCountLinks() which counts entity anchor links in tbody — one per row by construction, not the number of rows that actually have confirmed front artwork. Fixed by replacing all three with reads from the toggle button count badge (.mb-caa-toggle-count / .mb-eaa-toggle-count), which is incremented once per successful img.onload in _artInitBigPics and therefore reflects only rows with real artwork. When loading has not yet completed the badge is 0 and the stats panel correctly shows '—' instead of a premature count. _showCaaCompletionToast live-refresh extended to also update per-sub-table '—' cells in Table Details cards: on toast fire it iterates all table.tbl elements, reads each per-table button badge, locates the matching stats card by positional index, and replaces the col-5 placeholder with the final count. The global '#mb-stats-art-count-val' placeholder update path is unchanged.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: getCleanColumnText — replaced the TreeWalker FILTER_REJECT approach with a guaranteed clone-and-strip strategy. Root cause of the v9.99.270 non-fix: in some browser / Tampermonkey execution environments, returning FILTER_REJECT from acceptNode() for an ELEMENT node does NOT reliably prune that element's TEXT-NODE children from the walk when SHOW_TEXT is also present in the whatToShow mask — the spec says children of a FILTER_REJECT node should be skipped, but this is not universally observed in practice. As a result, the emoji text nodes inside .mb-art-cache-hint-inline (which is a child of .mb-caa-inline-ph, which was FILTER_REJECT'd) were still being visited and included in the extracted text. Fix: at the start of getCleanColumnText, when the element contains any of the known decorative/cache spans (tested with a cheap querySelector), the element is shallow-cloned with cloneNode(true) and all matching elements are removed from the clone via querySelectorAll+remove before the TreeWalker is run. The clone pass is gated behind the querySelector so ordinary cells (no inline thumbnails) pay zero cloning cost. The FILTER_REJECT list is retained as a belt-and-braces secondary guard. The _CLEAN_STRIP_SEL constant documents the full set of stripped selectors. The artSearchUl mbArtSearch lookup continues to query the ORIGINAL element (dataset attributes are preserved by cloneNode, so this is equivalent, but querying the live element is canonical).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: getCleanColumnText — added mb-art-cache-hint-col-wrap to the TreeWalker FILTER_REJECT set. This wrapper spans sits inside CAA/EAA-bearing cells (e.g. the Release column on release-group pages) and contains two children: the cache-status emoji span (.mb-art-cache-hint-col, text: 🟢/🟡/🔵/⚠️) and the count badge span (.mb-caa-count-badge / .mb-eaa-count-badge, text: the image count number). Both are display metadata, not part of the real cell value. Before this fix, getCleanColumnText walked into the wrapper and included the emoji and count in the text used for the unique-values dropdown — causing entries like '5 🔵 The Dark Side of the Moon' to appear instead of just 'The Dark Side of the Moon'. Rejecting the entire wrapper with FILTER_REJECT (which prunes the subtree, not just the wrapper node itself) removes both the emoji and the count badge from unique-value collection, column filter matching, and sort keys in one step.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: configSchema (UI APPEARANCE) — sa_ui_thead_th_synthetic_bg renamed to sa_ui_thead_th_extracted_bg; label updated to 'Extracted column header background color'; description updated to clarify it covers columns injected via columnExtractors and extractMainColumn (Country, Date, Place, Area, MB-Name, Comment, CAA, EAA, Video, Cancelled, Primary Alias, etc.). New setting sa_ui_thead_th_derived_bg added (label: 'Derived column header background color', default: #c8c8b0 — warm sandy beige, same brightness as #b8c8b8 but warm-hued) for headers injected via syntheticColumnExtractors (DD, MM, YYYY, Day, Month derived from Date; Event-Type, Event-Date, etc. derived from Comment). Both settings also document their role as row-tint colors in the Statistics panel Table Details section.

cleanupHeaders — activeSyntheticColumnExtractors loop now applies sa_ui_thead_th_derived_bg (headerDerivedBgColor) to derived <th> cells; activeColumnExtractors and extractMainColumn loops continue to use sa_ui_thead_th_extracted_bg (headerBgColor). The two synthetic tiers are now visually distinct in the rendered table header.

Statistics panel Table Details — per-column rows are now tinted by origin type: extracted columns (#b8c8b855 by default), derived columns (#c8c8b055 by default), original columns (no tint — inherits default row background). The _tableData column descriptor replaces the binary isSynth boolean with a three-way colType string ('original' | 'extracted' | 'derived'), classified first by mb-original/extracted/derived-extracted CSS class and falling back to bg-color / dataset.colName heuristic for legacy cells. Column-name <strong> accent color is applied to both extracted and derived columns (colType !== 'original'). All snapshot capture and live-DOM fallback paths updated to use sa_ui_thead_th_extracted_bg / sa_ui_thead_th_derived_bg instead of the old sa_ui_thead_th_synthetic_bg.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: cleanupHeaders — three new CSS classes are now stamped on every <th> in the rendered header row to precisely classify each column's origin: (1) mb-original-column: all headers that were present on the original MusicBrainz page after optional column removal (stamped once before any injection, idempotent on re-renders); (2) mb-extracted-column: headers injected by activeColumnExtractors (columnExtractors in pageDefinitions) and by the extractMainColumn feature (MB-Name, Comment); (3) mb-derived-extracted-column: headers injected by activeSyntheticColumnExtractors (syntheticColumnExtractors in pageDefinitions, second-pass derivation from extracted columns). These classes replace the old heuristic that tested th.dataset.colName or background-color to identify synthetics; a legacy fallback for cells from earlier sessions is retained.

Statistics panel — 'Global Statistics' section: replaced the single '✨ Total synthetic columns' row with two new rows: '🔀 Total extracted columns' (mb-extracted-column count) and '🔁 Total derived columns' (mb-derived-extracted-column count). The existing '🔲 Total original columns' (mb-original-column count) and '⬛ Total columns' (sum) rows are preserved. The snapshot capture now stores extractedCols and derivedCols separately; the live-DOM fallback in showStats also counts three buckets by class. A legacy heuristic (dataset.colName / backgroundColor comparison) handles cells stamped before this version.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _buildH2CountTooltip — SyntaxError 'Unexpected token ?' crash introduced in v9.99.266. Root cause: the str_replace that updated _colHTML in _buildH2CountTooltip accidentally consumed the following blank line AND the entire 'const _abs = (absoluteTotal !== null && absoluteTotal !== totalCount)' declaration, leaving only the dangling ternary continuation '? absoluteTotal : null;' as a bare statement — an unconditional SyntaxError at script load time. Fixed by restoring the deleted const _abs declaration on its own line immediately after _colHTML.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _buildH2CountTooltip — 3-tier branch (_abs !== null, multi-table with global filter active): the global filter expression pill was missing after the 'global' label. The branch already computed _gfVal but never emitted _mbttSpan(_gfVal, 'gf'). Fixed: '…after <global> filter;' → '…after <global> filter <sess>;', matching the format of every other filter-bearing branch in the function.

_buildH2CountTooltip and _updateSubTableH3Tooltip — column names in the _colHTML summary string were plain quoted text ('Length', 'Comment', …). Added new helper _mbttColName(name) which renders the column name with its text color set to the cf background color (sa_column_filter_highlight_bg, default #add8e6), creating a visual link between each column label and its adjacent filter-expression pill. Both tooltip builders now call _mbttColName(f.colName) instead of the plain '${f.colName}' literal. The quotes are emitted by _mbttColName itself so the visual appearance ('Name':value) is preserved.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _buildH2CountTooltip — 3-tier branch (absoluteTotal present, i.e. multi-table pages with a global filter active): the second and third numeric values in the tooltip string 'There are <pill>N</pill> rows visible; M after global filter; T total unfiltered' were plain unstyled numbers and the word 'global' was unstyled plain text. Fixed: ${totalCount} → _cN(totalCount), ${_abs} → _cN(_abs), and 'global' → _mbttLabel('global','gf'). All three values are now rendered as colored pills consistent with every other branch in the function and with the equivalent H3 tooltip strings.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: sa_ui_row_count_color / sa_ui_row_count_bg — descriptions were wrong: both settings were documented as controlling the (N of M) span on H2/H3 header lines. In fact they exclusively control the numeric count pills rendered inside the rich hover tooltips (the colored <span> elements produced by _mbttCount, e.g. the highlighted '109' in 'There are 109 rows total…'). Labels updated to 'Row-count stat tooltip: count text/background color'; descriptions now clearly state that the H2/H3 span itself is not affected by these settings and always inherits color/background from its parent header element. sa_ui_row_count_bg default restored to '#e8e8e8' (was incorrectly changed to 'transparent' in v9.99.263).

mb-row-count-stat CSS rule — previous versions injected color and background from sa_ui_row_count_color/bg into the .mb-row-count-stat span on H2/H3 lines, overriding the natural header background and producing a grey pill that clashed with colored H2/H3 backgrounds. Fixed by removing color, background, padding, and border-radius from the CSS rule entirely; the span now carries only font-weight:bold and margin-left:8px and inherits everything else from the parent H2/H3 element. The sa_ui_row_count_color/bg settings continue to apply inside _mbttCount (tooltip pills only).

_mbttCount — removed the 'transparent fallback' workaround introduced in v9.99.263 (it compensated for the now-reverted transparent default of sa_ui_row_count_bg). _mbttCount once again reads sa_ui_row_count_bg directly with a '#e8e8e8' JS-level fallback, matching the original solid-pill behavior for tooltip count numbers.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: updateH2Count (place/events and other single-table pages with EAA/CAA) — row-count stat span was rendered AFTER the EAA art-toggle and retry buttons instead of immediately after the h2 title text. Root cause: the re-anchoring pass at the end of updateH2Count used the selector ':scope > .mb-global-art-toggle-btn, :scope > [id$="-global-retry"]', which only matches the global art buttons created by _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton. On single-table pages (tableMode !== 'multi') _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton exits early, so no global button is ever created; the per-table buttons (.mb-eaa-art-toggle-btn / .mb-caa-art-toggle-btn and their retry counterparts [id^="mb-eaa-toggle-btn-retry-"] / [id^="mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-"]) are placed as direct h2 children by _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton/_artCreateOrUpdateRetryButton and are not matched by the old selector. After removing and reinserting the count-stat span the floating per-table buttons ended up after it. Fixed by extending the ':scope >' selector to also include .mb-eaa-art-toggle-btn, .mb-caa-art-toggle-btn, [id^="mb-eaa-toggle-btn-retry-"], and [id^="mb-caa-toggle-btn-retry-"]; the ':scope >' (direct-child) guard keeps multi-table per-table buttons (which live inside h3 elements) from being incorrectly matched.

mb-row-count-stat background color — the (N of M) counter span in H2/H3 headers was always rendered with a grey (#e8e8e8) pill background, regardless of the H2/H3 header's own background color, making it visually inconsistent. Root cause: sa_ui_row_count_bg defaulted to '#e8e8e8' and the CSS rule unconditionally applied padding and border-radius. Fixed by (1) changing the sa_ui_row_count_bg default to 'transparent' so the span inherits the header background; (2) suppressing the padding/border-radius in the CSS rule when the setting is transparent (a non-zero padding on a transparent pill creates an invisible but layout-affecting box); (3) updating _mbttCount so that when sa_ui_row_count_bg is transparent it falls back to '#e8e8e8' for the count pills rendered inside rich tooltips — the tooltip overlay has its own dark background and a transparent pill there would be invisible. Users who previously relied on the grey pill can restore it by setting sa_ui_row_count_bg to '#e8e8e8' in UI Appearance settings.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: configSchema (EXPORT) — new `sa_export_include_sort_state` (default `true`): when enabled the active sort-state glyph (▲ or ▼, captured from `.sort-icon-btn.sort-icon-active` before the button is removed) is appended to the exported column header text (e.g. 'Length ▼'). When disabled all glyphs and priority superscripts are stripped, leaving only the plain column name. Applied in `_exportCleanHeaderText` used by all four exporters.

configSchema (UI APPEARANCE) — `sa_ui_row_count_color` and `sa_ui_row_count_bg` now correctly colorize the numeric row counts (e.g. 6 and 47) inside row-count tooltips via a new `_mbttCount(n)` helper, rather than the stat span container itself. `_nR` (h2) and `_nRows` (h3) now call `_mbttCount` so the row counts in 'There are N rows' are wrapped in a colored pill. Bare count variables (`totalCount`, `_total`, `_denom`) used after 'from' in both `_buildH2CountTooltip` and `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip` are also wrapped via a local `_cN(n)` alias.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — 'Table Details' second toggle (▶ between 🗃️ and label): was querying `table.tbl .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn` in the live data tables (the multi-row cell column-header buttons) instead of the `[data-col-expanded]` `colToggleBar` divs inside each stats card. Fixed to iterate `body.querySelectorAll('[data-col-expanded]')`, check whether each bar's `nextElementSibling` has `dataset.colDetailOf`, and directly update `bar.dataset.colExpanded`, `_detail.style.display`, and the icon span's `textContent` — no reliance on `.click()` or external DOM scope.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: configSchema (UI APPEARANCE) — two new color-picker settings for the `.mb-row-count-stat` span (the `(N of M)` counter in H2/H3 headers): `sa_ui_row_count_color` (text color, default dark black `#111111`) and `sa_ui_row_count_bg` (background color, default light grey `#e8e8e8`). The CSS rule is updated to use these values and now also applies a small `padding` and `border-radius` so the pill shape is visible at any color.

Tooltip colorization — added `_mbttLabel(word, type)` helper that wraps a filter-type label word with the same pill colors as `_mbttSpan` uses for filter expressions (using the same CSS custom-property values). The tooltip strings in both `_buildH2CountTooltip` and `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip` now render the words 'global' (gf — yellow bg / red text), 'sub-table' (stf — green bg / black text), and 'column level' / 'column' (cf — blue bg / red text) in their characteristic filter colors, making it visually obvious which filter type is being referred to.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize — Event column (and any column with a `<a class='wrap-anywhere'>` anchor) still wrapped even after the previous two fixes (sampling removal + auto table layout). Root cause confirmed: `el.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'` and `el.style.overflowWrap = 'normal'` are normal inline style declarations and are beaten by any `!important` rule in an external stylesheet (MusicBrainz CSS uses `.wrap-anywhere { overflow-wrap: anywhere; }` which in some build variants carries `!important`, and similarly for `word-break`). An `!important` inline declaration — set via `el.style.setProperty(name, value, 'important')` — wins the cascade over ALL author stylesheet rules including those with `!important`. All text-wrap property assignments in both `toggleAutoResizeColumns` and `toggleSubTableAutoResize` (for the `measureDiv` container itself, all descendant element loops, and all `liClone` loops) have been changed to `setProperty(..., 'important')`. This guarantees that no external CSS rule can reintroduce wrapping during measurement.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: pageDefinitions — `area-events` and `artist-events`: added 'Place', 'Area', 'Country' to `collapsableColumns` so the three synthetic columns produced by the new multi-row-aware `splitLocation` extractor participate in the collapse/expand toggle infrastructure.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize — Event column (and any column containing an inline-block element such as `.mb-eaa-inline-ph` followed by a `<a class='wrap-anywhere'>` anchor) wrapped prematurely after auto-resize. Root cause: `table-layout:fixed` constrains every column to exactly the colgroup width; a `wrap-anywhere` anchor inside a column that is sized to exactly the measured single-line width has no headroom and breaks at the first soft-wrap point (typically the space before a `<span class='comment'>`). Fix: switch both `toggleAutoResizeColumns` and `toggleSubTableAutoResize` from `table-layout:fixed` to `table-layout:auto`, and instead set `min-width` on each `<th>` header cell. Under auto layout the browser uses the measured minimum as a floor but is free to widen the column when content requires it, eliminating spurious wraps while still preventing columns from collapsing below their measured content width. `restoreOriginalTableState` now also clears `th.style.minWidth` on restore.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: splitLocation extractor — multi-row cell aware: when the source 'Location' column contains a `<ul><li>` multi-row structure (e.g. an artist-events page where one event has multiple venues), the extractor now processes each source `<li>` independently and builds parallel `<ul><li>` lists for the three synthetic columns 'Place', 'Area', and 'Country'. Each `<li>` in the source becomes exactly one `<li>` in each output cell, keeping the rows aligned across the three columns. A shared `_processNode(node, containerP, containerA, containerC)` helper handles the link classification logic (place href → Place, area href without flag → Area, area href inside .flag → Country) for both the multi-row and flat paths. When the source cell has no `<ul><li>` structure the original flat behaviour is preserved.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — second 'Table Details' toggle (▶ between 🗃️ and label): was switching glyphs correctly but not affecting any tables. Root cause: the handler walked `.mb-subtable-collapse-btn` → h3 → next table sibling, a fragile path that failed silently when bigbox divs sat between h3 and table, and the state check compared against the wrong span class. Rewritten to use the identical strategy as `rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti`: iterate all `table.tbl .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn` in the document, call `_applyCollapseState` per cell directly, then update sub-table button glyphs.

Statistics panel — 'Columns (N)' expand/collapse glyph in table-detail cards showed ▲ (up-pointing triangle) instead of ▶ on the third state. Root cause: `colToggleBar`'s click handler set `colIcon.style.transform = 'rotate(-90deg)'` on the ▶ character, which rotates it to point upward. Fixed by removing the transform and using only `textContent` changes: ▶ when collapsed, ▼ when expanded.

Export — Country/Date (and all cells with adjacent sibling elements) now export with space-separated constituents. Root cause: `.release-event` spans are INSIDE `<li>` items, so the previous `lis.length > 0` branch fired first and called `li.textContent.trim()` which concatenates all child element text without separators (e.g. 'US1973-01-05Fri'). Fixed by a recursive `_insertSpaces(node)` helper that runs on the cloned cell before any text extraction: it inserts a space text node before each Element child throughout the entire clone, so that `textContent` naturally includes spaces between adjacent element contents. The `.release-event` special-case branch has been removed as it is no longer needed.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel sub-table toggle (▶ between 🗃️ and 'Table Details') — the toggle was visually switching between ▶/▼ but not actually expanding/collapsing sub-tables. Root cause: the code called `btn.click()` on `.mb-subtable-collapse-btn` elements (synthetic untrusted click, may be suppressed), and the state-detection read `.mb-col-collapse-glyph` which does not exist inside `makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML`'s output. Fixed by: (1) reading state from `btn.querySelector('span').textContent` (the first child span carries ▶ or ▼), (2) finding the table associated with each button by walking forward from its `.mb-toggle-h3` ancestor, and (3) directly calling `_applyCollapseState` per cell and updating header-button glyphs, bypassing synthetic click events entirely.

Sub-table collapse button glyph cycle — after clicking Expand (▶) the button correctly showed ▼ (Collapse), but after clicking Collapse the button showed ◀ instead of reverting to ▶. Root cause: `makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML(false)` used ◀ as the arrow. Fixed to use ▼ for the collapsed→expand state (▶) and the expanded→collapse state (▼), making the cycle ▶ → ▼ → ▶.

Export — Country/Date source column (original `.release-event` DOM structure) now exports with space-separated constituents. `_exportCleanCellText` now detects `.release-event` child elements and joins each event's part texts (country code + date + weekday) with a single space, and joins multiple events with ' / '. Previously `clone.textContent` concatenated them directly (e.g. 'US1973-01-05Fri'). Multi-row `<ul><li>` cells (split Country / Date columns) continue to be joined by space as before.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Export — Country/Date (and all multi-row ul/li cells) now export with space-separated constituents. Previously `_exportCleanCellText` used `clone.textContent` which concatenates all text nodes with no separators (e.g. 'US1973-01-05Fri'). Fixed by detecting `ul > li` items and joining them with a single space (e.g. 'US 1973-01-05 Fri'). Falls back to plain `textContent` for non-list cells.

Auto-resize — 'Primary Alias' and other single-item synthetic columns (`
  • text
`) were measured at zero or collapsed width. Root cause: block `
    `/`
  • ` elements inside the inline `measureDiv` may not expand to their full text width. Fixed by measuring each `
  • ` individually as `display:inline-block` (including hidden ones from collapsed multi-row cells) and folding the max into `columnWidths`. Applied to both `toggleAutoResizeColumns` (global) and `toggleSubTableAutoResize` (per-sub-table). CAA art `
  • ` items are skipped.

Statistics panel 'Table Details' — added a second toggle icon between the 🗃️ emoji and the 'Table Details' text. Clicking it cycles all visible `.mb-subtable-collapse-btn` buttons in the live rendered table through expand-all/collapse-all states. The icon starts as ▶ and flips to ▼ and back.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: configSchema — new '📤 EXPORT' section inserted between '🎨 UI FEATURES' and '📊 STATISTICS PANEL'. Contains the master `sa_enable_export` toggle (moved from UI FEATURES) plus four new export-cleanup settings (all default false): `sa_export_strip_caa_glyphs`, `sa_export_strip_title_glyphs`, `sa_export_convert_multirow_glyphs`, `sa_export_include_uniq_counts`.

Export — `sa_export_strip_caa_glyphs` (default false): when true, removes CAA/EAA expand-button glyphs (`[data-caa-expand-btn]`) and status-emoji cache-hint spans from artwork cells before export; only the thumbnail count badge text is preserved.

Export — `sa_export_strip_title_glyphs` (default false): when true, removes leading ERG `▶` buttons (`[data-erg-btn]`) from title cells and then strips any remaining leading ▶/▼/◀/▲ character from the extracted plain text.

Export — `sa_export_convert_multirow_glyphs` (default false): when true, converts `.mb-cell-collapse-toggle` spans (e.g. `▶2▤`) to a bracketed count suffix appended to the cell text (e.g. `AT2003-05-05Mon (2)`). When false the toggle span is silently removed as before.

Export — `sa_export_include_uniq_counts` (default false): when true, the new `_exportCleanHeaderText(th)` helper appends the `.mb-col-uniq-count` badge value in parentheses to each exported column header, e.g. `Label (7)`. All four exporters (CSV, JSON, Org-Mode, HTML) now use `_exportCleanHeaderText` instead of ad-hoc regex stripping for consistent header text across formats.

Statistics panel — 'Table Detail' section heading renamed to 'Table Details' when there is more than one sub-table in multi-table mode. A ▼/▶ collapse toggle icon is prepended to the heading in that case; clicking it hides or shows all `[data-stats-card]` table-detail cards without closing the panel.

Statistics panel — new 📤 Export button added to the panel header bar (between the quick-filter clear button and the ✕ close button). Clicking it calls `_exportStatsToHTML()`, which clones the panel body, force-expands all table-detail cards, strips the quick-filter input, wraps the result in a minimal self-contained CSS stylesheet, and invokes `showExportDialog` with the standard `_assembleExportFilename` base name suffixed with `_STATISTICS` before the `.html` extension (e.g. `MB-Artist-(Name)-Releases-(42)_2026-03-20T10-00-00_STATISTICS.html`).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: configSchema — `sa_enable_save_load` moved from the '🎨 UI FEATURES' section to the beginning of the '💾 LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK' section where it logically belongs as the master switch for that entire section.

pageDefinitions — `area-releases` (button-level features): added `{ sourceColumn: 'Tracks', extractor: 'sumTracks', syntheticColumns: ['Total Tracks'] }` to `columnExtractors`. The 'Total Tracks' column is already declared in `integerColumns` but the extractor that populates it was missing, so the column was always empty.

pageDefinitions — `work-recordings-filtered` and `work-recordings`: added `columnExtractors: [ { sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'video', syntheticColumns: ['Video'] } ]` so that video-type recordings get the synthetic 'Video' column, matching the behaviour of other recording-list page types.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table collapse buttons (column-header ▶N▤ and global ▶ Expand ALL) had no effect when clicked, while individual cell toggles worked. Root cause: all three button handlers called `toggle.click()` to drive cell toggles, which dispatches a MouseEvent with `isTrusted = false`. Some browsers and userscript managers (including Tampermonkey in strict-site-policy mode) silently suppress untrusted synthetic click events when dispatched from within another click handler. Fix: new `_applyCollapseState(toggle, expand)` helper directly performs the exact expand/collapse DOM operations (li display, glyph text, aria-expanded, aria-label, title, expandedCells sync, hidden-match indicator, sub-table + global button tint update) without relying on event dispatch. All four button handlers — column-header button, `rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti` onclick, `createSubTableCollapseButton` onclick, and single-table global button onclick — have been rewritten to call `_applyCollapseState` per cell and directly update header button glyphs and aria-expanded attributes.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: integerColumns align:':' — split now uses `lastIndexOf` instead of `indexOf`. This correctly handles the optional-hour time format `[H:]M:S[.ms]` (e.g. `2:02:05` now splits at the M/S boundary: left=`2:02`, right=`05`). Single-separator values (e.g. `5:44.226`) are unaffected. Applied in both `applyIntegerColumnStyling` (pass 1) and `_repairTreleasesTd` (Jesus2099 repair).

integerColumns split-align separator not vertically centred across rows: `finalizeSplitAlignedColumns` previously only set `min-width` on `.mb-ic-left` spans. Because `.mb-ic-right` spans auto-sized to their content, rows produced `.mb-ic-wrap` blocks of different total widths, causing `text-align:center` on the `<td>` to place the separator at different horizontal positions. Fixed by also computing `maxRightLen` and setting `min-width:maxRightLen ch` on every `.mb-ic-right` span, giving all wraps the same total width and pinning the separator to a fixed centre column. `_repairTreleasesTd` min-width re-sync updated identically.

Stale concatenated JSDoc removed: a double `/**` opener above `_repairTreleasesTd` combined the old obsolete `initTreleasesObserver` description with the current `_repairTreleasesTd` description. The stale outer block has been removed.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: integerColumns — split-align generalised: `align: ':'` is now just one instance of a general split-character alignment. Any single non-L/R/C character is accepted as a split separator. `buildActiveIntegerColumns` validates this, `applyIntegerColumnStyling` uses `const _isSplit / splitChar = entry.align` to drive splitting. The new `align: '.'` value is now also supported (used for the '#' track-number column in `recording-releases`).

integerColumns — new `finalizeRLCColumnWidths(rows, descriptors)` pass-2 function for L/R/C columns: scans all collected rows to find the max `.mb-ic-val` text length per column and sets `min-width:Nch` on every span, giving all values a uniform block width so that right-aligned values like '86' and '124' sit at the same horizontal anchor. The L/R/C value span now carries class `mb-ic-val` (replacing the old CSS-variable `min-width`). Called paired with `finalizeSplitAlignedColumns` at all 6 render sites.

integerColumns — `finalizeColonAlignedColumns` renamed to `finalizeSplitAlignedColumns`; internal `colonDescs` filter updated to match any non-L/R/C align value; debug log updated to include `splitChar`.
pageDefinitions — `artist-relationships-filtered` and `artist-relationships`: added `integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: ':'} ]`.
pageDefinitions — `work-recordings-filtered`: added `integerColumns: [ {sourceColumn: 'Length', align: ':'} ]` (same as `work-recordings`).
pageDefinitions — `recording-releases`: changed `{sourceColumn: '#', align: 'L'}` to `{sourceColumn: '#', align: '.'}` — track numbers like '1.1' align on the decimal point.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: integerColumns align:'R' not working (artist-releasegroups 'Releases' column): the L/R/C span was given `min-width:var(--mb-int-col-min-width, 2ch)` which is content-independent; the spans auto-sized to different widths ('86' vs '124') so right-alignment was invisible. Fixed by `finalizeRLCColumnWidths` which computes the true column-wide max and sets an explicit `Nch` min-width on every span.

initTreleasesObserver: previous approach removed the `<td class='treleases'>` element, which shifted all subsequent columns. Root cause: Jesus2099 does not add a new <td> — it mutates the existing one in place (setting class, title, style, and text). Fixed by replacing the remove logic with `_repairTreleasesTd(td)`: strips Jesus2099's class/title/style, re-applies our split or L/R/C alignment to the updated text, and re-synchronises min-width across the column. The MutationObserver now also watches `attributes/attributeFilter:['class']` to catch in-place class mutations in addition to childList replacements.
Misplaced JSDoc comment 'High-performance batch renderer for large datasets' removed from above `initTreleasesObserver` (it belongs to `renderRowsChunked` which already has its own correct JSDoc).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Config schema — new '🔢 NUMERIC COLUMN ALIGNMENT' section (inserted between '💬 TOOLTIPS' and '🧮 OPTIONAL COLUMN REMOVAL') with one new setting: `sa_enable_numeric_alignment` (boolean, default `true`). When `true`, all integerColumns styling is applied as before (centered inline-block for L/R/C columns, colon-split alignment for ':' columns, and the treleases MutationObserver). When `false`, all eight call sites — `applyIntegerColumnStyling` (both row-import branches), `finalizeColonAlignedColumns` (6 render/filter sites), and `initTreleasesObserver` (renderFinalTable + renderGroupedTable) — are bypassed and the affected cells are left with their original MusicBrainz styling unchanged.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Syntax error fix: the str_replace that added `initTreleasesObserver()` to the end of `renderGroupedTable` (v9.99.247) accidentally consumed the opening `/**` of the following function's JSDoc block (`_artRebuildBigPicsForTable`), leaving orphaned `* fill or the subsequent restore pass.` comment lines as bare code statements. This caused a `SyntaxError: Unexpected token '*'` at parse time. Fixed by restoring the missing `/**` opening line.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: integerColumns align:':' — correct approach for stripping jesus2099 'mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL Ⅱ X TURBO' Length-cell annotations: the previous attempt (v9.99.246) called `cell.querySelectorAll('[class="treleases"]…').forEach(el => el.remove())` inside `applyIntegerColumnStyling` (pass 1, during row import). This ran too early — jesus2099 injects its `treleases` nodes into the *live rendered table* after our entire pipeline has completed, so the removal was a no-op. Fixed by: (1) removing the ineffective pass-1 strip entirely, and (2) introducing `initTreleasesObserver()` — a `MutationObserver` that watches every `table.tbl tbody` via `{ childList: true, subtree: true }` and removes any `[class="treleases"]` element the instant it is inserted, before the browser paints. A module-level `WeakSet _observedTbodies` prevents double-observing the same tbody across repeated re-renders. `initTreleasesObserver()` is called at the end of both `renderFinalTable` and `renderGroupedTable` so all table modes are covered.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: integerColumns align:':' — fix jesus2099 'treleases' annotation stripping: the previous approach (v9.99.246) called `cell.querySelectorAll('[class="treleases"]…').forEach(el => el.remove())` inside `applyIntegerColumnStyling`, which runs during row import — before rows are inserted into the live DOM. The jesus2099 'mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL Ⅱ X TURBO' userscript injects its `<… class="treleases" title="SUPER MIND CONTROL…">` nodes into the *rendered* live table after our entire pipeline has completed, so the early removal had no effect. Fixed with a `MutationObserver` approach: new `initTreleasesObserver()` function installs an observer on every `table.tbl tbody` that fires synchronously on `childList + subtree` mutations and removes any `[class="treleases"]` element the instant it is inserted, before the browser paints. A module-level `WeakSet` (`_observedTbodies`) prevents double-observing the same tbody across re-renders. `initTreleasesObserver()` is called at the end of both `renderFinalTable` and `renderGroupedTable` so all render paths (initial, load-from-disk, runFilter) are covered. The ineffective `querySelectorAll` strip was removed from `applyIntegerColumnStyling`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Changes
items: integerColumns align:':' — corrected centering technique: the previous implementation used `text-align:right` on the `<td>`, which pushed the whole value to the right edge of the column instead of centering the colon. The three spans (`mb-ic-left`, `mb-ic-sep`, `mb-ic-right`) are now wrapped in a `<span class='mb-ic-wrap' style='display:inline-block; white-space:nowrap'>` container. The `<td>` receives `text-align:center` so the wrapper is horizontally centred in the column and the ':' separator sits exactly at the column's center axis. The `mb-ic-left` span grows rightward (right-aligned, min-width set by finalizeColonAlignedColumns) to meet the colon; `mb-ic-right` grows leftward (left-aligned) away from it. `querySelector('.mb-ic-left')` in `finalizeColonAlignedColumns` continues to work unchanged because it descends into the wrapper.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: integerColumns — new `align: ':'` colon-split alignment mode: implements decimal-alignment using ':' as the separator character. Modelled on CSS decimal-alignment but performed in DOM so it works across all browsers. `applyIntegerColumnStyling` (pass 1, per-row, during import) splits each cell's text content at the first ':' and produces three sibling spans: `<span class='mb-ic-left'>` (right-aligned, tabular-nums, no min-width yet), `<span class='mb-ic-sep'>` (literal ':'), and `<span class='mb-ic-right'>` (left-aligned, tabular-nums). Values containing no ':' are treated as pure right parts with an empty left span. The cell receives `white-space:nowrap; text-align:right` to anchor the block to the column edge.

integerColumns — new `finalizeColonAlignedColumns(rows, descriptors)` function (pass 2, post-collection): for each colon-align descriptor scans all collected rows, determines the maximum left-part character count, and sets `min-width:Nch` on every `.mb-ic-left` span so that ':' separators line up vertically across all rows. Called just before each of the four render entry points: main fetch completion (single + multi), load-from-disk, and runFilter re-render (single + multi). Emits a `Lib.debug('render', …)` line per column noting the computed max left-part width.

pageDefinitions — `Length` column alignment changed from `'R'` to `':'` (colon-split) in all 3 page definitions that declare it: `artist-recordings`, `recording-releases`, and `release-recordings`. Track lengths (format `mm:ss.mmm`) now align vertically on their ':' separator.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: pageDefinitions — `artist-releasegroups`: added `{sourceColumn: 'Releases', align: 'R'}` to `features.integerColumns`. The 'Releases' count column now receives the centered inline-block + right-aligned content styling and participates in numeric sort comparison via `isNumericCol`.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: pageDefinitions — changed alignment of `DD` and `MM` integerColumns from `'C'` (centered) to `'R'` (right-aligned) across all 7 page definitions that declare date-part columns. Day and month numbers now right-align inside their centered inline-block span, consistent with `YYYY` and other numeric columns such as `Total Tracks` and `Length`.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: integerColumns feature — new `features.integerColumns` array in `pageDefinitions` entries: each element is `{ sourceColumn: string, align?: 'L' | 'R' | 'C' }` and triggers the "centered inline-block + content-align" rendering technique on matching table columns. The `<td>` receives `text-align:center` so the inner `<span>` is visually centred within the column; the `<span>` itself carries `display:inline-block`, `text-align:{left|right|center}` (per-column `align` setting, defaulting to `'C'`), `font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums`, and `min-width:var(--mb-int-col-min-width, 2ch)`. The `--mb-int-col-min-width` CSS custom property lets callers override the inner span's minimum width via CSS without touching JS. Idempotency is guaranteed via `cell.dataset.mbIntColStyled = '1'` so re-render cycles never double-wrap cells.

integerColumns — new `buildActiveIntegerColumns(def)` resolver function mirrors the pattern of `buildActiveColumnExtractors` / `buildActiveRenderMultiRowCols`: reads `features.integerColumns`, normalises `align` to `'C'` when absent or invalid, and initialises `colIdx` to `-1` for each descriptor.

integerColumns — new `applyIntegerColumnStyling(row, descriptors)` function applies the inline-block wrapping to every cell whose `colIdx` has been resolved. Called as step 6 in both row-processing branches (artist-releasegroups and generic else-branch), immediately after step 5 (MB-Name / Comment append), so the full final cell count is in place when `colIdx` is used.

integerColumns — colIdx resolution uses a dedicated post-scan pass that builds the FINAL column name list (original headers minus excluded indices, then primary extractor synthetics, then second-pass synthetics, then MB-Name / Comment) so that purely synthetic columns like DD, MM, YYYY are correctly addressed. Columns not found emit a `Lib.warn` and are silently skipped.

integerColumns — runtime variable `activeIntegerColumns` added alongside `activeColumnExtractors`, `activeColumnErasers`, and `activeRenderMultiRowCols`; initialised in `startFetchingProcess` by `buildActiveIntegerColumns(activeDefinition)` with a debug log listing configured source columns and align values.
integerColumns — per-page colIdx reset added to the header-scanning block so stale indices from a previous page do not bleed into subsequent pages.

integerColumns — `isNumericCol` helper rewritten: primary lookup now checks `activeIntegerColumns` (a `Set` of declared source column names) so columns explicitly listed in the page definition are always sorted numerically regardless of their name pattern; the legacy heuristic (`name.includes('Year')` etc.) is retained as a fallback for pages that carry no `integerColumns` declaration.
integerColumns — debug log line `Detected indices →` extended with `integerColumns=[…]` section showing each descriptor's `sourceColumn`, `align`, and resolved `colIdx`.

pageDefinitions — fixed all duplicate `integerColumns` object keys: JavaScript silently discards all but the last duplicate key, so pages that declared two separate `integerColumns` arrays (e.g. `{ DD/MM/YYYY }` + `{ Total Tracks }`) effectively lost the first. All affected page definitions have been merged into single arrays: `area-releases` (button-level features), `artist-releases` (top-level features), two `label-releases` variants, `release-group-releases`, `recording-releases`, `work-recordings`, and `release-recordings` (which carried three separate arrays for DD/MM/YYYY, Length, and #).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Config schema — new '💬 TOOLTIPS' section (inserted before '🧮 OPTIONAL COLUMN REMOVAL') with three new settings: (1) `sa_enable_count_stat_tooltip` (boolean, default true) — master switch for the rich row-count tooltip system; when false, `_initStatTooltip()` is not called, `data-mbtt` attributes are not set on count-stat spans, the `cursor:help` CSS rule is omitted, and the h2/h3 native title-suppression logic is bypassed so the standard collapse/uncollapse tooltip is shown instead. (2) `sa_count_stat_tooltip_bg` (color picker, default `#1e1e2e`) — background color of the `#mb-stat-tooltip` box. (3) `sa_count_stat_tooltip_color` (color picker, default `#cdd6f4`) — foreground text color of the tooltip box. Both color settings are injected as `background`/`color` overrides inside the settings-driven `#mb-stat-tooltip { … }` CSS block, alongside the existing CSS custom property variables for filter-expression highlight colors.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Colored count-stat tooltip — h2/h3 native title conflict: the `.mb-row-count-stat` span is a child of the h2/h3 element whose `title` attribute reads 'Click to collapse/uncollapse section …'. The browser renders both the native h2/h3 title tooltip and the custom `#mb-stat-tooltip` simultaneously, with the native one obscuring the custom one. Fixed in `_initStatTooltip`: on `mouseover` of a count-stat span, the nearest h2/h3 ancestor's `title` is saved into `data-mbtt-saved-title` and cleared to `''`; on `mouseout` the saved value is restored and the data attribute deleted. This suppresses the native browser tooltip exclusively while the cursor is over the count-stat span, without permanently altering the collapse behaviour.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table h3 tooltip — Bug 1 (initial tooltip missing on first render): `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip(table)` was placed before `container.appendChild(h3/table)` so `findH3ForTable` couldn't locate the h3 (table not yet in DOM). Moved to immediately after the DOM insertion block.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Rich colored tooltips on `.mb-row-count-stat` spans: replaced plain-text native `title` attributes with a custom `#mb-stat-tooltip` floating div system. Filter expressions are now rendered with the same highlight background/text colors used in table cell highlighting: global filter → `.mbtt-gf` (gold bg, red text matching `sa_global_filter_highlight_bg/color`); sub-table filter → `.mbtt-stf` (light green bg, black text); column filter → `.mbtt-cf` (light blue bg, red text matching `sa_column_filter_highlight_bg/color`). CSS custom properties on `#mb-stat-tooltip` pick up the user-configured colors via the settings-driven stylesheet.

New `_mbttSpan(expr, type)` helper: HTML-escapes the filter expression and wraps it in `…`. Used by both `_buildH2CountTooltip` and `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip` to produce the colored HTML stored in `data-mbtt`.

New `_initStatTooltip()` function: creates the singleton `#mb-stat-tooltip` div and installs three delegated event listeners (`mouseover`, `mouseout`, `mousemove`) on `document` using capture. The tooltip appears on hover over any `.mb-row-count-stat[data-mbtt]` element and follows the cursor with viewport-boundary clamping. Called once after the main CSS stylesheet is injected.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table h3 tooltip — Bug 1 (no tooltip on initial render): `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip` was only called from `updateSubTableRowCount` (STF events) and `runFilter` (filter events). On the initial render — where `renderGroupedTable` builds the h3 via `h3.innerHTML = '…(N)'` — no tooltip was set. Fixed by calling `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip(table)` immediately after `table.dataset.mbTotalRows = group.rows.length` in both the initial-render branch (new h3) and the filter-update branch (existing h3, where `countStat.textContent` was set without a tooltip).

Multi-table h2 tooltip — Bug 2 (wording 'in this table' vs 'in ALL sub-tables'): `_buildH2CountTooltip` now checks `activeDefinition.tableMode === 'multi'` and uses `_tableLabel = 'in ALL sub-tables'` for multi-table pages and `'in this table'` for single-table pages. The `_tableLabel` variable is appended to all tooltip variants (plain N, global-only, column-only, global+column).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Column name extraction — `_cleanColHeaderText` fix: the previous implementation removed all `[class^="mb-"]` descendants from a cloned `<th>`, but the column label text lives inside `.mb-col-hdr-flex` (which itself starts with `mb-`), so the entire text was erased, producing empty column names in all tooltips. Fixed with a three-tier resolution: (1) `th.dataset.colName` — set by `makeTableSortableUnified` on every processed header (original and synthetic) and by the synthetic-column injectors; this is the authoritative source when present. (2) First text node of `.mb-col-hdr-flex` — the column name is the first child text node of the flex container, before the sort and unique-value spans. (3) Clone fallback — removes only the specific named elements (`.mb-col-uniq-wrap`, `.sort-icon-btn`, `.column-resizer`, `.mb-col-hdr-flex`) from the clone and reads remaining `textContent`. (4) Last resort — glyph-stripping regex.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Changes
items: Column name extraction — new `_cleanColHeaderText(th)` module-level helper: clones the `<th>`, removes `.mb-col-uniq-wrap` (unique-value dropdown + count badge), `.sort-icon-btn`, `.column-resizer`, and all `[class^="mb-"]` children, then reads `textContent`. Replaces the previous `textContent.replace(/[⇅▲▼📊▶◀▤⁰¹²³⁴⁵⁶⁷⁸⁹]/g, '')` regex which stripped digits from legitimate column names (e.g. 'Country/Date 1' where '1' was the unique-value badge count, not part of the name). Now used in `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip` for column-filter name resolution.

Single-table h2 count-stat tooltip — new `_buildH2CountTooltip(filteredCount, totalCount, absoluteTotal)` module-level helper: builds context-sensitive tooltip text for the `.mb-row-count-stat` span in the h2 header, mirroring the h3 tooltip logic. Called from `updateH2Count` immediately after `span.textContent = countText` — sets `span.title` via DOM property. Cases: plain `(N)` → 'There is/are N row(s) total unfiltered'; `(M of N)` with global filter only → 'There is/are M … after global filter "…" from N total'; with column filters only → 'There is/are M … after column filters …'; with both → combined message. Uses `_cleanColHeaderText` for column names and the `_nR(n)` / `_r(n)` singular helpers.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sub-table row-count tooltips — singular/plural: 'There are 1 rows' is now 'There is 1 row'. Added `_nRows(n)` helper that returns 'There is N row' for N=1 and 'There are N rows' otherwise. All count references in tooltip strings (visible, denom, total) apply the same `n === 1 ? 'row' : 'rows'` ternary.

Sub-table row-count tooltips — all-three-filters case: when global filter, sub-table filter, and column-level filters are all simultaneously active (`_denom !== _total` AND `_stfVal` AND `_activeCol.length > 0`), the bracketed `(n of M)` tooltip now reads: 'There are n rows left after the global filter "…", the sub-table filter "…" and the column level filters \'col\':\"expr\" from N total unfiltered rows in this sub-table'. Previously the column filters were silently ignored in the STF-active branch.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sub-table row-count tooltips — column-filter clear not reflected: the tooltip on `(M of N)` correctly showed column-filter info when filters were applied, but was not reset to the global-filter-only tooltip when column filters were cleared. Root cause: `updateSubTableRowCount` (inside each STF closure) was only wired to STF events — column filter changes go through `runFilter` → `renderGroupedTable`, which never called `updateSubTableRowCount`. Fix: extracted all tooltip logic into a new module-level `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip(tbl)` function that reads the current global filter, STF value, and column filters directly from the live DOM (no closure dependencies). `updateSubTableRowCount` now delegates to this helper. `runFilter`'s per-table status-update loop (lines 16440–16470) now calls `_updateSubTableH3Tooltip(table)` after the filter-status span update, so every column-filter apply or clear triggers an immediate tooltip refresh.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Sub-table row-count tooltips — column-filter awareness: `updateSubTableRowCount` now inspects the active column-level filter inputs on each specific sub-table (`thead tr.mb-col-filter-row .mb-col-filter-input`) and resolves column names from the corresponding header cells by `data-col-idx`. The `(M of N)` tooltip (no STF active, but rows are filtered) now has three distinct messages: (1) column filters only: 'There are M rows filtered by the sub-table column filters on \'col A\':"expr", \'col B\':"expr" from N total rows'; (2) global + column filters: 'There are M rows filtered by the column filters on … from N rows, further narrowed by the global filter "…"'; (3) global filter only (unchanged): 'There are M rows filtered from N rows … by the global filter "…"'. Column filter expressions are read via `stripFilterPrefix` and set only as DOM `.title` properties — never interpolated into HTML — so any character in a filter expression is safe.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sub-table row-count tooltips — Bug 1 (tooltip not always shown on '(M of N)'): the tooltip text was set via `countStat.title = _tipGlobal` and then immediately overwritten by `countStat.innerHTML = '(M of N)'` — innerHTML resets all properties including title. Fixed by switching to `countStat.textContent` (which does not reset title) for the global-filter-only case, and by rebuilding all cases with `countStat.textContent = ''; countStat.title = ''` first.

Sub-table row-count tooltips — Bug 2 (truncated tooltip on '(n of M)/N'): tooltip text was embedded as `title="..."` inside an innerHTML string. Any `"` character in the filter expression (e.g. a search term containing a quote) terminates the HTML attribute, truncating the tooltip at that point. Fixed by replacing all `innerHTML` usage with imperative DOM construction: a `_mkSpan(text, title)` helper creates `<span>` nodes and sets `.title` as a DOM property (not an HTML attribute string), so any character — quotes, angle brackets, backslashes — appears correctly in the tooltip.
Sub-table row-count tooltips — Bug 3 (no tooltip on plain '(N)'): the unfiltered `(N)` case had no tooltip. Now sets `countStat.title = 'There are N total unfiltered rows in this sub-table'`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Changes
items: Config schema — '🏷️ PAGE HEADER CONFIGURATION' section renamed to '🏷️ PAGE HEADER AND BODY CONFIGURATION'.

Config schema — new boolean `sa_enable_h2_section_relocation_on_final_page` (default true) added to the renamed section. The trailing-h2 section relocation code in `finalCleanup` is now guarded by this setting.

pageDefinitions — `area-recordings`: added `columnExtractors: [{ sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'video', syntheticColumns: ['Video'] }]` to match the video-column extraction present on other recording page types.

Sub-table row-count stat tooltips: `updateSubTableRowCount` now sets context-sensitive `title` attributes on `.mb-row-count-stat` spans in h3 headers. Three formats: (1) plain `(N)` — no tooltip; (2) global-filter-only `(M of N)` — single tooltip on the whole span: 'M rows filtered from N total rows by global filter "…"'; (3) STF + global filter `(n of M)/N` — two separately titled `<span>` children: the bracketed `(n of M)` part gets 'n rows filtered by sub-table filter "…" from M rows already filtered by global filter "…"', and the `/N` part gets 'N total unfiltered rows in this sub-table'.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Trailing h2 relocation (fix for 9.99.228): only the bare `<h2>` element was being moved, not the section content that follows it (relationship lists, related-work tables, etc.). Fixed by collecting each trailing h2 plus all of its following siblings up to (but not including) the next sibling h2 as a 'section node list', then moving the entire list before the data h2. Sections are processed in reverse document order so that after all insertions the final order before the data h2 matches the original trailing order. Sibling node collection walks `nextSibling` (not `nextElementSibling`) to capture text nodes and comments as well as elements.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Multi-table STF clear — Bug 1 (h2 count not updated when sub-table filter cleared): Two previous attempts used `updateH2CountFromSubtables()` from `clearSubFilter`, but that function always produced the wrong 3-tier `(N of N)/N` format after clearing because by the time it ran, both `data-mb-stf-hidden` markers and the STF input value had already been cleared — making all its guards return the same bad result. Root fix: `clearSubFilter` now calls `runFilter()` directly after restoring rows. `runFilter` re-evaluates the active global + column filters and sets the h2 badge via the standard 2-tier `updateH2Count(filtered, total)` path, which is exactly the correct display when no STF is active.

Trailing h2 relocation: on the final rendered page, `finalCleanup` now detects h2 elements in `#content` that appear after the data h2 (identified by `.mb-row-count-stat`) and moves them all to immediately before the data h2, preserving their relative order among themselves. This fixes pages like release-group and recording pages where MusicBrainz renders 'Relationships' and 'Related works' h2 sections after the data table h2.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Exporters — Bug 1 (invisible JSON garbage in cells): MusicBrainz/React inlines serialised relation data as `<script type="application/json">` elements directly inside table cells (e.g. the 'Artists' column on event pages). These were included verbatim when reading `textContent`, producing multi-kilobyte JSON blobs in exports. Fixed by removing all `script` elements from the cloned cell in `_exportCleanCellText` before reading `textContent`.

Exporters — Bug 2 (column number in header): column headers like 'Artists 105' included the unique-value count number as part of the exported column name. The unique-value count badge is rendered inside `.mb-col-uniq-wrap` which `_exportCleanCellText` already strips — but `_exportNameFor`'s `_cleanHeading` now also strips `.mb-row-count-stat` spans and all `[id^=mb-]`/`[class^=mb-]` elements from heading clones.

Exporters — Bug 3 (single-table pages show 'Table 1' instead of section name): `_exportNameFor` walked `previousElementSibling` which on single-table pages hits filter wrappers and other injected elements before finding an h2, returning the fallback 'Table N'. Fixed by adding a second-pass fallback: search `#content` for the h2 carrying `.mb-row-count-stat` (the main data section heading MusicBrainz always renders), clean it with `_cleanHeading`, and return its text. This gives 'Events', 'Releases', etc. on all single-table pages.

Multi-table filter — Bug 4 (h2 count not updated when STF cleared): `updateH2CountFromSubtables` returned early with `if (!anySubtableFilterActive) return` — but when clearing the filter the input is already empty before this check runs, so the h2 badge stayed at the filtered count. Fixed by also checking `anyStfHiddenRows` (whether any rows still carry `data-mb-stf-hidden`): the early return is now only skipped when BOTH no STF is active AND no rows are STF-hidden, ensuring the h2 is correctly updated when a filter is cleared.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Config schema — new '🏷️ PAGE HEADER CONFIGURATION' section: `sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_initial_page` and `sa_enable_h1_comment_span_relocation_on_final_page` moved here from the '🔬 EXPERIMENTAL FEATURES' section.

Config schema — new boolean `sa_enable_legal_name_relocation_on_final_page` (default true) added to '🏷️ PAGE HEADER CONFIGURATION'. When true, on artist-releasegroups pages the 'Legal name: …' paragraph is moved from its default location further down the page to immediately after `

` (which contains the fetch-status and info displays), making it visible alongside artist type/period info. An `data-mb-legal-moved` attribute guards idempotency across re-renders.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: All exporters (CSV, JSON, Org-Mode) — invisible metadata stripped from cells: sort-key spans (`mb-caa-sort-key`, `mb-cancelled-sort-key`, `mb-video-sort-key`, and any other `display:none` descendants) were previously included in exported cell text via plain `textContent`. Added shared `_exportCleanCellText(cell)` utility: clones the cell, removes all `[style*="display:none"]` descendants and script UI widgets (sort buttons, uniq-count wraps, resize handles, CAA/EAA toggle buttons, inline placeholders), then returns `clone.textContent.trim()`. All three text-based exporters now call this instead of `cell.textContent` directly.

Org-Mode export — section heading symbol changed from `#` to `*`: `#` is an Org-Mode comment character, not a heading. Section headings are now proper Org outline entries (`* Events`, `* Album`, etc.) making the exported file a valid navigable Org document.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: JSON export — unified `tables` structure for both single and multi-table pages. Previously single-table pages used a flat `{ meta, headers, rows }` structure and multi-table used `{ meta, tables: […] }`. Now always `{ meta, tables: [{ name, headers, rows }, …] }` regardless of table count. `name` is derived from the h2 heading (single-table) or h3 heading (multi-table) via the new shared `_exportNameFor(tbl, idx)` utility.

Org-Mode export — section heading (`* Name`) now emitted for both single and multi-table pages. Single-table pages previously emitted no heading; now a `* Events` (or whatever h2 heading precedes the table) is prepended before the table, making the output structure consistent.

Added two shared module-level export utilities: `_exportCleanCellText(cell)` (strips hidden metadata, returns clean text) and `_exportNameFor(tbl, idx)` (derives clean heading text from the h2/h3 preceding the table, with index fallback). Both are reused by CSV, JSON, Org-Mode, and HTML exporters.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: HTML export — Bug 1: the exported page title included the entire contents of the `#mb-show-all-controls-container` div (all button labels, filter inputs, etc.) because it was reading `h1.textContent` directly and the script injects the controls bar inside the `<h1>` element. Fixed by cloning the h1, removing `#mb-show-all-controls-container` and any other `[id^="mb-"]` / `[class^="mb-"]` elements from the clone, then reading `clone.textContent`.

HTML export — Bug 2: collapsed sub-tables were omitted from the export because the table list was filtered by `t.style.display !== 'none'`. Collapsing is a viewing preference and should not exclude content from export. Fixed by removing the display filter — all `table.tbl` elements are now exported; only individual rows hidden by active filters (display:none on `<tr>`) are skipped.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: JSON export — multi-table support: on pages with `tableMode: 'multi'` (or multiple `table.tbl` elements), the exported structure now uses `{ meta, tables: [{ name, headers, rows }, …] }` instead of the single-table `{ meta, headers, rows }` flat structure. `name` is derived from the h2/h3 heading that precedes each sub-table. Single-table pages continue to use the original flat structure (backward-compatible).

Org-Mode export — multi-table support: on multi-table pages, each sub-table is exported as a separate pipe-delimited Org table preceded by a `# SubTableName` comment line (derived from the h3 heading). Sub-tables are separated by a blank line. The metadata comment block is emitted once at the top. Single-table pages continue to emit a single table (backward-compatible).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items:
Export — new 'HTML' format added to the 💾 Export dropdown (after Org-Mode). Calls `exportTableToHTML()` which produces a fully self-contained `.html` file using the same `showExportDialog` / `_assembleExportFilename` infrastructure as CSV, JSON, and Org-Mode. Features: (1) Preserves full cell innerHTML — hyperlinks and inline markup are kept intact, not flattened to plain text; (2) Images (``) are replaced with their alt text or a 🖼️ placeholder so the export is purely textual; (3) Script UI widgets (sort buttons, unique-value badges, resize handles, CAA/EAA toggle buttons) are stripped from the cloned cells before serialisation; (4) Multi-table pages are fully exported — each visible `table.tbl` becomes its own `
` with the h2/h3 heading above it; (5) An embedded minimal CSS stylesheet gives the output a clean MusicBrainz-style appearance (green headers, hover highlight, border-collapse) without any external dependencies; (6) Metadata (timestamp, URL, page type, entity name, button label, script name) is embedded as an HTML comment block immediately before ``; (7) Page h1 title is used as both `` and the `<h1>` at the top of the exported page, with a hyperlink back to the source URL.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: artist-releasegroups pageDefinition: re-introduced the two official-only fetch buttons '🧮 Official RGs' (`{ all: '0', va: '0' }`) and '🧮 Official VA RGs' (`{ all: '0', va: '1' }`) as the first two entries in the `buttons` array, before the existing combined '🧮 Artist RGs' and '🧮 Various Artists RGs' buttons. `params` alignment fixed to use consistent spacing across all four button entries.

Tooltip chain: added two new `else if` branches for the re-introduced buttons. Branch order (most-specific first): 'Official VA RGs' → 'Various Artists RGs' → 'Official RGs' → 'Artist RGs'. Tooltips: '🧮 Official RGs' → 'Fetch all official artist release groups from the MusicBrainz backend database'; '🧮 Official VA RGs' → 'Fetch all official various artists release groups from the MusicBrainz backend database'.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel font size: default startup font size raised from 88% to 125% (`_FS_DEFAULT = 125`). The slider maximum is also raised from 125% to 150% so the slider still has range above the new default.

Statistics panel font size: slider position is now persisted alongside position and size in GM storage key `sa_stats_panel_geometry` (new `fontSize` field). On the next open the stored font size is restored immediately via `_applyFs(_restoredFs)` — before the `setTimeout(0)` that restores position/size — so the panel never flickers at the wrong font size. The slider `input` event now calls `_saveGeo()` after each adjustment so the new size is saved immediately. The Reset button resets to the new 125% default.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Hotfix for 9.99.220: syntax error — `SyntaxError: Unexpected token ':'` at the '🔲 Total original columns' stat row in `globalRows`. The node heredoc script that wrote the globalRows block emitted the object comments and `stat:` key directly after the previous `},` without the required opening `{`. Fixed by inserting the missing `{` before the comment block. Additionally fixed a copy-paste error in the IDB comment string: `_cs.icon.idb` was used twice (`icon + icon`) instead of `icon + inline`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — Bug 1 (live artwork timing): the 'Network loading time' and 'IndexedDB loading time' rows in Global Statistics previously always showed '—' because `_caaFetchStats` is complete only after all images have loaded (async). Added a live-update hook at the end of `_showCaaCompletionToast`: when that function fires (indicating all artwork has loaded), it checks if the stats panel is open, forces a fresh `_captureStatsSnapshot()` call, updates any '—' timing cells by scanning for stat rows whose label contains 'Network loading time' / 'IndexedDB loading time', and fills in the elapsed time. Also updates the artwork count placeholder (`#mb-stats-art-count-val`) if the panel was opened before the count was known.

Statistics panel — Bug 2 (CAA/EAA label mismatch): all artwork-related strings in the statistics panel ('Network loading time (CAA)', 'IndexedDB loading time (CAA)', 'Total CAA front artwork count', 'Filtered CAA artwork count') were hardcoded to 'CAA'. On EAA pages (`addEAA: 'Event'`), these now correctly show 'EAA'. Fixed by adding `_getActiveArtCtx()` helper (returns `EAA_CTX` when `activeDefinition.features.addEAA` is set, `CAA_CTX` otherwise), deriving `_artKey = _artCtx.key.toUpperCase()` in `showStatsPanel`, and using `_artKey` in all stat row labels and the 'Filtered ${_artKey} artwork count' column header.
