Statistics panel — Bug 3 (EAA artwork count shows 0/—): `_captureStatsSnapshot` and the live per-table count in `showStatsPanel` were calling `_artCountLinks(CAA_CTX, tbl)` unconditionally. On EAA-only pages (e.g. /events), the CAA column does not exist so `_artCountLinks(CAA_CTX)` returns 0. Fixed by using `_getActiveArtCtx()` in both `_captureStatsSnapshot` (stores the art context key in `_statsSnapshot.artCtxKey`) and in the per-table `_caaCount` computation in `showStatsPanel` (`_artCountLinks(_artCtx, tbl)`, button fallback uses `_artCtx.btnPrefix + '-' + tIdx` and `_artCtx.countClass`).

Statistics panel — Bug 4 (wrong original/synthetic column counts): the column classifier only checked `th.dataset.colName` (set by cleanupHeaders injectors). But `th.style.backgroundColor` set to `sa_ui_thead_th_synthetic_bg` is the most reliable marker since it is always set by all three injector paths. Fixed in both `_captureStatsSnapshot` and the live fallback in `showStatsPanel`: added `||` check comparing `th.style.backgroundColor` (normalised) against the configured synthetic bg color. The `isSynth` flag is now also stored on each column datum in `_tableData.columns`, and the Table Detail column breakdown applies a semi-transparent tint (`synthBgColor + '55'`) to synthetic column rows as a visual side-effect.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — Bug 1 (new column-count rows): Global Statistics table now shows three column-count rows after 'Total CAA front artwork count': '🔲 Total original columns' (columns from the original MusicBrainz page table, identified as headers without `dataset.colName`), '✨ Total synthetic columns' (columns injected by columnExtractors / syntheticColumnExtractors / extractMainColumn, all of which set `th.dataset.colName`), and '⬛ Total columns' (total effective columns = original + synthetic). Values are sourced from the first-render `_statsSnapshot` (captured after `cleanupHeaders` runs) with a live-scan fallback.

Statistics panel — Bug 2 (panel only horizontally resizable): the panel used `overflow:hidden` which prevents the native browser resize handle from working vertically. Changed to `overflow:auto`; the inner body `div` already has `overflow-y:auto` for scrolling, so visual behavior is unchanged.

Statistics panel — Bug 3 (multi-table cards start expanded): in multi-table mode pages, each Table Detail card's column breakdown now starts COLLAPSED (▶) to avoid an overwhelming wall of data. Single-table pages continue to start EXPANDED (▼). `_startExpanded = (tableMode !== 'multi')` controls the initial `colToggleBar.dataset.colExpanded` and `colDetail.style.display`.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Config schema — new '↔️ COLUMN RESIZE FEATURE' section: `sa_enable_column_resizing` (moved from '🎨 UI FEATURES') and new `sa_auto_resize_columns` (boolean, default false). When `sa_auto_resize_columns` is true, `toggleAutoResizeColumns()` is called automatically via `setTimeout(0)` after the table is rendered — equivalent to manually clicking the '↔️ Resize' button on every page load.
Config schema — `sa_enable_stats_panel` moved from '🎨 UI FEATURES' to the '📊 STATISTICS PANEL' section where it logically belongs alongside the panel dimension settings.

Config schema — section ordering is now: … → '↔️ COLUMN RESIZE FEATURE' → '📊 STATISTICS PANEL' → '💾 LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK' → … The '// LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK SECTION' comment block now sits directly in front of `divider_save_load` as requested.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — Bug 1 (Global Stats): '🖼️ CAA artwork count' row renamed to '🖼️ Total CAA front artwork count' and '📋 Total table rows' row added (after Sub-tables). Both values are now sourced from a new first-render snapshot (`_statsSnapshot`) captured once by `_captureStatsSnapshot()` immediately after the initial render pipeline completes (called from both the single-table CAA-init path and the `renderGroupedTable` tail). The snapshot stores `totalRows` and `totalCaaCount` before any filter is applied, so re-opening the stats panel after filtering shows the stable baseline values rather than the filtered counts. A `captured` flag prevents re-capturing on subsequent filter/sort/view-switch re-renders.

Statistics panel — Bug 2 (Table Detail card): the 'Active filter' column in the card header (formerly 'Table filter') now shows the global filter value for single-table pages (where no per-table sub-table filter exists). The tooltip indicates the filter source: 'Sub-table filter: ...' or 'Global filter: ...' or 'No filter active'. The column label in row 2 was renamed from 'Table filter' to 'Active filter' to reflect this broader semantics.

Statistics panel — Bug 3: label 'Total artworks' in the Table Detail card header renamed to 'Filtered CAA artwork count' to accurately describe that the value shown is the count of CAA artwork links in the currently-filtered (visible) rows, not the unfiltered total (which now appears in the Global Statistics section).

Statistics panel — Bug 4: closing the panel when resizing via the native `resize:both` handle and releasing outside the panel bounds. Root cause: the browser fires a `click` event after `mouseup` at the release position (which is outside the panel), triggering `_onClickOutside` and closing the panel. Fixed with a `_lastMousedownInPanel` flag: a `mousedown` listener on the panel sets the flag true; the `_onClickOutside` handler checks the flag and suppresses the close if the drag started inside the panel, then clears the flag. A separate `document.mousedown` listener resets the flag on any click that starts genuinely outside, so normal outside-clicks still close the panel.

label-releases pageDefinition — `columnErasers` for the 'Release' source column now includes '➕' in addition to '▶', removing the plus-button glyphs from the Release cell text alongside the expand button glyphs.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — Table Detail card header: restored the original two-row structure. Row 1 (data): entity-type label | h1 entity name | active sub-table filter | row count | artwork count. Row 2 (labels): 'Table name' | table name from h3/h2 | 'Table filter' | 'Filtered rows' | 'Total artworks'. The entity-type label in row 1 col 1 is now derived from the last segment of the first action button's `data-label` attribute — e.g. 'Show all Events for Place' → 'Place'; 'Show all Releases for Artist' → 'Artist'. Falls back to 'h1' if no action button is found or the label does not match the 'Show all X for Y' pattern. The old 'h2/h3' label in row 2 col 1 is replaced with 'Table name'.

Statistics panel — '🖼️ CAA artwork count' row added to the Global Statistics table (after Sub-tables). Value is the sum of `_artCountLinks(CAA_CTX, tbl).count` across all sub-tables — a synchronous link scan that counts all unique artwork entity-href links regardless of image-load state. Previously this information was absent from the global section; the per-table artwork count in Table Detail was already correct (using _artCountLinks since 9.99.216), but there was no aggregate row in the global summary.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — 'Total artworks' column showed 0 or blank. Root cause: `_caaCount` was read from `mb-caa-toggle-btn-{idx}` badge which starts at 0 and only increments as images load (async). If the stats panel opened before all images finished loading, the badge showed a partial or zero count. Fixed by calling `_artCountLinks(CAA_CTX, tbl).count` directly, which synchronously counts all unique artwork entity-href links in the table's current tbody — the same source used to create the bigbox — with a fallback to the badge if the function is unavailable.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel — removed the 'h1' / 'h2/h3' identifier column from the Table Detail cards. The two-row layout (row 1: h1 text / row 2: h3 or h2 name) is replaced with a single clean two-row layout: row 1 is a column-label row ('Table', 'Table filter', 'Filtered rows', 'Total artworks'); row 2 is the data row showing the table name (from h3/h2), the active sub-table filter, the row count, and the artwork count. The h1 entity name (which is the same for every card on a page) is no longer shown inline per-card.

Statistics panel — font-size slider added. A 'Reset' button + range slider (65%–125%, step 5%, default 88%) + percentage label appear right-aligned inside the 'Global Statistics' section heading. Dragging the slider resizes the panel body font (`body.style.fontSize`). Clicking 'Reset' restores font size to 88% and also resets panel width/height to the configured sa_stats_panel_width / sa_stats_panel_max_height defaults.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — quick-filter highlight still only showed the first character despite the TreeWalker rewrite. Root cause: the mark-clear pass (`<mark>` → text node) split each originally-contiguous text into fragments; the normalize() call that should re-merge them only targeted `td[data-stats-td]` elements — but no `<td>` in the panel has that custom attribute, so normalization never actually ran. Fixed: the clear pass now calls `td.normalize()` on every `<td>` in the panel (unconditionally), and the per-row highlight pass also calls `tdEl.normalize()` immediately before calling `_highlightNode` so that by the time the TreeWalker runs it always sees single, unsplit text nodes regardless of how many mark-removal passes have occurred.

Statistics panel — qfClearBtn (✕ filter clear) was unclickable on first render before any drag/resize. Root cause: the drag `mousedown` handler on the header bar called `e.preventDefault()` which suppresses the native click-event generation chain on some browsers. Although the guard `e.target.closest('button, input')` was supposed to return early and skip `preventDefault`, the `stopPropagation` on the button's own `mousedown` meant the header bar's handler never saw the event — so the early-return never fired and `preventDefault` was called from a different code path. Fixed by removing `e.preventDefault()` from the drag mousedown entirely; text-selection during drag is prevented by the already-present `user-select:none` CSS on the header bar, making `preventDefault` redundant.
Statistics panel — restored the JSDoc block comment above `showStatsPanel` that was lost when the 9.99.213 str_replace inadvertently left an orphaned function body behind.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Hotfix for 9.99.213: the `str_replace` that introduced the revamped `showStatsPanel` matched only the JSDoc opening signature, leaving the entire old function body (739 lines) as orphaned top-level code after the new function's closing brace. Tampermonkey's bundler rejected the file with 'Uncaught SyntaxError: Failed to execute appendChild on Node: Unexpected token function'. Fixed by surgically removing the orphaned block (lines 8451–9189 of the 9.99.213 build).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Statistics panel — quick-filter highlight only worked for the first character of a multi-character query. Root cause: the previous `_highlightNode` implementation walked child nodes and mutated the DOM during the walk, so after inserting the first `<mark>` the remaining text nodes were already split and the loop lost track of where to find subsequent matches. Fixed by replacing the recursive walk with a `TreeWalker(SHOW_TEXT)` that first collects all leaf text nodes into a snapshot array, then processes each one independently — finding all occurrences of the query in that node's text in a single pass and replacing the node with a DocumentFragment containing alternating plain-text and `<mark>` nodes.

Statistics panel — per-table column detail tables now start UNCOLLAPSED (▼) when the panel opens, instead of collapsed (▶). `colToggleBar.dataset.colExpanded` is initialised to `'true'` and the detail `<div>` starts with no `display:none`.

Statistics panel — Escape key / ✕ icon two-press semantics: when the quick-filter field contains text, the first Escape (or clicking the ✕ icon next to the filter) now clears only the filter and keeps the panel open; a second Escape (or clicking the panel's ✕ close button) with an empty field closes the panel. Previously both keys always closed the panel.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel — drag-to-move: the header bar is now a drag handle (cursor:grab). Dragging moves the panel within the viewport (clamped to bounds). Position is saved to GM storage key `sa_stats_panel_geometry` on drag-end.

Statistics panel — resize persistence: the panel has `resize:both` (native browser resize handle at bottom-right). A ResizeObserver saves geometry (top, left, width, height) to `sa_stats_panel_geometry` with a 300 ms debounce on every resize. Geometry is restored on next open, clamped to the current viewport.

Statistics panel — new configSchema section '📊 STATISTICS PANEL' added with two settings: `sa_stats_panel_width` (max width in px, default 860) and `sa_stats_panel_max_height` (max height in vh, default 82), replacing the previously hardcoded `min(860px,92vw)` / `82vh` values.

Statistics panel — a dedicated ✕ clear-filter button is added next to the quick-filter input (separate from the panel close button) so the filter can be cleared without accidentally closing the panel.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Statistics panel revamp (single-table mode): the old compact grid panel is replaced with a full-width scrollable panel (max 860 px, 82 vh) containing two sections. (1) Global Statistics table with 14 rows covering: 🌐 Network/🗄️ IndexedDB CAA loading time (from _caaFetchStats telemetry, split by tier), 💾 Memory usage (JS heap via performance.memory when available in Chromium; 'n/a' otherwise — replaces the old fictional 100-bytes-per-row estimate), 📄 Page type, 🗂️ Table mode, 📑 Sub-tables, ⬛/👁️/🙈 Total/Visible/Hidden columns, 🔍 Global filter, 📋 Sub-table filters, 🔠 Column filters, ↔️ Resize status, 📐 Manually resized columns. (2) Per-table detail section with a card per sub-table showing h1/h2-h3 header text, active table filter, filtered/total row count, and CAA artwork count in a header row, followed by a ▶ collapsable per-column table with 8 columns: #, Column name (strikethrough when hidden), 👁️ Visible, ↕ Sort direction (▲ asc / ▼ desc / ⇅ none), 📊 Unique value count, 🗋 Multi-row cell count, 🔍 Column filter value, ↔️ Resize state (manually N px or default). A quick-filter input at the top of the panel filters and highlights matching text across all rows in both sections. Panel closes on ✕ click, Escape key, or click outside.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups — h2 global CAA artwork badge count wrong after switching merged → non-official (showed merged total e.g. 32 instead of non-official total e.g. 9). Root cause: every `img.onload` handler inside `_artInitBigPics` unconditionally increments the global badge (`globalBadge.textContent + 1`). When `_artRebuildBigPicsForTable` is called in the restore pre-pass (switching away from merged), it queues new async image loads for the restored official table. Those `img.onload` callbacks fire AFTER `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton` has already computed the correct non-official count — but the callbacks do not check whether their toggle button's owning h3 is currently hidden by the active discography view filter. Each callback increments the global badge unconditionally, adding the entire official section's artwork count on top of the already-correct non-official count. Fixed by adding a `closest('h3.mb-toggle-h3')` → `dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true'` guard inside the img.onload global-badge increment path: if the toggle button sits inside a h3 that is currently hidden by the discography view filter, the global badge increment is skipped.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups 'merged' view — CAA/EAA artwork toggle-button badge showed stale count from the official section only (e.g. 'Album + Live' showed 22 instead of 32 = 23 official + 9 non-official). Root cause: `_artInitPics` builds each bigbox from the table's current tbody links and keys it by the table's DOM position index. When the merged pass replaced the tbody with combined rows, `_artInitPics` was not re-run for that table, so the bigbox and toggle-button badge still reflected only the original official rows. Fix: added `_artRebuildBigPicsForTable(table)` helper that mirrors the single-table path of `_artInitPics` (count links → create/update toggle button → create/update retry button → init bigbox) for both CAA_CTX and EAA_CTX. Called immediately after the merged tbody fill and also in the restore pre-pass (when leaving merged view) so bigbox counts are always in sync with the current tbody content.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Bug 1 — 'Complete Discography (merged)' view: after switching away from merged and back, or after visiting official/non-official view after a merged pass, row counts inflated on repeated cycles (e.g. 'Album' official showed 34 instead of 32 after merged filled it with 32+2=34 combined rows). Root cause: the merged tbody fill used `_tbody.innerHTML = ''` which detached the original row DOM nodes from the tbody, then inserted cloneNode copies. On switching back to 'all', the 'all' restore path left the merged-filled tbody untouched (34 clones), so every subsequent view of that table showed the inflated count. The original row DOM nodes in `groupedRows[idx].rows` were still referenced but were now detached orphans — so the next merged pass pushed 32 detached originals + 2 non-official = 34 instead of 36. Fix: (a) each first-occurrence table in merged mode is now marked with `data-mb-merged-tbody='true'`; (b) a new pre-pass at the start of `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` restores every marked table's tbody from `groupedRows[idx].rows` (re-inserting the original live row nodes, not clones) before applying the new section; (c) the merged tbody fill continues to use `cloneNode(true)` for the combined rows so the originals in `groupedRows` are never detached.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Button label fix: '🧮 Various Artist RGs' renamed to '🧮 Various Artists RGs' (plural 'Artists') in both `pageDefinitions` entries (main fetch `label` and pre-fetch `mainLabel`) and the tooltip `includes()` check.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Bug 1 — 'Various Artist RGs' button tooltip was showing the same text as 'Artist RGs'. Root cause: the `else if` chain checked `conf.label.includes('Artist RGs')` before `conf.label.includes('Various Artist RGs')`. Since the VA label ('🧮 Various Artist RGs') is a superset of 'Artist RGs', it always matched the first branch and never reached the VA branch. Fixed by swapping the order: 'Various Artist RGs' is now checked first.

Bug 2 — CAA/EAA per-table and global artwork toggle buttons stopped working after a discography view switch. Root cause: the previous fix for the global badge count set `_artBtn.style.display = 'none'` explicitly on toggle buttons inside hidden h3s. This interfered with `_artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton`'s re-render path which unconditionally resets `btn.style.display = 'inline-flex'` on every `_artInitPics` pass, overwriting the 'none'. Worse, the explicit 'none' broke the per-table button's own click handler (which looks up the bigbox by ID and toggles its display — a side-effect-free operation that cannot be broken by display manipulation on the button itself, but the user interaction was impossible with a visually hidden button). New approach: instead of manipulating art button display directly, we tag the h3 element with `data-mb-disc-hidden='true'` when hiding it, and patched `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton` to skip sub-table toggle buttons whose `closest('h3.mb-toggle-h3')` has `dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true'`. The global toggle click handler was also patched to skip bigboxes belonging to hidden-section h3s.

Bug 3 — Density (and all other) buttons appeared broken after collapsing then re-expanding the h2 section header while a discography view filter (Official/Non-Official/merged) was active. Root cause: `makeH2sCollapsible`'s `contentNodes` array is captured at page-render time — before `_artInitBigPics` inserts bigbox divs, and before `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` sets `h3.style.display = 'none'`. On h2 expansion, the handler set `node.style.display = ''` on ALL h3 nodes in contentNodes unconditionally, erasing the `display:none` that our view filter had set. This left ALL h3s visible regardless of the active view, creating an inconsistent state (wrong h2 heading, wrong row counts, wrong button tints, misplaced bigboxes) that made every subsequent button click appear non-functional. Fixed: the h2 expansion path now checks `node.dataset.mbDiscHidden === 'true'` and skips restoring h3s (and their associated tables) that are currently hidden by the active discography view. Fixed additionally: the h2 collapse path now calls `_artHideBigboxesForTable(node)` for each table in contentNodes before hiding it, preventing CAA/EAA artwork stripes from floating when the h2 is collapsed (this was a pre-existing bug exposed when bigboxes were present).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups — 'Complete Discography (merged)' view: categories that appeared only in the official OR only in the non-official section were not always shown in merged view. Root cause: the category name used as the merge key was read from `h3_all_category_header_array[_idx]` in a two-pass approach where the row-accumulation pass re-scanned all h3s for each first-occurrence. This worked in simple cases but was fragile when the array and DOM were out of alignment or when `h3_all_category_header_array` was shorter than the DOM h3 count. Fixed with a single clean pass that builds `_mergedFirstIdxByName` (first DOM index per category), `_mergedRowsByName` (pre-accumulated combined rows per category), and `_mergedVisibleIndices` (DOM indices of visible h3s) simultaneously. The category key now uses `h3_all_category_header_array[_idx]` with a text-node fallback so every h3 is covered.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactoring / Cleanup
items: Artist-Releasegroups — removed `h3_all_category_unique_header_array` and all its references (declaration, three reset sites, Step 2 build, debug log, inject-buttons log). The array was only used as the conceptual basis for the 'merged' view; the merged algorithm now derives the unique-category set directly from `h3_all_category_header_array` at view-apply time without needing a pre-built unique list.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups — Bug 1 ('Complete Discography' button not green initially): `_applyDiscButtonTints('all')` was called before the buttons were inserted into the live DOM, so `document.getElementById(id)` returned null for all four buttons and no tint was applied. Fixed by moving the `_applyDiscButtonTints` call to after the `controlsContainer.insertBefore` block that inserts the buttons.

Artist-Releasegroups — Bug 3 (global CAA artwork badge shows stale total after view switch): The global CAA/EAA toggle button in the h2 aggregates its count from per-sub-table `.mb-caa-art-toggle-btn` elements whose own `style.display !== 'none'`. When an h3 is hidden (`h3.style.display = 'none'`), the toggle button inside it inherits visibility from its parent but its own inline `style.display` remains `'inline-flex'`, so it passes the filter and inflates the global badge. Fixed by explicitly setting `display:none` on every `.mb-caa-art-toggle-btn` and `.mb-eaa-art-toggle-btn` inside hidden h3s (and `display:inline-flex` inside visible ones) during `_applyDiscographyViewFilter`, then calling `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(CAA_CTX)` and `_artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton(EAA_CTX)` immediately after to recompute the badge from the corrected button states.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Artist-Releasegroups — 'Complete Discography (merged)' mode now truly merges rows: when switching to 'merged' view, same-named h3 groups (e.g. an 'Album + Compilation' that exists in both the official and non-official sections) are combined — the first-occurrence h3/table is shown and its tbody is repopulated with all rows from every groupedRows entry sharing that category name (official + non-official), giving a true union. The h3 row-count stat and `data-mb-total-rows` are updated to the combined count. Subsequent duplicate h3/table pairs for the same name are hidden. Returning to 'all' view restores the original per-section tables (groupedRows entries are not modified, only cloneNode copies are used).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — Bug 1 (artwork stripes floating): `_artInitBigPics` inserts `div.mb-caa-bigbox` / `div.mb-eaa-bigbox` elements immediately before each `table.tbl` in the DOM. The prior hide/show logic used `h3.nextElementSibling` to locate the table, which returned the bigbox div when artwork had been loaded — the bigbox was neither hidden nor shown, causing it to float above (or below) all sub-tables when switching views. Fixed by walking forward from the h3 collecting all consecutive bigbox divs then the table, and synchronising their visibility with the h3/table pair in all cases: force-hidden when hiding a section; restored to their tracked `caaVisible`/`eaaVisible` dataset state when revealing a section.

Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — Bug 2/3a (h2 count shows 'X of grand-total'): the h2 `mb-row-count-stat` was formatted as '(sectionRows of grandTotal)' which is misleading because in a single-section view the user is not filtering — they are simply seeing that section's own data. Fixed by always displaying only `(sectionRowTotal)` in the h2 stat; the total is accumulated as the sum of `groupedRows[idx].rows.length` for all visible h3 indices, making it self-consistent for every view mode.

Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — Bug 3b (h3 sub-table count '168 of 36'): `updateSubTableRowCount()` reads `table.dataset.mbTotalRows` to determine the denominator. This dataset attribute was set at initial render time from `group.rows.length` using the combined-fetch index, so switching views could leave a table tagged with the wrong group's row count (e.g. official 'Album + Compilation' has 36 rows but non-official has 168). Fixed by patching `table.dataset.mbTotalRows` to `groupedRows[idx].rows.length` for each visible table when applying a specific section view.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Artist-Releasegroups discography view buttons — added '📋 Complete Discography' button as the first (leftmost) button in the group. It shows the full combined discography (same as the initial render state) and starts with the active green tint since that is what is displayed after the initial render. Clicking any other view button deactivates it; clicking it again from any other view resets back to the complete view.

Artist-Releasegroups discography view buttons — added '🗂️ Complete Discography (merged)' button as the last button in the group. It shows all h3/table pairs but hides duplicate category names (using h3_all_category_unique_header_array), so each category appears only once regardless of whether it exists in both the official and non-official sections.

Artist-Releasegroups discography view buttons — the green active-tint always follows the last-clicked button. Extracted into a dedicated `_applyDiscButtonTints(activeSection)` helper that manages all four button IDs uniformly.

Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — h2 heading labels updated: 'all' → 'Complete Discography', 'merged' → 'Complete Discography (merged)', 'official' → 'Official Discography', 'non-official' → 'Non-Official Discography'. The heading strip regex was also updated to cover the new 'Complete' prefix variants.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — table show/hide on 'all' (reset) was incorrect: when restoring to the combined view, the code was re-evaluating the h3 expand/collapse icon state and setting `table.style.display` accordingly, which forced tables back to hidden if their h3 happened to be in the collapsed state. Fix: when `section === 'all'` and the h3 is being un-hidden, the table's `display` property is left untouched — the h3 click-handler already owns that state — so only the h3 visibility is restored.

Artist-Releasegroups `_applyDiscographyViewFilter` — h2 header rename regex was too greedy: the previous strip pattern `replace(/\b(Official|Non-Official)\s*Discography\b/i, '')` + `replace(/\bDiscography\b/i, '')` could leave stale fragments in multi-word headings. Replaced with a single anchored pattern `replace(/\b(Official\s+|Non-Official\s+)?Discography\b.*$/i, '')` that strips from 'Discography' to the end in one pass, then the new label is appended cleanly.

Artist-Releasegroups Step 4 (h3 stamping) — removed dead `_section` variable that was computed using a Set-based heuristic but immediately discarded because the replay-loop below it independently recomputed `_sectionTag`. The dead code could have caused confusion during future maintenance.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Artist-Releasegroups pre-fetch pass (Bug 1) — fetchProgressOuter / fetchProgressWrap was not visible during pass 1 of 2 because `fetchProgressWrap.style.display = 'inline-flex'` was only set later in the main-fetch UI setup block (after the isLoaded / stopOtherScripts gates). Fixed by explicitly showing the wrap and initialising fetchProgressFill at the very start of the pre-fetch block, before the per-page label updates are written.

Artist-Releasegroups pre-fetch pass (Bug 2) — the two new 'Official Discography' / 'Non-Official Discography' buttons never appeared on the final rendered page. Root cause: a second unconditional reset of all four h3 category arrays (including h3_official_category_header_array) was executed inside the main-fetch setup block (the `stopRequested = false; allRows = []; …` section), which runs after the pre-fetch pass completes and wiped the 17 official categories that were just collected. Because h3_official_category_header_array.length was then 0, the guard condition `&& h3_official_category_header_array.length > 0` was never satisfied and `_injectDiscographyViewButtons()` was never called. Fixed by replacing the full-array reset with a partial reset that only clears h3_all_category_header_array, h3_all_category_unique_header_array, and h3_non_official_category_header_array (the arrays populated by the main fetch), deliberately preserving h3_official_category_header_array which was already populated by the pre-fetch pass.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Artist-Releasegroups — Official / Non-Official Discography split: for pages with pageType 'artist-releasegroups' a new two-pass fetch pipeline is introduced. Pass 1 (pre-fetch) fetches only the official (all:'0') pages and extracts the ordered h3 category header names into `h3_official_category_header_array`, without rendering anything. Pass 2 is the existing combined (all:'1') main fetch. After rendering, `h3_all_category_header_array` is built from groupedRows; `h3_all_category_unique_header_array` (de-duplicated, first-occurrence order) and `h3_non_official_category_header_array` (entries not in the leading official sequence) are derived from it. Every rendered h3.mb-toggle-h3 element (and its following table.tbl) is stamped with `data-mb-disc-section='official'` or `data-mb-disc-section='non-official'`. Two new buttons — '📀 Official Discography' and '📼 Non-Official Discography' — are injected into the controls bar; clicking one hides the opposite section's h3/table pairs, renames the h2 header, and adjusts the `mb-row-count-stat` span to reflect only visible rows. Clicking the active button again resets to the combined 'all' view. Pre-fetch progress is surfaced via `globalStatusDisplay` ('Loading (pass 1 of 2)…') and the fetch progress bar (light-blue fill). The subType:'artist-releasegroups' entry in `pageDefinitions` provides per-button `pre_fetch_type:'Official'` and the official-only params used by the pre-fetch pass.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load dialog filter-input auto-resize — typing into the filter field was shrinking the dialog width even after the user had manually resized it (or a saved size was restored from GM storage). Root cause: resizeDialog() unconditionally overwrote dialog.style.width on every input event with no awareness of a user-set size. Fixed by introducing a _userResized flag that is set to true when (a) a stored geometry is restored from GM_getValue on dialog open, or (b) the ResizeObserver fires for the first time after the initial layout paint (i.e. the user drags the resize handle). resizeDialog() now returns immediately when _userResized is true, so the text-fit heuristic never overrides a user-chosen width.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Clear (✕) buttons on all three filter inputs (#sa-filter-clear-btn, #sa-hist-qf-clear-btn, #sa-epl-qf-clear-btn) — icon is now rendered bold (font-weight:bold) for improved visual weight and legibility.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load dialog filter input (#sa-load-filter-input) — Escape key now uses two-press semantics: first press clears the filter text and keeps the dialog open (focus stays in the input); second press (field already empty) closes the dialog. Previously, a single Escape always closed the dialog regardless of whether the field was non-empty.

Edit Pinned Filter List dialog quick-filter (#sa-epl-qf) — Escape key now uses two-press semantics: first press clears the quick-filter field and keeps the dialog open; second press (field already empty) triggers the Cancel action. Previously, a single Escape always cancelled the dialog.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: History dropdown quick-filter (#sa-hist-quick-filter) — added an inline red clear (✕) button absolutely positioned at the right edge of the input field, matching the existing pattern of #sa-load-filter-input. The button is hidden when the field is empty and appears when it contains text. Clicking it clears the quick-filter, refreshes the list and returns focus to the input. tabindex=-1 keeps it out of the tab order.
Edit Pinned Filter List dialog quick-filter (#sa-epl-qf) — added the same inline red clear (✕) button as above.
Load dialog filter input (#sa-load-filter-input) clear button — changed color from grey (#aaa / hover #555) to red (#c0392b / hover #7b241c) to match the two new clear buttons.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load dialog filter row — fix broken HTML structure introduced in 9.99.198: a misplaced was closing the outer flex row immediately after the input wrapper, pushing the '+' pin button and 'History' toggle button outside the flex container and onto a new line at reduced size. Fixed by removing the extra so all three elements (input wrapper, pin button, history button) remain correctly inside the flex row.

History dropdown quick-filter — pressing Escape when the quick-filter field is non-empty was also closing the Load Table Data dialog. Root cause: stopPropagation only stops DOM bubbling but not other document-level keydown listeners. Fixed by using stopImmediatePropagation, which prevents the load dialog's handleKey from also receiving the Escape event. Behaviour is now: first Escape clears the quick-filter and keeps the panel open; second Escape (field empty) closes the panel only, never the load dialog.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Edit Pinned Filter List dialog — pressing Escape, clicking Save or Cancel now closes only the edit sub-dialog, not the parent Load Table Data dialog. Root cause: _eplHandleKey used stopPropagation which does not prevent other document-level listeners from firing. Fixed by using stopImmediatePropagation and registering the handler in capture phase (useCapture=true) so it intercepts events before the load dialog's bubbling handler.

Edit Pinned Filter List dialog — clicking inside the dialog (including the quick-filter input) was triggering the load dialog's outside-click handler and closing it. Fixed by exempting clicks whose target is inside #sa-edit-pin-list-dialog from the load dialog's _outsideHandler.

Load dialog Phase 2 — Case Sensitive and Regular Expression checkbox labels were missing title tooltip attributes. Added: 'Match filter expression with exact case (uppercase/lowercase must match)' and 'Interpret filter expression as a regular expression (JavaScript RegExp syntax)'.
Prefilter toggle button — changed label text segment from ' out of N' to ' of N' and shortened title from 'Toggle prefilter highlighting on/off' to 'Toggle prefilter highlighting' (both occurrences).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load dialog filter input — added an inline clear (✕) button (#sa-filter-clear-btn) absolutely positioned at the right edge of the input field. The button is hidden when the field is empty and shown when it contains text. Clicking it clears the input and returns focus to the field. tabindex=-1 keeps it out of the tab order.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load dialog filter history — add a second persistent 'Pinned' list stored under GM key 'persistent-sa-hist-list'. The existing LRU list is renamed internally to 'lru-sa-hist-list' (with migration from the old 'sa_load_filter_history' key). The history dropdown now shows both lists in two independently scrollable sections: 📌 Pinned (green header) above 🕐 Recent/LRU (blue header), each with its own count footer. The quick-filter searches both lists simultaneously.

Filter input row: new '+' pin button (green, id: sa-hist-pin-btn) between the filter input and the History toggle. Clicking it saves the current filter expression + checkbox states to the persistent list (deduplicates by query). A brief green flash confirms the save.
Checkbox row: new '✎ Edit Pinned' button (id: sa-hist-edit-btn, amber style, Alt+E) added to the right of the checkbox group.

New showEditPersistentListDialog() — full-featured modal for managing the persistent list. Features: sortable table view with # / Filter Expression / Cs / Re / Ex columns; quick-filter with yellow highlight; Add (Alt+A) / Edit (Alt+E) / Remove (Alt+R) operations via an inline form; Save (Alt+S) and Cancel (Alt+C / Escape) with unsaved-changes warning; double-click a row to open it for editing; draggable header. Changes are only committed to GM storage when Save is clicked.

History dropdown keyboard navigation updated for two-list model: ArrowDown flows from quick-filter → pin list → lru list; ArrowUp flows in reverse; Escape from list → quick-filter focus; Escape with quick-filter empty → close panel.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load Table Data dialog — replace hardcoded 'min-width:320px' with configurable 'sa_ld_dialog_min_width' (default 640 px, range 280–1200, in the 💾 LOAD AND SAVE section after 'sa_ld_dialog_width'). The value is read with robust numeric coercion so both string and number values from GM storage are handled correctly.

Load Table Data dialog — persist position and geometry across sessions using GM storage key 'sa_load_dialog_geometry' ({top, left, width, height}). Geometry is saved: (1) on drag-end after moving the dialog; (2) debounced 300 ms after any ResizeObserver callback (i.e. when the user drags the resize handle); (3) on dialog close (closeDialog). On open, if stored geometry exists it is restored and clamped to the current viewport so the dialog never opens fully off-screen. The ResizeObserver is disconnected in closeDialog to avoid memory leaks. The button-anchor and centred-fallback positioning only apply when no stored geometry exists.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load dialog — replace the simple filter-history dropdown with a full-featured history panel. History entries are now stored as objects {query, useCase, useRegex, useExclude} instead of plain strings (legacy string entries are transparently normalised on read). New configSchema setting 'sa_load_history_dropdown_size' (default 10, range 3–50) controls how many rows are visible at once in the scrollable list; 'sa_load_history_limit' max raised from 50 to 200. History toggle button redesigned: '🕐 History ▼' label with Alt+H shortcut underline.

History panel features: (1) quick-filter input at the top — typing narrows the list in real-time with the matched substring highlighted in yellow; (2) scrollable, keyboard-navigable entry list showing each query with coloured badge glyphs for active flags (Cs=Case Sensitive in blue, Re=Regular Expression in purple, Ex=Exclude in red); (3) entry count footer; (4) clicking or pressing Enter on an entry applies the query AND restores its checkbox states, then closes the panel and focuses the filter input.

Keyboard behaviour: ArrowDown from quick-filter moves focus to the list; ArrowUp past the top returns focus to quick-filter; Escape inside the list returns focus to quick-filter; Escape with quick-filter non-empty clears it; Escape with quick-filter empty closes the panel (dialog stays open); second Escape with panel closed closes the dialog. Alt+H toggles the panel from anywhere in the dialog.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load Table Data dialog — 'Render All Rows' button: changed background from grey (#f0f0f0) to green (#4CAF50, hover #45a049) matching the 'Load Data' button; renamed label from '▶ Render all' to '▶ Render All Rows'.

Load Table Data dialog — keyboard shortcut for 'Render All Rows' changed from Alt+W to Alt+R (context-sensitive): when Phase 3 ('Render Data') is visible Alt+R fires renderBtn as before; when Phase 2 is visible and Phase 3 is hidden (i.e. the user has not yet run Filter Data), Alt+R fires renderNoFilterBtn instead. The two phases are mutually exclusive so there is no ambiguity.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Load Table Data dialog — add '▶ Render all' button (id: sa-render-no-filter-confirm) placed to the right of the 'Filter Data' button in Phase 2, using the same flex:2/flex:1 layout as the 'Load Data'/'Cancel' pair. Clicking it clears the filter input and all checkboxes (Case Sensitive, Regular Expression, Exclude Matches), syncs the cleared state to the always-visible prefilter bar, then calls loadTableDataFromDisk with empty filter params — rendering all rows from the loaded file without any filtering. This bypasses Phase 3 entirely. Keyboard shortcut: Alt+W (without filtering). Also adds hover (#e0e0e0) and active (translateY/inset shadow) CSS for the new button, consistent with the existing Cancel button style.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: sa_dialog_save_export_autoclose_ms was not honoured in showSaveDialog or showExportDialog. Root cause: the guard 'typeof autoCloseMs === "number"' is always false when VZ_MBLibrary returns the setting value as a string (GM storage is string-based), so the hardcoded 1800 ms fallback was always used regardless of what the user had configured. Fixed by replacing the typeof guard with robust numeric coercion: 'Number.isFinite(+_rawAutoClose) ? Math.max(0, Math.round(+_rawAutoClose)) : 1800'. This correctly handles both string ('3000') and numeric (3000) values from GM storage, preserves 0 as a valid disable value (unlike '|| 1800' which would treat 0 as falsy), and falls back to 1800 only when the setting is absent or non-numeric.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Export filenames (CSV, JSON, Org-Mode) now use the same naming convention as Save-to-Disk, e.g. MB-Artist-(Bruce Springsteen)-Relationships-composed-(894)_2026-03-16T12-21-41.csv. Previously they used the old musicbrainz-<pageType>-<timestamp>.<ext> scheme.

Extract shared _assembleExportFilename(extension, rowCount) helper that encapsulates all filename slug logic (toSlug, toTitleSlug, detailSlug computation, entity/section assembly, settings-controlled prefix/rowCount/timestamp/detail segments). Returns both the assembled filename string and the four metadata components (entityType, entityName, sectionSuffix, detailSegment) so callers avoid redundant computation. saveTableDataToDisk now delegates to this helper instead of containing its own ~80-line filename assembly block, which is removed entirely.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Add configSchema setting 'sa_dialog_save_export_autoclose_ms' (label: 'Save / Export dialog auto-close delay (ms)', default: 1800, range: 0–30000) in the 💾 LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK section, after 'sa_ui_download_notification_font_size'. Controls how long the 'Save Table Data' (showSaveDialog) and 'Export Table Data' (showExportDialog) dialogs remain open after a download has been initiated before auto-closing. Setting to 0 disables auto-close entirely — the dialog must then be dismissed manually via Escape, outside-click, or the × drag-handle button. Both dialogs now read this setting instead of using the previously hardcoded 1800 ms value.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Replace the old showExportNotification popup for all three export formats (CSV, JSON, Org-Mode) with a new showExportDialog() — a full dialog matching the Save Table Data dialog in look, feel, and behaviour: same CSS/settings keys, draggable header, Escape/outside-click dismissal, resizable, Alt+S shortcut, 1.8 s auto-close after download.

showExportDialog is parametrized per-format: header shows '📥 Export Table Data — <format>', a format-specific HTML description explains the output structure and how metadata is embedded, the shared buildMetaBlockHTML() block previews what will be written, an editable monospace filename input is pre-filled with the default filename, and Save Data / Cancel buttons drive the download.

Add _buildExportMetaLines(prefix) helper: builds a plain-text comment block (exported-at, URL, pageType, entityType, entityName, buttonLabel, script) with a configurable line prefix (e.g. '# ' for CSV/Org-Mode) to prepend before data rows.
Add _buildExportMetaObject() helper: builds the equivalent as a JS object for JSON exports, embedded as a top-level 'meta' property in the exported JSON structure.
CSV export: metadata now prepended as '# comment' lines before the header row.
JSON export: metadata now embedded as a top-level 'meta' object inside the exported JSON (alongside 'headers' and 'rows').
Org-Mode export: metadata now prepended as '# comment' lines before the org-table header.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Replace the old 'showDownloadNotification' popup for the Save-to-Disk path with a full 'Save Table Data' dialog (id: sa-save-dialog-overlay). The new dialog matches the look and feel of the 'Load Table Data' dialog: same width/font-size settings (sa_ld_dialog_width, sa_ld_dialog_header_font_size, sa_ld_status_font_size), draggable via the header, dismissable with Escape or outside-click, resizable. It shows: (1) a header with description text, (2) the shared metadata block (entity type/name, section, detail, page type, table mode, rows, button label, saved timestamp, version, URL), (3) an editable filename input pre-filled with the default assembled filename, (4) 'Save Data' (Alt+S) and 'Cancel' buttons. On Save the download is triggered, the top-bar info display is updated, a brief confirmation line appears, and the dialog auto-closes after 1.8 s. Cancel revokes the blob URL without downloading.

Extract shared buildMetaBlockHTML(data, totalRows, file) helper used by both the Save dialog and the Load dialog, eliminating ~90 lines of duplicated rendering code. The Load dialog's inline meta block is now a single buildMetaBlockHTML() call.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: saveTableDataToDisk — entityName / sectionSuffix were still null in the saved JSON despite the 9.99.186 fix: root cause was that all metadata field assignments (dataToSave.entityName, dataToSave.sectionSuffix) happened AFTER JSON.stringify(dataToSave) in the code, so the serialized string always captured the initial null values. Fixed by moving ALL metadata computation (toSlug, toTitleSlug, extractEntityName, detailSlug, entityName, sectionSuffix, entityType) to before the JSON.stringify call, and writing all four fields into dataToSave before serialization.

saveTableDataToDisk — replace the two ad-hoc fields (entityName, sectionSuffix) with four fields that exactly mirror the filename components: entityType (e.g. 'Artist'), entityName (e.g. 'Bruce Springsteen'), sectionSuffix (e.g. 'Relationships'), detailSegment (e.g. 'composed'). This makes the JSON self-documenting and consistent with the filename format.

Load Table Data dialog — meta block updated to show all four new fields (Entity type, Entity name, Section, Detail). Legacy files (pre-9.99.187) that lack these fields fall back to parsing the filename: entityType from the token before the first '(' in the filename, entityName from the first '(…)' token, section from the text between the first and last '(…)' tokens, detail shows '—' since the slug cannot be reliably reconstructed from the filename alone.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: saveTableDataToDisk — entityName was always null in the saved JSON: root cause was that extractEntityName() calls document.querySelector('h1') at save time, but by then the DOM has already been mutated by performClutterCleanup / cleanupAfterInitialLoad (and on -filtered relationship pages MusicBrainz may render the h1 without a <bdi>). Fixed by introducing a module-level _cachedEntityName variable that captures the entity name immediately at init time from the pristine DOM (using the already-resolved headerContainer), before any DOM mutations occur. saveTableDataToDisk now uses _cachedEntityName with extractEntityName() as a late fallback.

saveTableDataToDisk — sectionSuffix was wrong for -filtered relationship pages (e.g. artist-relationships-filtered): the stored value was the bare page-type section ('Relationships') instead of the meaningful relationship-type label ('instruments'). Fixed by storing detailSlug directly as sectionSuffix for filtered pages, and storing section + detailSlug for normal multi-button pages.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: saveTableDataToDisk: add 'entityName' (e.g. 'Bruce Springsteen', extracted from the h1 <bdi> element) and 'sectionSuffix' (e.g. 'Relationships-published', the Title-cased page-section portion of the slug plus any detail segment) to the serialized JSON metadata. Both fields are populated after slug assembly so they reflect exactly what appears in the filename.

Load Table Data dialog: after the '✅ Loaded N rows from …' status line, render a collapsible '📄 File Metadata' table block separated by a horizontal divider. The block shows: Entity, Section, Page type (with '(filtered)' badge when applicable), Table mode (coloured multi/single badge), Rows, Button label, Saved (human-readable local timestamp), Version, and URL (clickable link). Legacy files that predate the entityName/sectionSuffix fields display '—' for those fields, with the entity name falling back to a '(from filename)' parse of the first parenthesised token in the filename. The meta block is hidden on error or page-type-mismatch cancel.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML: move the toggle arrow glyph (▶/◀) out of the first flex column and render it as a standalone leading span (align-self:center) in front of both side-by-side columns. The first flex column now contains only 'Expand'/'Collapse' + 'ALL'.
collapseHdrBtn tooltips: capitalize 'all' → 'ALL' in both the initial Expand title and the click-handler Collapse/Expand titles, consistent with the global button tooltip style.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: mb-col-collapse-all-btn: restructure button label so arrow, action word ('Expand'/'Collapse'), and 'ALL' stack vertically in one column (centered), followed by a second column with 'multi-row' / 'cells' — also centered. Previously the arrow and action word were a single inline span.
mb-col-collapse-all-btn: update tooltip from 'Expand/Collapse all …' to 'Expand/Collapse ALL … in EVERY …' to match the visual emphasis.
collapseHdrBtn (per-column header button): include the multi-row cell count in the tooltip for both Expand and Collapse states, e.g. 'Expand all multi-row "Title" cells (12) in this table column'.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Harmonize tooltips

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix ergInjectCaaInlineThumbnails not injecting thumbnails into expanded release-group sub-tables (e.g. on Artist-Recordings pages, 'Release groups' column). Root cause: the querySelectorAll selector used 'tbody tr td:first-child a[href^="/release/"]', which requires an explicit <tbody> wrapper. However, ergParseReleaseGroup builds the sub-table DOM imperatively (createElement + appendChild), so no implicit <tbody> is created and the selector matched nothing. Fixed by removing 'tbody' from the selector: 'tr td:first-child a[href^="/release/"]'. The table reference is already scoped to the specific sub-table, so no false positives are introduced.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix CAA/EAA inline expand button tooltip showing wrong archive label after clicking. Root cause: ensureCollapseDelegate is idempotent — only the first caller's ctx is captured in the tbody click-handler closure. Since initCaaPics always runs before initEaaPics, EAA cells always got 'CAA cover art images' in the tooltip on click. Fixed by deriving the label dynamically from the DOM inside the click handler (.mb-eaa-count-badge presence distinguishes EAA from CAA) instead of relying on the closed-over ctx.toggleLabel. Secondary fixes: (1) rebuild-branch title format had (n) before the label instead of after — now consistent with the canonical 'Verb all Label (n) in this cell' format used by the click handler and first-build; (2) _stripTransientCellState now also resets btn.title to the correct collapsed tooltip when forcing the collapsed state, preventing a stale expanded tooltip from surviving a collapse-all or load-from-disk reset.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: ERG (Expand Release Groups): inject CAA front-cover inline thumbnails into the release rows of expanded release-group sub-tables. When a release-group ERG button is clicked and its release list appears, each release row now gets a 20×20 px mb-caa-inline-ph thumbnail (identical in style to the inline thumbnails in the main table) inserted after its ▶ expand button. Fetches are throttled through the shared _caaQueue, use the full three-tier IDB/memory/network cache stack, and honour sa_enable_caa_pics, sa_caa_pics_inline, sa_caa_hover_preview, and sa_rt_enable/sa_rt_show_inline. Releases with no front image simply show an empty spacer.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Four fixes for the two-pass Artist RGs / VA RGs split view: (1) Action button green background on success — activeBtn.style.backgroundColor='#ccffcc' is now set at the end of the multi-pass block, matching the normal single-pass path. (2) Global super-header h2 click events — makeH2sCollapsible() assigns contentNodes=[] to the global h2 (the two section h2s are h2s themselves so the walker stops before collecting them). After makeH2sCollapsible() runs, the global h2's _mbToggle is overridden to call both _officialH2._mbToggle and _nonOffH2._mbToggle with the same forceExpand value, so clicking or Ctrl-clicking the global h2 collapses/expands all h3/table content in both sections. (3) Section filter highlight — _makeSectionFilter now clears existing mb-global-filter-highlight marks before each filter run (_clearHighlights walks the cell and replaces <mark> spans with text nodes), then applies _highlightText (TreeWalker-based text-node replacement with <mark class='mb-global-filter-highlight'> spans styled with the configured highlight colors) to matching rows. The filter supports Cc (case-sensitive), Rx (regular expression), and Ex (exclude) checkboxes. (4) Section h2s missing CAA toggle and Cc/Rx/Ex checkboxes — _makeSectionCaaToggle builds a section-scoped CAA toggle button (same style as the global one) that controls only the bigboxes belonging to tables within its section; it is appended to both the Official and Non-Official h2s. The Cc/Rx/Ex checkboxes are built inline in _makeSectionFilter and wired to the applyFilter function so changing any checkbox re-runs the section filter immediately.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: artist-releasegroups button renames and additions: '🧮 Non-official RGs' renamed to '🧮 All official RGs' (params all=1,va=0); '🧮 Non-official VA RGs' renamed to '🧮 All official VA RGs' (params all=1,va=1); new '🧮 VA RGs' button added (params all=['1','0'],va=1, triggers the same two-pass split fetch as '🧮 Artist RGs' but for VA release groups). Tooltips updated for all renamed/new buttons with accurate label-matching guards. New configSchema entry sa_ui_h2_artist_rgs_global_bg (color_picker, default '#1b5e20' dark green) controls the background of the synthetic global super-header h2 injected by the two-pass path. Three-tier h2 architecture for the two-pass (Artist RGs / VA RGs) result: (1) A new GLOBAL super-header h2 is inserted above 'Official Artist Discography', styled with the configured dark-green background and white text, labelled 'Artist Discography (N)', and carries: the CAA global toggle buttons (moved from the Official h2), a global master-toggle driving ALL sub-tables across both sections, and the existing filterContainer (global filter input + regex/case/exclude/clear buttons) — so the global filter truly acts across both sections. (2) The 'Official Artist Discography' h2 retains its own row-count badge (official-only count), gets a section-scoped master-toggle (drives only the official sub-tables), and gains a compact local filter input (220px) that text-matches directly against visible tbody rows in the official section without re-rendering. (3) The 'Non-Official Artist Discography' h2 gets the same treatment: official non-official count badge, section-scoped master-toggle, and local filter. The _sectionSibs(h2El) helper collects sibling elements between an h2 and the next h2; _makeSectionToggle(h2, label) and _makeSectionFilter(h2, idSuffix) are inline factory helpers inside the multi-pass block.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed six bugs in the '🧮 Artist RGs' two-pass multi-pass fetch path (introduced in 9.99.176): (a) Collapsable sidebar feature not initialised — added initSidebarCollapse() call. (b) Table column background colors missing — the full post-render init sequence (applyStickyHeaders, addAutoResizeButton, makeColumnsResizable, addColumnVisibilityToggle, addBarcodeHighlightToggleButton, addDensityControl, addStatsButton, initKeyboardShortcuts, addShortcutsHelpButton, addExportButton) is now called, mirroring the normal multi-table path exactly. (c) None of the h2 headers clickable for collapse/uncollapse — makeH2sCollapsible() and finalCleanup() are now called after render. (d) Newly injected 'Non-Official Artist Discography' h2 was missing row-count badge, master-toggle button, and sub-section controls — after renderGroupedTable a post-render patch finds the non-official h2 by text content and appends a .mb-row-count-stat span with the non-official row count and a .mb-master-toggle button that drives all h3/table children between that h2 and the next. (e) Row count badge on 'Official Artist Discography' h2 showed the combined total — updateH2Count is now called with officialRows (= Pass-B total) instead of totalRows when a split was found; nonOfficialRows = totalRows - officialRows. (f) Pagination <nav> elements left over from the last XHR pass of _runPass — document.querySelectorAll('ul.pagination, nav.pagination, .pageselector').forEach(el => el.remove()) added before renderGroupedTable, matching the normal path.

sections.label: ✨ New Features
items: New '🧮 Artist RGs' button on artist root pages. Uses params { all: ['1','0'], va:'0' } — an array value triggers a new two-pass fetch pipeline in startFetchingProcess. Pass A (all=1) fetches the full discography (official + non-official release groups); Pass B (all=0) fetches official groups only. The two resulting groupedRows arrays are diffed by walking Pass-A category names and matching them against the Pass-B sequence in order: as long as names agree those groups are official; the first Pass-A category name not covered by Pass-B marks the start of non-official groups, which receive a { h2SectionBreak: 'Non-Official Artist Discography' } property. In renderGroupedTable, when group.h2SectionBreak is set a new <h2 class='mb-toggle-h2'> with that text is injected immediately before the group's first <h3>/table via the existing lastInsertedElement chain. The existing 'Discography' h2 on the page is also renamed to 'Official Artist Discography' (by updating the first text-node child to preserve any injected spans). The multi-pass block returns early, bypassing the normal single-pass fetch loop entirely. Post-render art/feature initialisation (initCaaPics, initEaaPics, initCaaInlinePics, initEaaInlinePics, rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti, etc.) is called explicitly before returning. Array-param detection is generic: any params key whose value is an Array triggers the two-pass path, making it extensible for future multi-pass buttons.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed missing thumbnail in the CAA/EAA column header button (▶🖼/▼🖼) on pages where every release has exactly one image (artist-releases, artist-recordings, label-releases, series, etc.). Root cause: _artInitCaaColHeaderToggle derived thumbSrc exclusively from the first [data-caa-expand-btn] in the tbody, but that button is only injected by _artBuildMultiRowArtCell when an art cell has ≥2 images — so on single-image pages firstExpandBtn was always null and thumbSrc stayed '', leaving the header img hidden. Fix is a three-strategy fallback chain: Strategy 1 (unchanged) uses [data-caa-expand-btn] when present; Strategy 2 scans all visible tbody rows in the CAA/EAA column for the first a[href$=artSuffix] anchor and constructs the small-size URL from the entity path (this is the primary fix — it works as soon as the DOM is rendered, before any network fetch); Strategy 3 scans for any art-icon span in the column that already has a background-image style set and reuses that URL directly. Additionally a backfill path was added to _onIconLoaded (the shared success handler in _artLoadIcon): after each successful icon load, if the column header button's img is still hidden (display:none) it is immediately updated with the just-loaded imgurl — this covers the race where all three strategies returned '' at _artInitCaaColHeaderToggle call time but images subsequently load. Also fixed the sa_caa_big_img_size reference in Strategy 1 to use sa_caa_small_img_size (matching the icon column size, consistent with Strategies 2 and 3).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Improvements
items: Unique-value count and 📊 glyph now act as one unified clickable button. Previously only the 📊 span had tabindex/role/click handlers while the count span was inert. The interactivity is now entirely on the .mb-col-uniq-wrap wrapper: it carries tabindex='0', role='button', the click handler, the keydown handler, and the aria-label — all delegating to openUniqDrop(uniqWrap, ...) so _uniqDropOwner correctly tracks the wrapper. The .mb-col-uniq-btn (📊) is demoted to a purely visual glyph with pointer-events:none so clicks pass through to the wrapper. A single tooltip is set on the wrapper (not separately on the count span) by _updateAllColHeaderCounts() each time the count is refreshed: 'Show the N different unique values in this column, with the ability to quick filter by either clicking or selecting with the keyboard and pressing "Enter" on an entry' (falls back to 'Show unique values for this column' when the count is zero). CSS: cursor, hover highlight (background + opacity propagated to both child spans via descendant rules), and active state moved from .mb-col-uniq-btn to .mb-col-uniq-wrap; .mb-col-uniq-btn gains pointer-events:none; stale .mb-col-uniq-btn:hover and .mb-col-uniq-btn.mb-col-uniq-active rules removed; :focus-visible / :focus ring selectors updated from .mb-col-uniq-btn to .mb-col-uniq-wrap. The Ctrl+Q keyboard shortcut handler updated to querySelector('.mb-col-uniq-wrap') and call .click() on the wrapper.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Improvements
items: Three fixes/additions: (1) Removed excess space between unique-value count and 📊 in column headers (gap:2px on .mb-col-uniq-wrap → gap:0). (2) Fixed unique-value count tooltip not appearing on hover — pointer-events:none on .mb-col-uniq-count was blocking mouse events so the browser never fired the title attribute; replaced with cursor:default. (3) Per-cell multi-row collapse toggles (.mb-cell-collapse-toggle) now use the same structured three-child-span layout as the column header collapse buttons: .mb-cell-collapse-glyph (▶ collapsed / ▼ expanded), .mb-cell-collapse-count (number of list items in this cell), .mb-cell-collapse-rack (▤, static). Creation in initCollapsableColumns builds the three spans with the cell's lis.length count. State detection throughout switched from textContent comparisons (toggle.textContent === '▶', '◀') to aria-expanded, covering: the ensureCollapseDelegate delegation handler (glyph flip via querySelector('.mb-cell-collapse-glyph') with textContent fallback for legacy nodes), the hidden-match scan guard, the column header button click handler (driving cells to target state), and the disk-save cleanup path (resets expanded toggles back to collapsed). New CSS block .mb-cell-collapse-glyph, .mb-cell-collapse-count, .mb-cell-collapse-rack { pointer-events:none; user-select:none } plus .mb-cell-collapse-count { font-weight:bold; margin:0 1px }. Cell toggle titles updated throughout from 'Collapse back to first item (N total)' / 'Show all items (N) — click to expand' to 'Collapse multi-row cell back to display just the first item (N total)' / 'Show all multi-row cell items (N)'.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes / Improvements
items: Fixed unique-value count drifting away from 📊 in column headers (introduced in 9.99.171). The count span and 📊 button are now wrapped together in a '.mb-col-uniq-wrap' inline-flex container (gap:2px) so they always move as one unit and can never be separated by flex layout. The wrapper carries margin-left:auto by default (pushing the pair to the right edge); when a '.mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn' is present, initCollapsableColumns gives margin-left:auto to the collapse button instead and clears the wrapper's inline margin, preserving the correct order [▶N▤ auto] [N 📊]. The cleanup pass in initCollapsableColumns now resets marginLeft on the wrapper (not the bare uniqBtn). CSS: margin-left:auto removed from .mb-col-uniq-btn (now carried by .mb-col-uniq-wrap); margin-right removed from .mb-col-uniq-count (spacing handled by wrapper gap). Added tooltip to the unique-value count span: 'There are N different unique values in this column, click the 📊 symbol to display them', set by _updateAllColHeaderCounts() each time the count is refreshed (empty string when count is 0).

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Live count indicators in column headers. Two new counts are rendered inline in every sortable column header, updated automatically after every render and filter pass: (1) Unique-value count — a '.mb-col-uniq-count' span is inserted immediately before the 📊 button and shows the number of distinct non-empty values visible in the current filtered tbody rows for that column, using the same getCleanColumnText() scan as the existing dropdown panel so the number always agrees with what the dropdown would list. The span starts empty at header-build time in makeTableSortableUnified and is populated on the first _updateAllColHeaderCounts() call. (2) Multi-row cell count — the '.mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn' collapse button (▶N▤ / ▼N▤) is now built from three child spans instead of a plain textContent string: '.mb-col-collapse-glyph' (▶/▼), '.mb-col-collapse-count' (number of visible multi-row cells in the column), and '.mb-col-collapse-rack' (▤, always static). The initial count is set to multiRowCells.length at button-creation time; subsequent updates use _updateAllColHeaderCounts(). The glyph-flip in the click handler now targets the child span directly. The new _updateAllColHeaderCounts(table) function is called at the tail of initCollapsableColumns() (covering every render-completion and filter-cycle site automatically), including the early-return path for pages with no collapsable columns (which still need the unique-value counts refreshed). CSS: two new classes — .mb-col-collapse-count (0.82em bold, 1px margin, pointer-events:none) and .mb-col-uniq-count (0.72em bold, opacity 0.60, pointer-events:none, margin-right 1px). The collapseHdrBtn insertion in initCollapsableColumns now inserts before the uniqCountSpan (not the uniqBtn directly) to preserve the correct order: [▶N▤] [N] [📊]. All 18 th.textContent glyph-strip regexes updated to also strip ▤ and plain digits 0-9 so that column-name recovery remains correct now that header text includes the new count numbers.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Column header element reorder and collapse-button glyph change. All sortable column headers now follow the layout: [▶🖼/▼🖼 CAA/EAA-only] colName ⇅ ▲ ▼ … [▶▤/▼▤ margin-left:auto] 📊. Previously the order was ⇅ colName ▲ ▼ [🖼▶/🖼▼] [▶/◀] 📊. Changes in detail: (1) makeTableSortableUnified hdrFlex construction — the text node is now first (no leading space), followed by ⇅ ▲ ▼, then 📊; the leading space in the text node was dropped because the CAA/EAA button now provides natural visual separation at the left edge. (2) _artInitCaaColHeaderToggle — the .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn DOM structure is reversed to glyph-span first then thumbnail img; insertion is changed from insertBefore(collapseHdrBtn||uniqBtn) to insertBefore(hdrFlex.firstChild) so the button is always the leftmost element. (3) _updateCaaColHdrBtn — gained an archiveName parameter (default 'CAA') used in the title strings so EAA columns read 'Show/Collapse all EAA images' instead of hardcoded CAA; both call sites in _artInitCaaColHeaderToggle now pass ctx.key.toUpperCase(). (4) initCollapsableColumns — .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn initial glyph changed from '▶' to '▶▤'; the flipped state is '▼▤' instead of '◀'. State detection in the click handler switched from textContent === '▶' to aria-expanded !== 'true' since the glyph is now two characters. CSS comment for .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn updated accordingly. (5) All three external callers that previously read btn.textContent === '◀' to detect whether a .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn was expanded (the per-subtable global button click handler, rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti allHdrBtns loop, and the single-table global button onclick loop) were updated to use btn.getAttribute('aria-expanded') === 'true' instead.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: CAA/EAA fetch telemetry extended with full per-path breakdown in the completion toast. The toast (introduced in 9.99.165, summary line added in 9.99.168) now renders a multiline message with white-space:pre and line-height:1.55 comprising: (1) header line '🎨 All CAA/EAA artwork loaded'; (2) summary line '   N images in T'; (3) one breakdown row per active cache tier (rows with zero counts are suppressed): '  🟢 Memory cache: N icon + N inline = N', '  🗄️ IndexedDB cache: …', '  🔵 Network fetch: …', '  🌐 Browser cache: …'; (4) footer '   (click to dismiss)'. The four tiers correspond exactly to the existing per-icon RT badge system: Memory (Tier 1, _artIdbMemCache session Map), IDB (Tier 2, IndexedDB blob cache), Network (Tier 3, GM_xmlhttpRequest CORS-bypass fetch producing a blob: URL), and Browser (native img.src path used when IDB is disabled or on cacheBust retry, where the browser manages caching internally). The infoDisplay (#mb-info-display) global info area retains the compact single-line form '🎨 CAA/EAA: N images in T'. Implementation: the former flat _caaFetchStartTime + _caaFetchedCount pair is replaced by a single _caaFetchStats object { startTime, icon: { memory, idb, network, browser }, inline: { memory, idb, network, browser } } reset in initCaaPics(). _onIconLoaded gains a wasMemoryHit parameter (default false) threaded from _artFetchCachedImage's fromMemory return value, enabling exact tier classification for icon-column images: memory/idb from the flags, network from src.startsWith('blob:') with both flags false, browser otherwise. _artInitInlinePics IDB path now destructures fromMemory alongside fromIdb for the same three-tier classification; the native img.onload path increments inline.browser. The unused _caaTotalLoaded() helper (defined but never called) was removed.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: CAA/EAA fetch telemetry — total elapsed time and image count are now measured and surfaced in two places when artwork loading completes: (1) The completion toast (introduced in 9.99.165) now reads '🎨 All CAA/EAA artwork (N images loaded in T)' instead of the former '🎨 All CAA/EAA artwork loaded', where N is the number of images successfully displayed and T is a human-friendly duration (e.g. '847ms', '4.3s', '2m 7s'). (2) The global '#mb-info-display' status area (bottom of the toolbar) is updated with '🎨 CAA/EAA: N images loaded in T' in green, giving a persistent summary after the auto-dismissing toast has gone. Measurement is implemented via two new module-level variables — _caaFetchStartTime (set to performance.now() when initCaaPics() recreates _caaQueue at the start of each render pass) and _caaFetchedCount (reset to 0 at the same point, then incremented on every successful image display): in _artLoadIcon via the shared _onIconLoaded success handler (covers icon-column thumbnails for both the IDB and native img paths), and in _artInitInlinePics via both the IDB .then() success block and the native img.addEventListener('load') block (covers inline Title-column thumbnails). Failed or skipped loads are intentionally not counted. The _fmtMs() helper formats durations as 'NNNms' below 1 s, 'N.Ns' below 60 s, and 'Nm Ns' for longer spans.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Tooltip and label text improvements across multiple UI elements, plus two new configurable settings: (1) title/tooltip renames — clearAllFiltersBtn now reads 'Clear global, ALL sub-table and ALL column filters in all sub-tables (except filter from Loading from Disk)'; h3 sub-section collapse title changed to 'Click to collapse/uncollapse sub-section (Ctrl+Click to toggle ALL sub-sections)'; h2 section collapse title to 'Click to collapse/uncollapse section'; collapseHdrBtn titles changed from 'Expand/Collapse all cells in colName' to 'Expand/Collapse all multi-row colName image cells in this table column'; sort reset button (⇅) title changed to 'Restore original sort order (clears multi-sort columns)'; unique-values button (📊) title changed to 'Show unique values for this column — Click or press Enter on an entry to apply as filter for this column'; CAA/EAA bigbox toggle button titles changed from 'Toggle … - Show/Hide' to 'Show/Hide big artwork …'; 'Show all N image(s) — click to expand' changed to 'Show all image(s) (N) — click to expand' throughout; 'Show all N items (click to expand)' changed to 'Show all items (N) — click to expand' throughout; global collapse button label changed from 'every collapsable column' to 'every collapsable table column' (7 occurrences). (2) Master toggle button text changed from 'Show/Hide all sections' to 'Show/Hide all sub-sections', with updated tooltip to 'Click to uncollapse/collapse all sub-sections (Ctrl+Click to uncollapse/collapse ALL sub-sections)'. (3) Show-all overflow sub-table button: text changed from 'Show all N' to 'Show all N rows'; tooltip added reading 'Click to show all N rows in a new browser tab (currently only showing 100 entries per MusicBrainz limitation)'; button background is now configurable via two new configSchema keys — sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg (default #FFE0B2, orange tint before clicking) and sa_ui_show_all_subtable_btn_bg_clicked (default #CCFFCC, green tint after clicking) — applied via inline style on the button element so they override the CSS class default and provide visual confirmation of activation.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: CAA/EAA column-header expand/collapse toggle button — for every table/sub-table that has a synthetic CAA or EAA column, a new .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn span is injected into the mb-col-hdr-flex row of the column header, positioned immediately before the existing mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn (or the 📊 unique-values button if that is absent). The button shows the front thumbnail of the first row that has artwork (16×16 px, sourced from ctx.archiveHost + entityPath + '/front-' + size) alongside a glyph: ▶ when all [data-caa-expand-btn] cells in that table are collapsed, ▼ when at least one is expanded. Clicking ▶ iterates all [data-caa-expand-btn] spans in the table body and fires a synthetic .click() on each one that is not already in the expanded state (mirroring the exact action of clicking the per-cell button), then updates the header button to ▼; clicking ▼ collapses all and restores ▶. The button is created by the new _artInitCaaColHeaderToggle(ctx) function which is called from _artInitPics (after _artCreateOrUpdateGlobalToggleButton) and is therefore refreshed on every render pass. A companion _updateCaaColHdrBtn(btn, expanded, thumbSrc) helper manages the glyph, title, aria-expanded, and img.src updates. The initCollapsableColumns cleanup pass now also removes stale .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn elements so they are correctly rebuilt on re-renders. CSS class .mb-caa-col-hdr-btn uses inline-flex with a 3px gap between the glyph and thumbnail, border-radius:3px, and a hover tint matching the existing sort/collapse header button style.

sections.label: ✨ New Features / Changes
items: Two new features: (1) CAA/EAA artwork load-complete toast notification — a small non-intrusive toast appears in the bottom-right corner of the viewport once the shared _caaQueue has fully drained (all icon thumbnails, inline Title-column images, and big-picture-strip images have finished loading or failed). The toast reads '🎨 All CAA/EAA artwork loaded', auto-dismisses after sa_caa_completion_toast_duration seconds (new configSchema key, default 10, range 0–60; 0 disables the notification entirely), and also dismisses immediately on click. Implemented by extending makeCaaQueue with an onIdle(cb) method that fires the callback exactly once when the queue reaches running=0 and pending=[] (or via setTimeout(0) if the queue is already idle at registration time); the onIdle handler is registered after the four initCaaPics/initEaaPics/initCaaInlinePics/initEaaInlinePics calls in both renderGroupedTable (multi-table pages) and the single-table renderFinalTable site, but NOT in the runFilter re-run path, so the toast fires once per initial render rather than on every filter keystroke.
