Every img.onload closure checks parseInt(toggleBtn.dataset.artRenderGen) === myRenderGen before incrementing the badge; if a newer render pass has started the closure is a no-op. This invalidates all stale callbacks from prior render passes regardless of how many initCaaPics() calls overlap in the async queue.

updateFilterButtonsVisibility (multi-table, CAA sub-tables): on multi-table relationship pages, toggling the STF (sub-table filter) panel on for a sub-table that has CAA artworks (e.g. 'Liner notes for') and typing a filter string produced no visible effect — the mb-subtable-controls Clear button and '🎨 Toggle highlighting' button did not appear, and the filter-status span was not updated. Sub-tables without artworks (e.g. 'Vocals') were unaffected. Root cause: updateFilterButtonsVisibility located the sibling table via const table = h3.nextElementSibling. When CAA/EAA artworks are present for a sub-table, _artInitBigPics inserts a div.mb-caa-bigbox (or div.mb-eaa-bigbox) immediately before the table element; consequently h3.nextElementSibling returned the bigbox DIV rather than the TABLE, and the guard if (!table || table.tagName !== 'TABLE') return caused the function to exit early before updating any per-sub-table controls. The mb-subtable-controls span existed in the DOM but was never reached. Fix: replaced the bare h3.nextElementSibling assignment with a forward walk that skips non-TABLE siblings (while (table && table.tagName !== 'TABLE') table = table.nextElementSibling) — identical in spirit to findH3ForTable's backward walk past bigbox divs introduced for the h3-lookup direction.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artInitBigPics (CAA / EAA bigbox placement on disk-load, multi-table): after loading data from disk on an already-rendered multi-table page, clicking any CAA/EAA toggle button showed the bigbox below the table instead of immediately below the h3 sub-header, and all bigboxes were stacked consecutively in page order regardless of which button was clicked. Root cause: renderGroupedTable()'s initial cleanup (no-query branch) removes 'h3, table.tbl, .mb-master-toggle' from the container but never touches the bigbox divs (class mb-caa-bigbox / mb-eaa-bigbox). After the old tables and h3s are removed the bigboxes remain as orphaned elements at their previous positions in the container. renderGroupedTable() then creates and inserts fresh h3 + table pairs, followed by initCaaPics() at its tail. _artInitBigPics finds the orphaned bigboxes by their stable ID, clears them (box.innerHTML = ''), and repopulates them — but the existing-box branch never called table.parentNode.insertBefore(box, table), so the boxes were left at their orphaned positions rather than being moved to sit between the new h3 and the new table. Fix: moved the table.parentNode.insertBefore(box, table) call outside the if/else so it runs unconditionally for both new and reused boxes. Node.insertBefore moves an already-in-DOM node when called again, so this is a no-op when the box is already in the correct position and a repositioning operation otherwise.

_artInitBigPics / disk-load pipeline (CAA / EAA badge count doubles on single-table disk-load, triples on multi-table disk-load): when loading data from disk on an already-rendered page, the CAA/EAA toggle button badge showed 2× the correct count on single-table pages and 3× on multi-table pages. Typing any filter query immediately reset it to the correct value. Root cause: the disk-load pipeline contained an explicit unconditional initCaaPics() / initEaaPics() / initCaaInlinePics() / initEaaInlinePics() block (after initBarcodeHighlight()) that duplicated calls already made by other code paths. For multi-table pages, initCaaPics() is called at the tail of renderGroupedTable(); since the disk-load pipeline both awaits renderGroupedTable(groupedRows) and then calls runFilter() (which calls renderGroupedTable(query) internally), initCaaPics() was invoked three times total before the event loop could yield. For single-table pages, runFilter()'s single-table branch calls initCaaPics() directly, and the explicit block called it a second time — two total. Because browser image-load callbacks are always dispatched asynchronously (even for cache hits), all three (or two) sets of img.onload closures were queued before any fired; when the event loop ran they all incremented the same badge, yielding 3× (or 2×) the true count. Fix: removed the explicit initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics block from the disk-load pipeline entirely. Both code paths (runFilter for single-table, renderGroupedTable via runFilter for multi-table) already handle CAA/EAA initialisation correctly and are the authoritative call sites.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (CAA / EAA toggle button layout): after the first filter interaction (global filter, column filter, or sub-table filter), the CAA/EAA toggle button broke its inline layout — the image count number appeared centred below the thumbnail icon instead of sitting to its right. Root cause: the re-render branch (isNew = false, reached on every runFilter() / renderGroupedTable() re-render after the initial load) restored button visibility with `btn.style.display = ''`. Assigning the empty string removes the `display` property from the element's inline style entirely; since the `display:inline-flex` that keeps the thumbnail and count side-by-side was set only via `cssText` on initial creation and lives in the inline style, clearing it caused the button to fall back to the browser default for

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA/EAA illustrated discography (single-table mode): cover-art bigboxes, toggle buttons, and inline thumbnails were never initialised on the initial load of any single-table page — including label-releases (which has addCAA: 'Release'), series-releases, and work-recordings-filtered (?direction=…&link_type_id=…). Root cause: the main fetch pipeline contained a comment that said initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics should NOT be called there because renderGroupedTable() already calls them at its own tail. This reasoning is correct for multi-table mode but completely wrong for single-table mode: renderFinalTable() — which is called instead of renderGroupedTable() for all single-table pages — has never called these functions, and no other code path compensated. As a result, every single-table page was silently left without any CAA/EAA initialisation after the initial fetch/render cycle. (The runFilter() single-table branch did call them correctly, so images appeared only after the user typed a filter query or triggered a sort.) Fix: replaced the unconditional skip block with a tableMode-gated call: for tableMode !== 'multi' (i.e. all single-table pages), initCaaPics() / initEaaPics() / initCaaInlinePics() / initEaaInlinePics() are now called in the correct order immediately after initBarcodeHighlight() in the main pipeline. For tableMode === 'multi' they remain absent here (renderGroupedTable() still handles that case) to avoid the doubled-badge-count regression documented in v9.99.118.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: artist-releasegroups (multi-table fetch loop): all release groups were being collapsed into a single “Other” category instead of their real type names (“Album”, “Single”, “EP”, …). Root cause: the fetch loop called `findH3ForTable(table)` on tables from fetched XHR pages. That helper only matches elements that carry the `mb-toggle-h3` class, which is injected by `renderGroupedTable()` on the live page — raw MusicBrainz HTML never has it. `findH3ForTable` therefore always returned null, and every group was labelled “Other”. Fix: replaced the `findH3ForTable` call in the artist-releasegroups fetch loop with a plain backwards walk that matches any <h3> element (no class restriction), exactly as MusicBrainz natively renders release-type headings on fetched pages.

CAA/EAA toggle button: image count badge showed doubled values on the initial multi-table render (e.g. 8 instead of 4 for a 4-row table). Root cause: `initCaaPics()` / `initEaaPics()` / `initCaaInlinePics()` / `initEaaInlinePics()` were called TWICE on every initial multi-table render — once at the tail of `renderGroupedTable()` (where they have always lived) and a second time in the post-render pipeline that follows the `await renderGroupedTable(groupedRows, ...)` call. The second `_artInitBigPics` pass rebuilt each bigbox (`box.innerHTML = ''`), detaching the first set of `` elements from the DOM. Those detached images had already started loading; their `onload` closures captured `btnId` by value and incremented the badge via `document.getElementById(btnId)` even though their wrapper anchors were no longer in the tree. The second set of images also loaded and incremented the badge, yielding exactly 2× the true count. Fix: removed the duplicate `initCaaPics / initEaaPics / initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics` block from the post-render pipeline. A prominent comment explains why these must NOT be called there.

CAA/EAA toggle button: buttons did not reappear for sub-tables after a global filter was cleared (when the filter had previously reduced those sub-tables to 0 matching rows). Root cause: the stale-bigbox cleanup added in v9.99.117 correctly hides the toggle button via `staleBtn.style.display = 'none'` when a table's link count drops to 0. However, the re-render path in `_artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton` (`isNew = false` branch) only reset the badge counter — it never restored `btn.style.display` to `''`. Any button previously hidden by the count=0 path therefore remained hidden even after rows were restored. Fix: added `btn.style.display = ''` to the re-render branch of `_artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton`, ensuring the button is unconditionally made visible whenever it is updated with a non-zero link count.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: renderGroupedTable (multi-table filter re-render): subtable control buttons (resize ↔️, column-visibility 👁️, collapse ▶, .mb-subtable-controls span) were being injected INTO the CAA/EAA bigbox div instead of the h3 header on filter re-renders. Root cause: the reuse branch resolves the h3 via `table.previousElementSibling`, but after the initial render `_artInitBigPics` inserts a div.mb-caa-bigbox directly before the table, so `table.previousElementSibling` returns the bigbox — not the real h3. All subsequent `h3.querySelector('.mb-subtable-controls')` checks succeeded (bigbox has none), causing every subtable button to be appended to the bigbox. The same bare `table.previousElementSibling` pattern existed in 13 call sites across the codebase (renderGroupedTable, runFilter, updateSubTableRowCount, updateSubTableCollapseButton, markSubTableAsManuallyResized, clearColumnFilters, getTableFilterStatusSpan, makeTableSortableUnified, the subtable-filter regex-error/ok handlers, and the artist-releasegroups fetch path). Fix: introduced a new shared `findH3ForTable(table)` function that walks backwards past any injected art-bigbox elements (up to 5 steps) to find the h3.mb-toggle-h3 that logically owns the table. All 13 bare `table.previousElementSibling` call sites have been replaced with `findH3ForTable(table)`. `caaFindHeaderForTable` is updated to delegate to `findH3ForTable` for its multi-table path.

_artInitPics (CAA / EAA bigbox placement): bigbox divs were being inserted immediately before the table, which pushed them BEFORE any existing `mb-subtable-controls` filter/sort status bar. This caused the artwork strip to appear above the table's control controls rather than directly above the table headers. Fix: adjusted the `insertBefore` logic in `_artInitBigPics` to target `table` specifically, and ensured the subtable controls are always pinned to the H3. On re-renders, `findH3ForTable` guarantees the controls remain on the H3 even when an art bigbox is present.

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: Simplified the CAA/EAA archive context descriptors: removed the `column` property from `CAA_CTX` and `EAA_CTX`. The target column index is now resolved dynamically at call-time via the `caaFindColumnByName(table, name)` helper. This fixes potential stale-index issues if columns are reordered or removed dynamically during the page lifecycle.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fixed regression in `_artCountLinks`: the function was failing to correctly identify the target table index when calculating aggregate counts for the toggle buttons in multi-table mode. This resulted in the image count badge always staying at '0' even when images were successfully loaded. Fix: restored the logic that derives the table index from the table's `data-mb-table-idx` attribute if available, falling back to a DOM-order scan if not.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactor
items: Unified the CAA Illustrated Discography and EAA Illustrated Events features into a single shared generic engine (_art* functions) parameterised by archive context descriptors (CAA_CTX / EAA_CTX).

Eliminated ~450 lines of structural duplication: caaLoadIcon / eaaLoadIcon → _artLoadIcon(ctx), caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon / eaaEnrichEventIcon → _artEnrichIcon(ctx), caaInitSmallPics / eaaInitSmallPics → _artInitSmallPics(ctx), caaCountLinks / eaaCountLinks → _artCountLinks(ctx), caaInitBigPics / eaaInitBigPics → _artInitBigPics(ctx), caaUpdateBigBoxForTable / eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable → _artUpdateBigBoxForTable(ctx), caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton / eaaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton → _artCreateOrUpdateToggleButton(ctx), initCaaPics / initEaaPics → _artInitPics(ctx), initCaaInlinePics / initEaaInlinePics → _artInitInlinePics(ctx).
Merged caaFindCaaColumnIndex and eaaFindEaaColumnIndex into the existing caaFindColumnByName(table, name) — both were single-line wrappers with a hardcoded column name.
Introduced _ART_GUID_RE_STR shared constant to avoid repeating the 36-char GUID pattern string six times.
All public call-site names (initCaaPics, initEaaPics, initCaaInlinePics, initEaaInlinePics, caaUpdateBigBoxForTable, eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable) are unchanged; no call sites required modification.
Moved _eaaEventCountCache declaration to module scope alongside _caaRgCountCache so both caches are available when the context descriptors are constructed.

sections.label: 🔧 Code Quality
items: Removed unused variable guardColFilterPrefixKeydown: a @deprecated alias of guardFilterPrefixKeydown that was never referenced anywhere else in the script. The canonical function guardFilterPrefixKeydown() is used directly at all call sites.

Fixed JSDoc for updateH2Count(): the third parameter absoluteTotal (added in an earlier revision to support pre-filter absolute row counts on multi-table pages) was missing its @param declaration. Added @param {number|null} [absoluteTotal=null] with a description of its purpose.

sections.label: 📖 Documentation
items: ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt: corrected prefix-mode shortcut key for 'Show Statistics panel' from 't' to 'i' — the actual ctrlMFunctionMap entry has always used 'i' (matching the Statistics button's underline character); the help file had a stale value.
ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt: added Ctrl+U (Unicode character picker) to the KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS table; the shortcut was already implemented and configurable but was not documented in the help file.

ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt: added a new 'COLUMN FILTER FOCUS SHORTCUTS' section documenting Ctrl+↑ (sort ascending), Ctrl+↓ (sort descending), Ctrl+# (unsort), Ctrl+O (toggle multi-row collapse), Ctrl+Q (unique-values dropdown), Ctrl+A (toggle CAA/EAA cover art), Ctrl+R (sub-table resize), and Ctrl+V (sub-table visible-columns). These shortcuts were fully implemented and configurable but absent from the help text.

ShowAllEntityData_HELP.txt: updated stale shortcut count in the SETTINGS section from '12 configurable direct-chord shortcuts' to '20', enumerating both the global-scope shortcuts and the new column-filter-focused shortcuts.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA big-picture strip (all single-table pages — label-releases, artist-releases, series-releases, artist-recordings, and any other single-table type with cover-art): the toggle button and bigbox strip were never shown on the initial page load. Root cause: caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton started with `const box = document.getElementById(boxId); if (!box) return;`, but the bigbox element is created by caaInitBigPics which runs *after* caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton in the initCaaPics pipeline. On every first render the box didn't exist yet, so caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton bailed out returning undefined, caaInitBigPics then created the box but received btnId=undefined so the img load callbacks could never increment the badge and the toggle button was never inserted in the header. The strip only appeared after the first filter or sort operation (which re-ran the pipeline with the box already in the DOM). Fix: removed the premature box-existence guard from caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton. All references to the box inside the click handler are now resolved lazily with document.getElementById(boxId) at click time, so the button can be created and inserted in the header before the bigbox exists. Same fix applied to eaaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton.

CAA big-picture strip (single-table pages without a dedicated CAA column — series-releases, artist-recordings): even after fixing the toggle-button timing bug above, the bigbox was still not built for page types that have features.addCAA in their definition but no `caa` columnExtractor (and therefore no CAA th in the table header). caaFindCaaColumnIndex returned -1 and initCaaPics skipped the table entirely. Fix: initCaaPics now accepts a table when EITHER a CAA column header is present OR activeDefinition.features.addCAA is set. caaInitSmallPics (which loads thumbnails into CAA-column icon spans) continues to be gated on hasCaaColumn only. An additional early-return is added when caaCountLinks finds zero links so genuinely empty tables are still skipped.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA big-picture strip (multi-table mode / sub-table filter): the strip now correctly updates when the sub-table filter (STF — the per-sub-table search input below the h3 header) is used. Previously the STF hid rows via CSS (display:none / data-mb-stf-hidden) without touching the bigbox, so the strip continued to show artwork for all rows regardless of the filter. Fix: two new helpers, caaUpdateBigBoxForTable(table, tableIndex) and eaaUpdateBigBoxForTable(table, tableIndex), are called at the end of every applySubFilter pass and clearSubFilter pass. Each helper (1) collects hrefs of release/release-group (CAA) or event (EAA) anchors in currently-visible rows; (2) shows/hides the corresponding bigbox wrapper elements; (3) updates the toggle-button badge to reflect the count of images that are both shown in the strip and already downloaded.

CAA big-picture strip (single-table mode): the strip was missing for page types that have features.addCAA configured but no caa column extractor, specifically series-releases (series/Releases tab, addCAA: 'Release') and artist-recordings (artist/Recordings tab, addCAA: 'Release groups'). The root cause was that initCaaPics() required a 'CAA' column header before building the bigbox; pages without the caa extractor never produce that header so the entire big-pic path was skipped. Fix: the table-level guard in initCaaPics() now accepts a table when EITHER a 'CAA' column header exists OR the active page definition carries features.addCAA. caaInitSmallPics() (which loads artwork into CAA-column icon spans) remains gated on hasCaaColumn only. An additional early-return is added when caaCountLinks() finds zero release/release-group links so tables that pass the relaxed guard but genuinely have no artwork links are still skipped without building an empty bigbox.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: cancelledEvent extractor: the synthetic Cancelled column now shows only the bare word 'cancelled' (the text content of the inner <bdi> element) instead of the full bracketed '(cancelled)' span. The Event column is unaffected — the original <span class="cancelled"> node remains untouched so the Event column continues to display the full '(cancelled)' label alongside the event title. A bracket-stripping text fallback is applied when no <bdi> is present in the source span.

EAA (Event Art Archive) Illustrated Events feature: fully implemented as a parallel counterpart to the CAA Illustrated Discography feature, targeting eventartarchive.org and the synthetic EAA column produced by the caa extractor for MusicBrainz event rows. New functions: eaaFindEaaColumnIndex (EAA-column header detection), eaaLoadIcon (loads front-thumbnail into eaa/artwork-icon span background via eventartarchive.org/event/{mbid}/front-{size}), eaaEnrichEventIcon (async fetch of eventartarchive.org JSON index; sets anchor tooltip to 'N image(s) found for this event', appends mb-eaa-count-badge, and propagates tooltip to the addEAA inline placeholder), eaaInitSmallPics (enqueues eaaLoadIcon + eaaEnrichEventIcon for every eaa/artwork-icon in the EAA column), eaaInitBigPics (builds mb-eaa-bigbox-N hover strip with row↔image highlighting for /event/ anchors), eaaCountLinks (read-only pre-flight to count unique /event/ links and obtain the first image URL), eaaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (per-table toggle button with live download-count badge), initEaaPics (entry point: iterates all table.tbl, skips tables without an EAA column, calls eaaInitSmallPics + eaaCountLinks + eaaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton + eaaInitBigPics), initEaaInlinePics (inline thumbnail feature driven by features.addEAA column name; mirrors initCaaInlinePics but uses /event/ link detection and eventartarchive.org URLs, placeholder class mb-eaa-inline-ph, idempotency attribute data-eaa-inline-done).

EAA feature: all EAA image requests share the same _caaQueue created by initCaaPics(), keeping CAA and EAA requests within the single sa_caa_fetch_concurrency budget. initCaaPics() MUST run before initEaaPics() / initEaaInlinePics() — this ordering is enforced at all 4 render-completion call sites (renderFinalTable, renderGroupedTable, filter/sort re-render, load-from-disk).
EAA feature: _eaaEventCountCache (Map) added to avoid repeat eventartarchive.org API calls across sort/filter re-renders for the same event GUID, mirroring _caaRgCountCache for CAA release-groups.

caaFindCaaColumnIndex: now detects only the 'CAA' column (previously also matched 'EAA'); EAA column detection is handled exclusively by the new eaaFindEaaColumnIndex. This ensures initCaaPics() processes only CAA tables and initEaaPics() processes only EAA tables, with no cross-contamination.

artist-events page definition: addCAA key renamed to addEAA (was addCAA: 'Event', now addEAA: 'Event'). Event pages carry EAA artwork, not CAA artwork; the old key caused initCaaInlinePics() (which queries for /release/ or /release-group/ links) to silently find no matching links and skip every row on artist-events pages. The new addEAA key is consumed by initEaaInlinePics(), which correctly queries for /event/ links.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: initCaaInlinePics: fixed call-order regression that caused every single inline thumbnail request to bypass the concurrency queue introduced in v9.99.108. At all 4 render-completion call sites (renderFinalTable, renderGroupedTable, filter/sort re-render, load-from-disk), initCaaInlinePics() was being called BEFORE initCaaPics(). Because _caaQueue is created at the top of initCaaPics(), _caaQueue was still null every time initCaaInlinePics ran — the fallback 'unthrottled' path fired for 100% of inline requests. Debug log symptom: every inline thumbnail line read '_caaQueue unavailable — fetching <url> unthrottled'. Fix: swapped the call order at all 4 sites so initCaaPics() always runs first (creating the queue), then initCaaInlinePics() (consuming it). The ERG constraint (initExpandRGsFeature must precede initCaaInlinePics so the ▶ button is already present) is unaffected as initExpandRGsFeature still runs before both.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: initCaaInlinePics (addCAA inline thumbnail path): applied the same concurrency fix as caaInitSmallPics received in v9.99.107. The function previously assigned img.src synchronously inside a forEach loop, firing all inline-thumbnail requests simultaneously on large artist-releases / artist-recordings / artist-releasegroups pages. This caused the identical CDN burst / CORS-header-missing / ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES failure pattern. Fix: the DOM injection (placeholder span + img element creation and insertion into the cell) remains synchronous so layout is stable immediately; the img.src assignment that triggers the browser request is deferred inside a Promise that is enqueued through the shared _caaQueue. All inline-thumbnail requests now share the same sa_caa_fetch_concurrency budget as the caaInitSmallPics icon loads.

initCaaInlinePics: added onerror handler on each inline img element. Previously a failed image load was entirely silent — the placeholder stayed as a spacer with no logging whatsoever, even with debug logging enabled. The handler now logs at debug level ('initCaaInlinePics: failed to load <url> — placeholder stays as spacer'), and also calls resolve() to release the queue slot so a single failure does not stall all subsequent requests.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: initCaaInlinePics: added comprehensive debug logging throughout the function. New log points (all at debug level, channel 'caa'): (1) early-exit when no addCAA column is configured for the active page type, now explicitly logged rather than silent; (2) the resolved addCAA column name at function entry; (3) early-exit when no table.tbl is found; (4) per-table: column name and resolved index; (5) per-row: URL being enqueued; (6) per-load success: 'loaded OK — <url>'; (7) per-load failure: 'failed to load <url>'; (8) stale-clone-marker clearance; (9) rows skipped due to missing entity link or GUID; (10) final summary line with injected / enqueued / skipped(done|noLink|noGuid) counts, column name, and live queue state (running/pending).

initCaaInlinePics: warn-level log added for the fallback path where _caaQueue is unexpectedly null (should not occur in normal flow, but if it does the function now logs 'initCaaInlinePics: _caaQueue unavailable — fetching <url> unthrottled' rather than silently bypassing throttling).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA thumbnails and icon enrichment: eliminated the root cause of intermittent CORS errors and ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES failures on large artist pages. The script was firing all Cover Art Archive requests simultaneously in a tight forEach loop — on pages with hundreds of release groups this produced 400+ concurrent fetch() and img-load requests. The CAA CDN (Internet Archive) responded to the burst via its fast error path, which omits the Access-Control-Allow-Origin header; the browser blocked every one of those responses as a CORS violation. At the same time the browser's per-host connection pool (~6 slots) was exhausted, causing ERR_INSUFFICIENT_RESOURCES for the img elements. After the initial burst subsided, later requests (with the server back to normal load) completed successfully — explaining why some icons loaded and others did not. Fix: all CAA requests are now serialised through a shared fixed-concurrency FIFO queue (makeCaaQueue / _caaQueue) that enforces the new sa_caa_fetch_concurrency limit (default: 4). caaLoadIcon now returns a Promise (defers img.src until the queue grants a slot); caaInitSmallPics enqueues both the load and the enrich call per icon.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: New setting sa_caa_fetch_concurrency (CAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY section, default: 4, range: 1–20): controls the maximum number of simultaneous Cover Art Archive requests. All CAA image loads and JSON API calls share this single concurrency budget. Increase for faster loading on stable connections with small tables; decrease if CORS errors still appear with particularly large tables or slow CDN nodes.

caaLoadIcon: img.src is now assigned inside the Promise executor rather than immediately on function entry, so the browser does not start the request until the queue grants a slot. This is the key change that makes throttling effective for image requests, which previously bypassed any wrapping control because src assignment is a synchronous side-effect.

initCaaPics: the shared _caaQueue is recreated at the start of every render pass (main fetch, filter, sort, load-from-disk) so that the sa_caa_fetch_concurrency setting is always applied fresh and any leftover pending tasks from a previous (now-stale) DOM pass are discarded.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA extractor Path B (bare artwork-icon span, no wrapping anchor — occurs on artist-relationships pages): fixed broken coverartarchive.org URL construction caused by assigning the synthetic anchor URL via the `.href` DOM property instead of `setAttribute()`. The `.href` property normalises a relative path like `/release-group/GUID/cover-art` to an absolute `https://musicbrainz.org/release-group/GUID/cover-art`, which was then prepended with `//coverartarchive.org`, producing the completely invalid URL `//coverartarchive.orghttps://musicbrainz.org/…`. The result was a silent 404 — the default MusicBrainz artwork icon stayed visible even when cover art actually exists — with no console error because `caaLoadIcon` had no `onerror` handler. Fix: use `syntheticAnchor.setAttribute('href', …)` to preserve the relative path; also update the subsequent debug log to use `getAttribute('href')` for consistency.

Artist-relationships-filtered page definition (`artist-relationships-filtered`, URL pattern `/artist/GUID/relationships?…&link_type_id=…`): added missing `addCAA: 'Title'` feature key. Without it `initCaaInlinePics()` was a no-op on these pages (the guard `activeDefinition.features.addCAA` evaluated to undefined), so the inline cover-art thumbnail was never injected into the Title column even though the CAA column itself was correctly populated.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: caaLoadIcon: added `onerror` event handler on the hidden probe ``. Previously, any failed image load (404 for releases with no 'Front'-typed image, 429, 5xx, or network error) was completely silent — the default MusicBrainz artwork icon stayed and nothing was logged even with debug logging enabled. The handler now logs at `debug` level (`caaLoadIcon: failed to load — icon stays in default state`), making failures visible when `sa_enable_debug_logging` is on.

caaLoadIcon: the constructed coverartarchive.org URL is now logged at `debug` level before the request is issued (`caaLoadIcon: fetching <url>`), and a success message is logged on `load` (`caaLoadIcon: loaded OK — <url>`). Together with the new `onerror` log, every icon fetch now has an observable start / success / failure trace in the console when debug logging is active.

caaLoadIcon: the `if (!anchor) return` no-op branch now logs a `debug` message (`caaLoadIcon: skipped — no wrapping anchor found for icon`) instead of returning silently, making it easier to spot mis-extracted icons that were placed in the CAA cell without a cover-art anchor wrapper.

caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon: HTTP errors are now split into two severity levels — 404 ('no CAA entry') stays at `debug` (expected noise for release-groups with no artwork at all), while 429 and any 5xx status code is promoted to `Lib.warn` so it surfaces in the console without enabling full debug mode.
caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon: network-level failures (offline, DNS, CORS abort) are now logged at `Lib.warn` instead of `Lib.debug`, since they are unexpected and actionable.

caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon: HTTP error branches (non-200 responses) now write a sentinel value of 0 to `_caaRgCountCache` and set `anchor.dataset.caaEnriched = '1'` before returning. Previously neither was done, causing every filter/sort re-render that rebuilds the DOM from clones to issue a fresh network request to an endpoint that had already returned an error — potentially dozens of repeated failed requests per page interaction. Network-level failures (catch block) are intentionally not cached, as they may be transient.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — pressing Enter on a regular value now keeps the column filter field focused with the cursor positioned after the inserted value (filtering still triggers immediately via the synthetic input event). Previously the dropdown closed and focus was lost entirely.

Unique-values dropdown — 'Cell structure' synthetic entries (empty cells, single-row cells, collapsed/expanded multi-row cells) now correctly show a blue highlight on both mouse-hover and keyboard focus (ArrowDown/Up). Previously the resting background (#f0f4f8) was applied as an inline style, which always defeated the CSS :hover and .mb-uniq-focused rules regardless of specificity. The fix moves the resting background and font-style to the .mb-col-uniq-multirow-item CSS class (specificity 0,1,0), allowing the existing :hover and .mb-uniq-focused rules (specificity 0,2,0) to override it as intended.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Per-sub-table Column Visibility menu now has full keyboard control, identical to the global Visible menu: ArrowDown/Up and Tab/Shift-Tab cycle through all focusable items (checkboxes then action buttons) with focus trapped inside the menu; Space toggles the focused checkbox; Alt+S / Alt+D / Alt+C trigger Select All / Deselect All / Choose current configuration; Enter fires a focused action button or saves+closes when focus is on a checkbox row.
Sub-table menu open: first checkbox now receives focus and a blue-highlight immediately (was a bare subCheckboxes[0].focus() with no highlight).

Sub-table action buttons (Select All, Deselect All, Choose current configuration) now use makeButtonHTML() for underlined Alt-shortcut hints, carry tabIndex=0, and have mousedown/mouseup/mouseleave press-feedback styling — matching the global menu exactly.

sections.label: 🧹 Refactor
items: Extracted buildColVisMenuKeyboard(opts) — a shared keyboard-navigation factory placed immediately before addColumnVisibilityToggle(). It accepts { menu, checkboxes, selectAllBtn, deselectAllBtn, chooseConfigBtn, saveState, menuBtnBase } and returns { moveFocusTo, menuKeyHandler }. The handler is registered in capture phase so Tab preventDefault beats the browser's native focus-traversal. Both addColumnVisibilityToggle() and createSubTableColumnVisibilityButton() call this factory; all previously duplicated updateCheckboxFocus(), moveFocusTo(), menuFocusables, and the 130-line inline switch-based menuKeyHandler have been removed from addColumnVisibilityToggle().

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Ctrl+V and Ctrl+R (column-filter-focused) always fell back to the global button — root cause: the h3 lookup used `_cfTable.previousElementSibling` directly, but in multi-table mode when CAA big-pics are active a `div.mb-caa-bigbox` sits between the h3 and the table, so `previousElementSibling` returned the bigbox (not the h3) and the `classList.contains('mb-toggle-h3')` guard evaluated to false. Fix: replaced the single-step lookup with a `_findOwningH3()` helper that walks up to 5 `previousElementSibling` steps (mirrors the identical strategy used by `caaFindHeaderForTable()`). Both Ctrl+R and Ctrl+V now share this single resolved `_cfH3` variable computed once at the top of the column-filter-focused block.

sections.label: 🔧 Changes
items: Unsort column shortcut changed from Ctrl+Shift+= to Ctrl+# — '#' (Shift+3 on US/UK keyboards) is reported by the browser as e.key='#' without any shifted-key ambiguity, so the handler is now a plain isShortcutEvent() call; the complex parsePrefixShortcut() + multi-key fallback workaround has been removed. Config key: sa_shortcut_col_unsort, new default: 'Ctrl+#'.

Ctrl+V (Visible Columns) now targets the per-sub-table visible-columns button (id pattern mb-stf-${safeId}-vis-btn, class .mb-subtable-vis-btn) in the preceding h3.mb-toggle-h3 header when focus is inside a sub-table column filter field; falls back to the global #mb-visible-btn in single-table mode (no per-sub-table button present). Previously the sub-table handler always used the global button.

showShortcutsHelp(): updated help-dialog entry for Ctrl+V (column filter context) to document the per-sub-table-first lookup; updated Ctrl+# entry; updated footer note to list Ctrl+# and describe Ctrl+R/Ctrl+V sub-table-vs-global fallback logic.

sections.label: 🔧 Changes
items: Removed sa_expand_show_all_cells setting — auto-expanding '(show N more)' cells before row extraction is now unconditional and integral to the script's functioning; the guard check in expandShowAllCells() has been dropped and the config entry removed.
expandShowAllCells(): updated JSDoc to reflect that the feature is always active; removed reference to the now-deleted sa_expand_show_all_cells flag.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Column-filter-focused keyboard shortcuts — when a column filter input has focus, the following new shortcuts operate directly on the focused column's header controls: Ctrl+↑ (sort ascending, emulates ▲ icon), Ctrl+↓ (sort descending, emulates ▼ icon), Ctrl+Shift+= (restore original sort order, emulates ⇅ icon; matches both = and + for US keyboards), Ctrl+O (toggle multi-row cell collapse, emulates ▶/◀ header icon), Ctrl+Q (open unique-values dropdown, emulates 📊 header icon), Ctrl+A (toggle CAA cover art images for the enclosing table).

Ctrl+R (Resize) redefined at sub-table level — when focus is inside a column filter of a sub-table (multi-table mode), Ctrl+R clicks the per-sub-table .mb-subtable-resize-btn button in the preceding h3 header instead of the global resize; falls back to global toggleAutoResizeColumns() in single-table mode.

Ctrl+V (Visible Columns) consumed at column-filter level — when focus is inside a column filter, Ctrl+V still opens the global visible-columns menu but the event is consumed by the column-filter handler so it does not bubble to the global handler (consistent behaviour).

Six new configSchema entries in the KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS section (type: keyboard_shortcut): sa_shortcut_col_sort_asc (Ctrl+ArrowUp), sa_shortcut_col_sort_desc (Ctrl+ArrowDown), sa_shortcut_col_unsort (Ctrl+Shift+=), sa_shortcut_col_toggle_collapse (Ctrl+O), sa_shortcut_col_unique_dropdown (Ctrl+Q), sa_shortcut_col_toggle_caa (Ctrl+A).

showShortcutsHelp(): added a new 'Column Filter Controls (when a column filter has focus)' section listing all eight column-filter shortcuts; updated the bottom note to mention the focus-guard on these shortcuts.

sections.label: ⚙️ Design notes
items: Column-filter-focused shortcuts are injected at the very top of the initKeyboardShortcuts() keydown listener, before all global handlers; when a shortcut is matched the handler returns early so global shortcuts do not double-fire.

Ctrl+Shift+= (unsort) bypasses isShortcutEvent() and uses parsePrefixShortcut() directly with an explicit '=' / '+' / e.code==='Equal' fallback, because the Shift modifier causes e.key to be '+' on US keyboards while the stored config value holds '='.

CAA table-index resolution: the focused filter's closest('table.tbl') is looked up in Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('table.tbl')) to obtain the zero-based index used by the mb-caa-toggle-btn-N naming convention.

sections.label: 🔧 Changes
items: CAA count badge: removed custom font-size/color/margin styling — count is now rendered as plain normal-weight inline text after the cover-art anchor in the CAA cell.

CAA icon tooltip: fixed tooltip not appearing when hovering the thumbnail — anchor.title alone was insufficient because the browser shows the title of the element directly under the pointer; the icon span (which carries the CSS background-image) may shadow the anchor's title if it has its own (pre-populated or stale) title attribute. The tooltip is now set on both the wrapping anchor and the child span.caa-icon / span.eaa-icon / span.artwork-icon directly.

sections.label: 🔧 Changes
items: caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon: removed count prefix span prepended before the title link in the addCAA column cell — the count is now only surfaced as: (1) tooltip on the cover-art anchor in the CAA cell, (2) inline count badge after the anchor in the CAA cell, and (3) tooltip on the .mb-caa-inline-ph placeholder in the addCAA column cell (e.g. Title). No text is injected into the Title column.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: CAA image count decoration — caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon now renders the API-fetched image count in three places: (1) as the tooltip (title attribute) on the cover-art anchor in the synthetic CAA column (already present but previously blocked by a guard bug); (2) as a small inline count badge <span class="mb-caa-count-badge"> appended after the anchor inside the CAA cell, layout: [icon] N; (3) as a count prefix <span class="mb-caa-count-prefix"> prepended immediately before the primary entity title link in the addCAA column cell (e.g. 'Title'), layout: [▶] [inline-img] N Title. All three decorations carry the same tooltip text. Idempotency is guaranteed by data-caa-enriched='1' on the anchor node (prevents duplicate work when sort/filter re-renders reuse live DOM nodes) and by class-presence guards on the badge and prefix spans.

_caaRgCountCache — new module-level Map<entityPath, count>; caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon stores every successfully fetched count so that DOM-rebuild re-renders (renderFinalTable / filter) obtain the count instantly from the cache without a second network round-trip to coverartarchive.org.

sections.label: ⚙️ Design notes
items: Re-render idempotency strategy: data-caa-enriched on anchor covers same-node reuse; .mb-caa-count-badge / .mb-caa-count-prefix querySelector guards cover both same-node and fresh-clone cases; _caaRgCountCache avoids redundant fetches in all cases.
addCAA column resolution inside caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon uses the existing caaFindColumnByName() helper and reads activeDefinition.features.addCAA from module scope — no new parameters needed.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug fixes
items: CAA icon tooltip always showed the stock MusicBrainz title 'This release group has artwork in the Cover Art Archive' instead of the enriched count 'N image(s) found in this release': caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon guarded with `if (anchor.title) return`, which fired immediately on every anchor because MusicBrainz pre-populates that string on all cover-art links; the API was actually called and returned correct counts (visible in debug log) but the title assignment was never reached. Fix: replaced the blunt non-empty guard with a pattern check (`/^\d+ images? found/.test(anchor.title)`) that skips only anchors already enriched by a previous re-render pass, leaving the stock MB title transparent to the guard.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug fixes
items: CAA small thumbnails not rendered on artist-releasegroups pages (e.g. /artist/70248960-…) when the jesus2099 'mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL' script is active: the jesus2099 columnEraser strips the cover-art anchor before the caa extractor runs; Path B then moved the bare <span class="artwork-icon caa-icon"> into the CAA cell without any wrapping anchor, so caaInitSmallPics (selector: a[href$="/cover-art"] > span.caa-icon) and caaLoadIcon (bails when closest('a[href]') is null) both silently no-op'd. Fix: Path B now looks for the entity title link already present in the source cell (a[href*="/release-group/"] etc.), synthesises a <a href="ENTITY_PATH/cover-art"> anchor, and wraps the bare span inside it before appending to tdCaa — restoring the same structure that Path A produces and enabling the full thumbnail-loading pipeline.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Inline CAA thumbnails in Release/Title columns — new feature initCaaInlinePics(); inserts a fixed 20x20 px placeholder span (class mb-caa-inline-ph) into every row of the addCAA-configured column, positioned between the ERG ▶ expand button and the entity title link; cover-art images are fetched asynchronously from coverartarchive.org; on load the img becomes visible; on error the placeholder remains as an invisible spacer so titles stay aligned; guarded by sa_enable_caa_pics (master) and new sa_caa_pics_inline (sub-toggle, default true); stale clone markers cleared on re-render
sa_caa_pics_inline — new config checkbox (default true) in the 🖼️ CAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY section; independently enables/disables the inline thumbnail feature

features.addCAA per-pageDefinition property — set for 7 page types: artist-releasegroups (Title), artist-releases (Release), releasegroup-releases (Release), series-releases (Release), label-releases (Release), artist-recordings (Release groups), recording-releases (Release title)

isExpandRGsPage() extended — now also returns true for /recording/<UUID> (root recording page only); enables initExpandRGsFeature() to inject ▶ expand buttons in the Release title column on recording pages
ERG ▶ button tooltip — ergCreateButton() now sets button.title = "Toggle display of underlying entity page"
recording-releases columnErasers updated — added ▶ to the Release title erasers list so source-page ▶ spans are cleaned at fetch time, consistent with other release page types

sections.label: ⚙️ Design notes
items: columnErasers already run during initial rendering — applyColumnErasers is called on document.importNode copies inside the fetch pipeline, before rows are accumulated; source page DOM is never mutated; this is correct design: erasing at fetch time avoids a second DOM pass and ensures extractors like caa never see the ▶ or jesus2099 spans
caaFindColumnByName(table, name) — new general-purpose column-index resolver; used by initCaaInlinePics(); prefers th.dataset.colName then falls back to regex-stripped textContent

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA badge double-count in multi-table mode — root cause: the previous two-phase init called caaInitBigPics twice per table; the first call (no btnId) created N img elements and started their network requests; the second call cleared box.innerHTML and created N new imgs with the same src; even though the first-batch imgs were detached from the DOM, browsers continue firing their load events on detached elements — but because those elements had no btnId in their closure the badge was not incremented by them directly; the real doubling occurred because browsers serve cached images near-synchronously, so both the detached-batch and the live-batch load events could fire close together, and in some timing windows the badge was incremented by unexpected re-entrant paths; fix: introduced caaCountLinks(table) — a pure read-only pre-flight that scans tbody anchors and returns {count, firstImgUrl} without touching the DOM; initCaaPics() now calls caaCountLinks → caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (gets btnId) → caaInitBigPics once with btnId, so exactly one set of img elements is ever created per render pass
CAA toggle button tooltip text renamed from 'Toggle Cover Art Archive - Hide/Show' to 'Toggle CAA cover art images - Hide/Show'

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: caaCountLinks(table) — new pure helper (no DOM side-effects); mirrors the href-matching logic of caaInitBigPics but only accumulates seen hrefs and records the first image URL; used as the single source of {count, firstImgUrl} in initCaaPics()
initCaaPics() simplified to a single-phase loop: caaCountLinks → caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton → caaInitBigPics(table, i, btnId); removed the two-phase / double-build pattern
Removed orphaned duplicate JSDoc block for caaFindHeaderForTable that was left over from a previous refactor

caaInitBigPics: badge reset now atomic — .mb-caa-toggle-count is reset to "0" inside caaInitBigPics itself, immediately after box.innerHTML='' and before any img.src assignments; this ensures that cached images whose load event fires synchronously during the forEach cannot increment a stale non-zero badge value

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA toggle button landing on h2 in multi-table mode — root cause: caaInitBigPics inserts div.mb-caa-bigbox immediately before the table, so table.previousElementSibling was the bigbox div rather than the h3; caaFindHeaderForTable now walks backwards through up to 5 previousElementSibling steps looking for h3.mb-toggle-h3 before falling through to the h2 scan, so each sub-table button is correctly placed in its own h3 header
sa_caa_pics_initially_collapsed default changed from false to true — artwork strip is now hidden on first render by default; images still download in the background

CAA toggle button badge now shows downloaded image count, not total link count — badge starts at 0; each img load event in caaInitBigPics increments .mb-caa-toggle-count on the corresponding toggle button (identified by btnId passed as a third parameter); images that 404 do not increment the badge; on filter/sort re-renders the badge resets to 0 and climbs again as images reload

sections.label: ⚙️ Internal
items: caaInitBigPics(table, tableIndex, btnId) — optional third parameter; when present, each img load handler does document.getElementById(btnId) and increments .mb-caa-toggle-count; also sets .mb-caa-toggle-thumb src on the first successful load if it has not loaded yet

initCaaPics() two-phase init — first pass calls caaInitBigPics without btnId (returns {count,firstImgUrl}) then calls caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton (returns btnId); second pass calls caaInitBigPics again with btnId so load handlers can update the badge; the second call is a cheap rebuild because the bigbox already exists in the DOM

caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton — badge initialised to '0' (not count); on re-render (isNew===false) badge is reset to '0' so it accurately reflects the current download progress; returns btnId string on success

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: sa_caa_pics_initially_collapsed config — new checkbox (default: false) in the '🖼️ CAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY' settings section; when enabled the big CAA picture strip starts hidden after rendering but images are still downloaded in the background so revealing the strip via the toggle button is instant

CAA toggle button (#mb-caa-toggle-btn-N) — per-table button injected immediately after the .mb-row-count-stat span in the h2 header (single-table mode) or h3.mb-toggle-h3 header (multi-table mode); button content: thumbnail of the first available CAA cover art + numeric image count badge; tooltip: 'Toggle Cover Art Archive - Hide' or '… Show' reflecting the current visibility state; only injected when the table has at least one release/release-group link in its CAA/EAA column
caaFindHeaderForTable(table) — helper that returns the h3.mb-toggle-h3 (multi-table) or the last preceding h2 (single-table) for a given table.tbl; mirrors the h2-detection logic in updateH2Count()

caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton(table, tableIndex, count, firstImgUrl) — idempotent factory; on first call creates the button and inserts it via caaFindHeaderForTable; on re-renders updates thumbnail src and count badge in-place without re-attaching the click handler; skips injection if count === 0 or no header is found; click handler is e.stopPropagation()-guarded so it does not bubble to the h3 collapse handler in multi-table mode

caaInitBigPics() refactored to return { count, firstImgUrl } — count is the number of unique release/release-group anchors found; firstImgUrl is the coverartarchive.org URL of the first image; both are consumed by caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton

Bigbox visibility state preserved across re-renders — caaInitBigPics() reads box.dataset.caaVisible before clearing innerHTML; if the user collapsed the strip it stays collapsed after a filter/sort re-render; on first render the initial state is read from sa_caa_pics_initially_collapsed
initCaaPics() updated to destructure { count, firstImgUrl } from caaInitBigPics() and pass them to caaCreateOrUpdateToggleButton

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA release-group image-count tooltip missing from small-pic icons — caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon(anchor) added: fires an async fetch to https://coverartarchive.org/release-group/{GUID} for every release-group anchor inside the CAA column; on a successful 200 response with at least one image, sets anchor.title to 'N image(s) found in this release' (mirrors jesus2099's XHR branch for types[t]=="release-group" and its title: RGCAA.images.length + " image" + … expression); skips anchors that already carry a title (idempotent across re-renders); skips non-release-group anchors silently; logs failures at debug level only

caaInitSmallPics() updated to call caaEnrichReleaseGroupIcon(anchor) for every processed icon in addition to caaLoadIcon() — the two calls are independent: the image background loads immediately as before, and the API fetch for the title runs asynchronously without blocking the thumbnail

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: CAA column detection in initCaaPics() was always returning -1: caaFindCaaColumnIndex() previously compared th.textContent.trim() against 'CAA'/'EAA', but after makeTableSortableUnified wraps every header in a mb-col-hdr-flex div and appends sort-icon glyphs (⇅▲▼📊), th.textContent is no longer the bare column name — fixed by consulting th.dataset.colName first (authoritative, set at render time), with a regex-stripped textContent fallback (replace(/[⇅▲▼📊▶◀]/g,'').trim()) identical to the pattern used by addColumnVisibilityToggle and other SA helpers

data-col-name attribute stamped on all th elements: (1) makeTableSortableUnified now sets th.dataset.colName = colName immediately before th.appendChild(hdrFlex), covering every column in both native and synthetic tables; (2) the three synthetic-header injection loops in cleanupHeaders() (columnExtractors, syntheticColumnExtractors, MB-Name/Comment) each set th.dataset.colName = colName on the newly created th so the attribute is present even before makeTableSortableUnified processes the header row

Restored missing totalRenderingTime = performance.now() - renderingTimeStart; assignment (main fetch pipeline): the line was accidentally omitted during the initCaaPics() wiring in v9.99.89; without it totalRenderingTime held its previous value (or 0) causing the 'DOM rendering finished in X.XXms' log and the renderSeconds status display to show stale/incorrect timings
Restored missing Lib.debug('render', 'Finished renderGroupedTable.') call (renderGroupedTable): the debug line was accidentally omitted during the initCaaPics() wiring in v9.99.89

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Barcode highlight toggle button (#mb-barcode-highlight-btn) height fixed: the old code hard-coded height:20px via a regex replacement on uiActionBtnBaseCSS() output, making the button shorter than all other controls-bar buttons; the height override is removed and only the horizontal padding (now 4px) and font-size (0.65em) are replaced, so the button's height is now identical to #mb-visible-btn while remaining narrower horizontally

Barcode highlight toggle button visibility guard: addBarcodeHighlightToggleButton() now checks for the presence of a Barcode column before injecting the button — it looks for (1) any table.tbl th whose trimmed text is 'Barcode', or (2) any td.barcode-cell already present in the DOM; if neither is found the function returns silently at debug level, suppressing the button on all non-release page types (events, works, recordings, aliases, etc.)

CAA Illustrated Discography feature integrated — functions from jesus2099's 'mb. FUNKEY ILLUSTRATED RECORDS' userscript (CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 / GPL-3.0-or-later) are extracted and integrated into SA so the feature runs on SA-consolidated tables that contain a CAA or EAA column
sa_enable_caa_pics config — new master checkbox (default: true) in a dedicated '🖼️ CAA ILLUSTRATED DISCOGRAPHY' settings section; guards initCaaPics() at all call sites
sa_caa_pics_small config — toggle for small CAA thumbnails (default: true); maps to jesus2099's pics_settings.small
sa_caa_pics_big config — toggle for big picture strip (default: true); maps to jesus2099's pics_settings.big
sa_caa_small_img_size config — number (250/500/1200, default 250); coverartarchive.org size suffix for small thumbnails; maps to jesus2099's hardcoded '/front-250' in loadCaaIcon()
sa_caa_big_img_size config — number (250/500/1200, default 250); size suffix for big strip images; maps to jesus2099's hardcoded '/front-250' in the big-pics loop
sa_caa_big_max_height config — number (default 125 px); maximum display height of images in the big strip; maps to jesus2099's hardcoded maxHeight:'125px'
sa_caa_highlight_colour config — color picker (default #ffff00); hover highlight colour applied to row links and strip image borders; maps to jesus2099's var colour = 'yellow'

caaLoadIcon(caaIcon) — loads a cover-art thumbnail into the background-image of one caa/eaa/artwork-icon span; resolves the URL via closest('a[href]') supporting both standard MB anchors and jesus2099's ref attribute; silently skips bare spans; mirrors jesus2099's loadCaaIcon()
caaFindCaaColumnIndex(table) — finds a 'CAA' or 'EAA' th, returns its 0-based index or -1; used to skip tables that have no cover-art column
caaInitSmallPics(table) — queries all td a[href$='/cover-art'] > span.{caa,eaa,artwork}-icon and calls caaLoadIcon on each; mirrors the SMALL PICS branch

caaInitBigPics(table, tableIndex) — creates/rebuilds a div#mb-caa-bigbox-N before the table; populates it with lazy-loaded img elements for every unique release/release-group link in tbody; uses event delegation on tbody for row↔image hover highlighting with stale-listener cleanup on re-renders; mirrors the BIG PICS branch + updateA + updateBig

initCaaPics() — entry point; guarded by sa_enable_caa_pics; skips tables without a CAA/EAA column; calls caaInitSmallPics + caaInitBigPics on qualifying tables; wired into all four render-completion paths (main fetch pipeline, runFilter() single-table branch, renderGroupedTable(), load-from-disk pipeline) immediately after initBarcodeHighlight()

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Barcode highlight button made smaller: uses font-size:0.65em / padding:1px 5px / height:20px via regex-replacement on uiActionBtnBaseCSS() output so user-configured button styles are still respected as a base

Per-sub-table column visibility button (multi-table mode): createSubTableColumnVisibilityButton(table, categoryName) factory added — creates a 👁️ button inserted into the h3 row right after the ↔️ resize button; button is identical in structure to the global Visible dialog but calls toggleColumnInTable() so only the one sub-table is affected
toggleColumnInTable(table, columnIndex, show) helper added — mirrors toggleColumn() but scoped to a single table including colgroup/width recalculation when auto- or manual-resize is active

subTableColVisRegistry Map added — each createSubTableColumnVisibilityButton() call registers { applyGlobalConfig(state) } under a safeId key so the global button can push overrides to every sub-table widget at once

Global 'Choose current configuration' override: refactored saveColVisState into getCurrentColVisState()+saveColVisState(); chooseConfigBtn.onclick now reads getCurrentColVisState() and calls applyGlobalConfig() on every registry entry, propagating the global column layout to all sub-tables and persisting it under each sub-table's own GM storage key

Last-write-wins storage strategy: sub-table key 'vz-mb-colvis-{pageType}-sub-{safeId}' is used first on load; falls back to global key 'vz-mb-colvis-{pageType}' when no sub-table override exists yet; global push writes both keys so the next reload still sees the correct config

Vis button injected in all three renderGroupedTable code paths: (1) !query initial render — created alongside resize btn; (2) reuse-existing-h3 defensive branch — guarded by .mb-subtable-vis-btn absent check; (3) filter re-render defensive branch — same guard

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Barcode highlight toggle button — addBarcodeHighlightToggleButton() injects a new controls-bar button with the glyph label '▌█▌▐█▐▌██▐▌█' positioned between the 👁️ Visible and 📏 Density buttons; idempotent (skipped if already present); guarded by sa_enable_barcode_highlight

Toggle behaviour — first click turns highlighting Off: clearAllBarcodeHighlights() strips background-color, font-weight, padding, border-radius and cursor from every td.barcode-cell and detaches the barcodeToggleMergeCheckbox click handler; button turns dark grey (#555555 bg / #333333 border / #ffffff text) and tooltip reads 'Toggle barcode highlightning on'; second click turns highlighting On: initBarcodeHighlight() re-applies all highlights, button reverts to default appearance, tooltip reads 'Toggle barcode highlightning off'

isBarcodeHighlightActive module-level flag tracks the on/off state; initBarcodeHighlight() checks the flag at entry so filter/sort re-renders never silently re-apply highlights while the user has toggled them off

updateBarcodeHighlightBtnState(btn) — shared helper applies the correct inline style and tooltip to the toggle button from a single source of truth; called both at button-creation time and after every click

clearAllBarcodeHighlights() — new helper that removes only the four inline properties set by barcodeProcessTable (background-color, font-weight, padding, border-radius, cursor) from all td.barcode-cell elements and detaches the click listener; used by the toggle and available to other code paths

addBarcodeHighlightToggleButton() wired into both render-completion paths (main fetch pipeline and load-from-disk pipeline) immediately after addColumnVisibilityToggle and before addDensityControl, preserving the Visible | Barcode | Density button order

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Barcode Highlight feature integrated — functions from chaban's 'MusicBrainz: Highlight identical barcodes and toggle merge checkboxes' userscript (MIT) are extracted and integrated into SA so the feature runs on SA-consolidated tables instead of (or in addition to) the original MB page
sa_enable_barcode_highlight config — new checkbox setting (default: true) in a dedicated 'BARCODE HIGHLIGHT' settings section; guards initBarcodeHighlight() at all call sites
barcodeGetRandomColor() — generates a random pastel hex colour (channels 8–F so every value ≥ 128) avoiding duplicates via barcodeUsedColors Set; mirrors chaban's getRandomColor
barcodeRemoveLeadingZeros(barcode) — strips leading zeros so '0074643190312' and '74643190312' are treated as the same barcode group; mirrors chaban's removeLeadingZeros

barcodeToggleMergeCheckbox(event) — click handler attached to highlighted cells; selects/deselects all merge checkboxes in the same group and clears all others; operates only within table.tbl so it never interferes with other page elements; mirrors chaban's toggleMergeCheckbox

barcodeProcessTable(table) — scans one table.tbl for Barcode column cells (by 'Barcode' th header text; falls back to existing .barcode-cell class); groups cells by normalised-barcode+format identifier; assigns stable pastel colours to groups with ≥ 2 members; stamps cells with data-barcode-identifier and the .barcode-cell class; attaches barcodeToggleMergeCheckbox click handler when merge checkboxes are present; clears highlights from singleton cells to undo any leftover state from a previous render

initBarcodeHighlight() — entry point; clears barcodeIdentifierToCheckboxes (drops stale DOM references), then iterates all table.tbl elements and calls barcodeProcessTable on each; hooked into all four render-completion paths: main fetch pipeline, runFilter() single-table branch, renderGroupedTable() (multi-table re-renders), and load-from-disk pipeline — always after initExpandRGsFeature()

barcodeIdentifierToColor / barcodeIdentifierToCheckboxes / barcodeUsedColors — three module-level state containers; the colour map is permanent (colours survive re-renders); the checkbox map is reset at the start of each initBarcodeHighlight() call to avoid holding stale DOM references

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sub-table resize button not updated on manual column drag — markSubTableAsManuallyResized(table) is now called from the makeColumnsResizable mousedown handler immediately after isManuallyResized is set; it snapshots the sub-table state into subTableOriginalStates (once), sets subTableResizedStates true for that table, calls applySubTableResizeBtnActiveState() to mark the button active, and calls updateGlobalResizeBtnFromSubTables() so the global 'Resize*' amber hint fires on the first drag just as it does for auto-resize

Sub-table resize button active colour was too pale — active state was #e8f5e9 bg / #4CAF50 border / #2e7d32 text, visually close to the default grey; replaced with dark solid green #1b5e20 bg / #0a3d12 border / #ffffff text via the new shared applySubTableResizeBtnActiveState() helper, clearly distinguishable from both the default button and the global Resize button's lighter active shade

applySubTableResizeBtnActiveState(btn, categoryName) — new shared helper that applies the dark-green active style and correct tooltip wording ('Restore original column widths…') in one place; used by both toggleSubTableAutoResize (auto-resize path) and markSubTableAsManuallyResized (manual-drag path)

resetSubTableResizeBtnState(btn, categoryName) — new shared helper that reverts button to default appearance and tooltip; replaces duplicated inline style assignments in toggleSubTableAutoResize restore path and the global-restore forEach loop

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: cleanupBarcodeHighlights — new cleanup function called from performClutterCleanup() that removes inline highlight styles (background-color, font-weight, padding, border-radius, cursor) injected by chaban's 'Highlight identical barcodes and toggle merge checkboxes' userscript from all td.barcode-cell elements; only cells that actually carry the chaban highlight (non-empty background-color property) are touched, leaving all other inline styles intact; the count of cleaned cells is logged at debug level

Per-sub-table ↔️ Resize / Restore button in multi-table mode — createSubTableResizeButton(table, categoryName) factory creates a compact button inserted into each h3 sub-table header immediately before the 🔍 mb-subtable-filter-toggle-icon; the button mirrors the global Resize/Restore toggle but acts exclusively on the single sub-table it was created for

toggleSubTableAutoResize(table, btn, categoryName) — new function that performs a full measure-and-apply auto-resize cycle on one sub-table (sampling up to 100 tbody rows, measuring both headers and data cells via an off-screen div, applying widths through a colgroup, and optionally re-adding column-resizer handles); on the second call the sub-table is restored from the snapshot stored in subTableOriginalStates

subTableResizedStates / subTableOriginalStates — two new module-level Maps that track per-sub-table resize state and pre-resize snapshots; cleared automatically when the global Restore action runs so the two state systems stay in sync

updateGlobalResizeBtnFromSubTables — new function called after every per-sub-table toggle to update the global ↔️ Resize button: when at least one sub-table is resized and the global resize is inactive the button label changes to '↔️ Resize*' with an amber (#fff3e0 / #FF9800) colour scheme; when all sub-tables are restored and no global resize is active the button reverts to its default appearance

updateResizeButtonState extended — the else-branch now queries subTableResizedStates for any truthy entry before deciding the button label/colour so the amber 'Resize*' state is always applied consistently regardless of call site

Global Restore clears sub-table state — toggleAutoResizeColumns restore branch now calls subTableResizedStates.clear() and subTableOriginalStates.clear() and resets all .mb-subtable-resize-btn inline styles so the per-sub-table buttons revert to their default appearance when the user performs a global restore

Defensive code-paths patched — both 'Adding missing sub-table controls' branches in renderGroupedTable (reuse-existing-h3 and filtering case) now also inject a .mb-subtable-resize-btn when one is absent, positioning it before the 🔍 filter-toggle icon

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: eventParts synthetic extractor refined: improved right-to-left location parsing logic to specifically handle granular address structures for 'UK', 'USA', and 'Canada' (mapping Venue, Venue2, City, State, and Country); ensures consistent column population even when optional segments like Event-Detail or Venue2 are missing; normalized date handling for Unicode non-breaking hyphens.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unicode character picker integrated from 'mb.unicodechars' userscript by Smeulf: pressing the configurable shortcut (default Ctrl+U) while a text input or textarea has focus opens a floating picker menu listing 18 Unicode characters (quotes, dashes, ellipsis, primes, multiplication sign, etc.); the menu is positioned directly below the focused field; clicking a row or pressing Enter inserts the character at the cursor position and closes the menu; ArrowUp/ArrowDown navigate rows; Escape closes without inserting; the trigger key is configurable via the new 'Shortcut: Unicode Character Picker' (sa_shortcut_unicode_chars) setting in the Keyboard Shortcuts config section (default Ctrl+U); the picker is documented in a new 'Input Assistance' section of the 🎹 Keyboard Shortcuts help dialog; integrated as a self-contained SA feature: no jQuery scanning loop, no localStorage, no unsafeWindow, no GM_addStyle — CSS is injected via a <style> element, state is module-level, and the menu is built once via initSaUnicodeCharsFeature() at render completion

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: MBID_REGEX not defined — ReferenceError in ergInjectReleaseButton / ergInjectReleaseGroupButton: the MBID UUID pattern was declared as a local const string inside isExpandRGsPage() and was therefore inaccessible to the two inject functions; fixed by hoisting a module-level const MBID_REGEX into the erg-functions block and updating isExpandRGsPage to use MBID_REGEX.source

erg expand buttons dead after sort or filter, and double ▶▶ icons on re-render — two related root causes: (1) renderFinalTable / renderGroupedTable insert cloneNode(true) copies of allRows into the DOM; cloneNode copies DOM attributes (including data-erg-injected and data-erg-btn) but does NOT copy event listeners, so every ▶ button became inert; (2) the prior fix that stripped data-erg-injected from clones before appending correctly allowed re-injection but also reset the idempotency guard, so initExpandRGsFeature() was called once per sort/filter AND once from the initial render path, injecting a second button into the same <td>; fixed by: (a) adding data-erg-btn='1' to every button span created by ergCreateButton so stale clones are unambiguously identifiable, (b) removing the data-erg-injected strip from all clone-append sites (renderFinalTable fast/chunked paths and renderGroupedTable append paths) so the guard attribute is preserved across re-renders, (c) moving stale-button cleanup into initExpandRGsFeature itself — before the injection guard check, all [data-erg-btn] spans are removed from the parent <td>, then data-erg-injected is cleared so the guard is reset and exactly one fresh live button is injected per cell per render cycle, (d) calling initExpandRGsFeature() after initCollapsableColumns() in the single-table runFilter branch and after rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti() in renderGroupedTable

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Expand Release Groups feature integrated — functions from the 'MusicBrainz: Expand/collapse release groups' userscript by Michael Wiencek are extracted and integrated into SA; all functions are prefixed with 'erg' to avoid name collisions; the original 'arguments.callee' usage (forbidden in strict mode) is replaced by named inner function declarations for self-referencing event listener removal; the original HTML-entity multiplication sign '×' is replaced by the Unicode character U+00D7 so 'textContent' can be used safely for format strings

ergCreateButton — lazy-loading toggle button factory; first mousedown fires an XHR to the MB Web Service, subsequent clicks toggle the inline table; on error a one-shot retry handler is installed; toggled state drives ▶ / ▼ glyph and domCallback invocation
ergInjectReleaseGroupButton — injects a ▶ button into the <td> of a release-group link; on expand fetches '/ws/2/release?release-group=…&inc=media' and renders via ergParseReleaseGroup

ergInjectReleaseButton — injects a ▶ button into the <td> of a release link; on expand fetches '/ws/2/release/<mbid>?inc=media+recordings+artist-credits' and renders via ergParseRelease; accepts optional pre-created tableParent, table, and mbid for reuse inside ergParseReleaseGroup

ergParseReleaseGroup — renders all releases of a release group sorted by date; each release row contains nested per-release expand buttons; a footer row with edit / add-release / editing-history links is appended

ergParseRelease — renders a release's full track listing grouped by medium with track number, recording link, duration, and artist credits; a footer row with edit / edit-relationships / history / add-cover-art links is appended
ergCreateAC — renders an artist-credit array as a <span> of linked artist names with join-phrases; link title attribute set to artist sort-name

initExpandRGsFeature — entry point; queries '#content table.tbl > tbody > tr > td a[href^="/release"]', routes each qualifying link to the appropriate inject function; data-erg-injected attribute on the parent <td> prevents double-injection on repeated calls; guarded by 'sa_enable_expand_rgs' setting and isExpandRGsPage() URL check

isExpandRGsPage — returns true for artist/* (all sub-pages), label/<UUID> (root only), release-group/<UUID> (root only), series/<UUID> (root only), matching the original expandRGs @match/@exclude patterns
sa_enable_expand_rgs config — new checkbox setting (default: true) in a dedicated 'EXPAND RELEASE GROUPS' settings section; guards both post-render injection calls

initExpandRGsFeature hooked into both render-completion paths — called after initSidebarCollapse() in the main fetch pipeline and in the load-from-disk pipeline, so buttons are injected on every SA table render

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: eventParts synthetic extractor — new SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.eventParts(sourceCell) parses a MusicBrainz recording Comment field encoding live-performance metadata in the free-form pattern '[Event-Type][, Event-Date][, Event-Detail][: Venue[, Venue2][, City][, State][, Country]]' and splits it into eight synthetic columns: Event-Type, Event-Date, Event-Detail, Event-Venue, Event-Venue2, Event-City, Event-State, Event-Country; all segments are optional so bare values like 'soundcheck' or two-letter ISO country codes like 'live: JP' parse gracefully; the U+2010 NON-BREAKING HYPHEN used by MusicBrainz in date strings is normalised to ASCII '-' before date-pattern matching; location parts are mapped right-to-left (Country ← last, State ← second-last, City ← third-last, Venue2 ← fourth-last, Venue ← fifth-last) so trailing Country/State/City are always filled first

work-recordings-filtered — added syntheticColumnExtractors entry with eventParts sourcing from 'Comment' to produce Event-Type / Event-Date / Event-Detail / Event-Venue / Event-Venue2 / Event-City / Event-State / Event-Country columns on specialised Work recordings pages

pipeline reorder: MB-Name/Comment now extracted before second-pass map build — in both pipeline branches (artist-releasegroups and the generic else-branch), MB-Name/Comment extraction is moved from step 2 (after second pass) to step 1b (before second pass); the primarySyntheticCellMap now includes 'MB-Name' → tdName and 'Comment' → tdComment entries when extractMainColumn is active; this allows any syntheticColumnExtractor to declare 'Comment' (or 'MB-Name') as its sourceColumn and receive the already-populated cell without any change to the descriptor format; the existing invariant (primary extractors mutate source cell first, THEN Comment is read) is preserved

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: SyntheticColumnDataExtractor registry — introduces a second-pass extraction registry (SyntheticColumnDataExtractor) that runs after all primary ColumnDataExtractor passes have finished; each entry in the new `syntheticColumnExtractors` page-definition feature key names a source synthetic column (by the column header text produced by a primary extractor) and an extractor function, and returns further synthetic <td> elements; the source cell is located per-row from a name-keyed map built from primary extractor outputs, so no colIdx bookkeeping is needed

buildActiveSyntheticColumnExtractors — new builder function parallel to buildActiveColumnExtractors; reads features.syntheticColumnExtractors from the merged activeDefinition and returns a shallow-cloned descriptor list; initialised in startFetchingProcess alongside the primary extractor and eraser lists

dateParts synthetic extractor — new SyntheticColumnDataExtractor.dateParts(sourceCell) parses a partial or complete ISO 8601 date string ('YYYY', 'YYYY-MM', or 'YYYY-MM-DD') from a synthetic Date <td> (multi-row <ul><li> structure handled: first non-empty <li> is used); produces five synthetic columns: DD (integer day), MM (integer month), YYYY (integer year), Day (weekday name e.g. 'Wednesday'), Month (month name e.g. 'January'); partial dates are handled gracefully — missing components yield empty cells; the Date-constructor round-trip guard prevents silent normalisation of invalid calendar dates (e.g. 'YYYY-02-30')

syntheticColumnExtractors wired into both extraction pipeline branches — the artist-releasegroups branch and the generic else-branch both now build a primarySyntheticCellMap after primary extractors run and execute activeSyntheticColumnExtractors in a second pass; second-pass cells are appended after primary synthetic cells and before MB-Name/Comment

cleanupHeaders extended for syntheticColumnExtractors — headers declared in syntheticColumnExtractors are injected into the consolidated table <thead> immediately after primary extractor headers, with the same background tint and idempotency guard

debug summary extended — the per-page 'Detected indices' log line now includes a syntheticExtractors=[…] field listing all active synthetic-column extractors; startFetchingProcess logs a one-line summary of configured synthetic extractors at init time

pageDefinitions: artist-recordings and releasegroup-releases — syntheticColumnExtractors descriptors corrected to the canonical 5-column form ['DD', 'MM', 'YYYY', 'Day', 'Month'] (the erroneous 'Weekday' sixth column has been removed)

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: splitCountryDate: strip chaban day-of-week span from Date synthetic column — the Date cell now clones the .release-date span and removes any <span class="mb-day-of-week …"> child before reading textContent; without this fix the abbreviated weekday injected by chaban's userscript (e.g. "Wed", "Tue", "Sat") was concatenated directly onto the date string ("1995-11-22Wed") in the synthetic Date column; the Country column and the source cell are unaffected

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
