items: synBox '○ empty cells' generalized to every column — the unique-value dropdown (📊) now shows the '○ empty cells' synthetic entry for all column types (plain text, extractor synthetic, CAA, Video, Primary Alias, …), not only for collapsable multi-row columns; for collapsable columns the full 'Cell structure' section (empty / single-row / collapsed / expanded / any) is preserved as before; for all other columns only the single '○ empty cells' entry is emitted (no section header, no unneeded clutter), making it easy to filter e.g. to events that have no primary alias or releases that have no cover art; makeSynItem is hoisted above the collapsable guard and a shared appendSynDivider helper avoids duplicating the divider logic

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: testRowMatch hasEmpty fix — the 'empty cells' multi-row state filter now evaluates hasEmpty as lis.length === 0 && !getCleanColumnText(cell) instead of the former lis.length === 0 alone; without this fix every row in a non-collapsable column (which never contains ul>li structure) would have matched the '○ empty cells' filter, returning the full unfiltered row set instead of only the genuinely empty ones

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: extraction pipeline ordering fix — column extractors now run BEFORE the MB-Name / Comment extraction in both pipeline branches (shared synthetic-column pipeline for special page types such as artist-releasegroups, and the generic else-branch); previously MB-Name / Comment read the source cell first, so mutating extractors like primaryAlias had not yet removed the <i title="Primary alias"> element, causing the Comment column to incorrectly contain both the primary alias text and the disambiguation comment; with the new order, any mutating extractor cleans the source cell DOM before Comment is read, so Comment contains only the actual disambiguation text

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: primaryAlias column extractor — new ColumnDataExtractor entry: extracts the MusicBrainz <i title="Primary alias"> element from a source Title/Event cell into a dedicated synthetic 'Primary Alias' column; the <i> element is removed from the source cell (not cloned), and any orphaned leading ", " text node left in the parent <bdi> is stripped so that a residual disambiguation comment stands alone; if the containing <span class="comment"> is left empty after removal it is also discarded; registered for Artist-Events ('Event' column) and Artist-release-groups ('Title' column) page types

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: new setting sa_sidebar_collapsed (default: false) — when enabled together with sa_collabsable_sidebar, the sidebar is automatically collapsed as soon as the consolidated view is first rendered, freeing up horizontal space without requiring a manual click on the toggle handle; the initial collapse is applied directly (no animation) by adding sidebar-collapsed / handle-collapsed CSS classes and calling applyStretching(true) immediately after the handle is appended to the DOM; a follow-up requestAnimationFrame repositions the handle at the viewport right edge

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: cancelledEvent column extractor — new ColumnDataExtractor entry for the 'events' page type: extracts the MusicBrainz cancellation indicator (cancelled) from the Event cell into a dedicated synthetic 'Cancelled' column; unlike the caa and video extractors the source span is cloned (not moved) so the Event column retains the cancellation label alongside the event title; an invisible sort-key is always appended containing 'yes' for cancelled events and 'no' otherwise, making the column fully sortable and filterable via the unique-values dropdown

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: unique-values dropdown — new synthetic '○ empty cells' entry at the top of the Cell structure section: for collapsable columns (renderMultiRowCell and splitCountryDate columns such as Country, Date, Label, Catalog#), a new entry is prepended as the very first item in synBox showing how many visible rows have a completely blank cell (no ul>li items and no text content — e.g. a release-group row whose Country/Date column has no release events); counting reuses the already-computed getCleanColumnText result from the valueCounts pass (zero extra DOM work per row — just an additional else branch on the existing lis.length chain); selecting '○ empty cells' applies an 'empty' multiRowMode filter; testRowMatch() handles 'empty' by testing hasEmpty = lis.length === 0 (no list structure at all) and matching rows where that is true; applyMultiRowStateFilter updated with the 'empty' label '○ empty cells' and docstring updated to list all five modes (empty / single / collapsed / expanded / any); synItemCount height estimation gains a +(emptyCellCount > 0 ? 1 : 0) slot; the synBox section header was renamed from 'Multi-row cell state' to 'Cell structure' since the section now covers the full cell lifecycle (empty → single-row → multi-row collapsed/expanded)

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: applyRenderMultiRowCells — single-row cells not wrapped in <ul><li>: the function skipped wrapping when a cell contained only one logical row (nonEmptyGroups.length < 2 guard caused a continue), leaving those cells as raw DOM nodes rather than <ul><li> containers; this broke (1) the collapsableColumns feature for renderMultiRowCell columns such as Label and Catalog# because initCollapsableColumns and testRowMatch both inspect ul > li count — lis.length === 1 signals a single-row cell but lis.length === 0 (un-wrapped) is invisible to both; (2) the '• single-row cells' synthetic filter entry in the unique-values dropdown never matched Label/Catalog# cells with exactly one entry; (3) the statistics panel counted them as neither single-row nor multi-row; fixed by changing the guard to nonEmptyGroups.length < 1 so only truly empty cells are skipped — all non-empty cells, including single-entry ones, are now always wrapped in <ul><li>, matching the long-established behaviour of splitCountryDate which has always wrapped even single-event cells; initCollapsableColumns correctly adds a ▶/◀ toggle only for lis.length ≥ 2 so single-<li> cells remain un-toggled; docstring updated to document the new wrapping contract

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: unique-values dropdown / multi-table mode — expanded cells never counted as expanded and always shown as collapsed: data-mb-row-idx was never assigned to rows in multi-table mode (both artist-releasegroups and generic multi push paths), so expandedCells Map keys were always null and every cell with ul>li count ≥ 2 fell into the collapsed bucket regardless of actual user expand actions; fixed by introducing a module-level _mbRowIdxCounter that is reset to 0 at the start of every fetch and disk-load, and assigning data-mb-row-idx = String(_mbRowIdxCounter++) to every row in all four push sites (artist-releasegroups live-fetch, generic multi/single live-fetch, multi-table disk-load, single-table disk-load) so that expandedCells, testRowMatch, initCollapsableColumns and openUniqDrop all share the same stable row identity across renderFinalTable and initCollapsableColumns reset cycles in both single-table and multi-table modes

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: unique-values dropdown — new synthetic '• single-row cells' entry: for collapsable columns, a new entry is prepended as the very first item in synBox showing how many visible rows have exactly one ul>li item (cells with a list structure but only one entry that therefore have no ▶/◀ toggle); selecting it applies a 'single' multiRowMode filter so only those rows remain visible; synItemCount height estimation updated to include the extra slot

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: unique-values dropdown / multi-row state filter — stale counts and wrong h2 total after clear+reselect: the dropdown counted toggle state by reading .mb-cell-collapse-toggle textContent (▶ or ◀) from the DOM, but initCollapsableColumns resets every toggle to ▶ after renderFinalTable clones rows; after the first filter+clear cycle all DOM toggles showed ▶ so multiRowExpandedCount was always 0 and multiRowCollapsedCount was inflated; selection of the 'expanded' entry matched no rows; subsequently selecting 'collapsed' matched only the pre-expansion count because testRowMatch also read toggle.textContent from clones (which likewise showed ▶); fixed by introducing an expandedCells Map (keyed 'rowIdx:colIdx') as the authoritative source of truth: the ensureCollapseDelegate toggle click handler now writes into / deletes from expandedCells; openUniqDrop counts from ul>li count + expandedCells instead of toggle.textContent; testRowMatch uses expandedCells instead of toggle.textContent; initCollapsableColumns reads expandedCells to restore the correct ▶/◀ state and hidden/visible li items after every re-render

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: multi-row state filter — rows not filtered after selecting a synthetic dropdown entry: applyMultiRowStateFilter() set input.dataset.mbMultirowMode and then dispatched a synthetic 'input' event to trigger runFilter(); however the column filter's own 'input' listener unconditionally deletes dataset.mbMultirowMode at its very first line (the guard intended to clear the mode when the user types manually), so by the time getColFilters() ran the mode was already gone and the display label string matched no row text — resulting in zero visible rows; fixed by replacing the input event dispatch with a direct runFilter() call, which bypasses the input handler entirely and leaves dataset.mbMultirowMode intact for getColFilters() to read

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: global collapse button — h2 section collapse triggered on click: the button uses innerHTML with nested <span> children; clicking on the text made e.target a <span>, which bypassed the 'BUTTON' tag guard in the h2 click handler and caused the entire h2 section to collapse or expand (including on Ctrl+click which triggered the all-h2 toggle); fixed by adding e.stopPropagation() at the top of the global button's onclick in both wiring locations (initCollapsableColumns single-table branch and rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti) so click events never reach the h2 handler

unique-values dropdown — synthetic multi-row state items not visible: synthetic entries were appended to listBox before renderItems('') was called; renderItems always starts with listBox.innerHTML = '' which wiped all synthetic items before the panel became visible; fixed by separating synthetic items into their own <div class='mb-col-uniq-syn-box'> that is inserted into drop between qfBar and listBox — renderItems only touches listBox so synBox content is never destroyed; allItems() now queries from drop (not listBox) so keyboard ArrowDown/Up and Enter correctly navigate synthetic entries; the Enter handler for keyboard selection now checks classList for 'mb-col-uniq-multirow-item' and delegates to focused.click() (which fires applyMultiRowStateFilter) instead of applyUniqVal; moveFocus scroll calculation updated to use el.offsetTop relative to drop rather than listBox+el.offsetTop since items can now live in either synBox or listBox

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: syncCollapseButtonsWithColumnVisibility — sub-table collapse buttons not restored in multi mode: when a hidden collapsable column was re-shown via the Visible button, the global collapse button correctly reappeared (driven by rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti) but each per-sub-table collapse button stayed hidden; fixed by adding a second forEach loop in syncCollapseButtonsWithColumnVisibility that calls updateSubTableCollapseButton(t) for every table.tbl after initCollapsableColumns and rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti have run

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML — label fix: renamed 'multi-rows' to 'multi-row' in the two-row button label

unique-values dropdown — synthetic multi-row state filter entries: for columns declared in activeDefinition.features.collapsableColumns, the 📊 unique-values dropdown now shows up to three pinned synthetic entries above the regular value list: '▶ collapsed multi-row cells' (count of visible rows with a collapsed ▶ toggle), '◀ expanded multi-row cells' (count with an expanded ◀ toggle), and '▶◀ any multi-row cells' (sum; shown only when both states are present); the synthetic items are visually distinguished by a light-blue italic background and a 'Multi-row cell state' section header, and are separated from the regular values by a thin divider; clicking a synthetic entry calls the new applyMultiRowStateFilter(mode, table, colIndex) helper which tags the column filter input with data-mb-multirow-mode and stores a human-readable display label; getColFilters() detects this attribute and emits a special { isMultiRowFilter, multiRowMode } descriptor instead of a text filter; testRowMatch() handles that descriptor by querying .mb-cell-collapse-toggle.textContent on the cell rather than matching text — mode 'collapsed' keeps rows whose toggle shows ▶, 'expanded' keeps rows whose toggle shows ◀, 'any' keeps any row that has a toggle; highlightText() is skipped for multi-row state filters since there is no text to highlight; the mode is auto-cleared when the user edits the filter field manually (input event) or clicks the ✕ clear button; multi-row badge height is factored into the panel height estimate used for flip-upward positioning

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: global collapse button — two-row label: the button text 'Expand columns' / 'Collapse columns' is now rendered as a two-row flex label — '▶ Expand' (or '◀ Collapse') on the left, and a stacked 'multi-rows' / 'cells' column on the right; the outer button uses display:inline-flex with align-items:center and gap:4px; state detection (startsWith('▶') on textContent) remains unchanged because textContent concatenates all descendant text correctly; implemented via the new makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML(expand) helper which returns the HTML string used for btn.innerHTML assignments throughout the collapse button machinery

sub-table collapse button — two-row label: the per-sub-table '▶ Expand' / '◀ Collapse' toggle buttons in multi-table mode now use the same two-row flex label as the global button via makeCollapseExpandBtnHTML(); createSubTableCollapseButton and updateSubTableCollapseButton updated to assign innerHTML instead of textContent and to set display:inline-flex when showing the button

sub-table collapse button — margin-left: added margin-left:8px to the sub-table collapse button style so it no longer overlaps the Ex label (mb-stf-*-ex-label) of the sub-table filter row, which was especially visible when the button was tinted during highlight mode

column visibility → collapse button sync (Feature 4): when a multi-row cell column is hidden via the 'Visible' button, the new syncCollapseButtonsWithColumnVisibility() helper (called at the end of toggleColumn()) now hides the global collapse button and — in multi-table mode — all per-sub-table collapse buttons as soon as ALL collapsable columns declared in activeDefinition.features.collapsableColumns are invisible; conversely, when a column is made visible again, initCollapsableColumns() is re-run for every table.tbl and rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti() is called in multi-table mode so the toggle infrastructure (cell toggles, column-header ▶/◀ buttons, global/sub-table buttons) is fully rebuilt for the newly visible column

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: splitCountryDate extractor — multi-row cell output: changed the Country and Date synthetic column cells from a flat comma-separated layout to a true <ul><li> multi-row structure; each release event now occupies its own <li> inside a <ul>, producing one visually distinct row per event instead of a single inline-concatenated string; single-event cells are also wrapped in <ul><li> for structural consistency with the collapsableColumns mechanism (initCollapsableColumns requires ul > li with ≥2 items to add a ▶/◀ toggle, so single-item cells are unaffected by the collapse feature); the i-parameter and the leading-comma guard (if (i > 0)) were removed from both the Country and Date loops since the separator is now structural rather than textual

renderMultiRowCell — new page-definition feature: added a new features.renderMultiRowCell: ['Label', 'Catalog', …] array to the page definition schema that instructs the row-collection pipeline to convert any named column's comma-separated cell content into a <ul><li> multi-row structure; implemented via three new components: (1) buildActiveRenderMultiRowCols(def) derives a descriptor list ({ columnName, colIdx: -1 }) from features.renderMultiRowCell, analogous to buildActiveColumnExtractors and buildActiveColumnErasers; (2) applyRenderMultiRowCells(row, descriptors) iterates the target <td>'s top-level child nodes, splits on text nodes matching /^\s*,\s*$/ (comma with optional whitespace), trims leading/trailing whitespace-only text nodes from each group, and replaces the cell content with a <ul> whose <li> children hold cloned copies of each group — cells with fewer than 2 logical rows are left unchanged; (3) the activeRenderMultiRowCols module-level runtime list is populated in startFetchingProcess and its colIdx fields are resolved during the header-scanning pass (alongside activeColumnExtractors and activeColumnErasers); applyRenderMultiRowCells is called in both the artist-releasegroups branch and the generic branch after applyColumnErasers and before the column-extractor pipeline so that transformed cell content is based on already-cleaned data and extractor colIdx references remain valid; the releasegroup-releases page definition already declares renderMultiRowCell: ['Label', 'Catalog'] making Label and Catalog cells render as stacked rows instead of comma-joined inline text

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: collapsableColumns in multi-table mode — per-sub-table ▶/◀ collapse button: each sub-table h3 bar now carries its own ▶ Expand / ◀ Collapse toggle button inserted immediately BEFORE the .mb-subtable-controls span; clicking it drives only the column-header toggle buttons (.mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn) that belong to that sub-table, toggling all multi-row columns in that sub-table between expanded and collapsed state — independent of every other sub-table; the button is hidden when the filtered-in rows of that sub-table contain no multi-row cells, and shown otherwise with its text always reset to ▶ Expand after a filter run so it reflects the post-filter collapsed state; implemented via two new helpers: createSubTableCollapseButton(table, categoryName) which creates the button element with a closure over the specific table reference, and updateSubTableCollapseButton(table) which locates the button via the h3 previousElementSibling and updates its display + tint; highlight tint mirrors the global button but uses stfBorderActive() (the sub-table filter input's active border colour, #008000 by default) as the background with white text and a matching box-shadow, applied whenever any td.mb-has-collapse-toggle inside that sub-table carries a filter-highlight span (visible or in collapsed items); the tint is cleared when no highlights exist; updateSubTableCollapseButton is called: (a) after initCollapsableColumns(table) in both the !query initial render and query filter-reuse paths, and (b) from the ensureCollapseDelegate click handler after each individual cell expand/collapse so the per-sub-table tint stays in sync with the cell state; both defensive 'missing controls' rebuild paths in renderGroupedTable also create the collapse button if it does not yet exist in the h3

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: collapsableColumns in multi-table mode: initCollapsableColumns() was never called for multi-table pages (tableMode: 'multi') — it was only invoked in the single-table branch of runFilter() and in the single-table initial render path; as a result, declaring collapsableColumns in a multi-table page definition (e.g. 'Country/Date' on release-group pages) had no visible effect; fixed by calling initCollapsableColumns(table) per sub-table inside renderGroupedTable() — once in the initial (!query) path immediately after makeTableSortableUnified() (so .mb-col-hdr-flex is already built), and once in the filter-reuse (query truthy) path after tints are re-applied; additionally, the global ▶/◀ toggle button wiring has been split out: initCollapsableColumns() now skips global button wiring when tableMode is 'multi' (each per-table call would otherwise overwrite onclick with only that table's buttons); instead, the new rewireGlobalCollapseButtonMulti() helper is called once at the end of the dataArray.forEach loop in renderGroupedTable() — it collects every .mb-col-collapse-hdr-btn across all table.tbl elements in the DOM, shows or hides the global button accordingly, and wires a single onclick that drives all column-header buttons across every sub-table; updateGlobalCollapseButtonHighlight() is updated to accept null as its table argument, in which case it queries the full document instead of a single table, enabling correct highlight-tint updates in multi-table mode; the ensureCollapseDelegate click handler is also updated to pass null instead of the single table in multi mode so the highlight tint re-scan covers all sub-tables after each individual cell expand/collapse

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: collapsableColumns — filter-aware toggle visibility (Feature 1): the column-header ▶/◀ icon for each collapsable column is now explicitly hidden when the current filter leaves no multi-row cells visible in that column, and shown again as soon as at least one multi-row cell is filtered back in; the global ▶ Expand columns button follows the same rule — hidden when no collapsable column has any multi-row cells in the filtered view, shown as soon as any column does; this already worked implicitly via the per-filter initCollapsableColumns() re-run, but the logic is now fully explicit and documented

collapsableColumns — hidden-match signalling on cell toggle icon (Feature 2): when a filter match falls inside the collapsed (hidden) list items of a multi-row cell, the right-aligned ▶ toggle icon now receives the CSS class mb-collapse-toggle-has-match which colours it with the user's Global Filter Highlight palette (background + text colour + matching box-shadow outline), giving the end user a clear visual cue that expanding the cell will reveal matching content; the highlight class is cleared automatically when the cell is expanded (all items visible) and re-applied if the cell is collapsed again while a match is still present in the newly-hidden rows; implemented in initCollapsableColumns() for the initial filter pass and in the ensureCollapseDelegate() click handler for subsequent individual cell expand/collapse operations

collapsableColumns — global button tint when any highlighted match exists (Feature 3): after every filter run or individual cell expand/collapse the new updateGlobalCollapseButtonHighlight() helper scans every td carrying a collapse toggle (both visible first-item content and hidden collapsed rows) for any filter-highlight span; if at least one is found the global ▶ Expand columns button's background colour, text colour, and box-shadow are set to the user's Global Filter Highlight colours, providing a top-level indicator that highlighted content exists somewhere in the multi-row cells; when no highlighted spans remain the inline overrides are cleared so the button reverts to its default appearance; the check covers all three highlight types (global, column, prefilter)

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: collapsableColumns global toggle button missing after Load from Disk: root cause was that initCollapsableColumns() was called BEFORE the subsequent runFilter() call in the disk-load path; runFilter() clones allRows and replaces tbody content, discarding all toggle modifications and leaving the DOM without toggle spans; additionally filterContainer is not in the DOM yet when initCollapsableColumns() ran there, so the global button was set to display:'' but inside an invisible container — the fix moves ownership of initCollapsableColumns() into runFilter()'s single-table branch (called after renderFinalTable + updateH2Count), so it always runs on the actual final DOM rows with filterContainer already in the DOM; this also fixes toggle state after every user-triggered filter/sort operation since initCollapsableColumns() is now idempotently called after every single-table re-render

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: collapsableColumns cell toggle — alignment: toggle span is now appended directly to the <td> (not inside the first <li>) and positioned with CSS position:absolute; right:4px; top:4px — it is always anchored to the top-right corner of the cell, never wraps to a new line, and stays right-aligned under any column width (auto-resize or manual drag); the <td> receives class mb-has-collapse-toggle which sets position:relative and padding-right:22px so cell content never slides behind the toggle

collapsableColumns cell toggle — sort/load toggling broken: replaced per-element addEventListener on each cell toggle with a single event-delegation listener on <tbody> (new ensureCollapseDelegate() helper, guarded by table.dataset.mbCollapseDelegate for idempotency); delegation survives sort re-renders (tbody element persists, only its child rows are reordered), disk-load re-renders (tbody.innerHTML='' removes rows but NOT the listener on tbody itself), and filter re-renders; delegation handler resolves <ul>/<li> siblings dynamically from the clicked cell at event time — no stale closure references

collapsableColumns cell toggle — sort/load toggling broken: column-header and global toggle buttons now drive cell toggles via toggle.click() which is caught by the delegation listener, ensuring consistent state management through any re-render

collapsableColumns column header/global toggle — state: removed hidden _expanded properties; all toggle state is now read directly from element textContent (▶ = collapsed, ◀ = expanded) making state always consistent with visible DOM after sort, disk-load, or individual cell clicks

collapsableColumns column min-width: after processing each column, measures the maximum scrollWidth of the first <li> element (with white-space:nowrap temporarily applied for accurate unwrapped measurement) across all multi-row cells and sets th.style.minWidth to that value + 28px toggle clearance, preventing the first item from wrapping and visually pushing the toggle below the cell content

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: collapsableColumns cell toggle: toggle span is now appended to the end of the first <li> element (inline, after the item text) instead of being appended to the <td> itself — previously it appeared on a new line below the entire <ul>

collapsableColumns column-header button: converted from one-directional 'expand-only' action to a true stateful toggle — ▶ (collapsed state) click drives ALL cell toggles in the column to ◀ (expanded); ◀ (expanded state) click drives ALL cell toggles back to ▶ (collapsed); header icon and aria-expanded update accordingly after each click

collapsableColumns global button: converted from one-directional 'expand-only' action to a true stateful toggle — ▶ Expand columns click drives all column-header buttons to expanded state (◀) and updates label to '◀ Collapse columns'; subsequent click drives all back to collapsed state (▶) and restores label '▶ Expand columns'; each column-header button is only clicked when it is not already in the target state, ensuring columns reach a uniform consistent state regardless of their individual prior states; global toggle state is reset to collapsed on every (re-)init

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: collapsableColumns: new feature — page definitions may now declare `features.collapsableColumns: [...]` to enable per-column collapsing of multi-row cells; `expandShowAllCells` is guaranteed to run before the collapse pass so all cell data is available

collapsableColumns cell toggle: body cells with ≥2 <li> items are initially collapsed (only first item shown) with a ▶ icon appended; clicking ▶ expands all items and changes icon to ◀; clicking ◀ re-collapses — fully toggleable at cell level

collapsableColumns column-header button: when at least one body cell in a collapsable column is multi-row, a ▶ span is inserted into .mb-col-hdr-flex immediately before the 📊 unique-values button (with margin-left:auto transferred so both remain right-aligned); clicking it emulates a click on every still-collapsed (▶) cell toggle in that column, expanding them all

collapsableColumns global button: a '▶ Collapse columns' button (id=mb-col-collapse-all-btn) is added to the filter bar before the 🎨 Toggle-highlighting button; clicking it emulates a click on every column-header ▶, which in turn expands all remaining collapsed cells in all collapsable columns; button is hidden when no collapsable columns are active

initCollapsableColumns(): new function — idempotent (safe for disk-load re-runs), removes previously attached toggles and resets <li> visibility before re-applying; called after makeTableSortableUnified in both the main fetch path and the disk-load path

headerText strip patterns updated: ▶ and ◀ are now stripped from header textContent in all replace(/[⇅▲▼📊]/g) patterns throughout the codebase (CSV/JSON/Org-mode export, column-visibility toggle, column-filter label, cleanupHeaders synthetic-column detection, makeTableSortableUnified colName + getCleanColName helper)

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: artist-releasegroups pageDefinition: added 'jesus2099' to columnErasers so the jesus2099 cover-art thumbnail anchor (<a><span class="caa-icon jesus2099userjs154481">) is removed during the erasure pass — before the 'caa' columnExtractor runs — matching the documented erasers-before-extractors contract

caa extractor: added Path B fallback for bare artwork spans (not wrapped in an <a>) — after the jesus2099 eraser removes the thumbnail anchor on artist-releasegroups pages, the native MusicBrainz indicator (<span class="artwork-icon caa-icon">) is a direct child of <td> with no parent anchor; Path A's 'a > span.caa-icon' selector cannot reach it, so Path B detects it with 'span.caa-icon, span.eaa-icon, span.artwork-icon', moves it into the synthetic CAA cell, and removes it from the source cell

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: caa extractor: always remove <span class="blank-icon"> from the source cell (if present) before processing — MusicBrainz injects this whitespace-only placeholder for rows without cover art, which caused a spurious visible gap in the synthetic CAA column

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: ColumnDataExtractor: added new 'caa' extractor that moves the Cover Art Archive (caa-icon) or Event Art Archive (eaa-icon) artwork anchor out of the source cell into a dedicated synthetic 'CAA' column

caa extractor: detection uses the compound selector 'a > span.caa-icon, a > span.eaa-icon, a > span.artwork-icon' — covers release/event pages where both artwork-icon and caa-icon/eaa-icon are present, AND artist release-groups pages where only caa-icon is present without artwork-icon
caa extractor: anchor is moved (not cloned) so neither the source column nor the CAA column contain duplicate markup
caa extractor: appends an invisible mb-caa-sort-key span ('yes'/'no') enabling sort and unique-value dropdown filtering identical to the existing 'video' extractor pattern

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: pageDefinitions artist-releases: fixed 'caa' extractor sourceColumn mismatch — was 'Title', corrected to 'Release' (matching extractMainColumn and the actual column header on /artist/{mbid}/releases pages)
pageDefinitions releasegroup-releases: same fix — sourceColumn 'Title' → 'Release' (matching extractMainColumn and the actual column header on /release-group/{mbid} pages)

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: columnErasers: added 'jesus2099' sentinel eraser type that removes the full <a href="…/cover-art"> anchor wrapping a <span class="caa-icon jesus2099userjs154481"> — covers both the background-image variant and the plain (no-style) variant injected by the jesus2099 'mb. SUPER MIND CONTROL' userscript

applyColumnErasers: erasers per descriptor are now partitioned into textErasers (glyph symbols, existing logic) and eraseJesus2099 flag (new anchor-removal strategy) so both strategies can be active simultaneously on the same column
applyColumnErasers: detection is class-based (span.caa-icon.jesus2099userjs154481) and independent of href value, background-image URL or any style attribute
buildActiveColumnErasers / applyColumnErasers: JSDoc updated to document both erasure strategies with the full supported symbol table

pageDefinitions: artist-recordings and recording-releases pages now carry columnErasers with the 'jesus2099' sentinel to strip cover-art icons from 'Release groups' and 'Release title' columns respectively

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: Disk-load action button colouring: replaced single-strategy label comparison with a robust three-strategy cascade
Strategy 1: slugify(data.buttonLabel) vs slugify(btn.dataset.label) — immune to emoji, superscript digits, case and decoration (works for files saved with v9.99.49+)

Strategy 2: extract the detail slug from the filename (MB-<pageType>-<detail>-<rows>-<ts>.json.gz) and compare against slugify(btn.dataset.label) — covers all files saved before buttonLabel was added to the payload
Strategy 3 (fallback): colour all action buttons green when neither slug strategy finds a match
detailSlugFromFilename() helper: splits the filename after the pageType prefix, collects tokens until the first all-digit (row-count) token, rejoins with underscores to reconstruct the detail slug

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: Disk-load action button colouring: fix all buttons turning green instead of only the matching one — root cause was that row-count superscripts (e.g. "official_rgs⁵⁷⁹") are appended to button textContent after a live fetch, causing the textContent comparison to fail and the fallback (colour all) to trigger

Action buttons now carry a data-label attribute (stamped at creation time from conf.label) that is immune to textContent mutations; disk-load colouring compares against data-label with a textContent fallback for forward-compatibility

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix
items: Disk-load: synthetic column headers (MB-Name, Comment, Country, Date, etc.) now restore with the original dark background — header cell style attribute is serialized and re-applied on load

Disk-load: the action button matching the loaded data type is now coloured green (#ccffcc) after a successful disk load; falls back to colouring all action buttons when no button-label was stored (old files)
Filename slug: strip leading (and trailing) underscores from the detail segment so emoji-prefixed button labels yield e.g. "official_rgs" instead of "_official_rgs"

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: Navigation guard: a document-level click listener (capture phase, idempotent) intercepts anchor clicks inside rendered table.tbl rows while data is loaded and shows a confirm() dialog before same-tab navigation — prevents silent loss of loaded data, filters, sorts, column widths and other session state
Navigation guard exemptions: fragment-only links, javascript: pseudo-links, target=_blank links, same-page URL variants, and clicks outside table.tbl are all passed through without prompting

sections.label: 🐛 Bugfix / Debug
items: columnErasers: added Lib.debug/Lib.warn statements throughout the erasing pipeline
applyColumnErasers: Lib.warn when a sourceColumn is not found in table headers (colIdx=-1) or cell index is out of range
applyColumnErasers: Lib.debug per removed span (symbol + column name + colIdx); Lib.debug when no matching spans are found in a cell
indices summary line: extended with eraserSummary showing configured symbols, sourceColumn and resolved colIdx per eraser descriptor
button-click handler: Lib.debug lists all configured columnErasers at activation time

sections.label: 🚀 Features
items: columnErasers: new per-page feature that strips specific marker spans (▶, ➕) from configured source columns before row rendering
buildActiveColumnErasers() builds the runtime eraser descriptor list from pageDefinition features
applyColumnErasers() removes matching spans by text-content detection (style-attribute-independent)
Eraser column indices are resolved per-page in the header-scanning pass and reset between pages
Applied in both the artist-releasegroups branch and the generic row-processing branch, before column extractors run
Active for: series-releases (▶, ➕ on Release), label-releases (▶ on Release), artist-recordings (▶, ➕ on Release groups), releasegroup-releases (▶ on Release)

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Video column — sortable and filterable via invisible sort-key text: the `video` ColumnDataExtractor now appends a `<span class="mb-video-sort-key" aria-hidden="true" style="display:none">` to every synthetic `Video` cell. The span contains the literal text `"video"` when the `<span class="video">` indicator was present, or `"audio"` otherwise. The span is never rendered by the browser, but both `getCleanColumnText()` and `getCleanVisibleText()` use a `TreeWalker` that does NOT skip `display:none` nodes, so all three consumers that depend on them pick up the text transparently: (1) **Sorting** — `createMultiColumnComparator()` calls `getCleanVisibleText(cell)` and now receives `"audio"` or `"video"`, making ascending/descending sort place audio recordings before video recordings in the expected alphabetical order. (2) **Unique-values dropdown** — `openUniqDrop()` calls `getCleanColumnText(cell)` and now produces exactly two entries (`"audio"` and `"video"`) with correct occurrence counts. (3) **Filter matching** — `applyUniqVal()` writes the raw value (`"video"` or `"audio"`) into the column filter input; the filter loop calls `getCleanColumnText(cell)` and the string matches correctly, so clicking `"video"` shows only rows with the icon and clicking `"audio"` shows only rows without it. No changes were required to any of these consumers — only the `video` extractor was modified.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: New `video` ColumnDataExtractor for `artist-recordings` and `place-performances` page types: the `<span class="video" title="This recording is a video">` icon is now *moved* (not copied) out of the `Name` / `Title` source column and placed into a dedicated synthetic `Video` column. Removal is done via `videoSpan.remove()` on the live source `<td>` before the node is re-inserted into the new synthetic cell, so no duplication occurs and the title column stays uncluttered. Rows without the video indicator receive an empty placeholder `<td>` to keep the table structure consistent. The extractor is wired into `pageDefinitions` via a canonical `columnExtractors` descriptor: `{ sourceColumn: 'Name', extractor: 'video', syntheticColumns: ['Video'] }` for `artist-recordings` (single-table, paginated) and `{ sourceColumn: 'Title', extractor: 'video', syntheticColumns: ['Video'] }` for `place-performances` (multi-table, non-paginated). No changes were required to the extraction pipeline — only the new registry entry in `ColumnDataExtractor` and the two updated `pageDefinitions` entries.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: New config section '💾 LOAD AND SAVE DATA TO/FROM DISK' in the settings dialog: `sa_load_history_limit` relocated from ⚡ PERFORMANCE SETTINGS; `sa_ui_save_btn_style`, `sa_ui_load_btn_style`, and `sa_ui_download_notification_font_size` relocated from 🖌️ ELEMENT UI STYLES. Three new settings added: `sa_ld_dialog_width` (initial pixel width of the Load dialog, default 600, range 300–1400), `sa_ld_dialog_header_font_size` (font size of the description paragraph in the Load dialog header, default '1.00em'), and `sa_ld_status_font_size` (shared font size for the load-, filter-, and render-status containers, default '0.95em'). All values are read when the dialog opens.

'📂 Load Table Data' dialog is now draggable and resizable: the header area acts as a drag handle (`cursor:move; user-select:none`) — dragging it moves the dialog freely within the viewport. CSS `resize:both` enables corner/edge resizing with `min-width:320px` and `min-height:120px` guards. Drag listeners are de-registered in `closeDialog()` to avoid leaks.

Filter-prefix mouse-selection guard: new `attachFilterPrefixSelectionGuard(input)` replaces the previous broken `mouseup`-based approach. The previous approach failed because mouse-drag selections are updated continuously on mousemove and the mouseup event (even with a deferred microtask) only fires after the drag is already complete. The new implementation listens to `document.selectionchange` — an event that fires synchronously for every selection change including during live drags — and clamps `selectionStart` to at least `prefix.length` the instant it drifts into the prefix region, while leaving `selectionEnd` untouched. A re-entrancy flag (`_clamping`) prevents the programmatic `setSelectionRange()` call inside the handler from retriggering selectionchange in browsers that fire it for programmatic changes. The guard is registered on `focus` and unregistered on `blur` for both the permanent global filter input and every per-column filter input created by `addColumnFilterRow()`.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Persistent column visibility per pageType: the "👁️ Visible" column-visibility dialog now saves and restores the checked/unchecked state of every column across page loads. State is stored in GM storage under the key `vz-mb-colvis-{pageType}` as a JSON object mapping column name → boolean (e.g. `{"Title": true, "Length": false}`). Column names are used as keys instead of indices so the stored state is robust against pages where injected or optional columns shift column indices between sessions. Save is triggered on every path that closes the menu: (1) clicking the 'Visible' button again to toggle it closed, (2) clicking outside the menu, (3) pressing Escape, (4) pressing Enter on a checkbox row, and (5) clicking the 'Choose current configuration' button (which previously only closed the menu without saving). Restore runs immediately after all checkboxes are built at init time — `toggleColumn()` is called for each checkbox whose stored value differs from the default (all-visible), and `updateButtonColor()` is called afterwards so the button tint correctly reflects the restored state before the menu is ever opened. The close-hint text at the bottom of the menu has been updated to read 'Click outside or press Escape to close and save' to make the save-on-close behaviour discoverable.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Sidebar toggle handle not shown after 'Load from Disk': `initSidebarCollapse()` was only called at the end of the live fetch/render path (the “🧮 Show all” button flow) and was never invoked when data was restored from a saved file via the “📂 Load” button. Fixed by adding the same `if (Lib.settings.sa_collabsable_sidebar) { initSidebarCollapse(); }` call to the Load-from-Disk post-render block, immediately after `addExportButton()`, matching the structure of the fetch path. An idempotency guard (`if (document.getElementById('sidebar-toggle-handle')) return`) was also added at the top of `initSidebarCollapse()` so that calling it a second time (e.g. the user loads a file on a page where the sidebar was already initialized by a prior fetch) is a safe no-op that simply logs a debug message and returns.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: SHOW_ALL_JSON_HANDLERS: add `"iswcs"` handler for the Artist-Works "ISWC" column — pages such as `https://musicbrainz.org/artist//works` can have a `` whose `

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items:
Auto-expand "(show N more)" cells before row extraction — new configurable feature (`sa_expand_show_all_cells`, default: enabled, under 🔬 EXPERIMENTAL FEATURES): on page types such as `Place-Events` and `Artist-Works`, MusicBrainz truncates certain columns and hides remaining entries behind a `
  • (show N more)
    • ` toggle. The complete data for all entries is always embedded in the static server-rendered HTML inside a `

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items:
recording-releases — skip action button rendering when the page has no releases: on recording pages (e.g. `/recording/`) where the `

Appears on releases

` heading is immediately followed by a `

No releases found which feature this recording.

` paragraph, there is nothing to fetch or display. The script now detects this condition early — right after `pageType` and `headerContainer` are resolved and before any UI elements are created — and exits cleanly without rendering the `🧮 Show all Releases for Recording` button or any other action buttons. The detection walks forward siblings of the `h2.appears-on-releases` element (skipping blank text nodes) and checks whether the first element sibling is a `

` whose text starts with "no releases found" (case-insensitive). When matched, a descriptive message is written to the browser console via `console.log` (always) and `Lib.debug` (when debug logging is enabled), then the initializer returns immediately.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize (↔️ Resize) — Event column still wrapping after v9.99.37 fix: v9.99.37 correctly eliminated the browser table-fixup problem (orphaned `<td>` causing anonymous table-row boxes with `white-space:normal`), but a second independent wrapping mechanism remained: MusicBrainz's own stylesheet sets `overflow-wrap:anywhere` directly on every `.wrap-anywhere` anchor element. CSS inheritance only fills property values that are not explicitly set — it does NOT override explicit stylesheet rules. Setting `overflow-wrap:normal` on the parent `measureDiv` therefore had no effect on descendant `<a class="wrap-anywhere">` nodes, which retained `overflow-wrap:anywhere` and continued to break text at arbitrary code points inside the measurement div, causing `offsetWidth` to reflect only the width of the wrapped first line rather than the full single-line content width. Fixed by traversing all cloned descendant elements with `measureDiv.querySelectorAll('*')` after they are appended and explicitly setting `el.style.whiteSpace = 'nowrap'`, `el.style.overflowWrap = 'normal'`, and `el.style.wordBreak = 'normal'` on each one. Inline styles always win over stylesheet rules regardless of specificity, guaranteeing that no descendant element can cause a line break during measurement. The same querySelectorAll pass is applied in both the header and data-cell measurement loops.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Auto-resize (↔️ Resize) — Event column cells containing a blank-icon/artwork-icon span not measured to their full single-line width: the column-width measurement loop cloned each `<td>` / `<th>` and appended it directly to a hidden `position:absolute` measurement `<div>`. Orphaned `<td>` / `<th>` elements appended outside a `<table>` trigger the browser's CSS table-fixup algorithm, which silently wraps them in anonymous table / table-row boxes. Those anonymous boxes carry the UA-stylesheet default of `white-space:normal`, overriding the `white-space:nowrap` that was set on the measurement div. As a result, content inside the cloned cell wrapped during measurement, and `offsetWidth` reflected only the widest non-breaking token (e.g. the fixed-pixel width of the `artwork-icon eaa-icon` span) rather than the full single-line width of icon + main-link text + comment. This made the Event column too narrow for cells that contained a long venue name followed by a `<span class="comment">` (e.g. `2025-10-18: David Geffen Theater … (5th Annual Academy Museum Gala)`) — the comment text wrapped to the next line. Fixed by cloning the element's *children* directly into the measurement div (via `Array.from(cell.childNodes).forEach(n => measureDiv.appendChild(n.cloneNode(true)))`) rather than the `<td>` / `<th>` itself, eliminating the table-fixup entirely. Additionally added `word-break:normal` and `overflow-wrap:normal` to the measurement div's `cssText` so that the `.wrap-anywhere` class (`overflow-wrap:anywhere`) present on event-link anchors cannot cause mid-word breaks even in the non-table measurement context.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — horizontal scroll position lost after selecting a value: clicking an item in the "Show unique column values" dropdown (📊 button in a column header) triggered `applyUniqVal()` which dispatched a synthetic `input` event and caused `runFilter()` to re-render the table. `runFilter()` saved and restored the vertical scroll position (`window.scrollY`) to prevent the page jumping, but the `window.scrollTo()` call at the end of the function hardcoded `0` as the horizontal argument — `window.scrollTo(0, __scrollY)` — unconditionally resetting horizontal scroll to the left edge on every filter run. When the table had been auto-resized via the ↔️ Resize button and the user had scrolled right to a column not visible in the default viewport (e.g. the "Comment" column), selecting any unique value from the dropdown snapped the viewport back to the leftmost position. Fixed by also capturing `window.scrollX` at the start of `runFilter()` (alongside the existing `__scrollY`) and restoring both axes: `window.scrollTo(__scrollX, __scrollY)`.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: event-aliases (Bug 1) — controls container inserted before disambiguation span: on entity alias pages (and any other page where the h1's `headerContainer` resolves to the inner `<a>` element rather than the `<h1>` itself), the controls `<div>` was inserted via `headerContainer.after(controlsContainer)` — immediately after the `<a>` link, pushing any pre-existing h1 siblings that followed the `<a>` (the date text node and a plain `<span class="comment">` disambiguation such as "(Bleachers)") to appear after the action buttons. `relocateH1CommentSpan()` then skipped the plain span (no `<i title>` inside) and left it stranded after the controls. Fixed by resolving the owning `<h1>` via `headerContainer.closest('h1')` and calling `h1El.appendChild(controlsContainer)` instead, which appends the controls at the end of the h1 so that any pre-existing text and comment siblings remain in their natural position before the buttons. A safe fallback to the previous `after()` behaviour is retained when no parent `<h1>` is found.

All *-aliases pages (Bug 2) — Locale column incorrectly split into MB-Name / Comment: `extractMainColumn: 'Locale'` was set on all 11 aliases page definitions (`instrument-aliases`, `area-aliases`, `place-aliases`, `series-aliases`, `label-aliases`, `work-aliases`, `artist-aliases`, `releasegroup-aliases`, `release-aliases`, `recording-aliases`, `event-aliases`). The MB-Name/Comment extraction pipeline then treated the Locale cell (e.g. `English United States`) as the main entity column — since the cell contains no `` link, the fallback extracted the first text node as MB-Name and the `primary` role indicator as Comment, producing wrong synthetic columns: MB-Name = "English United States", Comment = "primary". The Locale column on alias tables is purely descriptive plain text; it never contains entity links or entity disambiguation comments. Fixed by removing `extractMainColumn: 'Locale'` (and the surrounding `features` block where it was the sole entry) from all 11 aliases page definitions. The commented-out lines and an explanatory note are left in place for future reference.

sections.label: 📝 Documentation
items: HELP file updated to reflect the current state of the userscript (v9.99.33) and VZ_MBLibrary (v3.14.3). New and expanded sections: Unique-Values Column Dropdown (📊 button, quickfilter bar, count badges, keyboard navigation, Escape behaviour); Load-from-Disk three-phase dialog (Load → Filter → Render with Alt+L / Alt+F / Alt+R shortcuts); Filter Input States & Border Colors (idle / active / error states, ⋮ resizable handle for global and sub-table inputs); Cross-tag filter highlighting note added to the Filtering section; Synthetic Columns table expanded to include Place/Area/Country (from Location), MB-Area/Country (from Area), Format Types, and MB-Name/Comment; H1 Alias Line section describing the relocateH1CommentSpan() behaviour on Release Group and Event pages; Load Dialog Keyboard Shortcuts section; Settings section expanded with all new setting groups and entries (filter border colors, unique-values badge colors, sort progress threshold, render warning threshold, download notification font size, sub-table filter border colors, overflow tables in new tab); Changelog Dialog section updated to document live search with highlighting and ✕ clear button; Library Settings section added (🔧 Library Settings GM menu item, font size/family options); Multi-table Pages section updated with sub-table filter error reporting and overflow-tab setting; SAVE & LOAD section updated with informative filename format.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load-from-Disk dialog — history dropdown regression fixed: the 3-phase dialog rewrite introduced a regression where the history toggle button (▼) was invisible for the entire dialog session whenever the GM history store was empty at open-time. Root cause: the toggle's `display` was baked into the static template HTML (`display:${history.length > 0 ? 'inline-block' : 'none'}`) and never updated afterwards. As a consequence, the very first time a user typed a filter expression and clicked “Filter Data”, that expression was correctly saved to GM storage — but the toggle stayed hidden and the dropdown was never refreshed, making the feature appear entirely absent. Fixed by (1) extracting a `refreshHistoryDropdown()` local helper that rebuilds the dropdown's `innerHTML`, re-attaches item click-handlers, and syncs the toggle's `display` to `history.length`; (2) calling it once at dialog-setup time (superseding the static `historyItemsHTML` in the template); and (3) calling it again inside `filterBtn.onclick` immediately after `history = updated` so the toggle and item list stay in sync within the same dialog session. Additionally, `el.textContent.trim()` is now used when populating the filter input from a history item to guard against any leading/trailing whitespace in the rendered item text.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: artist-releasegroups — MB-Name / Comment extraction was silently skipped: the `artist-releasegroups` pageType uses a dedicated fetch branch (iterating `doc.querySelectorAll('table.tbl')`) that was completely separate from the generic `else`-branch where the MB-Name / Comment extraction pipeline lives. Consequently, setting `features: { extractMainColumn: 'Title' }` on the page definition caused the synthetic column *headers* to be injected correctly (that path reads `activeDefinition.features` before the branch split) but the synthetic column *cells* were never populated — the rows were cloned with `document.importNode` and pushed without appending `tdName` / `tdComment`. A second, compounding bug was an explicit guard `if (pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups')` in the else-branch that also suppressed appending those cells. Both issues are now fixed: (1) The `artist-releasegroups` branch received a full copy of the shared synthetic-column pipeline — MB-Name / Comment extraction (steps 1–2), active column extractor evaluation (step 2), excluded-column deletion (step 3), synthetic-cell appending (step 4), and conditional tdName / tdComment appending (step 5). (2) The stale `pageType !== 'artist-releasegroups'` guard in the else-branch was replaced by `if (mainColIdx !== -1)`, making the guard data-driven (does `extractMainColumn` resolve to a valid index?) rather than hardcoded to a single page type.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: H1 comment-span relocation (complex case) — the `relocateH1CommentSpan()` IIFE now handles the more complex structure found on event pages where the `` inside `` contains both an `ALIAS` element *and* trailing sibling text (extra show title) after the closing ``: e.g. `Southside Johnny & The Asbury Jukes at The Stone Pony, Southside Johnny & The Asbury Jukes Stone Pony 25th Anniversary Show`. Previously the entire span was removed and only ALIAS was surfaced, silently discarding the extra title. Now: (1) ALIAS is relocated to the alias line below h1 as before. (2) The extra sibling text (all text/element nodes following `` within the ``, or the `commentSpan` when no `` wrapper is present) is collected, its leading `", "` stripped, and re-injected into the h1 as a new `(EXTRA)` so it remains visible on the title line. When the extra text is blank after trimming the span is removed entirely (simple-case behaviour unchanged). Additionally, the early-return guard was tightened: spans without an `` element (plain MB disambiguation comments such as "(live)") are now explicitly skipped before any mutation occurs.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items:
H1 comment-span relocation — on release-group pages MusicBrainz may inject a `( … )` directly inside the h1, placing the alias text (with surrounding parentheses) on the same line as the entity title and the userscript action buttons. The new `relocateH1CommentSpan()` IIFE removes that span from the h1, extracts the alias label from the `` attribute (e.g. "Primary alias") and the alias text from the element's text content, then injects a replacement `

` styled in italics immediately above `

` (or directly after the h1 when no subheader is found). The rendered line reads «Primary alias: Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band at Athletic & Convocation Center», matching the requested layout: title line → alias line → subheader line. The injected element is removed and re-created on soft-navigation reloads to stay idempotent.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: saveTableDataToDisk filename — two glitches fixed: (1) `-filtered` suffix: the internal pageType for `link_type_id` pages (e.g. `artist-relationships-filtered`) included the `-filtered` marker in the filename even though the detail slug already identifies the relationship. Fixed by deriving `pageTypeSlug = pageType.replace(/-filtered$/, '')` and using that in the filename instead of the raw `pageType`. (2) Double underscores in detail slug: special characters such as `℗` (and their surrounding spaces) are converted to underscores then stripped, leaving consecutive underscores like `holds_phonographic_copyright__for`. Fixed by appending `.replace(/_+/g, '_')` as a final step in the `toSlug` helper. Example before: `MB-artist-relationships-filtered-holds_phonographic_copyright__for-87-….json.gz`. Example after: `MB-artist-relationships-holds_phonographic_copyright_for-87-….json.gz`.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: saveTableDataToDisk — informative filename format: the generated filename now follows `MB-<pageType>[-<detail>-]<rowCount>-<timestamp>.json.gz` instead of the bare `mb-<pageType>-<timestamp>.json.gz`. The optional `<detail>` slug is derived from the page context via a shared `toSlug()` helper (lowercase → whitespace to underscores → strip non-alphanumeric): (a) Multi-button pages (e.g. artist-releases with multiple fetch buttons): the label of the last clicked fetch button is slugified — tracked by the new module-level `lastClickedButtonLabel` variable which is written in `startFetchingProcess` alongside the `activeDefinition` update. (b) Filtered-relationship pages whose URL contains `?…link_type_id=…`: the quoted text inside the first `#content h2` (matching straight or curly quotes) is slugified — e.g. `"holds phonographic copyright (℗) for" relationships` → `holds_phonographic_copyright_for`. (c) All other pages: no detail segment. Examples: `MB-artist-releases-official_rgs-342-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`, `MB-artist-relationships-holds_phonographic_copyright_for-87-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`, `MB-artist-recordings-1204-2026-03-04T12-00-00.json.gz`.

showDownloadNotification — configurable message font size: a new `sa_ui_download_notification_font_size` setting (type `text`, default `1em`) has been added to the `configSchema` UI APPEARANCE section. The value is applied as `msg.style.fontSize` on the message `<div>` inside the download notification popup, making it independently adjustable from the rest of the popup UI (e.g. `0.85em`, `14px`, `1.1rem`).

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Page-type mismatch warning broken after 'Load from Disk' dialog rewrite — the pageType mismatch check in `loadTableDataFromDisk` referenced `loadFromDiskBtn` (wrong anchor for `Lib.showCustomConfirm`) and `fileInput` (the outer hidden input, not the dialog's `dialogFileInput`), so cancelling the confirm had no effect: the dialog stayed open with `rawData`/`rawFile` already set and the user could still proceed to render. Fixed by moving the check into `showLoadFilterDialog`'s Phase 1 handler (`dialogFileInput.onchange`), immediately after the required-fields validation. On cancel the handler now resets `rawData`/`rawFile` to null, restores the Load button, displays a cancellation message in `loadStatus`, and returns without revealing Phase 2. The dead check block in `loadTableDataFromDisk` was replaced with a comment noting the moved location.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Header-text cleaning — all `replace(/[⇅▲▼]/g, '')` call sites now also strip the `📊` unique-values button emoji from column header text before comparison or export. Affected sites: CSV export header scan (line ~2890), JSON export header scan (~2998), Org-Mode export header scan (~3068), synthetic-column existence check (~9133), MB-Name column existence check (~9151), single-table sort column-name derivation (~2535), multi-table sort column-name derivation (~2042, which also strips superscript digits), and the sort-state persistence column-name read (~8661, unicode-range form). The two call sites that already included `📊` (getCleanColName at ~8983 and the stats-panel path at ~11971) were correct and left unchanged.

Sub-table filter status — singular 'row' when exactly one row matches: `✓ Filtered 1 rows […]` now correctly reads `✓ Filtered 1 row […]`. Implemented via a ternary `${rowsInTable === 1 ? 'row' : 'rows'}` at the single affected call site (~8986).

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Save-to-Disk filename disambiguation: the generated filename now includes an extra slug between the `mb-${pageType}` prefix and the timestamp so that files saved from different button contexts or query-string variants of the same page type are immediately distinguishable without opening them. Two cases are handled: (1) Multi-button pages (e.g. artist-releases with 'Official RGs', 'Non-official RGs', 'Official VA RGs', 'Non-official VA RGs' buttons): when `activeDefinition.buttons.length > 1` and `activeDefinition.label` is set, the clicked button's label is lowercased, spaces are converted to underscores, and all non-alphanumeric characters are stripped to form the suffix (e.g. `mb-artist-releases-official_rgs-2026-03-04T…json.gz`). (2) Query-string pages filtered by `link_type_id` (e.g. `/area/…/releases?direction=1&link_type_id=835`): the text content of the first `#content h3` element is lowercased and slug-converted to form the suffix (e.g. `mb-area-releases-recorded_at-2026-03-04T…json.gz`). Pages that match neither condition continue to use the original `mb-${pageType}-${timestamp}.json.gz` format.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: normalizeExtractedText — Step 3: remove spurious space before comma separators between list items. MusicBrainz area, label, and catalog-number columns render multiple values separated by a bare comma+newline (e.g. '<bdi>Honolulu\n</bdi>,\n<a …>') which, after the existing join-and-collapse pipeline, produced an artificial space before the comma: 'Honolulu , Hawaii'. This caused both column-filter matching and highlight-span placement to diverge from what the browser renders ('Honolulu, Hawaii'), so a user typing 'Honolulu, Hawaii' would get no match and no highlighting even though the row was visually correct. Fixed by appending '.replace(/ ,/g, ",")' as a new Step 3 in normalizeExtractedText, applied after whitespace collapse (Step 1) and bracket-interior-space removal (Step 2). The fix is safe because no MusicBrainz data format uses an intentional space before a comma. All four affected column types confirmed: area lists ('Honolulu, Hawaii, United States'), comment-disambiguated items ('Ithaca (city), New York'), multi-label columns ('Columbia (…), Sony BMG Music Entertainment (…)'), and catalog-number columns ('CBS S 65480, S 65480'). The stale orphaned JSDoc stub ('Helper to get visible text only…') that preceded the real block was also removed.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values dropdown — count badge alignment: all '(n)' badges within a single column dropdown now share a uniform width derived from the digit count of the largest occurrence count in that column. Badges are rendered in a monospace font with right-aligned text so the numbers line up vertically regardless of their digit count (e.g. '   (12)' above '(21345)'). The width is computed per column open via 'maxDigits = String(maxCount).length' and 'badgeChWidth = maxDigits + 2' (adding the two parentheses), applied as 'min-width: Nch' on the badge span. The previous fixed 'min-width: 1.6em' and 'text-align: center' are replaced by the dynamic ch-unit width with 'text-align: right'. The 'font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums' property (redundant with monospace) has been removed.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values dropdown — occurrence count badge: each entry in the unique-values dropdown now shows a styled '(n)' badge to its left indicating how many currently visible rows contain that value. The badge is purely presentational (aria-hidden); clicking an entry or pressing Enter copies only the raw column value into the filter field, exactly as before. The badge colors are configurable via two new settings: 'sa_uniq_count_color' (text color, default dark-black #111111) and 'sa_uniq_count_bg' (background color, default light-yellow #fffacd), both exposed as color pickers in the FILTER SETTINGS section of the configuration UI.

Unique-values dropdown — internal value tracking changed from a Set to a Map so that each distinct value's occurrence count is recorded in a single pass over the visible tbody rows, with no additional DOM traversal overhead.

Unique-values dropdown — 'renderItems' updated: the non-filter branch now uses 'item.appendChild(document.createTextNode(v))' instead of 'item.textContent = v' so that the count badge node that was already appended to the item is preserved when the item is rendered without an active quickfilter string.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Filter highlighting — cross-tag match highlighting now works for ALL three filter levels (global, sub-table, and column). The `highlightText` function (used by global and column filters) and the inline highlighting block inside `applySubFilter` (used by sub-table filters) both previously used a per-text-node TreeWalker approach that silently produced no highlight span for any search term whose characters spanned an inline element boundary (e.g. `<a>`, `<bdi>`, `<span>`). Both are now replaced by calls to the new shared `highlightCrossTag(root, regex, className)` helper introduced in this release.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Filter highlighting — new `highlightCrossTag(root, regex, className)` helper: concatenates the plain text of all text nodes within `root`, finds all regex matches in the concatenated string, then replaces each affected text node with a DocumentFragment whose segments are either plain `Text` nodes (outside matches) or `<span class="mb-*-highlight">` elements (inside matches). Text nodes are processed in reverse document order so that earlier nodes' DOM positions are never invalidated by later mutations. Shared by `highlightText` (global and column filters) and `applySubFilter` (sub-table filters).

Filter highlighting — cross-tag match highlighting: the `highlightText` function has been replaced by a new two-function pair (`highlightText` + `highlightCrossTag`). The new implementation concatenates the plain text of all text nodes within each `<td>`, finds all regex matches in the concatenated string, and then replaces each affected text node with a DocumentFragment whose segments are either plain `Text` nodes (outside matches) or `<span class="mb-*-highlight">` elements (inside matches). This correctly highlights search terms that span across multiple sibling or nested inline elements (e.g. `<a>`, `<bdi>`, `<span>`) — previously such cross-tag matches produced no visible highlight even though the row was correctly shown/hidden.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Load-from-Disk dialog — completely redesigned as a three-phase workflow: (1) Load: the user picks a file which is read and parsed entirely into memory with no filter applied; a spinner appears on the button during reading and a '✅ Loaded N rows from filename' status line is shown below the buttons when done. (2) Filter: revealed only after a successful load; the user may optionally enter a filter expression (with Case Sensitive / Regular Expression / Exclude Matches toggles); clicking 'Filter Data' counts matching rows in memory without touching the DOM and shows the count ('N of T rows match → will be rendered' or 'N rows will be rendered (M excluded)') below the button. (3) Render: revealed only when the filter count is > 0; clicking 'Render Data' calls the existing loadTableDataFromDisk() with the cached File object and the chosen filter parameters, shows a 'Rendering…' spinner, and closes the dialog when rendering completes. The dialog creates its own private file input so it is completely decoupled from the always-visible prefilter bar. Alt+L / Alt+F / Alt+R keyboard shortcuts activate each phase button respectively; Escape dismisses the dialog at any phase.
Load-from-Disk dialog — in-memory row counting for the Filter phase uses a lightweight HTML-strip helper (regex, no DOM construction) making it fast even for large datasets.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values column dropdown — quickfilter bar: a sticky 'Quick filter…' input field is now rendered at the top of every unique-values panel (the 📊 dropdown). The field is auto-focused when the panel opens so the user can type immediately. As the user types, the item list is narrowed in real time to only show values whose text contains the filter string (case-insensitive). The matching substring is highlighted with configurable foreground and background colors (new settings: 'Unique-Values Quickfilter Highlight Color' and 'Unique-Values Quickfilter Highlight Background' in the 🎨 Filter Highlight Colors section of Settings). A clickable '×' button appears (fades in) to the right of the input field when there is text; clicking it clears the filter and restores all items, keeping focus in the input. Pressing Escape once clears the filter (if non-empty); pressing Escape a second tgime (empty field) closes the panel. Arrow keys / Home / End / Enter continue to navigate and select the currently visible (filtered) items.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items:
Filtering — cross-tag text matching now works correctly: filter strings that span multiple inline HTML elements (e.g. 'Bruce Springsteen & The E Street Band' spread across two <a> elements and a bare text node) previously produced no results because getCleanColumnText / getCleanVisibleText joined the raw text-node values — each carrying its own trailing newlines and indentation — with a plain space, yielding a string such as 'Bruce Springsteen
    &
    The E Street Band' that never matched the user query. Fixed by appending .replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim() to the joined result in both helpers so all whitespace runs (including 
, 	, and multi-space indentation) collapse to a single space before any comparison. The HTML structure is left completely intact for display/rendering purposes; only the string used for the match/highlight test is normalised.

Unique-values column dropdown — script-tag JSON blobs (e.g. release-events and author-roles data embedded by MusicBrainz as <script type='application/json'> inside table cells) are now excluded from the dropdown: openUniqDrop previously used cell.textContent.trim() which includes the raw JSON. It now calls getCleanColumnText() which already skips <script> subtrees and also returns whitespace-normalised text, giving cleaner and shorter display labels consistent with what the filter input matches against.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Prevent unicodechar table popup when pressing Ctrl-M in text fields.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — Enter key now selects the keyboard-focused item: the original keydown listener on the 📊 button (attached at element-creation time) was firing before the per-invocation `kh` handler (registered inside openUniqDrop). When Enter was pressed with the panel open and an item highlighted via ArrowDown/ArrowUp, the original listener called openUniqDrop() which toggled the panel closed and cleared _uniqDropOwner, so kh saw no owner and returned early without applying the value. Fixed by guarding the original listener: if ev.key === 'Enter' and the panel is already open for this button (_uniqDropOwner === uniqBtn), the original listener returns immediately and lets kh handle selection.

Column header rendering — ▼ and 📊 no longer overlap on narrow columns (e.g. 'Year'): the previous float:right layout for the 📊 button did not widen the table cell, so the sort icons and 📊 overlapped on short column names. Replaced the float with a .mb-col-hdr-flex (display:flex; white-space:nowrap; width:100%) wrapper div that contains ⇅ colName ▲▼ 📊 in order; the 📊 button uses margin-left:auto to push itself to the right edge, and the flex container forces the column wide enough to show all symbols without overlap.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Unique-values column dropdown: a right-aligned '📊' button is now added to every column header cell by makeTableSortableUnified. Clicking it (or pressing Enter/Space while it has focus) opens a fixed-position scrollable panel (#mb-col-uniq-dropdown) listing all distinct non-empty values present in the column's currently visible tbody rows, sorted case-insensitively. Selecting a value (mouse click or keyboard Enter) writes it into the column's mb-col-filter-input and fires an immediate filter pass via a synthetic input event. The panel is a DOM singleton created lazily on first use.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Unique-values dropdown — mouse-wheel scroll no longer closes the panel: the global scroll close-listener now checks ev.target and skips closing when the scroll originates inside the dropdown element itself.

Unique-values dropdown — ArrowDown/ArrowUp now scroll through the full list: moveFocus() now directly manipulates drop.scrollTop to keep the focused item visible within the panel's own overflow container instead of calling el.scrollIntoView(), which was scrolling the page rather than the panel and caused navigation to stall at the last initially-visible item.

Unique-values dropdown — re-render safety: '📊' is stripped from colName (in headers.forEach) and from getCleanColName() so repeated calls to makeTableSortableUnified do not accumulate the emoji in column labels.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Fix ghost sidebar toggle handle: The sidebar toggle handle is now positioned dynamically via JS (updateHandlePosition) instead of relying on a hard-coded CSS 'right: 240px'. The root cause was that toggleAutoResizeColumns changes the sidebar from its default right-floated layout to 'position: sticky; left: 0', making the sidebar appear at the LEFT viewport edge during horizontal scrolling — so the fixed handle at 'right: 240px' ended up floating over the content area rather than being adjacent to the sidebar. updateHandlePosition() reads the sidebar's live getBoundingClientRect() on every scroll, resize, and toggle event and sets handle.style.right = (window.innerWidth - sidebar.left) px, keeping the handle flush against the sidebar's actual rendered left boundary at all times regardless of scroll position or CSS position changes. The CSS 'transition: right 0.3s ease' and the '.handle-collapsed { right: 0 !important }' rule have been removed to avoid CSS/JS fights on high-frequency scroll events.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Global filter: the 'sa_filter_focus_prefix' (default '🔍 ') is now permanently visible in the global filter field at all times — focused or not, empty or not. The field is initialized with the prefix on creation and every programmatic reset (fresh fetch, disk load, clear-all, ✕ button) restores it to prefix-only rather than to an empty string. This is different from column filter inputs, where the prefix appears only while the field has focus. All values read from the global filter field pass through stripFilterPrefix() before use, so the invariant is transparent to all filtering logic.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactoring
items: The focus and blur event listeners on the global filter input have been removed. The blur listener's secondary responsibility (calling runFilter() after stripping the prefix) is no longer needed because the prefix is never stripped on blur. The keydown guard (guardFilterPrefixKeydown) and the prefix-safety-net inside the input listener remain.

sections.label: ✨ Enhancements
items: Setting 'sa_col_filter_focus_prefix' renamed to 'sa_filter_focus_prefix'. The decorative search-icon prefix now applies to the global filter field as well as column filter fields. Both types of filter input show the prefix while focused and strip it before using the value as a filter query.

Global filter: the field now gains the same focus-mode behaviour as column filters — the configured prefix (default '🔍 ') is prepended to the placeholder (or to the value if a filter string is already present) on focus, and stripped on blur. The keydown guard prevents Backspace/Delete/ArrowLeft/Home/Ctrl+A from reaching the prefix characters. Border colour continues to signal active state (no persistent background tint on blur, unlike column filters).
Escape key on the global filter now behaves like on column filters: first press retains the prefix and clears only the user-entered text; second press blurs the field.

Ctrl+G (focus global filter shortcut) now positions the cursor at the end of the editable content after the prefix, instead of calling select() which would have included the prefix characters in the selection.
The '✕' clear button for the global filter now resets the placeholder to the focus-state variant (prefix + base text) when the field remains focused after clearing.

sections.label: ♻️ Refactoring
items: The four column-filter-specific helpers (stripColFilterPrefix, applyColFilterFocusStyle, removeColFilterFocusStyle, guardColFilterPrefixKeydown) have been replaced by four generalized equivalents: getFilterFocusPrefix(), stripFilterPrefix(value), applyFilterFocusStyle(input, blurPlaceholder, focusBg?), removeFilterFocusStyle(input, idlePlaceholder, hasActiveBg?), and guardFilterPrefixKeydown(input, e). The old names are retained as deprecated const aliases so no existing call site breaks. All internal call sites have been updated to the new names.

sections.label: 🐛 Bug Fixes
items: Load from Disk — RegExp pre-filter now correctly applied: when the 'Regular Expression' checkbox in the Load dialog was checked, no rows were loaded at all.
